Furthermore, we apply our adapted OVD to the Unseen Object Instance Segmentation (UOIS) task, showing that high-quality box prompts from our optimized detector, when fed into Segment Anything Model 2 (SAM 2), are sufficient to achieve state-of-the-art performance with only a few box-annotated training images. Code and trained models are at https://github.com/cvlab-stonybrook/PromptAdaptOVD.

title: VidMP3: Video Editing by Representing Motion with Pose and Position Priors
authors: Sandeep Mishra, Oindrila Saha, Alan C. Bovik
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Motion-preserved video editing is crucial for creators, particularly in scenarios that demand flexibility in both the structure and semantics of swapped objects. Despite its potential, this area remains underexplored. Existing diffusion-based editing methods excel in structure-preserving tasks, using dense guidance signals to ensure content integrity. While some recent methods attempt to address structure-variable editing, they often suffer from issues such as temporal inconsistency, subject identity drift, and the need for human intervention. To address these challenges, we introduce VidMP3, a novel approach that leverages pose and position priors to learn a generalized motion representation from source videos. Our method enables the generation of new videos that maintain the original motion while allowing for structural and semantic flexibility. Both qualitative and quantitative evaluations demonstrate the superiority of our approach over existing methods.

title: AdCorDA: Classifier Refinement via Adversarial Correction and Domain Adaptation
authors: Lulan Shen, Ali Edalati, Xiangyu Li, Brett H. Meyer, Warren Gross, James J. Clark
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Improving neural network performance often requires significant computational resources and architectural modifications. We propose AdCorDA (Adversarial Correction and Domain Adaptation), a lightweight yet effective two-stage method that improves model accuracy with minimal overhead. In the first stage, adversarial correction identifies misclassified training samples and replaces them with adversarially corrected versions, forming a refined training set. In the second stage, domain adaptation fine-tunes the model by adapting from the corrected training set back to the original one. Extensive experiments demonstrate that AdCorDA improves accuracy by over 5% on CIFAR, 1% on CINIC-10, and 1-2% on small subsets of ImageNet compared to standard fine-tuning. Our approach does not heavily rely on the neural network architecture and datasets, improving models' performances in most cases within just a few training epochs. Furthermore, it enhances robustness against adversarial attacks, making it a practical and effective refinement technique.

title: Not Only Grey Matter: OmniBrain for Robust Multimodal Classification of Alzheimer's Disease
authors: Ahmed Sharshar, Yasser Ashraf, Tameem Bakr, Salma Hassan, Hosam Elgendy, Mohammad Yaqub, Mohsen Guizani
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease affects over 55 million people world-wide and is projected to more than double by 2050, necessitating rapid, accurate, and scalable diagnostics. However, existing approaches are limited because they can-not achieve clinically acceptable accuracy, generalization across datasets, robustness to missing modalities, and explainability all at the same time. This inability to satisfy all these requirements simultaneously undermines their reliability in clinical settings. We propose OmniBrain, a mul-timodal framework that integrates brain MRI, radiomics, gene expression, and clinical data using a unified model with cross-attention and modality dropout. OmniBrain achieves 92.2±2.4% accuracy on the ANMerge dataset and generalizes to the MRI-only ADNI dataset with 70.4 ± 2.7% accuracy, outperforming unimodal and prior multimodal approaches. Explainability analyses highlight neuropatho-logically relevant brain regions and genes, enhancing clinical trust. OmniBrain offers a robust, interpretable, and practical solution for real-world Alzheimer's diagnosis.

title: Multi-Objective Optimization for Deep Neural Network Calibration
authors: Dexter Neo, Tsuhan Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: The rapid adoption of deep neural networks underscores an urgent need for models to be safe, trustworthy and well-calibrated. Despite recent advancements in network calibration, the optimal unification of techniques remains relatively unexplored. By framing the task as a multi-objective optimization problem, we demonstrate that unifying state-of-the-art methods can further boost calibration performance. We feature a total of seven state-of-the-art calibration algorithms and provide both theoretical and empirical motivation for their equal and weighted importance unification. We conduct experiments on both in-distribution and out-of-distribution computer vision benchmarks, investigating the speeds and contributions of different components.

title: A Survey on Reading Order, Table of Contents, and Structure Extraction in Document Analysis
authors: Simone Giovannini, Simone Marinai
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Understanding the structure of complex documents is a challenging goal in document analysis, encompassing tasks such as reading order identification, table of contents extraction, and hierarchical structure prediction. This survey has a twofold aim: first to categorize these tasks based on their problem formulation, identifying key challenges and defining the scope of each task. Second, to group the proposed methods for addressing these tasks according to the fundamental components they rely on. We analyze their methodologies, advantages, and limitations across various document types, highlighting recent advances in deep learning and multimodal processing. Additionally, we present comparative insights into different paradigms, such as graph neural networks and attention-based models, to identify promising directions for research in this area. Although we do not include a quantitative comparison of methods or datasets due to space constraints, this work serves as a first step toward a more comprehensive evaluation, aiming to highlight conceptual similarities and dif-ferences that can guide future research.

title: Document Haystack: A Long Context Multimodal Image/Document Understanding Vision LLM Benchmark
authors: Goeric Huybrechts, Srikanth Ronanki, Sai Muralidhar Jayanthi, Jack Fitzgerald, Srinivasan Veeravanallur
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: The proliferation of multimodal Large Language Models has significantly advanced the ability to analyze and understand complex data inputs from different modalities. However, the processing of long documents remains under-explored, largely due to a lack of suitable benchmarks. To address this, we introduce Document Haystack<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>Dataset: https://huggingface.co/datasets/AmazonScience/document-haystack<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>Code: https://github.com/amazon-science/document-haystack, a comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate the performance of Vision Language Models (VLMs) on long, visually complex documents. Document Haystack features documents ranging from 5 to 200 pages and strategically inserts pure text or multimodal text+image “needles” at various depths within the documents to challenge VLMs' retrieval capabilities. Comprising 400 document variants and a total of 8,250 questions, it is supported by an objective, automated evaluation framework. We detail the construction and characteristics of the Document Haystack dataset, present results from prominent VLMs and discuss potential research avenues in this area.

title: A GenAI-Powered Cybersecurity Mesh for Real-Time Risk Detection in Digital Payments
authors: Utham Kumar Anugula, Vijayanand Ananthanarayanan
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: The surge in digital payment systems has introduced significant challenges in securing real-time financial transactions against sophisticated cyber threats. This paper presents a GenAI-powered cybersecurity mesh architecture designed to detect and mitigate risks dynamically within decentralized digital payment ecosystems. Our approach integrates transformer-based deep learning models, explainable AI (XAI) mechanisms, and decentralized threat telemetry across a microservices-driven architecture. The system supports autonomous policy enforcement and real-time fraud detection using SHAP-explainable risk scores aggregated from user behavior, device fingerprinting, and transaction metadata. Deployed within a zero-trust security model, the mesh adapts to adversarial patterns through self-improving AI agents. Experimental results on simulated fintech datasets show over 96% detection accuracy with sub-200ms latency, demonstrating viability for high-throughput environments. The proposed architecture advances the state-of-the-art in real-time payment security and paves the way for scalable GenAI-driven threat response infrastructures in financial services.

title: An Integrated Model CINO-CNN-BiLSTM+Pooler for Tibetan Text Sentiment Classification
authors: Kunkun Li, Zhenzhou An
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Communication (ICAIRC)
abstract: This paper proposes a Tibetan text sentiment classification architecture (CINO-CNN-BiLSTM+Pooler) that integrates pre-trained models and multi-path features. Based on the minority language pre-trained model CINO, the proposed model extracted local features via CNN, captured contextual dependencies through BiLSTM, and incorporated global features processed by [CLS] token pooling, thereby achieving effective fusion of multi-dimensional semantic information. Systematic experiments were conducted on two Tibetan sentiment datasets, namely TSTD (three-category classification) and TL_SA (binary classification). The results demonstrated that: on the TSTD dataset, the CINO (large)-CNN-BiLSTM+Pooler model achieved an accuracy of 81.08% and an F1-score of 74.07%, which represented a significant improvement over the baseline model; on the TL_SA dataset, the CINO (base)-CNN-BiLSTM+Pooler model achieved an accuracy of 91.74% and an F1-score of 91.51%, which was better than the current optimal method. Experimental analysis showed that the multi-path feature fusion strategy effectively enhances the model's ability to represent complex Tibetan semantics, and the model shows excellent convergence and robustness.

title: Region-Aware Pose Modeling and Permutation Decoding for Signer-Independent Sign Language Recognition
authors: Sieu Tran, Duc Nguyen Minh, Truong Nguyen Thanh, Hao Vo, Thanh Duc Ngo, Tien Do, Khiem Le, Duy-Dinh Le
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Continuous Sign Language Recognition (CSLR) under signer-independent conditions remains a major challenge due to the high intra-class variability in body structure and articulation styles. While RGB-based systems dominate the field, pose-only input offers a lightweight and privacy-preserving alternative, though it has typically underperformed. This paper proposes a skeleton-only CSLR framework that addresses two core limitations of prior approaches: imbalanced regional features and rigid decoding constraints. Our method introduces region-specific GCN encoders and auxiliary gloss decoders for the hands, lips, and torso, encouraging semantically diverse and region-aware representations. Additionally, we replace CTC with a permutation-trained Transformer decoder that captures context-rich, bidirectional gloss dependencies without assuming monotonic alignment. Using only 2D pose input, our model achieves a word error rate of 13.5% on a signer-independent subset of the Isharah dataset, surpassing previous RGB-based methods on comparable splits. These results highlight the potential of region-aware pose modeling and permutation decoding for robust signer-independent CSLR.

title: A B-Spline Interpolator for Dual-Robot Mirror Milling of Thin-Walled Parts with Optimized Trajectories
authors: Guangxi Li, Zhizhen Ren, Qingpo Xu, Haitao Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: This article introduces an effective synchronous trajectory planning method for dual-robot mirror milling of thin-walled workpieces. First, the pose mapping relationship between the end-effectors is mathematically derived, and the mirror milling path is formulated using synchronized tool position paths for both robots. Subsequently, a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{C}^{\mathrm{3}}$</tex>-continuous corner smoothing algorithm is implemented, which ensures shared path parameters for both robots while eliminating angular velocity discontinuities at path junctions. The end-effector pose mapping relationship is maintained along the remaining path segments through adaptive modification of B-spline control polygon lengths. Additionally, a synchronous feedrate scheduling strategy is proposed based on the established dual-robot feedrate mapping principles. A constant feedrate is maintained along B-spline paths under multiple kinematic constraints, with smooth feedrate transitions achieved using S-shaped acceleration/deceleration profiles. Superior tracking performance for both robots is confirmed through simulations and experimental validations, resulting in significant improvements in wall thickness control precision during thin-walled component manufacturing.

title: A Novel AI-Enhanced Textile-Embedded Wearable Biosensor for Non-Invasive Vocal-Fold Monitoring
authors: Sajjad Hussain Mian, Amir Sohail, Izaz Ali Shah, Muhammad Shumail Malik, Hyoungsuk Yoo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: This paper presents a novel, AI-enhanced, textile-integrated spoof surface plasmon polariton (SSPP) biosensor for real-time, noninvasive monitoring of vocal-fold activity. The sensor is embedded in a soft smart choker, operates at 2.45 GHz (ISM band), and is interrogated by a software-defined radio (SDR) to measure minute phase variations induced by vocal-fold vibration. A combined driven-modal/eigenmode design flow yields a compact tapered SSPP structure with strong through-transmission and enhanced evanescent coupling. A cotton-based prototype fabricated with conductive adhesive tape and flexible SMA terminations was evaluated on volunteers. Recorded RF data were processed using a phase-demodulation/windowing pipeline and classified with lightweight multilayer perceptrons (MLPs), achieving 95.9 % mean accuracy (SD = 0.0092) under five-fold cross-validation. Specific absorption rate (SAR) analysis on a realistic human model measured 0.616 W/kg (1 g) and 0.163 W/kg (10 g), below IEEE limits, supporting long-term wear. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first SSPP wearable biosensor enabling vocal-fold monitoring, providing a foundation for intelligent, real-time laryngeal diagnostics.

title: Modeling the Impact of Urban Street Geometry on Automotive Radar-Communication Co-Existence
authors: Astitva Mehrotra, Ankit Kumar, Gourab Ghatak
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: We investigate the co-existence of millimeter wave (mm-wave) radar and communication features in vehicular networks with time and frequency resource sharing. First, we highlight the importance and applicability of signal to interference and noise ratio (SINR) as a metric to characterize the radar performance. Then, we characterize the probability of the joint event that the radar detection rate and the data communication rate exceed predetermined thresholds. We study the trends of the joint success probability either with timedivision or frequency-division resource sharing between the radar and the communication functionalities. Furthermore, we define and optimize a metric called the effective range resolution (ERR) that jointly takes into account the detection probability with the bandwidth dedicated to the radar system. Next, we analyze the performance of the co-existing system with respect to a dedicated system, which serves as a benchmark and employs mm-wave band and sub-6GHz band for radar transmission and datacommunication respectively. Our study highlights that although a frequency-division co-existence may improve radar detection probability due to reduced interference, it may eventually result in a higher ERR due to the reduced bandwidth. On the contrary, the time-division protocol is attractive in a city with fewer streets and closer targets. As the streets become more dense, or the target distance increases, the vehicles should switch to a frequencydivision protocol.

title: High-Performance 1200-V p-GaN Gate HEMT on Standard 650-V GaN-on-Si Epi-Wafer
authors: Hao Chang, Junjie Yang, Jingjing Yu, Yunhong Lao, Youyi Yin, Han Yang, Xuelin Yang, Jinyan Wang, Xiaosen Liu, Maojun Wang, Bo Shen, Jin Wei
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: There is a trend for electric vehicles to increase the bus voltage to 800-V, which drives the requirement for kilovolt GaN power devices. However, the mainstream GaN-on-Si power transistors are typically limited to a 650-V voltage rating. In this work, we develop a 1200-V p-GaN gate HEMT on a standard 650-V GaN-on-Si epi-wafer (buffer thickness <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim{\!}{\!}{\!}5~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m). To boost the device for 1200-V applications, a floating-substrate configuration is adopted, which enhances the breakdown voltage (BV) from 1068 to 2001 V. To address the notorious back-gating effect rooting from the floating substrate (i.e., the positive <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V} _{\text {SUB}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-induced buffer trapping and the consequent dynamic <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${R} _{\text {ON}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> degradation), a virtual body (VB) design is implemented where an AlGaN hole barrier layer is inserted underneath the 2DEG channel, holes injected from p-GaN gate accumulate and spread along the GaN/AlGaN interface to form a VB. The floating-substrate-induced buffer trapping is screened by the VB. Therefore, the device with VB exhibits a low dynamic <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${R} _{\text {ON}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>/static <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${R} _{\text {ON}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> ratio of 1.18 after 1200-V drain stress, showing a contrast to that of 2.25 in the device without VB. The device also sustains robust performance against prolonged reverse-bias testing with <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V} _{\text {DS-OFF}} =1200$</tex-math> </inline-formula> V, with negligible deviation in <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</small>-resistance and threshold voltage. Without a thick buffer or cost-prohibitive engineered substrate, this work demonstrates a cost-efficient approach toward 1200-V GaN-on-Si power transistors.

title: Preface
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 Fifth International Conference on Information Systems and Software Technologies (ICI2ST)

title: ConvertNet: Training-time Model Scaling for In-vehicle Continuous Learning
authors: Qinglong Zhang, Rui Han, Chi Harold Liu, Guoren Wang, Lydia Y. Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Vehicles are transforming from transportation means to mobile intelligent terminals, powered by artificial intelligence (AI) technologies such as large language (LLM) and vision large models (VLM). To maintain high learning accuracy in all situations, in-vehicle AI models encounter the acute need to continuously learn and dynamically retrain AI models on the fly. The volatile resource demands of such stochastic retraining jobs and inference jobs of higher priority however can not be accommodated simultaneously by existing training systems, which rely on pre-generated compressed models of fixed architecture. In this paper, we propose <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ConvertNet</monospace>, a novel continuous learning system that effectively scales up/down a compressed model through training-time neuron memorizer (TNM) - a max heap of neurons in tree structure. Based on TNM, <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ConvertNet</monospace> estimates the model's resource-accuracy trade-off per neuron, thus efficiently utilizing the limited available resources to scale up and (re)train the model to improve its accuracy. Evaluated on six representative in-vehicle scenarios, comparative experiments against eleven state-of-the-art techniques show that <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ConvertNet</monospace> achieves as much as 22.33% improvement in learning accuracy, and reduces energy consumption by 3.65x.

title: Service Function Chain Anomaly Detection Method for Cyber Threat Detection in Aviation Information System
authors: Yong Yang, Buhong Wang, Peng Luo, Jiwei Tian
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: The aviation information system is a large-scale critical national infrastructure that is rapidly evolving towards digitalization and intelligence in response to the growing demand for air traffic services. With the application of emerging network technologies such as cloud computing, edge computing, and network function virtualization (NFV), the openness and connectivity of the aviation information system are continually enhanced, while the attack surface for cyber threats is also expanding. In response to the cyber threats faced by the aviation information system, a service function chain (SFC) anomaly detection method based on Attention-LSTM-CoAE-SVDD-PSO (AMASS) is proposed. This approach incorporates an anomaly detection mechanism that processes prediction and reconstruction modules in parallel. First, the Attention-LSTM-based prediction module learns the temporal correlation of SFC data to facilitate SFC data forecasting. Second, the CoAE-based reconstruction module learns the spatial correlation of SFC data for data reconstruction. Finally, the SVDD-PSO-based classification module learns the characteristics of the prediction deviation and reconstruction deviation, adaptively determining the classification threshold to achieve unsupervised anomaly detection of SFC data. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively counter cyberattacks with varying attack patterns, intensities, and durations. Furthermore, comparisons with other methods proposed in the literature further validate the superior performance of AMASS in terms of detection rate, miss detection rate, and accuracy.

title: A Prototype Aware Sparse Residual MLP for Robust Hand Gesture Recognition From Multichannel EMG
authors: Thaneshwar Kumar Sahu, Pankaj Kumar Mishra, Saurabh Gupta
publish: IEEE Sensors Letters
abstract: Automated recognition of hand gestures from multichannel electromyography (MEMG) data is critical to intuitive human-machine interaction and prosthetic control. However, most current approaches are based on computationally intensive time frequency transformations or transformer based architectures, which restrict realtime deployment. MEMG classification remains challenging due to sensor noise, inter-subject variability, channel degradation, and difficulties in learning discriminative representations from high dimensional tabular features of EMG. In this paper, we propose the lightweight residual multilayer perceptron framework that combines prototype aware classification with supervised contrastive embedding learning, structured channel grouping, and a sparse gating mechanism to dynamically prune redundant neurons during training. Our model integrates channel dropout aware augmentation for enhanced robustness, GroupSE-based feature recalibration to enhance muscle activation selectivity, and a prototype distance-based head to provide stable class separation in the embedding space. A supervised contrastive loss encourages clustering of gesture representations while enforcing interclass separability. The SparseGate module progressively shuts off up to 70% of the hidden units, substantially reducing the computational cost without degrading accuracy. Extensive experiments conducted on a large MEMG dataset attains an overall accuracy of 98.26%, outperforming conventional MLPs, CNN, and transformer architectures. Additional evaluations highlight strong robustness to channel dropout and noise corruption, making the proposed approach suitable for realtime, resource constrained EMG interfaces.

title: Front Cover
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Journal on Miniaturization for Air and Space Systems

title: Gain Modulation using Shunting Inhibition in a Biased Subthreshold CMOS Dendrite
authors: Luke Parker, Suma G. Cardwell, Frances S. Chance, Scott Koziol
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Circuits and Systems
abstract: The study of computational properties in biological nervous systems serves two important purposes: it examines the physical mechanisms underlying biological computation while also providing new models that can be utilized in bio-inspired artificial systems. We analyze a dendrite circuit designed to model and implement shunting inhibition, a mechanism in which the dominant effect is a change in transmembrane conductance that produces multiplicative or divisive effects in the membrane potential’s response when integrating synaptic inputs. Building upon our previous work, this analysis focuses on the subthreshold-linear operating region of the dendrite circuit while also showing the effects of circuit biasing, which introduces the ability for approximating linear equations with an intercept term rather than performing multiplication alone. The real-time interaction between shunting conductance and biologically realistic current input is also studied, demonstrating the multiplicative effect of shunting inhibition using time-varying inputs of different modalities. The dendrite circuit is implemented on an analog VLSI platform and uses subthreshold transistors to emulate the electrical properties of membrane channels. This implementation serves as an analog computational unit for neuromorphic system design, exemplifying how biophysical mechanisms in dendrites can inform analog neuromorphic computation.

title: Robot Metacognition: Decision Making with Confidence for Tool Invention
authors: Ajith Anil Meera, Poppy Collis, Polina Arbuzova, Abian Torres, Paul F Kinghorn, Ricardo Sanz, Pablo Lanillos
publish: IEEE Intelligent Systems
abstract: Robots today often miss a key ingredient of truly intelligent behavior: the ability to reflect on their own cognitive processes and decisions. In humans, this self-monitoring or metacognition is crucial for learning, decision making and problem solving. For instance, they can evaluate how confident they are in performing a task, thus regulating their own behavior and allocating proper resources. Taking inspiration from neuroscience, we propose a robot metacognition architecture centered on confidence (a second-order judgment on decisions) and we demonstrate it on the use case of autonomous tool invention. We propose the use of confidence as a metacognitive measure within the robot decision making scheme. Confidence-informed robots can evaluate the reliability of their decisions, improving their robustness during real-world physical deployment. This form of robotic metacognition emphasizes embodied action monitoring as a means to achieve better informed decisions. We also highlight potential applications and research directions for robot metacognition.

title: Physics-Aware Initialization Refinement in Code-Aided EM for Blind Channel Estimation
authors: Chin-Hung Chen, Ivana Nikoloska, Wim van Houtum, Yan Wu, Alex Alvarado
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: This paper addresses the well-known local maximum problem of the expectation-maximization (EM) algorithm in blind inter-symbol interference (ISI) channel estimation. This problem primarily results from phase and shift ambiguity due to poor initialization, which a blind EM estimation is inherently unable to distinguish. We propose an effective initialization refinement algorithm that utilizes the decoder output as a metric for model selection. Finite candidate models are generated based on the physical properties of the channel and modulation format, incorporating a joint detection of phase and shift ambiguities. Our results show that the proposed algorithm significantly reduces the number of local maximum cases to nearly onethird for a 3-tap ISI channel under highly uncertain initial conditions. The improvement becomes more pronounced as initial errors increase and the channel memory grows. When used in a turbo equalizer, the proposed algorithm is required only in the first turbo iteration, which limits any complexity increase with subsequent iterations.

title: Cost-Effective Sub-THz Signal Generation and Transmission Using a Directly Modulated Laser
authors: M. Botella-Campos, P. T. Dat, K. Akahane, J. Mora, B. Ortega
publish: IEEE Photonics Journal
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive experimental analysis of a cost-effective and spectrally efficient approach for sub-terahertz (sub-THz) signal generation and transmission, consisting of a directly modulated laser (DML) combined with carrier-suppressed Mach–Zehnder modulator (CS-MZM) in a radio-over-fiber (RoF) architecture. The use of optical and electrical filtering by means of a fiber Bragg grating (FBG) and an electrical bandpass filter (EBPF), respectively, permits to mitigate undesirable optical and electrical signal components and reduce fiber dispersion effects. For the proof-of-concept demonstration, a standard-compliant 5G new radio 64-QAM OFDM signal at different intermediate frequencies was successfully transmitted over a fiber-wireless system in the W band (75-110 GHz). The system characterization under different hardware configurations was comprehensively analyzed to give insights for the system design under different operation conditions. The results demonstrate improved sideband rejection, minimized intermodulation distortion, and high spectral purity, making this technique a promising solution for future 6G fronthaul deployments.

title: A Modeling Attack Resistant PUF-enabled Robust Authentication and Key Agreement Scheme for Industrial Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: Sungjin Yu, Youngho Par
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Industrial wireless sensor networks (IWSNs) are emerging as a new network paradigm in Internet of Things (IoT) for smart manufacturing, monitoring, and automation systems. IWSNs seamlessly integrate with IoT platforms and cloud services to support advanced services such as remote monitoring, AI-powered analytics, and advanced automated control. However, these systems can be vulnerable to potential security attacks because an adversary can attempt to delete, modify, intercept, and block the transmitted messages over an insecure channel. Besides cybersecurity attacks, IoT devices may be vulnerable to physical security attacks because they are deployed in unattended environments. To resolve these security threats and weaknesses, robust and lightweight authentication and key agreement (AKA) schemes are essential. Many researchers have recently presented a PUF-based secure and lightweight AKA scheme for IWSN-based IoT. Unfortunately, we demonstrated that the previous scheme is susceptible to potential security threats and does not guarantee the necessary security functionalities. Moreover, the existing PUF-based AKA schemes may be vulnerable to machine learning (ML)-based modeling attacks. Thus, we propose a ML-based modeling attack-resistant PUF-enabled robust AKA scheme for IWSN-enabled IoT to improve the security flaws of the previous scheme, called PUF-IWSN. We evaluate the security of PUF-IWSN by performing formal security analysis such as AVISPA simulation and ROR oracle model. We demonstrate the analysis of performance comparison between PUF-IWSN and its related schemes. We present the implementation to evaluate the security of existing PUFs and the hybrid PUF against ML-based modeling attacks. Consequently, PUF-IWSN ensures superior efficiency and security than the previous schemes and can be suitable for practical IWSN-enabled IoT systems.

title: CECT-Mamba: A Hierarchical Contrast-Enhanced-Aware Model for Pancreatic Tumor Subtyping from Multi-Phase CECT
authors: Zhifang Gong, Shuo Gao, Ben Zhao, Yingjing Xu, Yijun Yang, Shenghong Ju, Guangquan Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Contrast-enhanced computed tomography (CECT) is the primary imaging technique that provides valuable spatial-temporal information about lesions, enabling the accurate diagnosis and subclassification of pancreatic tumors. However, the high heterogeneity and variability of pancreatic tumors still pose substantial challenges for precise subtyping diagnosis. Previous methods fail to effectively explore the contextual information across multiple CECT phases commonly used in radiologists' diagnostic workflows, thereby limiting their performance. In this paper, we introduce, for the first time, an automatic way to combine the multi-phase CECT data to discriminate between pancreatic tumor subtypes, among which the key is using Mamba with promising learnability and simplicity to encourage both temporal and spatial modeling from multi-phase CECT. Specifically, we propose a dual hierarchical contrast-enhanced-aware Mamba module incorporating two novel spatial and temporal sampling sequences to explore intra and inter-phase contrast variations of lesions. A similarity-guided refinement module is also imposed into the temporal scanning modeling to emphasize the learning on local tumor regions with more obvious temporal variations. Moreover, we design the space complementary integrator and multi-granularity fusion module to encode and aggregate the semantics across different scales, achieving more efficient learning for subtyping pancreatic tumors. The experimental results on an inhouse dataset of 270 clinical cases achieve an accuracy of 97.4% and an AUC of 98.6% in distinguishing between pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) and pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (PNETs), demonstrating its potential as a more accurate and efficient tool.

title: On the Generalization of Optical Flow: Quantifying Robustness to Dataset Shifts
authors: Katrin Bauer, Andrés Bruhn, Jenny Schmalfuss
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Optical flow models are commonly evaluated by their ability to accurately predict the apparent motionfrom image sequence data. Though not seen during training, this evaluation data generally shares the training data's characteristics because it stems from the same distribution, i.e., it is in-distribution (ID) with the training data. However, when models are applied in the real world, the test data characteristics may be shifted, i.e., out-of-distribution (OOD), compared to the training data. For optical flow models, the generalization to dataset shifts is much less reported than the typical accuracy on ID data. In this work we close this gap and systematically investigate the generalization of optical flow models by disentangling accuracy and robustness to dataset shifts with a new effective robustness metric. We evaluate a testbed of 20 models on six established optical flow datasets. Across models and datasets, we find that ID accuracy can be used as a predictor for OOD performance, but certain models generalize better than this trend suggests. While our analysis reveals that model generalization capabilities declined in recent years, we also find that more training data and smart architectural choices can improve generalization. Across tested models, effective robustness to dataset shifts is high for models that avoid attention mechanisms and favor multi-scale designs. Code is available at https://github.com/cv-stuttgartIOF-EffectiveRobustness.

title: MRDNet: A Manifold-Aware Rotated Detector Network for Remote Sensing Images
authors: Yuxin Luo, Hongwei Ding, Zhuguan Liang, Jinhua Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Communication (ICAIRC)
abstract: Due to the arbitrary orientation, dense arrangement and scale diversity of targets in remote sensing images, rotating target detection faces challenges such as insensitive feature extraction, interference from adjacent targets and loss function mismatch. In this paper, we propose MRDNet, a manifold aware rotation detection network. This method captures the context information of different directions through the backbone of CEResNet to enhance the feature discrimination ability of the network for rotating targets. Secondly, the BDFN bidirectional dense feature network is proposed to effectively improve the detection robustness in dense scenes through the bidirectional interaction mechanism. According to the geometric characteristics of remote sensing targets, the MARS loss function is innovatively proposed to effectively solve the geometric mismatch problem of loss. Experiments show that the proposed method achieves 72.29% and 88.3% mAP on DOTA and HRSC2016 datasets, respectively. It significantly improves the performance of rotating target detection” especially in complex direction scenes.

title: UKMADNet: Unified KAN-Based Multi-Scale Attention Network with Deformable Downsampling for Jaw Cyst Segmentation
authors: Zile Fang, Zhitao Zhang, Yiting Jin, Mingjie Jiang, Chao Li
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of jaw cysts is crucial for clinical diagnosis and treatment planning. However, existing methods often struggle with delineating complex boundaries and detecting minute lesions due to insufficient feature fusion and inadequate edge feature modeling. To address these limitations, this paper proposes UKMADNet, a novel U-shaped network based on the Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN), incorporating multi-scale attention mechanisms and deformable convolutions. UKMADNet integrates three key innovations: (1) a deformable KAN encoder for adaptive small-lesion feature extraction, (2) a multi-scale fusion module with channel and spatial attention mechanisms during upsampling, and (3) a deformable attention gating mechanism to optimize skip connections, further enhanced by multidimensional attention at the bottleneck. Extensive experiments were conducted on a jaw cyst dataset comprising 2,592 CT scans, UKMADNet achieves a dice score of 94.47%, IoU of 89.60%, and HD95 of 0.48, outperforming baseline U-KAN.

title: Med-VLM: Enhancing Medical Image Segmentation Accuracy Through Vision-Language Model
authors: Yihao Zhao, Enhao Zhong, Cuiyun Yuan, Yang Li, Man Zhao, Chunxia Li, Jun Hu, Wei Liu, Chenbin Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: We proposed Med-VLM (Medical Vision-language Model), an innovative approach that leverages textual descriptions of organs to enhance segmentation accuracy in medical images. Existing medical image segmentation methods face several challenges: (1) Current medical segmentation models often fail to effectively incorporate valuable prior knowledge, such as detailed descriptions of organ locations and characteristics. (2) Most text-visual models prioritize target identification, rather than focusing on enhancing overall accuracy. (3) While some approaches attempt to use prior knowledge for accuracy enhancement, they often fall short in effectively incorporating pre-trained models. To overcome these limitations, Med-VLM introduced several key innovations: low-rank adaptation, authoritative descriptions, BioBERT weights, and a feature mixer. We conducted a comprehensive evaluation of MedVLM using three authoritative medical image datasets, covering the segmentation of various human body parts. Our method demonstrated superior performance compared to existing state-of-the-art approaches, including Lvit, Med-SAM, SAM, and nnUnet. We designed a series of ablation experiments, which systematically assessed the contribution of each component of Med-VLM, providing insights into the model's performance characteristics.

title: AI in Cybersecurity: Fighting Hackers With Smarter Technologies
authors: Jampana Ruthvika, Utkarsha Pacharaney, Ankita Deorao Tijare, Palak Nandane, Sai Praveena Nulu, Gaurav Mahakalkar
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: As cyber threats evolve in complexity and scale, con-ventional security measures increasingly fall short in effectively defending digital assets. Artificial intelligence (AI) has emerged as a powerful ally in cybersecurity, offering advanced capabilities that enhance threat detection, automate incident response, and enable proactive defense strategies. This article examines the pivotal role of AI technologies-such as machine learning, deep learning, and behavioral analytics-in strengthening cyberse-curity systems against modern attacks. It highlights key AI-driven methodologies employed to detect anomalies, predict emerging threats, and respond swiftly, while also addressing the challenges and ethical considerations in implementing AI-based security solutions. The discussion extends to future directions where autonomous AI agents and explainable AI can further revolutionize the fight against cyber adversaries. Overall, this work underscores AI's transformative impact as a smarter technological force in safeguarding against hackers.

title: Dental Disease Detection Using EfficientNet
authors: Radha Wasudeo Wande, Utkarsha Pacharaney, Roshni Rathour
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: One of the biggest threats to global wellbeing is dental disease, which includes caries, gingivitis, tartar, and oral ulcers. For practical discussion and care, an accurate and timely identification of these illnesses is essential. Conventional diagnostic method acting is frequently sentence-by-sentence, human error-prone, and highly skilled. This theme proposes an AI-based dental disease signal detection organisation utilising the EfficientNet model to address these restrictions. EfficientNet, a state-of-the-art deep learning architecture, is known for its scalability, computational efficiency, and high performance in medical imaging practical applications. The arrangement aims to detect and sort out multiple dental diseases from panoramic X-ray images, which improves diagnostic accuracy with reduced computational overhead. In this study, we evaluate several fluctuations of the EfficientNet model, including EfficientNet-B0, B1, B2 and B3. Final Result shows that efficientnet-B0 achieves an accuracy of 93%, while efficientnet-B1, B2 and B3 achieved accuracies of 94. 47%, 95% and 90% respectively. The finding certifies the electric potential of EfficientNet-B2 in allowing honest, automated dental disease spotting, thereby enhancing symptomatic accuracy and reducing the workload of dental professionals. The nominee system promises to revolutionise dental diagnostics by extending a scalable, monetary value-effective, and efficient solution for early spotting and treatment of dental diseases.

title: LLMs as NAO Robot 3D Motion Planners
authors: Riccardo Catalini, Giacomo Salici, Federico Biagi, Guido Borghi, Luigi Biagiotti, Roberto Vezzani
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: In this study, we demonstrate the capabilities of state-of-the-art Large Language Models (LLMs) in teaching social robots to perform specific actions within a 3D environment. Specifically, we introduce the use of LLMs to generate sequences of 3D joint angles - in both zero-shot and one-shot prompting - that a humanoid robot must follow to perform a given action. This work is driven by the growing demand for intuitive interactions with social robots: indeed, LLMs could empower non-expert users to operate and benefit from robotic systems effectively. Additionally, this method leverages the possibility to generate synthetic data without effort, enabling privacy-focused use cases. To evaluate the output quality of seven different LLMs, we conducted a blind user study to compare the pose sequences. Participants were shown videos of the well-known NAO robot performing the generated actions and were asked to identify the intended action and choose the best match with the original instruction from a collection of candidates created by different LLMs. The results highlight that the majority of LLMs are indeed capable of planning correct and complete recognizable actions, showing a novel perspective of how AI can be applied to social robotics.

title: SOC Estimation Based on OCV-Ah with Differential Amplifier Circuit
authors: Xiongwen Liu, Kaijie Zhang, Yi Guo
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: In the Battery Management System (BMS), the accurate estimation of State of Charge (SOC) is a core link to ensure the safe operation of lithium-ion batteries, extend their service life, and improve the reliability of equipment. Traditional SOC estimation methods are often affected by factors such as noise from the lithium-ion battery charging and discharging, ambient temperature fluctuations, and circuit interference, resulting in insufficient sampling accuracy that falls short of the requirements for high-precision applications. To this end, this study proposes an SOC estimation scheme combining Open Circuit Voltage (OCV) sampling via a differential amplifier circuit and Ampere-Hour (AH) integration. The aim is to improve the accuracy of OCV detection by optimizing the signal sampling stage, thereby enhancing the SOC estimation precision.

title: An Explainable Machine Learning Framework for Predicting Software Defects in Large-Scale Software Systems
authors: Srikanth Kavuri
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: Software-intensive systems are rapidly growing in size and complexity, and early defect prediction is very important in enhancing reliability and minimizing the costs of maintenance. Traditional defect prediction models focus on performance but offer limited transparency, thereby hampering their application. This research introduces a transparent ML model for large-scale software defect prediction using the JM1 dataset. The framework also includes preprocessing systematic data, addressing class imbalance using SMOTE, and scaling features to improve learning efficiency. An Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) model is used to model complex, non-linear associations among software metrics. By combining local and global explanations of a prediction, explainable AI systems such as LIME and SHAP address the interpretability gap in black-box models. Experimental testing shows that the suggested method achieves 96.65% recall, 96.65% accuracy, and 96.65% F1-score. The findings indicate that the framework offers high predictability and transparency, supporting informed decision-making in software quality assurance.

title: Topology-Preserving Image Segmentation with Spatial-Aware Persistent Feature Matching
authors: Bo Wen, Haochen Zhang, Dirk-Uwe G. Bartsch, William R. Freeman, Truong Q. Nguyen, Cheolhong An
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Topological correctness is critical for segmentation of tubular structures, which pervade in biomedical images. Existing topological segmentation loss functions are primarily based on the persistent homology of the image. They match the persistent features from the segmentation with the persistent features from the ground truth and minimize the difference between them. However, these methods suffer from an ambiguous matching problem since the matching only relies on the information in the topological space. In this work, we propose an effective and efficient Spatial-Aware Topological Loss Function that further leverages the information in the original spatial domain of the image to assist the matching of persistent features. Extensive experiments on images of various types of tubular structures show that the proposed method has superior performance in improving the topological accuracy of the segmentation compared with state-of-the-art methods. Code is available at https://github.com/bw05025ISATLoss.

title: DroneRise: A Multi-Temporal UAV Dataset and Benchmark for River Water-Level Rise Detection
authors: Pengfei Lyu, Yi Tang, Hidekazu Shirai, Yasuharu Watanabe, Hiroshi Masui, Wei Xie, Toshio Eisaka
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Information Science, Parallel and Distributed Systems (ISPDS)
abstract: Early detection of river water-level rise is crucial for disaster prevention and water resource management. Existing datasets are limited by low temporal resolution, post-disaster scenarios, or incomplete annotations, hindering development of generalizable detection methods. We present DroneRise, a high resolution, multi-temporal UAV dataset capturing controlled river water-level changes in Hokkaido, Japan. The dataset comprises $\mathbf{3, 0 3 6}$ images annotated with $\mathbf{1 3}$ semantic classes and enriched with GPS coordinates, altitude, camera angle, and timestamp metadata. DroneRise enables precise evaluation of newly submerged areas and supports geospatial-temporal analysis. To demonstrate its utility, we benchmark seven state-of-the-art semantic segmentation models, providing baseline results for river water-level rise detection. DroneRise offers a unique public resource for UAV-based river monitoring and flood-related research.

title: A Fault Diagnosis Method for Planetary Gearboxes Based on Current Signal and Symplectic Geometry Modal Decomposition
authors: Wenqian Zhang, Wenpeng Ma, Yabo Yang, Xiaofan Zhai
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Information Science, Parallel and Distributed Systems (ISPDS)
abstract: The fault diagnosis method based on motor current signal analysis offers the advantages of low signal acquisition costs and reduced susceptibility to environmental factors and noise, especially when compared to traditional fault diagnosis methods that rely on vibration signal analysis. However, a significant challenge remains: the fault information can be obscured by the fundamental frequency of the current and its harmonics. To address this issue, this paper proposes a novel fault diagnosis method for planetary gearboxes that utilizes current signals in conjunction with Symplectic Geometry Mode Decomposition (SGMD). Additionally, we introduce a new time-domain parameter amplitude index and an enhanced spectral entropy measure tailored to SGMD decomposition characteristics. These innovations aim to more effectively filter out both the fundamental frequency and its harmonic components. Experimental results demonstrate that this approach can successfully uncover fault characteristic information within the current signal, thereby facilitating accurate fault diagnosis of planetary gearboxes.

title: SupConWI-RL: Wafer Inspection with Reinforcement Learning Enhanced by Supervised Contrastive Learning
authors: Aleksandr Dekhovich, Oleg Soloviev
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Monitoring manufacturing processes plays an important role in chip production. Current state-of-the-art approaches use the entire surface to classify defects with CNN- or Transformer-based models, resulting in considerable mea-surement costs. Therefore, new advanced techniques are required to reduce the cost of inspection. In this work, we ad-vocate for the reinforcement learning-based feedback loop with a classifier trained with supervised contrastive loss. In contrast to previous works in this manner, our approach is not limited to only one type of defect but can identify multiple defects on one wafer. We tested our algorithm on the publicly available WM-811 k and MixedWM38 datasets, showing a significant reduction in scanning time compared to CNN-based approaches while maintaining similar accu-racy. We demonstrate the reduction of up to 40% in costs as-sociated with wafer scanning in defect classification tasks, even if multiple defects are on the surface. Moreover, we demonstrate that in the multi-defect scenario, the trained model can be directly used to detect outliers, requiring only about 12.5% of the surface to find at least one type of defect.

title: Research on the Risk Model of Cost Deviation in the Budget Estimation of Power Transmission and Transformation Projects Based on Bayesian Network
authors: YuLing Cai, Ye Ke, Ying Wang, Cong Zeng, Zhiyuan Zhang
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: This study develops a Bayesian Network (BN)—based model to quantitatively assess cost-deviation risks in the budget-approval stage of power transmission and transformation projects. Using deviation information extracted from 299 projects, key variables—including cost categories, deviation causes, frequency, deviation rates, and expected contribution—are structured into a causal probabilistic network. The BN effectively quantifies risk probability distributions under different combinations of cost types and deviation drivers, revealing internal mechanisms of deviation formation. Empirical analysis shows that design changes in construction works carry high uncertainty, deviations in other costs follow a “high-frequency but low-impact” pattern, and adjustments in charging standards for equipment purchases typically present medium-level risks. The model provides a scientific tool for supporting the transition from experience-based judgement to quantitative, probabilistic risk prediction, thereby improving refined cost management in power-grid projects.

title: A Multi-Functional Radar Task Scheduling Algorithm Based on Task Augmentation and Graph Neural Networks
authors: Jingbei Yang, Yafeng Wang, Ang Liu, Xianzhe Xu., Luofang Jiao, Yuhao Yang, Linghao Xia, Qiang Cheng
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: Task scheduling is one of the core components of radar resource management. Modern multifunction phased-array radars face complex parameters, tight time windows, and constrained intervals, rendering task scheduling NP-hard. Traditional exact and heuristic methods perform well, yet struggle to guarantee solution quality or speed under dynamic, real-time, and highly variable workloads. This paper proposes a radar task scheduling algorithm based on a Graph Neural Network (GNN) solver. By discretizing and augmenting task requests, the time-window constraints of individual tasks are transformed into a series of conflicting task lists, converting a constrained optimization problem into a Quadratic Unconstrained Binary Optimization Problem (QUBO). A GNN is then constructed to solve the optimization problem. We benchmarked the method against heuristics and five GNN variants on representative scenarios. The results show that, among the various GNN algorithms, GraphSAGE achieved the best overall performance: compared with the traditional heuristic, it improved total task utility by 20% while cutting actual runtime by about 45%.

title: Research on Optimization of Enterprise Electricity Consumption Strategy and Cost Control Based on Electricity Price Signal
authors: Yang Xiang, Lei Chong
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: Against the backdrop of the deepening reform of the energy market, which has triggered sharp fluctuations in electricity prices, the electricity costs of large electricityconsuming enterprises have surged dramatically, and traditional electricity consumption models struggle to adapt to the dynamic electricity price environment. To address this issue, this paper proposes an integrated optimization model featuring “data-driven -predictive modeling-strategy optimization-collaborative regulation”: by analyzing enterprises' electricity consumption characteristics and leveraging high-precision models to accurately predict future electricity loads and prices, it establishes a dynamic electricity price classification and load priority mechanism, and further integrates demand response and energy storage systems to form a comprehensive electricity optimization solution. Taking energy conservation and emission reduction as the means to regulate electricity consumption strategies, this model not only reduces enterprises' electricity costs but also contributes to the achievement of the “dual carbon” goals; comparative experiments have verified its favorable application effects.

title: Adaptive Face Anti-Spoofing Through Enhanced Data Manipulation and Feature Contrast Techniques
authors: Jielong Wang, Kaifeng Huang, Zehua Lan, Senxu He, Minzhe Huang, Ruvi Cai
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Face anti-spoofing remains a challenging task, particularly in scenarios with imbalanced datasets and cross-domain testing, as exemplified in the 6th Face Anti-Spoofing Challenge at ICCV 2025. This paper presents a novel approach that effectively addresses these challenges by leveraging enhanced data manipulation and feature contrast techniques (DMFC-FAS). Our method begins by training a six-class classification model using cross-entropy loss, categorizing real faces and five distinct types of spoofed faces. To miti-gate data imbalance, we perform strategic data augmentation on the real face and physical attack classes, which are underrepresented. In addition, we apply the MixUp technique during training to improve model robustness. For testing, instead of relying on the classification layer outputs, we utilize the backbone network to extract feature vectors. We compute the average vector of real face features from the validation set to serve as an anchor. The cosine similarity between this anchor and the feature vectors of test samples is used as the prediction score. Additionally, we use Test- Time Augmentation (TTA) to enhance performance. This methodology significantly enhances the model's performance, particularly in distinguishing real faces from digitally adversarial examples, which are numerous and often indistinguishable from real faces. In this competition, we achieved 0.0134 ACER. The code is available at https://github.com/wangj11993/DMFC-FAS.

title: ORXE: Orchestrating Experts for Dynamically Configurable Efficiency
authors: Qingyuan Wang, Guoxin Wang, Barry Cardiff, Deepu John
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: This paper presents ORXE, a modular and adaptable framework for achieving real-time configurable efficiency in AI models. By leveraging a collection of pre-trained experts with diverse computational costs and performance levels, ORXE dynamically adjusts inference pathways based on the complexity of input samples. Unlike conventional approaches that require complex metamodel training, ORXE achieves high efficiency and flexibility without complicating the development process. The proposed system utilizes a confidence-based gating mechanism to allocate appropriate computational resources for each input. ORXE also supports adjustments to the preference between inference cost and prediction performance across a wide range during runtime. We implemented a training-free ORXE system for image classification tasks, evaluating its efficiency and accuracy across various devices. The results demonstrate that ORXE achieves superior performance compared to individual experts and other dynamic models in most cases. This approach can be extended to other applications, providing a scalable solution for diverse real-world deployment scenarios.

title: AI-Powered Interoperable Blockchain Framework Using Deep Learning and Lightweight Consensus for Enhanced Security and Scalability
authors: Prachi R. Dussawar, Kavita R. Singh
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Blockchain technology has emerged as a disruptive paradigm for secure and transparent data exchange; however, it continues to face significant challenges in scalability, interoperability, and security. Fragmentation across blockchain networks restricts seamless data integration, while traditional consensus mechanisms such as Proof of Work and Proof of Stake impose high computational costs and latency. To address these limitations, this article proposes an Intractive Blockchain structure to AI that takes advantage of deep learning and light consensus mechanisms to improve performance and safety. The proposed structure introduces three main contributions: (i) a model of detection of deep learning vulnerabilities that identifies real-time intelligent contract weaknesses to reduce application failures; (ii) a lightweight consensus protocol inspired by Byzantine failure tolerance (BFT) to minimize latency and improve the transfer rate, ensuring safe authentication; and (iii) a cross -chain interoperability layer that facilitates the perfect data exchange between heterogeneous blockchain networks. Experimental assessment of TensorFlow Hyperledger tissue shows that the proposed model improves the accuracy of vulnerabilities detection by up to 96 %, reaches a 23 % reduction in latency and increases the transfer rate by 18 % compared to conventional approaches. This research highlights the potential of AI-Empowered blockchain systems for scalable, secure and interpreter applications in financial, health and public services.

title: Quantum Fourier Transform Inspired Filtering for Enhanced Edge Preservation in SAR Imagery for Ship Detection
authors: Anwesha Dey, Susmita Deb
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th Conference on Applied Signal Processing (ASPCON)
abstract: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) imagery is an effective tool for maritime surveillance because it can be operated under all weather conditions and day and night. This article proposes a ship detection scheme based on a new Quantum Fourier Transform (QFT)-based median filtering approach. This paper presents an effective ship detection approach in Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) images, emphasizing superior edge preservation through Quantum Fourier Transform (QFT)-inspired median filtering. The proposed technique applies a frequency-domain transformation to local grayscale neighborhoods to extract median magnitudes, enhancing edge clarity while minimizing speckle noise. Combined with classical morphological operations and adaptive binarization, this approach significantly improves object segmentation and detection accuracy. Results demonstrate that QFT-inspired filtering outperforms conventional methods in retaining fine edge details crucial for robust ship identification.

title: Lasso Gripper: A String Shooting-Retracting Mechanism for Shape-Adaptive Grasping
authors: Qiyuan Qiao, Yu Wang, Xiyu Fan, Peng Lu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Handling oversized, variable-shaped, or delicate objects in grasping and transporting tasks is extremely challenging, mainly due to the limitations of the gripper's shape and size. Inspired by traditional tools like the lasso and the uurga, this paper proposes Lasso Gripper, which captures objects by launching and retracting a string. Contrary to antipodal grippers, which concentrate force on a limited area, Lasso Gripper applies uniform pressure along the length of the string for a more gentle grasp. The gripper is actuated by four motors, two for reeling the string in and two for launching it out. By adjusting motor speeds, the size of the string loop can be tuned to accommodate objects of varying sizes, eliminating the limitations imposed by the maximum gripper separation distance. To address the issue of string tangling during rapid retraction, a specialized mechanism was incorporated. Additionally, a dynamic model was developed to estimate the string's curve, providing a foundation for the kinematic analysis of the workspace. In grasping experiments, Lasso Gripper, mounted on a robotic arm, successfully captures and transports a range of objects, including bull and horse figures as well as delicate vegetables. The demonstration video is available here: https: / /youtu. be/PV1J76mNP9Y

title: Proton: Towards Multi-level, Adaptive Profiling for Triton
authors: Keren Zhou, Tianle Zhong, Hao Wu, Jihyeong Lee, Yue Guan, Yufei Ding, Corbin Robeck, Yuanwei Fang, Jeff Niu, Philippe Tillet
publish: 2026 IEEE/ACM International Symposium on Code Generation and Optimization (CGO)
abstract: Domain-Specific languages (DSLs) such as Triton enable developers to write high-performance GPU kernels in a Python-friendly manner; however, profiling these kernels with existing tools often incurs runtime and storage overhead while failing to deliver actionable insights for both kernel authors and framework developers. We present Proton, a multi-level, adaptive profiler tailored for the Triton programming language and compiler. Proton provides frontend APIs to selectively profile relevant regions, aggregate results, capture custom metrics not available through hardware counters, and query profiles using a SQL-like language. Proton’s backend design unifies vendor profiling APIs with instrumentation-based profiling, ensuring portability and extensibility. Using Proton, users are able to query custom and hardware metrics across the relevant levels of abstraction—full end-to-end model execution, isolated neural network layers, language-specific Triton operators, and compiler intermediate representations. We demonstrate the tool’s effectiveness through case studies on production-grade kernel development, continuous integration, multi-GPU analysis, language model inference, and intra-kernel profiling. Our evaluations on end-to-end workloads, as well as standalone Triton kernels, demonstrate that Proton imposes lower runtime overhead and delivers significant reductions in profile sizes relative to existing framework and vendor profilers while being fully open source.

title: Fusion Supervised and Self-Supervised Learning for Intelligent Case Classification in Telecom CRMs
authors: Manoj Kota
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing (MLNLP)
abstract: The competitive telecom business requires good Customer Relationship Management (CRM) that is able to handle high volumes of customer problems. In this study, a hybrid scheme of supervised and self-supervised learning is proposed to increase the accuracy of the routing and make it faster, known as fusion case classification scheme. This dataset is Telecom CRM Case Dataset, which consists of over 50,000 real customer support cases with such fields as case ID, customer ID, issue description, issue type (label), priority, and resolution status. The successive SSL module is trained with unlabeled case descriptions to enable more flexibility with respect to additional classes of cases whereas the successive SL module is trained with labelled CRM data to determine proper classification of common service problems. The fusion model achieved the best accuracy of 95% and F1-score of 90% but when compared to conventional SL models like SVM and Random Forest performed poorly when testing on this dataset. It was more effective and predictable than rule-based systems and reduced the time to route cases by 18%. This will improve customer satisfaction, fasten response and minimize individual engagement. The next step will be to incorporate continuing learning to cope with the changing trends of services in a dynamic telecom environment.

title: Microscopic Modeling and Analysis of Bipolar Corona Discharge from a Floating Conductor
authors: Wang Zhebo, Wang Yonggen
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: The discharge phenomenon from floating conductors is widespread during aircraft lightning strikes. However, the microscopic discharge process of bipolar corona from floating conductors is not yet fully understood. Based on the fluid dynamics governing equations and the chemical reaction processes of air discharge plasma, this paper establishes a plasma model for bipolar corona discharge from a floating conductor and conducts simulation analysis. The results indicate that the intensity of negative corona discharge is higher than that of positive corona. The positive polarity end is dominated by positive ions, which weaken the electric field. The negative polarity end is dominated by concentrated positive ions and dispersed negative ions, and the spatial electric field exhibits a pattern of first increasing sharply, then decreasing, and subsequently rising slightly. The analysis of the bipolar corona evolution characteristics enhances the understanding of the discharge process and characteristics of floating conductors.

title: Cooperative Monitoring of Unknown Moving Object Using Multi-FW-UAV System Fencing
authors: Umair Javaid, M. Imran Baig, Michael Basin, Yuanjiang Liao
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: This paper studies autonomous distributed cooperative monitoring of aerial robots moving at unknown speed using a multi-fixed-wing UAV (FW-UAV) system. The monitoring problem is addressed through a distributed cooperative control scheme that integrates adaptive formation restructuring and fencing maneuvers. The proposed observer-based distributed cooperative control (OB-DCC) framework uses formation and fencing strategies to ensure continuous tracking and containment of the target while maintaining inter-UAV coordination and collision avoidance. In addition, the proposed OB-DCC framework incorporates a vector field-based collision avoidance mechanism to enhance its robustness. Initially, a distributed position observer estimates the real-time target trajectory, which allows the FWUAVs to adjust the formation dynamically. An auxiliary state is introduced to handle uncertainties in target velocity estimation error and improve accuracy. The proposed OB-DCC framework considers the FW-UAV system dynamics, including aerodynamic limitations, kinematic constraints, and multi-agent communication topology. In addition, the fencing controller ensures uniform distribution of FW-UAVs around the target, preventing escape while minimizing energy consumption. The simulation results demonstrate the satisfactory performance of the proposed OBDCC method in maintaining persistent monitoring of UMO while optimizing FW-UAV resource allocation.

title: Research on Energy-Aware Ring Topology Routing Protocol: Location Privacy Protection and Network Lifetime Optimization for WSN Base Stations
authors: Yao Yu, Jiayang Chen, Tianshun Liu, Chunju Zhou
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Communication (ICAIRC)
abstract: Wireless sensor networks (WSN), a key component of the Internet of Things (IoT), have an open architecture that makes base stations (BS) vulnerable to eavesdropping attacks. Exposure of BS locations can lead to network downtime. To address the privacy deficiencies and energy imbalances of existing location privacy protection strategies, this paper proposes a ring-based routing protocol for base-station location privacy protection (RBRP). RBRP constructs a concentric multiring topology by introducing fake base stations (FBS), establishing data forwarding paths at each ring level. The deployment of fake BSs generates traffic away from real BSs, disrupting attackers' traffic analysis and thus improving privacy protection. Through uniform data forwarding along the ring paths, node energy consumption variations are limited to within 0.03 J, significantly improving network energy distribution and extending network lifecycles. OMNeT++ simulation experiments demonstrate that, compared to the ABP and RW algorithms, RBRP reduces transmission latency when the source node and BS are in the same or adjacent rings. At the same time, the increase in packet arrival rate and the randomization of traffic patterns reduce the attacker's base station positioning success rate, verifying the protocol's dual advantages in privacy protection and energy consumption balance.

title: Efficient Detection of Diabetic Retinopathy through Deep Learning Using MobileNetV2
authors: Moulali Noorbhash, Raju Duggepogu, Darsi Sailesh Kumar, Shaik Mohammad Mubeen, U.D. Prasan, P. Deepthi
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is a major worldwide cause of blindness, and early detection is the key to avoiding serious complications. This paper suggests an effective DR detection system which combines deep learning methods and existing web technologies to offer an interactive interface for physicians. A collection of 1,812 labeled retinal images annotated into five DR severity classes labeled as normal, mild, moderate, severe, and proliferative was utilized in model training. MobileNetV2, an efficient CNN model, is utilized by the proposed system for DR classification. DenseNet201 was also tested for benchmarking purposes, and performance outcomes indicate that MobileNetV2 performed better with training accuracy of 96.8% and validation accuracy of 99.6%, while DenseNet201 recorded 90.1% and 93.8%. Accurate labeling of the dataset permitted proper training of the model and an unbiased comparison. The strong performance of MobileNetV2, together with its integration into a web platform, indicates that the system can be useful in real-world clinical screening. Its simple design makes it accessible for medical professionals to test and adopt. Overall, the study provides a practical step toward improving early DR detection and supporting timely treatment.

title: Research on Anomaly Detection and Processing Method for Daily Maximum Load of Distribution Transformers Based on STL-S-H-ESD
authors: Ze Liu, Ruoxing Zhang, Jianxin Duan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy Power and Electrical Technology (CEPET)
abstract: This paper proposes an anomaly detection and correction method for daily maximum load data of distribution transformers based on Seasonal-Trend decomposition using Loess (STL) and Seasonal Hybrid Extreme Studentized Deviate (S-HESD). The method first collects daily maximum load data via the SCADA system and converts them into time series data. Then, the STL algorithm decomposes the series into trend, seasonal, and residual components through an iterative process involving inner and outer loops. Subsequently, the S-H-ESD algorithm, which employs the median and Median Absolute Deviation (MAD) for robustness, identifies anomalies in the residual component. Finally, anomalous values are replaced by the sum of the corresponding trend and seasonal components to preserve the underlying data characteristics. Experimental results on realworld data with injected anomalies show that, at a significance level of $\alpha=1 \times 10^{-11}$, the method achieves an accuracy of $99.79 \%$, a precision of $100 \%$, a recall of $90.91 \%$, and an F1-score of 0.95, significantly outperforming traditional methods such as the G-ESD method. The proposed approach provides a reliable solution for anomaly detection and correction, supporting the intelligent maintenance of power-system assets.

title: TAV-Nav: A Tool-Augmented VLM Navigator for Safe and Efficient Lunar Rover Path Planning
authors: Haodong Han, Aodi Wu, Xue Wan
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: To enhance the autonomous navigation capabilities of lunar rovers in unknown environments, this paper proposes TAV-Nav (Tool-Augmented VLM Navigator), a hierarchical control framework based on Vision-Language Models (VLM). The framework positions VLM as the decision-making “brain” and equips it with a modular toolset including visual analysis and LiDAR perception to achieve precise perception of the physical world. By integrating specialized tools, we provide complex scene understanding content to the VLM, enhancing decision-making safety. Experimental results show that compared to the end-to-end method relying solely on VLM (10% success rate), the TAV-Nav framework significantly improves task success rate to 90%. This research confirms that the “VLM brain + specialized toolset” approach can effectively overcome VLM's limitations in specialized tasks.

title: Crop Disease Diagnosis Model using Hybrid Model
authors: Dnyandeo Khemnar, Akash Saxena, Mukund Wagh
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: The productivity of common crops in agriculture is declining, leading to increased interest in alternative crops known for their adaptability, nutritional value, and resilience to harsh climates. Despite their benefits, these crops face threats from diseases that harm the agricultural economy. Traditional disease detection methods are labor-intensive, costly, and often questioned for their accuracy. Hence, this paper proposes a novel Crop Disease Diagnosis framework using Enhanced Convolutional neural network (CDD-EC) model.
