The process of CDD-EC starts with data acquisition, where images are gathered from a benchmark dataset that includes a variety of plant disease samples. These images undergo preprocessing using Gaussian filtering, which helps to diminish noise and improve image quality. Following this step, segmentation is conducted using a Coordinate Attention module assisted SegNet (CASNet) model, which efficiently isolates the diseased regions from the background. After segmentation, extraction of texture features through Updated Gabor Filter in Local Gabor Directional Pattern (UGF-LGDP), analyzing shape and spatial distribution using Pyramid Histogram of Oriented Gradients (PHOG), and obtaining structural shape features are performed. The combined features are then fed into a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory network (Bi-LSTM), which learns both backward and forward dependencies in the data. The output feature map from the Bi-LSTM is then passed to a Normalized Average pooling response Batch normalization-based Convolutional Neural Network (NAB-CNN) for additional refinement and classification. Finally, the system produces a categorized output that identifies the specific type of plant disease present in the image.

title: Seamless Perception-Prediction Switching: A Zero-Shot Approach to Robotic Following Under Occlusion
authors: Junru Kong, Yuhan Shi, Pengyu Wang, Zhaoxi Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Robotics and Artificial Intelligence (ICRAI)
abstract: Research on robotic following under occlusion remains challenging due to limitations in perception and prediction capabilities. Traditional methods struggle with temporary target disappearance and dynamic obstacles, while existing learning-based approaches often require extensive task-specific training. This paper presents a novel robotic following framework that dynamically switches between a fine-tuned YOLOv8n-seg model for target detection when the target is visible, and the pre-trained SpatialTrackerV2 model applied in a zero-shot manner for trajectory prediction upon target occlusion. This hybrid approach eliminates the need for collecting and annotating large-scale trajectory datasets for the prediction task. The system integrates these perception modules with the ROS navigation stack, enabling robust pursuit in complex environments. Experiments in Gazebo validate the robustness of our zero-shot prediction integration and dynamic mode switching. The results validate the effectiveness of our zero-shot prediction integration and the dual-mode switching mechanism for robust robotic following.

title: Detection of Emerging Respiratory Infections Using Chest Radiography
authors: Prasanth Vaidya S, Kandala N V P S Rajesh, Prabu M, Kiran Kumar Patro
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: Respiratory infections, including SARS-CoV-2 variants, influenza, and bacterial pneumonia, pose significant global health challenges and require rapid and accurate diagnostic solutions. This study introduces a deep learning model built on the Xception architecture to enable automated detection of emerging respiratory infections on chest radiographs. Unlike conventional CNN models, Xception improves variable selection efficiency and generalization while reducing computational complexity. Training is carried out using a dataset with multiple classes, allowing the system to differentiate among viral pneumonia, bacterial pneumonia, and normal healthy samples. By integrating an optimized training pipeline and explainable artificial intelligence (AI) techniques, the approach enhances interpretability and minimizes overfitting. The findings highlight the model’s robustness in real-world diagnostics, offering a scalable solution for pandemic preparedness and early disease detection.

title: Optimization Design of Magnetic Wheels to Enhance Adhesion Performance for Climbing Robots
authors: Lide Dun, Shiyao Qi, Xiang Zhang, Haifei Zhu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: The Magnetic Force-to-Mass Ratio (MFMR) of magnetic wheels critically influences the performance of climbing robots. To address the challenges of nonlinear magnetic force computation and the inherent mass-force trade-off in dimensional optimization, this paper proposes a systematic design approach integrating virtual prototyping, surrogate modeling, and a multi-objective genetic algorithm. The optimization model identifies key design variables through parametric analysis, incorporates the required adhesion force from mechanical analysis as a constraint, develops a magnetic force simulation model, and constructs a surrogate model using Design of Experiments and surrogate modeling techniques, with MFMR as the objective. Simulation results indicate a 30% improvement in MFMR over the initial prototype. The proposed methodology offers an effective, efficient, and generalizable framework for optimizing magnetic wheel design.

title: BiodiverseNet: Multitask Learning on Fused Multispectral and Radar Data for Scalable Ecosystem Monitoring
authors: Prasanth Yadla
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Monitoring global biodiversity is a pressing challenge given the accelerating rates of ecosystem degradation and loss. Earth observation (EO) offers a scalable solution, but existing methods struggle with heterogeneity in ecosystems, label sparsity, and modality limitations. We propose Bio-diverselvet<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>https://github.com/TransformerTitan/BiodiverseNet, a multitask learning framework that leverages fused multispectral (Sentinel-2) and radar (Sentinel-1) imagery to jointly predict key biodiversity metrics: canopy cover, habitat fragmentation, and landscape connectivity. Our model employs a Vision Transformer (ViT) backbone with DINOv2 pre-training, combined with task-specific heads and auxiliary objectives including land cover classification and NDVI prediction. Evaluated on a global benchmark spanning 15 ecoregions across five continents, BiodiverseNet achieves competitive performance with R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> scores of 0.76 for canopy cover, 0.62 for fragmentation, and 0.67 for connectivity, showing modest but consistent improvements of 2-2.5% over transformer baselines. The model demonstrates reasonable robustness across diverse biomes, though performance varies with ecosystem complexity and data availability.

title: Advance in Technology and Artificial Intelligence for Managing Snoring and Obstructive Sleep Apnoea
authors: Ambika Prasad Dash, Swarupa Chakole, Pramita Muntode, Mohd Basheeruddin
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Common sleep-related respiratory disorders with serious health and social repercussions include primary snoring and obstructive sleep apnoea (OSA). Alternative device-based therapies have emerged as a result of poor adherence to continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP), which is still the standard of care. Although they have little effect on sleep architecture, mandibular advancement devices (MADs), the most popular oral appliances, have been demonstrated to widen the upper airway, lower the apnoea–hypopnea index (AHI), and enhance quality of life. Similar advantages are provided by tongue retention devices (TRDs), however patient tolerance is poor. While nasal expiratory positive airway pressure (nEPAP) offers expiratory resistance that splints the airway and lessens collapsibility in certain individuals, nasal dilators can only subjectively alleviate nasal blockage with negligible objective benefit. By avoiding supine sleep, positional treatment devices (PTDs) help treat positional OSA, and more recent vibrotactile systems enhance monitoring and adherence. While hypoglossal nerve stimulation (HGNS) is a well-established surgical option for patients who are intolerant to CPAP and has shown strong efficacy in reducing the severity of OSA, neuromuscular electrical stimulation (NMES) is a newer technique that aims to strengthen pharyngeal muscles to reduce collapsibility. All things considered, these device-based treatments are beneficial supplements to or substitutes for CPAP, and cautious patient selection is still necessary to optimise treatment results.

title: FSD-DETR: A Lightweight and Accurate Synergistic Network for SAR Ship Detection
authors: Chuang Tan, Ying Xiong, Hao Long, Weihua Luo
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Communication (ICAIRC)
abstract: To address the challenge of balancing accuracy and efficiency in SAR ship detection, we propose FSD-DETR, an improved model based on RT-DETR. Our model integrates three key components: an efficient FasterNet backbone, a novel Synergistic Feature Refinement (SFR) module for noise suppression, and a DySample module for high-fidelity feature fusion. Compared to the baseline RT-DETR, FSD-DETR improves the mAP50-95 metric by 3.3% (from 69.8% to 73.1%), while simultaneously reducing the number of parameters by 44% (from 19.8M to 11.1M) and computational complexity by 49% (from 56.9G to 29.1G FLOPs). The proposed model achieves a superior balance between detection accuracy and efficiency, demonstrating its strong potential for real-time SAR ship detection applications.

title: Probabilistic Algorithmic Framework to Decrease Risk of Costs and Improve Supply Chain Dependability under Uncertain Condition
authors: Inderjeet Singh, Ashendra Kumar Saxena
publish: 2025 14th International Conference on System Modeling &amp; Advancement in Research Trends (SMART)
abstract: This paper offers a Probabilistic Algorithmic Framework that aims at reducing the supply chain expenses and maximizing reliability when faced with uncertain demand. This framework is based on considering demand uncertainty because, unlike traditional deterministic models, demand uncertainty is captured by the use of probabilistic models, i.e. Monte Carlo simulations, to represent changing customer demands within different demand zones. The supply chain consists of a number of suppliers and points of demand, where the suppliers and demand points have transportation costs, capacity constraints, and normallydistributed demands. The objective is to prespecify an optimal shipping strategy to minimize the anticipated total cost (i.e. transportation cost plus the cost of shortages) when there is high-demand fulfilment rate. The problem can be stated as a constrained mixed-integer optimization model, where the decision variables will be the size of shipments of a given supplier to a given demand area. The cost approximation function and stochastic demand model are built in MATLAB and an optimization solver is used to come up with the best distribution strategy. The framework reduces the estimated cost in performance and makes sure that the probability of the demand satisfactions is more than 0.98 in all the zones. The load is gained by using the manufacturer capacities to balance the assignment of the load. It leads to a strict and realistic solution to supply chain applications, including pharmaceuticals, FMCG and logistics, the variability of demand of which is a significant factor. Overall, the given model contributes to the effectiveness and resilience of the supply chain and is cost-effective since it takes uncertainty into account when planning, and, therefore, is a helpful and flexible tool that can be utilized by the decision makers in the processes of risk management and operations optimization under the conditions of dynamic business.

title: Development of a Pipe-diameter Adaptive Wheeled Climbing Robot
authors: Shiyao Qi, Yongjian Bu, Lide Dun, Haifei Zhu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Although wheeled magnetic-adhesion climbing robots are employed for inspecting and maintaining steel structures such as pipelines, ships, and storage tanks, they exhibit weak adaptability to varying pipe diameters and face challenges traversing complex steel structures. In this paper, we propose a pipe-diameter-adaptive climbing robot named PAClimbot, featuring a front-rear dual-wheel layout with auxiliary wheels installed on both sides, where both front and rear wheels possess steering capability. The magnetic wheels incorporate a coaxial dual-domain magnetic ring design, enabling adaptation to different pipe diameters while providing transition capabilities across diverse structural pipelines and obstacle avoidance functions. Through analytical verification of the robot's structural design and locomotion principles, we elucidate its fundamental working mechanisms. A prototype of the robot was developed and tested experimentally. The results demonstrate that the magnetic wheels autonomously adjust adhesion force on pipes with diameters greater than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 0 m m}$</tex>. The robot can climb straight pipes and planar surfaces, traverse bends and planar concave corners, and flexibly avoid obstacles on pipelines.

title: YogaVR: An AI and IoT-Based Posture Correction Approach for Yoga in Virtual Reality
authors: Akilesh S, Tarun Vinod Pai, Manasa Raghuraman, Muhammad Kifayathullah K A, Ilakiyaselvan N
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: Yoga offers significant physical, mental, and therapeutic benefits, but traditional classes can be challenging for neurodivergent individuals or those with sensory sensitivities. This work presents YogaVR, an AI-enabled virtual reality (VR) platform for real-time posture correction and guided sessions in a low-stimulation environment. Developed in Unity for the Meta Quest 3S, it integrates PoseNet-based pose estimation with an external camera on a Raspberry Pi 4-powered four-wheel drive (4WD) robot for autonomous multi-angle tracking. An OpenAI-powered voice assistant provides corrective feedback and personalized posture, diet, and exercise guidance, while WebXR and WebRTC enable real-time video streaming. In a focus group of 30 participants, YogaVR achieved a mean acceptance score of 8.1/10 (SD = 1.9), demonstrating strong usability and positive reception as an accessible alternative to conventional yoga instruction.

title: AI Enabled Secure Examination Environment with Real Time Surveillance and Automation
authors: Pranit Kumar Sahoo, Snaha Majee, Sudeshna Nath, Bidrohi Bhattacharjee, Suhas Deb, Sandipan Patra
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th Conference on Applied Signal Processing (ASPCON)
abstract: A smart examination system utilizes advanced technology for innovative approaches to a traditional examination credentialing process. The examination system was developed to improve the security, efficiency, fairness and friendliness of the examination process. It incorporates other comprehensive functions, including identity verification, AI powered detection, automatic attendance, automatic seat allocation and live monitoring. These features promote fairness for students and reduce the opportunity to cheat. Student activities during the exam can be closely tracked with the help of smart cameras and secure online systems. It monitors student movement, assigns specific seats dynamically, and detects any suspicious behavior. When any suspicious activity is detected, then it will display an alert on the invigilator's screen so that further investigation can be carried out. The need for constant human observation is reduced and smooth and fair AI, greater transparency and accuracy can be achieved in this process. For example, students can be identified through facial recognition, attendance can be marked automatically, and all exam rooms can be monitored simultaneously by this system. Ultimately the system supports academic integrity and aligns with the growing need for intelligent, scalable educational technologies.

title: Information Entropy-Based Global-Local Path Planning of Robotic Active Sensing for Multivariate Environmental 3D Spatial Estimation
authors: Yunxiao Wang, Shupeng Liu, Kaiqi Cao, Bingyu Yang, Teng Li
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: In complex and dangerous environments, robots have gradually replaced humans in performing environmental monitoring tasks. Robots determine the sampling location based on key information in the environmental field to achieve active sensing. The environmental field can be reconstructed through a Gaussian random field based on the sampled data. Design of an effective path planner is a critical task for robotic environmental monitoring. In this paper, an innovative active sampling planning method is proposed, which integrates a multivariate 3D Gaussian random field with a global-local sampling strategy driven by information entropy. Experiments carried out by both simulation and field test validate the effectiveness of the proposed method for robotic active sensing and multivariate environmental 3d spatial estimation.

title: Distributed Model Predictive Control of Grid-forming Power Converter for Wind Turbine System
authors: Ce Wang, Ruiying Cheng, Yongqi Guo, Yong Chang, Zongshang Li, Yifan Wang
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of wind power in the grid, the control strategy of wind power converters has gradually shifted from grid-following to grid-forming, enabling wind power to actively participate in grid support. To address the issue of coordinated control among multiple grid-forming converters, this paper proposes a distributed model predictive control strategy. First, mathematical models of the PMSG and grid-side converter are established, and the total power constraint of the wind turbine system is introduced to express the coupling relationship among the output powers of the converters. Subsequently, by transforming the power coupling constraint into the objective function of each converter, a local optimization problem is formulated. Finally, the Nash equilibrium method is introduced to solve the distributed model predictive control problem. The simulation results demonstrate the superiority of the proposed control strategy in dynamic performance and validate its effectiveness in coordinating converters' output powers.

title: A Review on Sentiment Analysis Approaches: Using Ensemble Deep Learning for Multimodal Data
authors: Anoopa Arya, Alankrita Aggarwal
publish: 2025 14th International Conference on System Modeling &amp; Advancement in Research Trends (SMART)
abstract: Opinion mining, another name for sentiment analysis (SA), is a branch of computer science. that focuses on analyzing the opinions and feelings expressed in text, audio and video. Sentiment analysis involves determining the sentiment expressed by person. The objective of the opinionmining field is to conduct subjectivity analysis, indicating whether a document is subjective or objective. Subjectivity implies the presence of sentiment, while objectivity signifies content devoid of sentiment. Currently, an abundance of information about a specific product is available, with a single product often garnering hundreds of reviews across various webpages. Numerous websites, such as imdb.com, amazon.com, idlebrain.com, among others, aggregate user information and expert opinions to publish reviews. Experts meticulously analyse reviews, extract opinions, and generate ratings related to the dataset provided by the requesting agencies. However, handling the vast amount of data is a labour intensive task for experts. The continuously growing volume of web data poses challenges in extracting precise opinions from content. Hence, there is a need to design a system that can efficiently perform these tasks with humanlike accuracy. This paper analyses various approaches which are capable of handling and analysing large amounts of reviews and generate the aspect level opinion mining (AOM).

title: From Design to Impact: A UX-Driven Approach to Gamified Online Education
authors: Julián Galindo-Losada, Betsabé Amaguai, Viviana Parraga-Villamar, Marco Santórum, Mayra Carrión-Toro, Vilma Rodríguez
publish: 2025 Fifth International Conference on Information Systems and Software Technologies (ICI2ST)
abstract: This study analyzes the impact of gamification on user experience (UX) within online training workshops conducted in Ecuador. The study aimed to enhance student motivation, content comprehension, and active participation by integrating interactive elements such as games, challenges, and rewards. The main contributions include the design of a structured experimental study, developing a gamified user interface, implementing a virtual workshop for teacher training, and evaluating gamifiparticipant motivation and engagement, creating a more dynamic and interactive learning environment. However, no substantial improvement was observed in collaborative work beyond the course itself. This research offers a methodological foundation for the future integration of UX and gamification strategies in online education settings.

title: Wearable Sensors and AI for Personalized Football Training and Injury Prevention
authors: Yicheng Liu
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: The present paper presents a novel concept of enriching real-time biomechanical monitoring with predictive analytics that is expected to enhance the performance of the player and reduce the risk of injuries. To overcome these constraints, the novel methodology the Dynamic Biometric-AI Synergy Model (DBASM) is presented which utilizes the innovative wearable sensors to capture multi-dimensional data that can capture the motion kinematics and muscle loading, heart rate variability, and fatigue indicators during workouts and competitions. DBASM, in contrast to traditional fixedthreshold systems features adaptive learning, which continuously refines model predication predictions as the conditions of each athlete vary, the history of work and recovery patterns. The framework facilitates coaches and medical staff to recognize the documentation they must make in the practice decisions at real time- personalized training changes, actionable information, and injury prevention exercises. Research on the improvement of performance through optimization of technique, identification of injury through micro-pattern analysis and rehabilitation of injury through load control are potential applications. The given solution is a scaling solution to the next-level athlete management systems, with the capability to present evidence-based and customised training concepts within both the professional and amateur football environments.

title: Noise is an Efficient Learner for Zero-Shot Vision-Language Models
authors: Raza Imam, Asif Hanif, Jian Zhang, Khaled Waleed Dawoud, Yova Kementchedjhieva, Mohammad Yaqub
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Recently, test-time adaptation has garnered attention as a method for tuning models without labeled data. The conventional modus operandi for adapting pre-trained vision-language models (VLMs) during test-time primarily focuses on tuning learnable prompts; however, this approach over-looks potential distribution shifts in the visual representations themselves. In this work, we address this limitation by introducing Test-Time Noise Tuning (TNT), a novel method for handling unpredictable shifts in the visual space. TNT leverages, for the first time, a noise adaptation strategy that optimizes learnable noise directly in the visual input space, enabling adaptive feature learning from a single test sample. We further introduce a novel approach for inter-view representation alignment by explicitly enforcing coherence in embedding distances, ensuring consistent feature representations across views. Combined with scaled logits and confident view selection at inference, TNT sub-stantially enhances VLM generalization and calibration, achieving average gains of +7.38% on natural distributions benchmark and +0.80% on cross-dataset evaluations over zero-shot CLIP. These improvements lay a strong foundation for adaptive out-of-distribution handling.

title: HiSS: Human-Inspired Semantic Segmentation for Vehicle Interior Scene Understanding
authors: Aleksander Kostuch, Joanna Jaworek-Korjakowska
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Computer vision plays a critical role in autonomous vehicle technology, with semantic segmentation being a key component by labeling each pixel in an image. This study evaluates the effectiveness of a bio-inspired hybrid approach that combines binary and multiclass segmentation process to improve semantic segmentation in vehicle interiors as part of a Cabin Monitoring System, drawing inspiration from human visual perception processes. Experiments were conducted on synthetic data from the SVIRO dataset using SegFormer, a transformer-based model for segmentation, and YOLO for detection, respectively. Various architectures with a novel hybrid approach were tested that mimics the hierarchical processing of the human attention mechanism by using different Gaussian and distance transform-based masks to refine the focus of the multiclass model. The innovative hybrid approach combining binary and multiclass segmentation improved the mean Intersection over Union from 0.55 to 0.61. Two perceptually-motivated postprocessing methods were developed: a basic approach inspired by visual completion phenomena that further enhanced mIoU to 0.76, and an extended context-aware variant that achieved better accuracy (0.87 vs. 0.84) by analyzing neighborhood information to determine appropriate replacement classes, simi-lar to human contextual integration processes. These methods are highly generic and can be adapted to a wide range of segmentation challenges beyond vehicle interiors. The integration of human perceptual principles into computational vision systems demonstrates the value of biomimetic approaches in advancing artificial intelligence. In addition, we provide a detailed discussion of the results and outline potential directions for future research.

title: Safety Without Semantic Disruptions: Editing-free Safe Image Generation via Context-preserving Dual Latent Reconstruction
authors: Jordan Vice, Naveed Akhtar, Mubarak Shah, Richard Hartley, Ajmal Mian
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Training multimodal generative models on large, uncurated datasets can result in users being exposed to harmful, un-safe and controversial or culturally-inappropriate outputs. While model editing has been proposed to remove or fil-ter undesirable concepts in embedding and latent spaces, it can inadvertently damage learned manifolds, distorting concepts in close semantic proximity. We identify limitations in current model editing techniques, showing that even benign, proximal concepts may become misaligned. To ad-dress the needfor safe content generation, we leverage safe embeddings and a modified diffusion process with tunable weighted summation in the latent space to generate safer images. Our method preserves global context without com-promising the structural integrity of the learned manifolds. We achieve state-of-the-art results on safe image generation benchmarks and offer intuitive control over the level of model safety. We identify trade-offs between safety and censorship, which presents a necessary perspective in the development of ethical AI models. We will release our code.

title: Explainable AI-Generated Image Forensics: A Low-Resolution Perspective with Novel Artifact Taxonomy
authors: Kaustubh Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: The rapid advancement of generative AI has led to synthetic images that are increasingly indistinguishable from real ones, posing significant challenges for detection systems, especially at very low resolutions (32×32 pixels). This paper tackles two core problems in digital media forensics: (1) reliably distinguishing real from AI-generated images at low resolution, and (2) providing explainable visual reasoning for synthetic image artifacts. We augment and diversify the CIFAKE dataset and benchmark multiple classifiers, identifying Con-vNeXt Tiny as the most effective model based on accuracy (98.65% on augmented CIFAKE), efficiency (13.4 ms inference per image), and adversarial robustness (96.80% post-adversarial training accuracy). To enable interpretability, we propose a novel hierarchical taxonomy of visual artifacts and introduce an explainability pipeline that combines SinSR-based super-resolution, CLIP-guided artifact detection, and vision-language models for explanation generation. Our framework offers a robust, interpretable solution for real-world low-resolution image forensics.

title: M3DocVQA: Multi-Modal Multi-Page Multi-Document Understanding
authors: Jaemin Cho, Debanjan Mahata, Ozan İrsoy, Yujie He, Mohit Bansal
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Document Visual Question Answering (DocVQA) offers a promising approach to extracting insights from large document corpora. However, existing benchmarks focus on evaluating multi-modal understanding within a single document. This gap hinders the development of methods integrating scattered information across pages and documents. To address this, we introduce M3Doc VQA, the first benchmark designed for multi-modal, multi-page, and multi-document understanding. M3DocVQA comprises over 3,000 PDF documents with more than 40,000 pages, offering a challenging environment where evidence is distributed across diverse sources and modalities. Along-side the dataset, we introduce M3DocRAG, a baseline method based on multi-modal retrieval-augmented generation. M3DocRAG flexibly handles both single and multiple document settings while preserving critical visual information, establishing a useful starting point for future work in open-domain multi-modal document understanding. Our experiments across three benchmarks (M3DocVQA, MMLongBench-Doc, and MP-DocVQA) show that existing methods struggle with open-domain question answering over extensive, multi-modal documents. Although M3DocRAG has shown promising performance, there is large room for future improvement. We provide comprehensive ablation studies of different indexing, multi-modal language models, and multi-modal retrieval models, along with qualitative examples to guide future research.

title: MG-Gen: Single Image to Motion Graphics Generation
authors: Takahiro Shirakawa, Tomoyuki Suzuki, Takuto Narumoto, Daichi Haraguchi
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: We introduce MG-Gen, a framework that generates motion graphics directly from a single raster image. MG-Gen decompose a single raster image into layered structures represented as HTML, generate animation scripts for each layer, and then render them into a video. Experiments confirm MG-Gen generates dynamic motion graphics while preserving text readability and fidelity to the input conditions, whereas state-of-the-art image-to-video generation methods struggle with them. The code is available at https://github.com/CyberAgentAILab/MG-GEN

title: A Malicious Traffic Detection Method based on Adaptive Ensemble
authors: Benhao Zhu, Jian Li, Zhengteng Wen, Xiao Chen, Haolei Wang, Yong Chen, Bangze Xia, Shiqi Liu
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Data Science and Information Technology (DSIT)
abstract: In recent years, malicious attack traffic is gradually transforming from unencrypted to encrypted forms, making traditional detection methods ineffective. Current encrypted traffic detection methods face severe challenges such as poor adaptability of static models, the contradiction between computing resources and unstable detection accuracy. This paper proposes a malicious traffic detection method based on adaptive ensemble learning, which can select appropriate ensemble learning algorithms according to the resource constraints of the deployment environment and detection accuracy requirements, realizing optimal detection results without decrypting traffic. We design a server-client distributed architecture to decouple computationally intensive optimization decisions from resource-constrained edge detection tasks. Besides, we propose an adaptive ensemble decision algorithm based on Pareto optimal solution set to solve the multi-objective optimization problem under resource constraints. Additionally, we introduce three innovative mechanisms (complexity-aware gating decision, load-aware dynamic sliding window and active test-based dynamic performance sensing) to improve detection efficiency. Experimental results show that our method achieves higher detection accuracy with relatively lower resource consumption compared with traditional ensemble methods.

title: Dual-Band Fixed-Beam Leaky-Wave Antenna for Point-to-Point Communication Applications
authors: Hongming Zang, Yangyang He, Min Li, Kaixue Ma
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: A dual-band fixed-beam leaky-wave antenna (LWA) is proposed. The design employs a microstrip line periodically loaded with pixelated structures optimized through a multiport method and a genetic algorithm (GA). By tailoring the dispersion characteristics, two bands with near-zero dispersion slopes are achieved, providing fixed beams at −32° and −16° within 12.9-13.4 GHz and 14.4-14.9 GHz, respectively. The antenna exhibits beam squinting below 1° and reflection coefficients better than −15 dB. With its simple structure and stable beam performance, the proposed LWA is promising for point-to-point communication systems.

title: DRISHTI: GAN-CNN Based SAR Colorization with K-Means Land Cover Analysis
authors: Devidas S. Thosar, Rajashree Thosar, Prathamesh Kharade, Lakshmi Sharma, Rohitoshav Dalve, Pratapsinh Nikam
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) has reliable imaging in all weather and day-and-night scenarios, though it is greyscale, so it is poorly visually interpretable. This is dealt with by the proposed DRISHTI, which is a combined approach, which will involve unsupervised land-use/land-cover (LULC) classification along with SAR image colorization. The first module is based on Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN) and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) to colorize SAR images to create realistic and optical-like images. The second module uses K-Means clustering to categorize the colorized outputs which categorize regions as urban, agricultural, forest and barren. It enhances the visual attributes and analytical worth of SAR images in DRISHTI having been educated on Sentinel-1 and −2 conjunctive collections. The system evidences the power of deep learning in Earth observation, which involves radar technology to give a viable solution to environmental analysis, city map, and agricultural surveillance.

title: Configuring the optimal service of an intermodal bulk cargo transport service on the Danube corridor
authors: Cyril Alias, Jonas Zum Felde, Herfried Leitner
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Service Operations and Logistics, and Informatics (SOLI)
abstract: A central question occurring when designing and deploying a new transport service is the precise service configuration. Typically, there are indicators and preconditions pointing at a particular configuration. Traditionally, bulk cargo transport – both on the waterway and on the road – are direct transports. However, in the case of a novel intermodal bulk cargo transport service on the Danube, there is little knowledge from history and reference cases to rely on. Hence, three different service configurations, i. e., direct transports, single-stop services, and liner services, are compared with one another by scrutinizing their logistics performance with the help of a discrete-event simulation study. Thereby, determining the optimal service configuration can be realized without having to run real-life transport services and collect the evaluation base from there. With the results of the simulation study, the different stakeholders involved are provided decision-support for the optimal configuration and deployment.

title: Fault Detection in Automotive Engines Through ConvNeXt-LSTM and Attention Mechanisms in IoV Systems
authors: L. Natrayan
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: The Internet of Vehicles (IoV) is transforming automotive safety and reliability by enabling real-time monitoring and predictive maintenance. Automotive engines, as the core of vehicle operation, demand robust fault diagnosis to prevent critical failures. This study proposes a hybrid framework that integrates ConvNeXt for depthwise spatial feature extraction, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for temporal analysis, and a self-attention mechanism for enhanced interpretability and detection of critical fault patterns. Engine sensor data from the Automotive Vehicle Engine Health dataset (Kaggle) were processed and classified using this architecture. Experimental results demonstrate 96.2% accuracy, a 95.3% detection rate, and a 95% Fl-score, validating the system's effectiveness in multi-class fault classification. The framework shows high diagnostic accuracy, improved interpretability, and robustness for real-time IoV applications in automotive engine monitoring and maintenance.

title: A Spatio-Temporal Semantic Algorithm for Video Anomaly Detection from Monitoring Perspectives
authors: Zihan Zhu, Hongyue He, Ming Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Communication (ICAIRC)
abstract: The detection of abnormal events in surveillance videos is a prominent research topic in the field of public security. To address challenges such as background interference and event sparsity encountered by traditional algorithms, this paper proposes a multi-semantic fusion anomaly detection algorithm. First, a multi-layer encoder module is designed to model and extract target features. Second, a spatiotemporal fusion attention mechanism is introduced to enable effective integration of spatial and temporal semantics. Finally, a decoder is employed to progressively reconstruct the spatiotemporal correlations across video frames. Extensive experiments are conducted on a widely used benchmark dataset to validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: Low-Loss Transmission Structures for 650 GHz Vacuum Electron Devices
authors: Zhiyu Chen, Hao Li, Junjian Wu, Jingrui Duan, Yinyu Zhang, Yuxin Wang, Yuan Zheng, Yubin Gong
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: A low-loss pill-box window and a low-loss corrugated waveguide transmission structure for 650 GHz vacuum electronic devices (VEDs) are presented in this paper. Traditional pill-box windows at Terahertz (THz) frequencies are extremely small and mechanically fragile. The proposed over-mode design addresses this challenge. Additionally, a novel corrugated waveguide transmission structure is introduced. This structure essentially consists of two symmetrical mode converters that convert the TE<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">10</inf> mode of a standard rectangular waveguide into a quasi-Gaussian HE<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf> mode distribution. This conversion reduces ohmic loss at the waveguide edges during energy transmission. Simulation results demonstrate that cascading the proposed window with the corrugated waveguide structure achieves a transmission loss of only 21.52 dB/m - significantly lower than the 35.07 dB/m loss observed when cascading the window with a conventional rectangular waveguide transmission line. Therefore, the combined low-loss over-mode pill-box window and corrugated waveguide transmission structure offer a promising approach for low-loss transmission in 650 GHz VEDs.

title: Dino2-DR: A Trustworthy and Explainable Vision Transformer for Cross-Domain Diabetic Retinopathy Grading
authors: Lucia Cascone, Luigi Di Biasi, Giuseppe Genito, Michele Nappi
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Deep learning models for diabetic retinopathy (DR) classification often exhibit performance degradation under domain shift, limiting their reliability in heterogeneous clinical environments. We introduce Dino2-DR, a robust and interpretable framework for DR severity grading. Built upon the self-supervised DINOv2 Vision Transformer, our approach is assessed under two complementary evaluation protocols that reflect realistic deployment conditions. For the Leave-One-Domain-Out protocol, we train six independent models, systematically withholding one fundus dataset (APTOS, DeepDRiD, FGADR, IDRiD, Messidor2, RLDR) for out-of-domain testing. This protocol achieves 85.73% AUC, 62.47% accuracy, and 50.15% F1-score averaged across all target domains, outperforming existing CNN and ViT-based approaches. The Fixed-Source Multi-Target (FSMT) protocol trains a single model on EyePACS + DDR and evaluates generalization to six external domains, yielding 87.83% AUC, 66.07% accuracy, and 50.46% F1-score. Beyond strong predictive performance, Dino2-DR prioritizes explainability and reliability: attention-rollout visualizations spotlight clinically relevant regions, and a light optional temperature-scaling step preserves a low calibration error (ECE less than 0.05) on every domain. An interactive interface allows clinicians to inspect predictions and associated evidence, facilitating potential adoption in routine clinical screening. Code: https://github.com/CASALab-Unisa/Dino2-DR

title: A Miniaturized and High-Isolation Dual-Band Wilkinson Power Divider Based on TSV
authors: Yue Deng, Fengjuan Wang, Xiangkun Yin, Ming Xu, Dingxi Zhang, Yuan Yang, Ningmei Yu
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: As microwave and radio frequency (RF) systems continue to evolve, there are increasing demands for RF devices to be more compact, support multiple frequency bands, and enhance performance to accommodate a broader range of frequencies. In this paper, a dual-band Wilkinson power divider based on through-silicon via (TSV) is proposed, which is implemented by bridged T-coil (BTC) circuit. In addition, symmetric modeling is used to further improve isolation, while achieving the goal of reducing design size, lowering power consumption and improving structural performance. It can be seen from the results that, the proposed dual-band power divider has center frequencies of 4.1 GHz and 9.1 GHz with insertion loss close to −3 dB. At an operating frequency of 4.1 GHz, the return loss is approximately −30 dB, and the isolation is approximately − 40 dB. At the operating frequency of 9.1 GHz, the return loss is − 28 dB, and the isolation is −37 dB. Therefore, the dual-band power divider has high amplitude balance, good transmission characteristics and isolation performance, and meets the function of constant amplitude and phase at the output end of the power divider.

title: Local Implicit Variational Autoencoder for Arbitrary-Scale Super-Resolution
authors: Yifei Zhang
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: The incorporation of probabilistic modeling into variational methods-a highly powerful framework-has effectively mitigated the inherent ill-posedness of the super-resolution (SR) problem. By learning the latent distribution of high-resolution (HR) images, these methods enable reconstructions that are both more realistic and diverse. However, most existing variational SR techniques are designed for fixed upscaling factors, which limits their adaptability to real-world scenarios involving arbitrary magnifications. In contrast, arbitrary-scale SR approaches exhibit strong scalability but often neglect the modeling of reconstruction uncertainty. Relying solely on deterministic pixel-wise objectives such as the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\ell_{1}$</tex> loss, these methods frequently yield over-smoothed results with insufficient texture detail. To address these challenges, we propose a comprehensive generative framework termed the Local Implicit Variational Autoencoder (LIVA). The LIVA simultaneously tackles both the ill-posedness and the scale generalization issues in SR. Built upon the LINF backbone, LIVA integrates a dedicated variational inference module that explicitly formulates pixel-level latent variables, thereby equipping the model with the ability to capture texture distribution patterns across multiple scales. By combining Fourier-based implicit representation learning with localized probabilistic reasoning, LIVA is capable of producing high-quality and realistic HR reconstructions that preserve fine-grained texture fidelity and maintain structural consistency.

title: A Metaheuristic Approach to Fake News Detection using Tuned Grey Wolf Optimized SVM
authors: Arpit Chaudhari, Hemang Shrivastava
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: The digital media is the fastest wat to spread Fake news, so it has to understand that effective ways to grab it right away. With the help of a Grey Wolf Optimized Support Vector Machine (GWO-SVM), metaheuristic-based system for finding fake news. The method uses the classification capabilities of Support Vector Machines (SVM) with the smart search abilities of the Grey Wolf Optimizer to make forecasts more accurate and dependable. All the news articles are gathered so as to understand the severity of the news, then, text cleaning is done to get rid of noise and make the data more consistent. Advanced vectorization methods are used to pull out relevant features. This lets the model pick up on important trends and related data. The Grey Wolf Optimizer is used to automatically tune the SVM's important factors, making sure that the decision boundaries for classification are the best ones. With this combination, the system can better handle feature areas with a lot of dimensions and complex data patterns. The method is made to work with different datasets and topics, so it can be used for both static and dynamic content analysis. Because it uses metaheuristic optimization, the model provides a clever and flexible answer to the growing problem of finding false information online. In the future, this method can be used to build more reliable, flexible, and real-time systems that can spot fake news.

title: AI-Enhanced Hierarchical Framework for Global Disaster Risk Assessment with Spatiotemporal Prediction and Priority-Based Intervention
authors: Jiedi Chen
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: This paper presents an artificial intelligence-driven framework for comprehensive disaster risk assessment integrating multi-dimensional aggregation, spatiotemporal prediction, and dynamic intervention prioritization. Leveraging the WorldRiskIndex dataset spanning 181 countries over 11 years, we develop a hierarchical model combining exposure quantification with vulnerability metrics through multiplicative interactions. Our spatial-temporal autoregressive framework with diffusion terms captures temporal dependencies and spatial diffusion patterns, while the dynamic categorization system enables evidence-based resource allocation. Experimental validation demonstrates robust risk stratification with 73 % classification stability and significant spatial autocorrelation (Moran's <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{I}=0.68$</tex>), providing actionable intelligence for disaster risk reduction strategies.

title: Design of Compact OAM Reflectarray Based on Dispersion-Aligned 4-bit Pixel Meta-Atoms
authors: Hui Li, Wenrui Zheng
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: This paper presents a compact reflective pixel metaatom for high-purity orbital-angular-momentum generation. A topology-change design, evaluated with a multiport network model, enables a 16-state (4-bit) phase library with near-uniform dispersion across states. The unit cell is miniaturized to approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.14 \lambda_{0}$</tex>, supporting dense phase sampling under a fixed aperture. Using the library, a reflectarray is synthesized for the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$l=+2$</tex> case at 10.5 GHz, achieving a high mode purity of 0.96. The approach offers a practical route to dispersion-consistent, miniaturized meta-atoms for OAM reflectors.

title: Paired-Sampling Contrastive Framework for Joint Physical-Digital Face Attack Detection
authors: Andrei Balykin, Anvar Ganiev, Denis Kondranin, Kirill Polevoda, Nikolai Liudkevich, Artem Petrov
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Modern face recognition systems remain vulnerable to spoofing attempts, including both physical presentation attacks and digital forgeries. Traditionally, these two attacks vectors have been addressed by separate models or pipelines, each targeted to its specific artifacts and modalities. However, maintaining distinct detectors leads to increased system complexity, higher inference latency, and a combined attack vectors. We propose the Paired-Sampling Contrastive Framework, a unified training approach that leverages automatically matched pairs of genuine and attack selfies to learn modality-agnostic liveness clues. Evaluated on the 6th Face Anti-Spoofing Challenge “Unified Physical-Digital Attack Detection” benchmark, our method obtained an average classification error rate (ACER) of 2.10%, outperforming prior solutions. The proposed framework is lightweight, requires only 4.46 GFLOPs and a training runtime under one hour, making it practical for real-world deployment. Code and pretrained models are available at https://github.com/xPONYx/iccv2025_deepfake_challenge.

title: GenEscape: Hierarchical Multi-Agent Generation of Escape Room Puzzles
authors: Mengyi Shan, Brian Curless, Ira Kemelmacher-Shlizerman, Steve Seitz
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: We challenge text-to-image models with generating escape room puzzle images that are visually appealing, logically solid, and intellectually stimulating. While base image models struggle with spatial relationships and affordance reasoning, we propose a hierarchical multi-agent framework that decomposes this task into structured stages: functional design, symbolic scene graph reasoning, layout synthesis, and local image editing. Specialized agents collaborate through iterative feedback to ensure the scene is visually coherent and functionally solvable. Experiments show that agent collaboration improves output quality in terms of solvability, shortcut avoidance, and affordance clarity, while maintaining visual quality.

title: A Robot Visual Localization Architecture Using Hybrid Map with Explicit-Implicit Combination
authors: Peiyu Guan, Xurong Gong, Jianjie Li, Zhiqiang Cao, Junzhi Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Visual localization is a fundamental capability for autonomous robots, which provides the robot pose for navigation and task execution. Although visual simultaneous localization and mapping enables efficient pose estimation, it possibly suffers from accumulated drift or tracking failures in complex environments, reducing the reliability of the localization. Moreover, practical environments are typically heterogeneous, where different regions impose distinct localization accuracy requirements. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a hybrid map-based visual localization framework for mobile robot. The environment is partitioned into multiple regions according to their localization requirements. Coarse regions are represented using explicit point cloud maps to enable efficient localization, while finegrained regions are modeled implicitly by coordinate regression networks for high localization accuracy. These region-specific maps are aligned and merged into a hybrid map, and a regionaware map selection mechanism is introduced to select an appropriate regional map based on recent localization history and current observation. In this way, the robot pose under tracking failure of the pose estimator is recovered. Navigation experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed localization framework.

title: KWE-ASG: Key Waypoint Extraction and Action Sequence Generation-based Imitation Learning for Dual-arm-hand Manipulation
authors: Panlong Qiu, Weixin Zhang, Congjia Su, Shaowei Cui
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Existing imitation learning methods often struggle with long-horizon dual-arm-hand manipulation tasks due to the lack of corrective feedback mechanisms, causing error accumulation. To address this, we propose an imitation learning approach based on key waypoint extraction and actionsequence generation. This approach encourages the policy to focus on predicting key waypoints and shortens the decision horizon during training, thereby effectively mitigating error accumulation. We deploy our approach on a real dual arm robot, successfully accomplishing complex tasks requiring precise coordination, such as cup pouring and cable winding. Experimental results demonstrate that our method achieves a success rate of 84 % on cup pouring and 76 % on cable winding, significantly outperforming other imitation learning methods. Additionally, tactile ablation experiment results show that the introduction of tactile feedback significantly improves task success rates, highlighting the crucial role of tactile perception in the advancement of dual-arm-hand manipulation.

title: Metal Ridge Loaded Semi-Cylindrical Cavity with Test Position-Insensitive Property
authors: Yu Tang, Chong Gao, En Li, Yunpeng Zhang, Xiangbao Zhu
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: This paper presents the design of the metal ridge loaded semi-cylindrical resonant cavity, which has been engineered to exhibit test position-insensitive characteristics. The structure is loaded with metal ridges, thereby ensuring the uniform distribution of electric field energy in the middle part of the converging rectangular region. The TE<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">011</inf> mode is adopted as the test mode, thus facilitating the accurate testing of the dielectric constant of elongated materials, such as fibers, utilizing the perturbation method of the resonance cavity, irrespective of the position of the material to be tested.

title: Flexible Broadband Symmetrically Folded Dipole Antenna for Head Imaging Systems
authors: Biqun Wu, Jian Wu, Zehai Wu, Nan Yang, Jian Sun, Jin Wu
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: A flexible symmetric folded dipole antenna is proposed using ecoflex and conductive fabric material. By combining the fundamental, second-order and third-order modes of the dipole, wideband design can be obtained. Meandered radiating arms are employed for miniaturization. Measurement results show a wide impedance bandwidth of 134% on a real head. Bending tests are also conducted, with almost the same results. The good performance illustrates its potential application for head imaging systems.

title: AI Based Non-Fatal Dermatological Disease Identification Using Raspberry Pi
authors: Swethaa T, Manjushri M, Lekha Chandhini A, Janapriya V G, Saranya G, Padmanabha Sarma A
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Non-fatal Dermatological diseases are very common these days, and also spreading through the world. The diagnosis is quite challenging due to the skin tone, colour, presence of hair. Skin disorders that do not pose a threat to life are classified as non-fatal skin diseases. Acne, eczema, psoriasis, rosacea, chickenpox, cowpox, and monkeypox are common examples. These conditions can be uncomfortable and change the appearance of the skin; Patients who seek out dermatological therapy may face a number of obstacles. The psychological impacts of skin diseases, therapeutic side effects, and the cost of continuing care are a few of them. We suggested an AI-based deep learning system to identify non-fatal dermatological conditions in order to do this. This research presents a solution for predicting different types of skin illnesses using a variety of deep learning-based computer vision algorithms. With changes for skin disease application, the system leverages publicly accessible image recognition architectures, specifically Inception net (Google net), and correctly displays the skin illness based on maximum vote from the network. The three stages of the system's operation are feature extraction, training, and testing/validation. With Inception, we achieved a 94-percentage accuracy rate in the training phase and 99 percentage in testing phase. A total of 755 original skin lesion images were used as the dataset and were augmented to 7532 samples with six disease classes of the publicly available M-Pox dataset on Kaggle. Although the data set provides diversity in conditions, it does not fully capture world skin colour and light variations which might be a limitation on model generalization. Future research will take into consideration more multicentre data gathering to increase diversity and equity.

title: Uncertainty-Aware Likelihood Ratio Estimation for Pixel-Wise Out-of-Distribution Detection
authors: Marc Hölle, Walter Kellermann, Vasileios Belagiannis
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Semantic segmentation models trained on known object classes often fail in real-world autonomous driving scenarios by confidently misclassifying unknown objects. While pixel-wise out-of-distribution detection can identify unknown objects, existing methods struggle in complex scenes where rare object classes are often confused with truly unknown objects. We introduce an uncertainty-aware likelihood ratio estimation method that addresses these limitations. Our approach uses an evidential classifier within a likelihood ratio test to distinguish between known and unknown pixel features from a semantic segmentation model, while explicitly accounting for uncertainty. Instead of producing point estimates, our method outputs probability distributions that capture uncertainty from both rare training examples and imperfect synthetic outliers. We show that by incorporating uncertainty in this way, outlier exposure can be leveraged more effectively. Evaluated on five standard benchmark datasets, our method achieves the lowest average false positive rate (2.5%) among state-of-the-art while maintaining high average precision (90.91%) and incurring only negligible computational overhead. Code is available at https://github.com/glasbruch/ULRE.

title: DiViD: Disentangled Video Diffusion for Static-Dynamic Factorization
authors: Marzieh Gheisari, Auguste Genovesio
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Unsupervised disentanglement of static appearance and dynamic motion in video remains a fundamental challenge, often hindered by information leakage and blurry reconstructions in existing VAE- and GAN-based approaches. We introduce DiViD, the first end-to-end video diffusion framework for explicit static-dynamic factorization. DiViD's sequence encoder extracts a global static token from the first frame and per-frame dynamic tokens, explicitly removing static content from the motion code. Its conditional DDPM decoder incorporates three key inductive biases: a shared-noise schedule for temporal consistency, a time-varying KL-based bottleneck that tightens at early timesteps (compressing static information) and relaxes later (enriching dynamics), and cross-attention that routes the global static token to all frames while keeping dynamic tokens frame-specific. An orthogonality regularizer further prevents residual static-dynamic leakage. We evaluate DiViD on real-world benchmarks using swap-based accuracy and cross-leakage metrics. DiViD outperforms state-of-the-art sequential disentanglement methods: it achieves the highest swap-based joint accuracy, preserves static fidelity while improving dynamic transfer, and reduces average cross-leakage.

title: Human Vision Constrained Super-Resolution
authors: Volodymyr Karpenko, Taimoor Tariq, Jorge Condor, Piotr Didyk
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Modern deep-learning super-resolution (SR) techniques process images and videos independently of the underlying content and viewing conditions. However, the sensitivity of the human visual system (HVS) to image details changes depending on the underlying image characteristics, such as spatial frequency, luminance, color, contrast, or motion; as well viewing condition aspects such as ambient lighting and distance to the display. This observation suggests that computational resources spent on up-sampling images/videos may be wasted whenever a viewer cannot resolve the synthesized details i.e the resolution of details exceeds the resolving capability of human vision. Motivated by this observation, we propose a human vision inspired and architecture-agnostic approach for controlling SR techniques to deliver visually optimal results while limiting computational complexity. Its core is an explicit Human Visual Processing Framework (HVPF) that dynamically and locally guides SR methods according to human sensitivity to specific image details and viewing conditions. We demonstrate the application of our framework in combination with network branching to improve the computational efficiency of SR methods. Quantitative and qualitative evaluations, including user studies, demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach in reducing FLOPS by factors of 2× and greater, without sacrificing perceived quality.

title: Embedded Parking Space Detection Based on Image Processing and SVM
authors: Jiaheng Yang, Yueming Liu, Zijun Liu
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Information Science, Parallel and Distributed Systems (ISPDS)
abstract: With the continuous increase in urban traffic pressure, the intelligent management of parking space resources has become a vital part of smart city construction. This paper proposes an embedded parking space detection system based on classical image processing and Support Vector Machine (SVM). The system uses images of parking lots captured by drones and accomplishes automatic segmentation and occupancy recognition of parking spaces through white mask extraction, Canny edge detection, ROI selection, and SVM classification. Experimental results demonstrate that the system maintains high recognition accuracy and real-time performance in various complex environments, operates stably on low-power embedded platforms, and offers a low-cost and scalable technical solution for intelligent parking management.

title: Whole-Body Control with Terrain Estimation of A 6-DoF Wheeled Bipedal Robot
authors: Cong Wen, Yunfei Li, Kexin Liu, Yixin Qiu, Xuanhong Liao, Tianyu Wang, Dingchuan Liu, Tao Zhang, Ximin Lyu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Wheeled bipedal robots have garnered increasing attention in exploration and inspection. However, most research simplifies calculations by ignoring leg dynamics, thereby restricting the robot's full motion potential. Additionally, robots face challenges when traversing uneven terrain. To address the aforementioned issue, we develop a complete dynamics model and design a whole-body control framework with terrain estimation for a novel 6 degrees of freedom wheeled bipedal robot. This model incorporates the closed-loop dynamics of the robot and a ground contact model based on the estimated ground normal vector. We use a LiDAR inertial odometry framework and improved Principal Component Analysis for terrain estimation. Task controllers, including PD control law and LQR, are employed for pose control and centroidal dynamics-based balance control, respectively. Furthermore, a hierarchical optimization approach is used to solve the wholebody control problem. We validate the performance of the terrain estimation algorithm and demonstrate the algorithm's robustness and ability to traverse uneven terrain through both simulation and real-world experiments.

title: Construction of an IT Operation and Maintenance Data-Driven Decision Support System for Power Grid Based on Multi-Dimensional Monitoring
authors: Yi Hu, Haodong Wang, Xiaoqi Jin, Zhiqin Zhao
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: This study proposes a data-driven decision support system for power grid operation and maintenance (O&M) based on multi-dimensional monitoring, aiming to enhance the efficiency and accuracy of power grid O&M. By employing innovative methods such as dynamic weight fusion models, distributed tracing technology, and Bayesian networks, the system achieves precise fusion of multi-dimensional monitoring data and efficient tracking of fault propagation paths. Experimental results show that the dynamic weight fusion model significantly improves early warning capabilities in anomaly detection compared to traditional methods, reduces false negatives, and enables accurate fault diagnosis. The model successfully reduced the early anomaly detection time to 4.2 minutes, improving by 54.3 % compared to static threshold detection (9.2 minutes), with a false negative rate reduced to 12 %, a 71.4 % decrease compared to traditional methods. The fault propagation path tracking algorithm, utilizing Bayesian networks, significantly improved fault localization accuracy, reducing the localization time from 120 minutes with manual inspection to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8. 7}$</tex> minutes. Ultimately, the decision support system demonstrated excellent early warning capabilities and fault handling efficiency in real-world O&M scenarios, shifting O&M from a traditional experience-driven model to a data-driven decision-making model, greatly enhancing the response speed and stability of the power grid system.

title: Advanced Machine Learning and AI-Driven Security Frameworks for Intrusion Detection in Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: Koustubh Kulkarni, Suresh Kumar Muthuvel, S. Sivasakthiselvan, M Venunath
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) are increasingly vulnerable to sophisticated cyber-attacks, necessitating robust intrusion detection systems (IDS). Traditional security mechanisms often fail to address the dynamic and resource-constrained nature of WSNs, creating a critical need for advanced solutions. Conventional methods, such as signature-based IDS and basic machine learning algorithms, struggle with high false positive rates and limited adaptability to evolving threats. This work proposes a hybrid AI-driven framework combining Long ShortTerm Memory (LSTM) and Random Forest, leveraging their complementary strengths for enhanced intrusion detection. The framework is evaluated using the WSN-DS and NSL-KDD datasets, which provide diverse attack scenarios and network traffic patterns. The proposed model surpasses other methods, as its experimental outcomes demonstrate an accuracy of 99.2%, a precision of 98.5%, a recall of 99.0%, and a false positive rate of 0.8%. Overall, the designed hybrid framework presents a reliable intrusion detection system for WSNs, while future implementation will focus on improving its execution speed and practical applicability.

title: Analyzing University Students’ Perceptions of ChatGPT: A Case Study at Ajman University, UAE
authors: Abdellateef Abdelhafez Alqawasmi, Najeh Rajeh Ibrahim Alsalhi, Mohd. Elmagzoub Eltahir, Sami Al-Qatawneh, Khaled Hussein Aljarrah, Aya Abdul Karim Al-Taani
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Social Networks Analysis, Management and Security (SNAMS)
abstract: This study examines how Ajman University students view the integration of ChatGPT into higher education. As AI and machine learning grow more significant in academia, universities are encouraged to reconsider their teaching methods and assessment strategies. The research explores students’ perceptions of ChatGPT’s educational advantages and possible obstacles, emphasizing its role in enhancing learning, increasing engagement, and aiding academic work. A quantitative approach was used, involving a structured questionnaire given to a representative student sample. Data analysis with SPSS included calculating averages, standard deviations, and reliability measures. Findings show that students generally view ChatGPT positively, valuing its real-time feedback, self-paced learning opportunities, and boost efficiency. Still, concerns about over-dependence, output accuracy, and potential risks to academic integrity were raised. The results indicate that ChatGPT can be a valuable teaching tool if integrated thoughtfully into instruction. The study suggests creating clear institutional policies, offering student training programs, and ongoing monitoring to promote responsible and ethical AI use in higher education.

title: Fusing Convolution and Vision Transformer Encoders for Object Height Estimation from Monocular Satellite and Aerial Images
authors: Furkan Gültekin, Alper Koz, Reza Bahmanyar, Seyed Majid Azimi, Mehmet Lütfi Süzen
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Accurate height estimation from aerial and satellite imagery is crucial for large-scale 3D scene modeling, which has applications in urban planning, environmental monitoring, and disaster management. In this work, we propose integrating convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and vision transformers (ViTs) to leverage both local and global feature extraction. Our experiments show that using a combination of CNN and ViT encoders significantly improves accuracy compared to relying on either one alone, as CNNs capture fine details while ViTs enhance contextual understanding. Additionally, we incorporate a segmentation head to enhance pixel-level precision, particularly at object boundaries. Evaluated on the DFC2019 and DFC2023 datasets, our proposed fusion approach outperforms baseline methods across multiple metrics. For instance, root-mean-squared error is reduced by 5%-13%, and accuracy is improved by 4%-9% in the delta threshold metric. The results also demonstrate strong generalizability across diverse sensors, acquisition altitudes, viewing angles, and real-world scenarios. Our models are released at https://github.com/Furkangultekin/FusedHE.

title: Reconstruction of robot teleoperation force information in simulated time-delay network based on LSTM-Transformer
authors: Zhongyu Sun, Huijun Li, Xianyi Zhu, Aiguo Song, Ye Lu, Baoguo Xu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Robotics and Artificial Intelligence (ICRAI)
abstract: Accurate force feedback is a prerequisite for high-transparency teleoperation, yet it is highly susceptible to distortion under time-varying network delays. To address this issue, this paper proposes a time-varying delay force feedback reconstruction method that integrates force sensor-based compensation with deep learning techniques. In this study, the least squares method is applied to estimate end-effector force sensor parameters for zero-offset removal and gravity compensation, followed by Kalman filtering to reduce noise in the six-axis sensor data. To simulate network disturbances, a bidirectional communication channel with stochastic time-varying delay (TVD) and packet loss is constructed using a time-delay network simulation algorithm. A cascaded LSTM-Transformer prediction architecture is proposed for accurate prediction of both time delay and contact force. Experimental validation on a teleoperation platform comprising an Omega7 haptic device and a Barrett WAM robotic arm demonstrates that the proposed method outperforms other comparative approaches in terms of Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Mean Squared Error (MSE), and Mean Absolute Error Percentage (MAEP) for both single-feature and multi-feature prediction tasks. In the final stage, the proposed architecture is utilized for network delay prediction. When the predicted delay exceeds a predefined threshold, the same model is further used to perform predictive compensation for the force feedback information. This strategy not only improves the precision of force feedback but also effectively mitigates packet loss caused by network fluctuations, providing a delay-resilient force feedback solution for high-transparency teleoperation systems.

title: Power-Weighted Pretraining-Based Feature Extraction for Model Adaptation of RF Power Amplifiers
authors: Qing Luo, Xiao-Wei Zhu, Zixuan Lin
publish: 2024 IEEE MTT-S International Conference on Numerical Electromagnetic and Multiphysics Modeling and Optimization (NEMO)
abstract: Pretraining approach can be used in digital predistortion (DPD) systems to reduce the complexity of DPD and accelerate the convergence of DPD extraction. The principle of pretraining is to retain the most useful information as feature extraction when dimension reduction is performed, based on the principal component analysis. Therefore, the entire offline measurement for feature extraction will be costly to cover the statistical distribution of the PA operation state, and the conventional linear testing arrangement is not optimal. To modify this, power-weighted pretraining is proposed to apply different weights to the original test results according to the PA output power. It can improve feature extraction efficiency and alleviate the impact of noise in low-power measurements, which are validated by experimental results.

title: Building A Model Combining State Policy, Artificial Intelligence (AI), And Folk Knowledge In Responding To Climate Change (CC) In Vietnam: Potentials And Challenges
authors: Lan Thi My Lam, Thuan Thi Nguyen, Nguyen Bang Nong
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: Vietnam faces increasingly severe climate challenges, disproportionately affecting coastal communities. This paper explores a holistic model integrating state policy, artificial intelligence (AI), and folk knowledge to enhance CC adaptation. Drawing on governmental directives, field surveys, and recent literature, the research article highlights how AI’s predictive capabilities can be leveraged in conjunction with local wisdom to enhance resilience. Folk knowledge—rooted in generations of observation—offers unique, location-specific signals for early disaster warnings. Meanwhile, Vietnam’s state policies provide the legal and infrastructural backbone for wide-scale implementation. Integrating these three pillars creates a multidimensional framework where policy serves as the brain, AI acts as the analytical tool, and folk knowledge serves as the intuitive guide. Despite implementation challenges such as limited data and high costs, the paper advocates for open climate databases and community-based AI networks to empower decision-making. This innovative model provides a path toward sustainable and inclusive climate adaptation in Vietnam.

title: SELDOM: Scene Editing via Latent Diffusion with Object-Centric Modifications
authors: Richard E. L. Higgins, David F. Fouhey
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: We introduce SELDOM to composably edit scenes—mixing and matching objects with backgrounds, camera changes, and object-centric edits. SELDOM is a 3D-aware diffusion editing method which conditions on sequences of “neural nouns and verbs”. Neural nouns represent scene state and are visual features extracted from source image(s). Neural verbs are learnt representations for image edits, formed either by explicitly parsing prompts or implicitly attending to them. Neural verbs combine with their associated neural nouns to convey object-centric transformations. Finally, a sequence of these tokens is composed with scene background tokens and used as conditioning for a fine-tuned latent diffusion model. Our factorization affords test-time compositionality, allowing us to compose edited objects from multiple datasets into a single scene. We further demonstrate our model's ability to photo-edit: SELDOM can convincingly edits scenes to change object hue and lighting, scale and rotate objects, apply diverse language-based edits, and control camera rotation and translation.

title: Automated Anemia Classification with MobileNetV2 and LIME for Enhanced Medical Image Analysis
authors: Sornambika R, Shrivarshini M, Sowmya A, Sabarimanikandan S, Deepa Balusamy, Geetha Shanmugam, Mythily V
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: Anemia is a significant global health problem, especially in resource-poor settings without laboratory facilities. One potential outcome in this paper is the use of analyses of conjunctival images and deep literacy in making an invasive diagnosis of anemia. In earlier studies, handcrafted features such as color histograms and pixel intensity were investigated, but these features were mostly sensitive to variations in lighting and device settings. While there are several multimodal approaches that use combinations of images of the tongue, nailbed, and conjunctiva that provide better individual performance at the expense of complexity, later CNN-based systems, including lightweight models like MobileNet, achieved superior sensitivity with efficiency.
