The proposed system uses unsupervised anomaly detection of system and security logs to extract behavioral deviations caused by events and models the resulting anomaly scores with behavioral features to predict churn using supervised classification. Experimentally, it has been demonstrated that the performance is significantly better than the behavior-only baselines with a churn detect probability of about 0.45 at 20 days, 0.68 at 10 days and 0.92 at 5 days before churn as compared to 0.75 with a baseline model. The structure also enhances the isolation of risks, and the median churn score is greater (0.69) as compared to the traditional methodologies (0.50). These findings indicate that event-log anomaly signals are quality early warning signs of customer churn.

title: A Tensegrity-based Spherical Mobile Robot with Multiple Rolling Gait Patterns
authors: Niansong Zhang, Xinfeng Shao, Rui Jiang, Bing Leng, Jiacheng Cai, Yixiang Liu, Yibin Li
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: In recent years, tensegrity structures are being increasingly used in mobile robots because of their outstanding properties, such as impact resistance, large load-to-weight ratios, and self-balancing. However, most rolling tensegrity robots use center-of-mass offsets to move, making the robots inefficient and less maneuverable. In this paper, a propulsion induced spherical movable tensegrity robot which can realize multiple rolling gait patterns is proposed. Firstly, the novel rolling tensegrity robot is designed by integrating a 6-rod spherical tensegrity structure and 12 propellers, and its multi-gait rolling mechanism is presented. Based on this design, not only are the three traditional rolling gaits along the sides of the ground contact triangle realized, but also three new rolling gaits around the vertices of the triangle. Secondly, the driving forces required for the six rolling gaits are analyzed and then the step length and path space of the two types of gaits are analyzed. Finally, a prototype of the proposed robot is developed, and experiments are carried out. The results show that the proposed robot exhibits superior gait versatility and improves rolling efficiency and maneuverability compared to traditional tensegrity robots.

title: Forecasting Customer Churn Using Machine Learning: A Marketing Analytics Approach
authors: Ashish Kumar Jha, Satish Kumar, D.K. Pandey, Sunil Upadhyay
publish: 2025 14th International Conference on System Modeling &amp; Advancement in Research Trends (SMART)
abstract: The modern business environment demands equal importance for customer retention as it does for customer acquisition. The practice of customers ending their relationships with brands represents a major business concern which affects all industries. Organizations now use big data and advanced analytics together with machine learning techniques to actively forecast and minimize customer churn. This research demonstrates a marketing analytics strategy for predicting customer churn through supervised machine learning algorithms which includes Logistic Regression and Decision Trees and Random Forests and Gradient Boosting Machines. The analysis uses a synthetic dataset which represents realistic customer data through variables that include age and tenure and monthly spend and support interaction frequency. The most important predictors of churn are determined through SHAP (SHapley Additive explanations) values and correlation matrices. The evaluation of model performance relies on accuracy and precision and recall and F1-score and AUC-ROC metrics. Ensemble methods demonstrate superior performance than basic models when analyzing complex nonlinear customer behavior patterns because they achieve an AUC-ROC score of 0.89.

title: PMTNet: A Patch-Based Multi-Scale Convolution-Transformer for Short-Horizon Kiln Tail Temperature Forecasting in Zinc Rotary Kilns
authors: Yingzi Wang, Xiaoming Wang, Yiming Li, Chaobo Zhang, Weihua Gui, Xiaojun Liang
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: Rotary kilns are core facilities in hydrometallurgical smelting, where kiln tail temperature directly reflects reaction intensity and stability. Accurate short-term forecasting is essential for effective process monitoring, preventing efficiency loss and unsafe fluctuations. Traditional mechanistic models require heavy calibration and fail under nonlinear dynamics, while existing deep models struggle with unstable training and limited multi-scale representation. To address these issues, we propose PMTNet, a hybrid convolution-transformer framework. Its delta-target strategy stabilizes optimization, patch-based embedding and multi-scale convolutions capture local and long-range dependencies, and a transformer encoder with learnable positional encoding enhances cross-variable interactions. Evaluated on 44,640 one-minute samples from a zinc kiln, PMTNet achieves RMSE 0.43° C and MAE 0.25° C, outperforming LSTM, GRU, CNN-LSTM, Transformer, Informer, Autoformer, and PatchTST.

title: SGBD: Sharpness-Aware Mirror Gradient with BLIP-Based Denoising for Robust Multimodal Product Recommendation
authors: Sarthak Srivastava, Kathy Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: The growing integration of computer vision and machine learning into the retail industry—both online and in physical stores—has driven the adoption of multimodal recommender systems to help users navigate increasingly complex product landscapes. These systems leverage diverse data sources, such as product images, textual descriptions, and user-generated content, to better model user preferences and item characteristics. While the fusion of multimodal data helps address issues like data sparsity and cold-start problems, it also introduces challenges such as information inconsistency, noise, and increased training instability. In this paper, we analyze these robustness issues through the lens of flat local minima and propose a strategy that incorporates BLIP—a Vision-Language Model with strong denoising capabilities—to mitigate noise in multi-modal inputs. Our method, Sharpness-Aware Mirror Gradient with BLIP-Based Denoising (SGBD), is a concise yet effective training strategy that implicitly enhances robustness during optimization. Extensive theoretical and empirical evaluations demonstrate its effectiveness across various multimodal recommendation benchmarks. SGBD offers a scalable solution for improving recommendation performance in real-world retail environments, where noisy, high-dimensional, and fast-evolving product data is the norm, making it a promising paradigm for training robust multi-modal recommender systems in retail industry.

title: Early Warning and Assessment of Market Power in Electricity Trading Centers Based on Combined Weighting TOPSIS
authors: Jianwei Cao, Chang He, Jiale Tian, Ruijia Huang, Heyun Dong
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: The backdrop of in-depth advancement of electricity market reform, the market power of market entities, as a key factor, directly affects the degree of market competition and the efficiency of optimal resource allocation. Scientifically evaluating and ranking the market power of major market entities helps to fully grasp the market operation status, optimize resource allocation, and enhance the overall market efficiency. In view of the differences in the characteristics of market power among different market entities, this paper proposes a comprehensive evaluation method for market power based on combined weighting TOPSIS. By constructing a multi-dimensional evaluation system covering market structure, entity behavior and market benefits, the weights of indicators are comprehensively determined by combining the entropy weight method and the analytic hierarchy process (AHP), and the TOPSIS method is used to realize the scientific ranking and comparison of the market power of each entity. The research demonstrates that this method can not only comprehensively quantify market power characteristics but also translate the evaluation results into actionable market power early-warning levels, providing a scientific basis for market risk prevention and control and resource optimization.

title: Structural Optimisation and Assist Performance Evaluation of a Flexible Hip Exoskeleton Inspired by Octopus Tentacles
authors: Meng Li, Zhongcai Pei, Yanan Fan, Zhiyong Tang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Aiming to address the limitations of traditional flexible hip exoskeletons in assistive comfort and performance enhancement, this paper proposes a novel flexible hip exoskeleton design (OT Exoskeleton) inspired by the soft structure of octopus tentacles and combined with Series- Wedge-Structures, in order to enhance lower limb interaction comfort and reduce motion energy consumption. First, through qualitative analysing the force characteristics of the exoskeleton on the thigh, the advantage of the new structure on assistive comfort is clarified. Secondly, an assistive torque function is constructed and an assistive comfort quantification index is defined. With these two as the optimisation objectives, a multi-objective optimisation is performed under multiple constraints using the genetic algorithm to obtain the optimal design parameters. Finally, virtual machine simulation verification is conducted based on Adams software. The results show: Compared with traditional flexible exoskeletons with Series- Wedge-Structures, the OT Exoskeleton offers significantly improved comfort; in addition, compared with working conditions without wearing the OT Exoskeleton, it can reduce the work done in a single gait cycle of the hip joint by 37.5%. This study verifies the effectiveness of the new exoskeleton design and provides theoretical and technical references for the structural optimisation and performance improvement of flexible hip exoskeletons.

title: Latent Gene Diffusion for Spatial Transcriptomics Completion
authors: Paula Cárdenas, Leonardo Manrique, Daniela Vega, Daniela Ruiz, Pablo Arbeláez
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Computer Vision has proven to be a powerful tool for analyzing Spatial Transcriptomics (ST) data. However, current models that predict spatially resolved gene expression from histopathology images suffer from significant limitations due to data dropout. Most existing approaches rely on single-cell RNA sequencing references, making them dependent on alignment quality and external datasets while also risking batch effects and inherited dropout. In this paper, we address these limitations by introducing LGDiST, the first reference-free latent gene diffusion model for ST data dropout. We show that LGDiST outperforms the previous state-of-the-art in gene expression completion, with an average Mean Squared Error that is 18% lower across 26 datasets. Furthermore, we demonstrate that completing ST data with LGDiST improves gene expression prediction performance on six state-of-the-art methods up to 10% in MSE. A key innovation of LGDiST is using context genes previously considered uninformative to build a rich and biologically meaningful genetic latent space. Our experiments show that removing key components of LGDiST, such as the context genes, the ST latent space, and the neighbor conditioning, leads to considerable drops in performance. These findings underscore that the full architecture of LGDiST achieves substantially better performance than any of its isolated components.

title: Adversarial Reality for Evading Deepfake Image Detectors
authors: Umur Aybars Çiftçi, Nicholas Solar, Emily Greene, Sophie Riley Saremsky, İlke Demir
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: As deepfake detection systems become more sophisticated, understanding their vulnerabilities becomes crucial for developing robust defenses. We present a comprehensive study of adversarial attacks against image-based deepfake detectors, introducing a novel approach that creates “adversarial reality” - synthetic images that maintain visual similarity to original deepfakes while successfully evading automated detection systems. Our method employs a generative framework with a UNet-style architecture to transform GAN-generated, diffusion-generated, and face-manipulated images into detector-fooling variants while preserving visual fidelity. Unlike traditional perturbation-based approaches that add noise patterns, our generative method learns image-specific transformations without requiring hand-crafted spectral filters. Through extensive evaluation on multiple datasets, generator types, and detector architectures, we demonstrate that our approach achieves misclassification rates of up to 98.83% on diffusion imagery and 83.36% on GAN-based content, while maintaining high perceptual quality with average PSNR scores above 35. Our findings reveal critical vulnerabilities in current detection systems and provide insights for developing more robust deepfake detectors.

title: Federated Learning based Distributed Firewall for Zero Trust Networks
authors: Rahul Mittal, Asis Kumar Tripathy
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: Modern network infrastructures face ever-more sophisticated cyber threats that render traditional perimeter-focused security deployments less effective. Centralized and traditional firewalls and intrusion detection systems are typically ineffective in offering security within dynamic and distributive settings where trust cannot be assumed. To address these issues, a federated learning-based distributive firewall designed for zero trust networks is proposed in this work. The system enables many network nodes to work together and build a global detection model without needing access to unsourced traffic data, thereby preserving data privacy while enhancing collective defensive strengths. Local firewall instances at every node process traffic pattern, update their model, and contribute in a secure aggregation process that broadens the overall system without leaking sensitive information. Experimental comparisons demonstrate that the suggested scheme increases detection accuracy, reduces false positive issues, and adjusts well in response to shifting attack patterns compared to existing centralized firewalls. This study provides a working framework for federated intelligence to create security apparatuses for zero trust environments which are scalable, reliable and considerate of client’s privacy.

title: A Hybrid Requirements Engineering Methodology for Certification of Medical Device Software
authors: Pablo F. Del Hierro Cadena
publish: 2025 Fifth International Conference on Information Systems and Software Technologies (ICI2ST)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel hybrid requirements engineering methodology designed to address the challenges of meeting stringent certification requirements for safety-critical medical device software. The methodology integrates User Stories, Use Case Diagrams, and Problem Frames for comprehensive requirements elicitation, followed by the Box Structure Development Method (BSDM) and Sequence-Based Specification (SBS) for precise behaviour modelling. This structured process enables a seamless and traceable translation into formal Communicating Sequential Processes (CSP), with automated verification performed using the Failures-Divergence Refinement (FDR) model checker. The approach reduces ambiguity, enhances traceability, and strengthens safety assurance, thereby providing objective evidence for certification. An infusion pump case study demonstrates the methodology’s practical applicability and benefits in supporting certification, highlighting its potential to improve software quality and reduce certification costs.

title: Notch Filter-Based Vibration Suppression Method for the Cooled Infrared Electro-Optical Platform
authors: Long Chen, Huhai Jiang, Tongtong Zhang, Guanya Li, Fengchen Hua, Honghao Yu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Communication (ICAIRC)
abstract: To address the image jitter induced by cryocooler vibration in electro-optical platforms, a vibration-suppression control method based on an adaptive notch filter is proposed. Analysis of the experimental data reveals the typical characteristics of the cryocooler's vibration, from which a design procedure for a double-T notch filter is derived. Considering that the peak frequency drifts significantly with operating time, a realtime online notch-filter control model featuring high filtering efficiency and low computational load is developed using a fast Fourier transform. System-level testing shows that the proposed method reduces the average variance of angular velocity and the average spike amplitude to 0.14 and 0.22 of their pre-filter values, respectively, demonstrating superior vibration attenuation.

title: Attack-Agnostic Adversarial Detection via Feature Enhancement
authors: Xingmeng Song, Lan Yang, Shichen Li
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: Deep neural networks (DNNs) achieve state-of-the-art performance in many vision tasks, yet they remain susceptible to adversarial examples, which are inputs perturbed by small, often imperceptible noise that causes incorrect predictions. In this work, we build on the Hessian and Eigen-decomposition-based Adversarial Detection (HEAD) framework by introducing a Grad-CAM-guided, entropy-adaptive spectral filtering module. This module strengthens the expressiveness of the least-significant component feature (LSCF) and the Hessian feature (HF). Specifically, Grad-CAM produces a spatial saliency map that guides a high-pass filter in the frequency domain, with the filter's strength modulated by each image's spectral entropy. This adaptive emphasis concentrates enhancement on decision-relevant regions while respecting image-specific frequency characteristics. Additionally, we append a directional sensitivity scalar, derived from Sobel edge responses, to the HF; this captures orientation-biased structural artifacts. Our proposed method—Grad-CAM-guided Entropy-Adaptive Spectral Filtering (GESF)—remains fully unsupervised and adds no additional training overhead to the HEAD framework. Empirical results demonstrate that GESF achieves ROC AUC scores of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$94.7 \%, 89.9 \%$</tex>, and 97.7 % on the CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and SVHN datasets, respectively.Code available: https://github.com/songky0530/Attack-Agnostic-Adversarial-Detection-via-Feature-Enhancement

title: Nonlinear Control of Pneumatic Soft Robots Based on Koopman Operator
authors: Xiaojian Li, Jiange Yang, Yudong Shi, Jin Fang, Zhehao Sun, Yang Zhao, Hangjie Mo
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: Compared with traditional rigid robots, soft robot offers intrinsic human safety and superior accessibility to confined spaces. However, strong nonlinearity, time variance, and hysteresis challenge modeling and control: physics-based approaches trade off real-time performance, robustness, and accuracy, whereas model-free methods often suffer from low sample efficiency and limited disturbance rejection. In this paper, we design a multi-chamber soft robot and a real-time experimental platform, and propose a Koopman-based quasi-linear control framework that embeds soft robot dynamics into a high-dimensional linear space. Within this space, we implement a feedforward-augmented linear quadratic regulator (LQR) that applies feedback on the embedding error and deploy it on the platform. We evaluate the method on a trajectory-tracking task. The results show smaller tracking errors than open-loop control, and the controller delivers smoother inputs with faster, non-oscillatory convergence.

title: Software-Defined Networking (SDN): A Comprehensive Survey
authors: Trishul Dhale, Pradnyawant M. Gote, Utkarsha Pacharney, Praveen Kumar, Ujwalla H. Gawande, Sasipriya A. S
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: 抓取摘要失败: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='ieeexplore.ieee.org', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /document/11377971/ (Caused by ReadTimeoutError("HTTPSConnectionPool(host='ieeexplore.ieee.org', port=443): Read timed out. (read timeout=15)"))

title: FedHS: Federated Learning Collaborating Heterogeneous IoT Devices to Achieve Large Server Model Fine-Tuning
authors: Shaoyuan Chen, Linlin You
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Information Science, Parallel and Distributed Systems (ISPDS)
abstract: Rapid proliferation of the Internet of Things (IoT) devices has created unprecedented opportunities to harness distributed data to improve large-scale neural networks. However, efficiently fine-tuning such models in IoT environments remains difficult, due to privacy issues, resource limitations, and device heterogeneity. Therefore, we propose FedHS, a federated learning framework that enables server-side large model fine-tuning through collaboration with heterogeneous IoT devices. In FedHS, each client trains a compact model tailored to its device capacity, and the server refines the large model via deep mutual learning with these diverse local models. Mild architectural alignment is imposed to permit partial parameter aggregation, while a lossaware weighting strategy adaptively balances contributions from heterogeneous participants. Experimental results demonstrate that FedHS substantially reduces local resource consumption, while outperforming the traditional federated knowledge transfer method in accuracy.

title: AI-Driven Electromagnetic Design and Optimization: A Survey of Methods and Applications
authors: Xinchen Shao, Jian Ren, Zhaohui Wei, Yingzeng Yin
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is profoundly transforming the field of electromagnetic design and optimization, offering powerful tools to address complex, nonlinear problems that are challenging for traditional methods. This paper presents a comprehensive review of AI-driven approaches in electro magnetics, focusing on the transition from conventional physics-based numerical techniques-such as the finite element method and the method of moments-to data-driven and hybrid AI models. These AI technologies have significantly accelerated design cycles and enabled real-time optimization, with broad applications in antenna design, electromagnetic scattering analysis, and microwave component synthesis. Despite promising advances, challenges remain in practical engineering deployment, including data scarcity, model interpretability, and generalization limitations. This survey aims to clarify the current state of the art and inspire further innovation toward intelligent and robust electromagnetic design.

title: The impact of Social Networking Programs Instagram and Tik Tok on Students’ Mental Pollution
authors: Hassna AlAhbabi, Khaleel Alarabi, Hana Shamsan, Fatima Alhosani, Asma Albreiki, Othman Abu Khurma
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Social Networks Analysis, Management and Security (SNAMS)
abstract: This study aimed to explore the impact of social media programs (Instagram and TikTok) on mental pollution among AI Ain University students by determining the degree of use of these programs, the effect of excessive use on mental distraction, and suggestions that students can adopt to reduce this effect. This study used a correlational descriptive approach with a simple random sample of 70 male and female students from Al Ain University. The data was collected using a questionnaire consisting of two sections: the first dealt with personal variables, and the second contained 15 items distributed over three main axes. The results showed that the degree of students’ use of social media programs was average, as students used these applications on a daily basis, but moderately. The effect of excessive use of social media software on distraction was moderate, as students found it difficult to focus on their assignments due to the excessive use of these applications. Regarding suggestions to minimize negative impacts, students pointed out the importance of setting certain times to stay away from these applications while studying or studying. The study concluded that students should be aware of the negative effects of the excessive use of social media programs and provide strategies to reduce these effects.

title: Feature-based Magnetotelluric Inversion with the Variational Autoencoder Regularization
authors: Hongyu Zhou, Rui Guo, Maokun Li, Fan Yang, Shenheng Xu
publish: 2024 IEEE MTT-S International Conference on Numerical Electromagnetic and Multiphysics Modeling and Optimization (NEMO)
abstract: We study a feature-based magnetotelluric (MT) data inversion regularized by the variational autoencoder (VAE). A proper geophysical dataset is synthetically generated, composed of the possible subsurface models sampled from the a priori resistivity distribution. By training a VAE with the dataset, a priori knowledge is extracted by establishing a regularized latent variable space. The pixel-based subsurface resistivity is reparameterized by the latent variables of the pre-trained VAE. Specifically, we adopt a 1D subdomain reparameterization scheme, i.e., the 2D resistivity model is divided into multiple 1D resistivity-depth models reparameterized by one 1D VAE. In the inversion, latent variables of all the subdomains are optimized with Gauss-Newton method. Experiments show that the proposed inversion effectively enhances the accuracy and resolution of MT data inversion compared with the conventional method.

title: A Circularly Polarized Horn Antenna Integrated with 3D Metal Lens
authors: Lei He, Zhi-Yi Zhang, Shan-Qiu Sun, Kwok Wa Leung
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: A novel gain-enhanced circularly polarized (CP) conical horn antenna with 3D metal lens is investigated in this paper. High gain is achieved by introducing a 3D metal lens to the conical horn. To validate this concept, a prototype working at 16 GHz was designed and simulated using ANSYS HFSS. The simulated results demonstrate that the proposed design achieves a realized gain improvement of over 9.5 dB.

title: Research on Optimal Scheduling of Microgrids with Electric Vehicle Energy Storage
authors: Zhe Yang, Yi Guo
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: With the large-scale popularization of electric vehicles (EVs), their unordered charging behavior poses severe challenges to the load regulation, operational stability, and economy of microgrids. To address this issue, this paper studies the coordinated optimal scheduling method between EVs and microgrids, proposing an orderly charging and discharging strategy integrating the energy storage characteristics of EVs. Firstly, an optimal scheduling model considering both the operating cost and environmental cost of the microgrid is constructed; secondly, based on user travel patterns, an orderly charging and discharging strategy for EVs is designed; further, to improve the model's solution efficiency and global optimization capability, an improved particle swarm optimization algorithm is adopted, which introduces Tent chaos mapping for population initialization and combines adaptive inertia weight and Cauchy mutation operations to enhance performance. Simulation results show that compared with the traditional unordered charging mode, the proposed strategy can effectively suppress grid load fluctuations and significantly reduce system operating costs, verifying its effectiveness in improving the economy and stability of microgrids.

title: Efficient Self-Supervised Neuro-Analytic Visual Servoing for Real-Time Quadrotor Control
authors: Sebastian Mocanu, Sebastian-Ion Nae, Mihai-Eugen Barbu, Marius Leordeanu
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: This work introduces a self-supervised neuro-analytical, cost efficient, model for visual-based quadrotor control in which a small 1.7M parameters student ConvNet learns automatically from an analytical teacher, an improved image-based visual servoing (IBVS) controller. Our IBVS system solves numerical instabilities by reducing the classical visual servoing equations and enabling efficient stable image feature detection. Through knowledge distillation, the student model achieves 11× faster inference compared to the teacher IBVS pipeline, while demonstrating similar control accuracy at a significantly lower computational and memory cost. Our vision-only self-supervised neuro-analytic control, enables quadrotor orientation and movement with-out requiring explicit geometric models or fiducial markers. The proposed methodology leverages simulation-to-reality transfer learning and is validated on a small drone platform in GPS-denied indoor environments. Our key contributions include: (1) an analytical IBVS teacher that solves numerical instabilities inherent in classical approaches, (2) a two-stage segmentation pipeline combining YOLOv11 with a U-Net-based mask splitter for robust anterior-posterior vehicle segmentation to correctly estimate the orientation of the target, and (3) an efficient knowledge distillation dual-path system, which transfers geometric visual servoing capabilities from the analytical IBVS teacher to a compact and small student neural network that outperforms the teacher, while being suitable for real-time onboard deployment.

title: Non-Intrusive Machine Learning-Based Anomaly Detection for Heterogeneous Embedded Platforms
authors: Pranav Gangwani, Alexander Perez-Pons, Himanshu Upadhyay
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: As the number of embedded devices in commercial markets increase, it becomes increasingly important to secure these devices from malicious actors. In this paper, we demonstrate various ways of non-intrusively extracting data from embedded devices through debugging interfaces and using this data with machine learning and deep learning models to detect anomalies in embedded devices. The results exhibit that deep learning models have great potential in embedded cybersecurity, with Convolutional Neural Network and Long Short-Term Memory network models scoring over 95% accuracy on the testing set of our data. This study not only contributes to the ongoing cybersecurity discussion for embedded devices but also creates a foundation for further advancements in the field.

title: HybridSoilNet: A Novel Approach to Classify Soil Nutrient Deficiencies
authors: Bhaskar Karra, M. Rama Krishnan, Ramesh Vaddi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: For agricultural production increase, early detection of plant diseases and nutrient deficiencies is essential. When it comes to identifying leaf patterns caused by nutritional deficiencies, deep learning techniques are highly effective. In this paper, we propose a novel hybrid method for classifying nutrient deficiencies, combining feature extraction approaches such as MobileNetV3, EfficientNet B0, Vision Transformer, and Swin Transformer with an SVM classifier. Among these proposed HybridSoilNet, the Swin Transformer with SVM achieved 99.2% accuracy in recognizing five nutrient deficiencies: iron, magnesium, nitrogen, potassium, and zinc. For interpretability, Grad-CAM++ was used to produce heatmaps, highlighting nutrient deficiency features better than other state-of-the-art (SOTA) models. This work proposes an efficient smart agriculture system improving production oversight and early disease detection.

title: Design of FPGA-Based Neural Network Acceleration System for Object Detection
authors: Tao Xu, Shengjie Xie, Taihua Shu, Tingting Xu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Communication (ICAIRC)
abstract: Object detection, particularly YOLO (You Only Look Once) algorithms, has gained attention for balancing detection accuracy and speed. However, increasing neural network complexity demands higher computational power. This paper proposes an energy-efficient FPGA-based hardware acceleration system for YOLO networks. The YOLOv8n network employs layer fusion optimization and 8-bit quantization via Quantization Aware Training (QAT) to reduce resource consumption. A reusable accelerator architecture with multi-node dynamic configuration enables unified acceleration of heterogeneous layers. Layer-specific optimizations and intra-/inter-layer pipeline mechanisms further enhance computational efficiency. The system uses hardware-software co-design for parallel collaboration between the acceleration engine and soft-core CPU. Implemented on the Xilinx VC707 platform, the system achieves 223.65 GOPS throughput at 200 MHz with 7.63 W power consumption, yielding 29.31 GOPS/W energy efficiency- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8.75 \times$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.41 \times$</tex> improvements over Intel I5-12400F CPU and NVIDIA RTX 4060 GPU, respectively, balancing detection performance and energy consumption.

title: Endonama: Graph Neural Networks Based In-Silico Transcriptomics from Histology Whole Slide Images for Fertility Diagnostics
authors: George Wright, Paul Brighton, Joanne Muter, Jan Brosens, Fayyaz Minhas
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Understanding the link between endometrial gene expression and corresponding histological phenotypes is crucial for assessing luteal phase timing and reproductive success. While current profiling techniques do not typically utilize tissue whole slide images (WSIs), this study underscores their importance, demonstrating significant potential benefits such as deeper spatio-molecular and temporal profiling, cost reduction, and scalability. We present Endonama, a graph neural network (GNN) that predicts the expression of 13 key genes directly from 1,834 CD56-stained endometrial WSIs, comprising over 5 million image patches, thereby reducing reliance on molecular assays. This represents the largest endometrial histology study to date, as well as the first to predict receptivity directly from WSIs. The model also accurately estimates clinically relevant biomarkers, including the SLC15A2/GPX3 ratio for personalized embryo transfer timing (Spearman's p = 0.731, 95% CI: 0.616-0.814) and the PLA2G2A/DIO2 ratio, an age-independent marker of miscarriage risk, supporting targeted interventions and long-term monitoring of recurrent pregnancy loss. Leveraging its graph-based design, Endonama produces interpretable spatial heatmaps that reveal localized and temporal variations in gene expression, providing insights into tissue heterogeneity and function. Endonama offers a powerful tool for personalized fertility assessment and paves the way for new advances in reproductive medicine. Interactive visualizations of Endonama's predictions are available at https://tiademos.dcs.warwick.ac.uk/bokeh_app?demo=Endonama.

title: Multi-Camera 3D Object Tracking via 3D Point Clouds and Re-Identification
authors: Jaewon Lee, Heecheol Kim, Doohee Lee, Kanghee Lee
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Multi-target multi-camera (MTMC) tracking in large-scale 3D environments is a critical challenge, demanding robust reasoning across geometry, time, and appearance amidst severe occlusion and sparsity. We propose a geometry-aware pipeline that tackles these challenges by first reconstructing a unified 3D point cloud from multiple RGB-D views. On this global representation, our method employs a transformer-based detector and a two-stage hybrid tracking framework to ensure persistent identity. This is complemented by a novel Embedding Similarity Classifier (ESC) that leverages discriminative 3D embeddings to resolve extreme class imbalance for rare objects. Evaluated on the 2025 AI City Challenge benchmark, our approach achieved a state-of-the-art 3D Higher Order Tracking Accuracy (HOTA) score of 69.91%, securing 1st place on the public leaderboard. This result validates the effectiveness of our integrated approach, demonstrating a robust solution for building persistent 3D situational awareness in complex environments. Code is publicly available at: https://github.com/ZIOVISION/AIC2025_Track1_ZV

title: Joint Spotting and Recognition of Micro-Expressions in Long Videos via Temporal Representation Learning
authors: Mingyang Yin, Aiguo Song, Chaolong Qin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Robotics and Artificial Intelligence (ICRAI)
abstract: Facial micro-expressions (MEs) are brief and subtle facial movements that reveal genuine emotions, making them critical cues for affective analysis and human-robot interaction. In this work, we propose a unified framework for micro-expression spotting and recognition in long video sequences. Our model is built upon the Perceiver IO architecture, which enables scalable temporal modeling across variable-length sequences by encoding global context into a fixed-size latent space. To simultaneously address spotting and recognition, we adopt a dual-branch decoder that estimates frame-wise expression likelihoods and emotional categories, respectively. To enhance stability and boundary sensitivity, we introduce two auxiliary learning strategies: a soft KL-divergence-based consistency loss that enforces emotion prediction coherence within expression segments, and a boundary-aware contrastive loss that sharpens temporal boundaries between expressive and neutral frames. In addition, we introduce a phased supervision scheme that leverages ground truth segments in early training and pseudolabels in later stages. Experiments conducted on CAS(ME)<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and SAMM-LV demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance.

title: The ID Net Architecture for Interpretable X-ray Baggage Image Denoising
authors: Dioline Sara, Ajay Kumar Mandava
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: The quality of X-ray baggage security imagery is frequently degraded by a mixture of signal-dependent Poisson and additive Gaussian noise. Our work focuses on removing this noise by introducing the Interpretable Denoising Network (ID-Net), a deep learning framework that combines a traditional Convolutional Neural Network with custom layers designed to operate on classical signal processing principles. Specifically, the network architecture uses parallel branches for Bayes-inspired soft thresholding and Sobel gradient extraction for explicit guidance from proven denoising and feature-detection logic. While conventional denoising methods often sacrifice detail for noise reduction and modern deep learning models typically lack interpretability, this work introduces a novel solution to bridge this gap. Our experiments demonstrate that the proposed ID-Net significantly outperforms conventional spatial filters, wavelet transforms, and denoising autoencoders in both noise suppression and the preservation of fine details. The proposed hybrid architecture that successfully integrates the high performance of deep learning with the transparency of classical algorithms removes the noise that complicates threat detection and offers a robust and interpretable solution for enhancing the fidelity of security screening imagery.

title: An Edge Computing Task Promotion Method for Grid Information Technology Innovation Terminals Based on an Improved NSGA-III Algorithm
authors: Guangao Li, Tong Li, Dongyu Li, Shuai Ren, Bo Yuan, Jielin Leng
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: In grid information technology terminals, consideration is limited to terminal resource constraints, resulting in low data processing efficiency. To address this, an upgraded edge computing task method for grid information technology terminals based on the improved NSGA-III algorithm is proposed. Inputting node parameters and network information into the improved NSGA-III algorithm, the algorithm undergoes initialization, crossover, mutation, and other operations to output configuration schemes and determine task transmission rates. Establish a grid information technology terminal edge computing task model. An alternating iteration framework is adopted: initial values are set, the algorithm generates a solution set, and the model is applied to evaluate and output the optimal solution. This yields terminal energy efficiency ratios, objective functions, and resource-latency correlation models, achieving efficient task upgrading for grid information technology terminal edge computing. Experimental results demonstrate that under a scenario with 5 edge computing nodes and 12 smart meter terminals, the design method achieves rational resource allocation. Each edge computing node handles at least 3 terminal tasks, maximizing node potential. After approximately 180 training iterations, the average task processing efficiency reached 87.6% and stabilized.

title: Research on Optimal Power Reservation Configuration for Microgrid Industrial Parks Considering Reliability
authors: Delidana Mansuer, Jianfei Liu, Dongyi Zhao, Quan Lv
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: Against the backdrop of clean energy transition, connecting to the main grid has become a key approach for microgrid industrial parks to achieve low-carbon operations. However, this integration introduces new reliability risks due to the single-source power supply characteristic. To address this, this paper proposes a two-layer optimization model for reliable reserve power allocation, balancing system economics with power supply reliability. The upper-layer model minimizes the sum of annual maintenance costs for reserve power and system outage losses to optimize gas turbine reserve capacity and location. The lower-layer model employs a sequential hybrid approach for full-system reliability assessment, establishing a minimum load shedding model based on Distflow power flow and load priority to precisely quantify outage risks. The model is solved collaboratively using a genetic algorithm and the GUROBI optimizer. Simulation results from an actual microgrid industrial park demonstrate that the proposed method effectively identifies system vulnerabilities. The optimized configuration reduces the system's power deficiency from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$360,722 \text{MWh}$</tex> to 215 MWh, validating the model's feasibility and effectiveness in enhancing system reliability and guiding the scientific allocation of reserve power sources.

title: Adaptive Narrative Coherence Modeling via Graph Embeddings and Reinforcement Learning
authors: Leonidas Boutsikaris, Spyros Polykalas
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Social Networks Analysis, Management and Security (SNAMS)
abstract: Understanding how political narratives evolve and maintain coherence in digital discourse presents fundamental challenges for computational analysis. We introduce a framework that combines subject-verb-object (SVO) triplet extraction with graph-based reinforcement learning to model narrative coherence in communication. Our methodology constructs dynamic narrative graphs from congressional tweets, where nodes represent political entities and edges capture semantic relationships through verb connections. We develop a coherence metric based on graph triangulation, clustering coefficients, and small-world properties, enabling quantitative assessment of narrative structure. The reinforcement learning component continuously optimizes graph configurations to maximize coherence while incorporating sentiment analysis to capture emotional resonance. Experimental validation using 100 congressional tweets demonstrates stable coherence scores, with clear Q-learning convergence patterns. Our analysis reveals distinct narrative strategies: Republicans employ more tightly interconnected security-focused messaging, while Democrats utilize broader, less centralized discourse spanning diverse social issues. The framework successfully identifies three dominant narrative clusters around Trump accountability, Biden policy implementation, and national security themes.

title: Explainable Anomaly Detection in Cloud-Native Systems via RAG-Enhanced Vector Retrieval
authors: Vishnupriya S Devarajulu, Sunitha Kanipakam, Tulasi Kavarakuntla
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Information Science, Parallel and Distributed Systems (ISPDS)
abstract: Modern cloud-native systems based on Kubernetes and microservices architectures generate massive volumes of telemetry data that challenge traditional anomaly detection approaches. While existing tools can identify potential issues, they fail to provide contextual explanations, resulting in prolonged incident resolution times and compliance challenges in regulated industries. This paper introduces a novel framework that combines Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with vector similarity search to deliver explainable anomaly detection for distributed systems. The approach retrieves relevant historical incidents using semantic embeddings, then generates humanreadable diagnostic explanations through large language models. Additionally, the framework incorporates compliance-aware audit mechanisms to satisfy regulatory requirements including GDPR, SOC 2, and HIPAA. Experimental evaluation on a realistic enterprise microservices deployment demonstrates a 42% reduction in mean time to resolution, 76% F1-score for anomaly detection, and 39% reduction in false positives. The system achieves 78% accuracy in technical explanation correctness and 89% compliance audit readiness, addressing critical operational and regulatory challenges in cloud-native environments.

title: Pyls: Enabling Python Hardware Synthesis with Dynamic Polymorphism via LCRS Encoding
authors: Bolei Tong, Yongyan Fang, Chaorui Wang, Qingan Li, Jingling Xue, Mengting Yuan
publish: 2026 IEEE/ACM International Symposium on Code Generation and Optimization (CGO)
abstract: Python dominates AI development and is the most widely used dynamic programming language, but synthesizing its polymorphic functions into hardware remains challenging. Existing HLS solutions support only static subsets of Python, forcing CPU offload with costly communication overhead. We present Pyls, the first framework that synthesizes dynamically polymorphic Python into monolithic hardware via Left-Child Right-Sibling (LCRS) encoding. Key to our approach is representing all Python objects as LCRS trees, enabling uniform hardware handling of dynamic types. Pyls automatically converts objects to fixed-width formats, generates XLS IR designs, and implements a tree memory architecture for efficient runtime type resolution. On FPGA platforms, Pyls demonstrates speedups of 5.19× and 3.98× over two ASIC CPUs, 303.29× over a soft-core processor, and 282.66× over a heterogeneous SoC design.

title: An LLM-Empowered Technical Approach and Practical Study for Engineering Quantity Calculation
authors: Li Zhang, Shiheng Zhou, Jing Guo, Zhousong Li, Xiaomin Lv, Ningning Wang
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Information Science, Parallel and Distributed Systems (ISPDS)
abstract: To address the challenges of the traditional engineering quantity calculation process, which is often tedious, labor-intensive, and inefficient, this paper proposes an intelligent engineering quantity calculation system framework centered on a Large Language Model (LLM). The framework features a modular, four-layer architecture and integrates Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) technology to enhance the LLM’s accuracy in this specialized domain. Based on this framework, we designed and developed an automated quantity surveying system that leverages Prompt Engineering and workflow orchestration to automate the process from engineering parameter data to quantity calculation results. Experimental results show that the system achieves excellent performance, with a calculation accuracy of 90.38% and an average runtime of 15.294 seconds per calculation instance. For data completeness recognition, the system achieved an accuracy of 92.59% and an $\mathbf{F 1}$-score of 94.00%. This study not only validates the effectiveness of applying LLMs and RAG technology to quantity surveying but also provides a feasible technical pathway and practical reference for building intelligent engineering applications.

title: A LightGBM-Based Framework for Detecting Non-Compliant Cross-Region Account Openings in Telecommunications
authors: Xiang Chen, Xiaodong Zhang, Yue Yang, Zhiyan Cai, Yuxia Cheng, Wanjiang Gao, Jinfeng Li, Yaobing Xu, Yao Chen, Jitao He
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Communication (ICAIRC)
abstract: The detection of non-compliant cross-region account openings poses significant challenges for telecom operators, as traditional rule-based methods often suffer from incomplete coverage, high false-positive rates, and inadequate real-time performance. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a detection framework centered on the LightGBM algorithm, which integrates a systematic process encompassing whitelist preprocessing, multi-source feature fusion, and a closedloop feedback mechanism. A comprehensive multi-dimensional feature system is constructed by fusing spatiotemporal, behavioral, and user profile data. To mitigate the severe class imbalance inherent in the data, a hybrid SMOTE-Tomek sampling strategy is employed. Experimental validation on one month of real-world data from a provincial telecom operatorcomprising 3000,000 legitimate and 6,000 non-compliant accounts-demonstrates that our model achieves a recall of 97.45%, an F 1 -score of 0.2894, and an AUC of 0.9983. These results represent a substantial improvement over conventional approaches, while the framework's design enables real-time detection at the second level, offering a robust solution for managing cross-regional account opening risks.

title: IMU-Based Activity Recognition and Payload Estimation for Augmentative Exoskeleton
authors: Sahil Nishad, Shubhashisa Sahoo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Robotics and Artificial Intelligence (ICRAI)
abstract: Augmentative exoskeleton is a wearable device which is used to enhance human physical capabilities by providing additional support and strength. Such exoskeleton uses a multi-tier control system to automatically adjust its support based on the user’s activity and the payload’s weight. The high-level control system establishes the foundation for adaptive control responses by analyzing the wearer’s movements and payload conditions. The mid-level controller maps these activities into precise control commands in form of torque and angular velocity at the joints of the exoskeleton. At the low level, a feedback controller executes these commands. In this study, a high-level control system is developed to utilize the data collected from the Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) to recognize crucial exoskeleton movements such as walking, lifting, and lowering, along with estimating the weights of the carried payload. Most of the existing studies primarily focuses on activity recognition, leaving payload estimation relatively unexplored, which is equally crucial for developing such controller. This study proposes a hybrid deep learning framework that combines a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) with a Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Unit (BiGRU), enhanced with an attention mechanism. The proposed model performs not only activity recognition but also the estimation of the carried payload’s weight while improving upon traditional methods with an F1-score of 95.14% for the activity recognition and 95.86% for the payload estimation on a publicly available open-source dataset.

title: Improved Tightly Coupled Dipole Array with Frequency Selective Surface for Enhanced Efficiency
authors: Zhixi Shi, Nengwu Liu
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: An improved tightly coupled dipole array (TCDA) loaded with a frequency selective surface (FSS) is presented in this paper. This improved antenna is modified from a conventional vertical dipole, with feeding implemented through a 200 Ω lumped port. The FSS, implemented as a row of metallic patches, significantly enhances the antenna's total efficiency across a broad operating bandwidth. The metal patches are horizontally oriented and arranged parallel to the dipole. It was found that both vertically oriented and cross-polarized placements provide minimal improvement in efficiency. Through optimization of the size and arrangement of the metal patches, it was found that too large metal patches or overly dense arrangements reduce the bandwidth, while smaller metal patches and sparse arrangements have negligible effects. Additionally, it was found that shifting the dipole and the coupled patches inward can increase the bandwidth. By optimizing the position of dipole and coupled patches, the antenna achieves improved impedance matching and expanded bandwidth characteristics(from 3.65GHz-8.5 GHz to 3GHz-10.85 GHz).

title: Research on Full-Cycle Dispersive Gradient Highpower W-Band Folded Waveguide Compact Traveling Wave Tube
authors: You Hui He, Luqi Zhang, Guowu Ma, Rui Song
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: In this paper, a novel 94GHz high-power compact traveling wave tube (TWT) is designed by integrating a Full-Cycle Dispersion Gradient Weak reflection folded waveguide and circular electron beam focus by superconducting. Firstly, novel electron-optical system (EOS) based on PPM is developed, which can reach a maximum magnetization of 1.0T and a high compression ratio Pierce electron gun, which is able to emit a 23.0 kV,180 mA circular electron beam. Under the focus of this magnetic system, the sheet electron beam which has a Horizontal dimension of 0.32mm*0.32mm can be 100% transmitted at the distance of 100mm. Meanwhile, based on this electron-optic system, a novel Full-Cycle Dispersion Gradient Weak reflection folded waveguide Slow-Wave Structure (SWS) of TWT is proposed, which is achieved by gradually transitioning widedispersion units to slow-wave units with stronger strong dispersion characteristics. The entire high-frequency system can achieve stable operation with high gain without being cut off by the attenuate, simulation results of beam-wave interaction show that the device can produce more than 200 W power output in the frequency range from 90GHz to 96.5GHz with a driven power of 0.05W, which corresponding to a power-bandwidth product of 3kW*GHz. The maximum stable power output at 91.5GHz is 465W and the corresponding gain is 39.5dB.

title: Evaluating Optimizers in Customized CNNs for Multi-Class Respiratory Diagnosis using Chest X-rays
authors: Keerthana R B, N P Ganesh Kumar, Vishnudasan K, Vaisshale R, Muthulakshmi M
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: Respiratory diseases are one of the major health concerns worldwide; especially in places with limited medical facilities. This project helps doctors to detect these diseases quickly and easily by using chest X-Ray images and artificial intelligence (AI). This proposed paper addresses key issues such as high computational cost and the challenge of real-time deployment. A customized 9-layer Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is designed to classify six types of lung disease from X-rays. The model achieved a test accuracy of 93.68% and performed better than ResNet-18 in all key metrics. The confusion matrices showed consistent results in the training, validation, and test sets. GradCAM++ heat maps also matched actual disease-affected regions, making the model easier to trust. The system can be used in rural or resource-limited hospitals to help doctors to make quick and reliable decisions. It can support early diagnosis and better treatment using just X-rays and a lightweight AI model.

title: CS3D: An Efficient Facial Expression Recognition via Event Vision
authors: Zhe Wang, Qijin Song, Yucen Peng, Weibang Bai
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Responsive and accurate facial expression recognition is crucial to human-robot interaction for daily service robots. Nowadays, event cameras are becoming more widely adopted as they surpass RGB cameras in capturing facial expression changes due to their high temporal resolution, low latency, computational efficiency, and robustness in low-light conditions. Despite these advantages, event-based approaches still encounter practical challenges, particularly in adopting mainstream deep learning models. Traditional deep learning methods for facial expression analysis are energy-intensive, making them difficult to deploy on edge computing devices and thereby increasing costs, especially for high-frequency, dynamic, event vision-based approaches. To address this challenging issue, we proposed the CS3D framework by decomposing the Convolutional 3D method to reduce the computational complexity and energy consumption. Additionally, by utilizing soft spiking neurons and a spatial-temporal attention mechanism, the ability to retain information is enhanced, thus improving the accuracy of facial expression detection. Experimental results indicate that our proposed CS3D method attains higher accuracy on multiple datasets compared to architectures such as the RNN, Transformer, and C3D, while the energy consumption of the CS3D method is just 21.97 % of the original C3D required on the same device.

title: Enhanced YOLOv8 for Robust Multi-Task Perception in Autonomous Driving Under Challenging Environments
authors: Linshu Song, Duanling Li, Chengeng Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: This study focuses on optimizing image segmentation and object detection tasks for autonomous driving and ground-based obstacle avoidance robotic systems, aiming to improve recognition accuracy and real-time performance in complex environments. A heterogeneous multi-source dataset is constructed, covering challenging scenarios such as low lighting, adverse weather, and occlusion. Based on the YOLOv8 framework, separate models are developed for object detection and instance segmentation. In traffic sign detection, CBAM attention mechanisms and BiFPN multi-scale feature fusion modules are integrated to enhance small object detection and feature aggregation. For instance segmentation, the model combines BiFPN (Bidirectional Feature Pyramid Network) with an SDI (Semantic-Detail Infusion) structure to strengthen semantic understanding and edge detail modeling, enabling more robust performance under varying lighting conditions and blurred backgrounds. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior robustness and accuracy compared to baseline models in complex scenarios, making it suitable for real-time autonomous driving applications. Moreover, thanks to its lightweight architecture and modular design, the system exhibits excellent hardware adaptability and can be effectively applied to perception and navigation tasks in various ground mobile platforms, such as service robots, inspection robots, and logistics robots.

title: Generation Modelling of Artificial Personalities Using Decision Dynamics and Curiosity-Driven Behavior
authors: Yuan Zhou, Siamak Khatibi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Communication (ICAIRC)
abstract: This paper introduces a generative framework for individual agent behavioral modelling based on artificial personalities (APs), which has been defined as distinct distributions over decision strategies in the given task. Using Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) sampling method, we generate action sequences that embody each AP's internal decision dynamics. The framework is evaluated through a numberguessing task, where five APs are compared based on two performance metrics: the length of the action sequence and a curiosity score, which quantifies deviation from the optimal solution. Results show that while agents demonstrated comparable task efficiency, their curiosity levels varied significantly, reflecting diverse exploratory tendencies. These findings support the feasibility of encoding strategic individuality in artificial agents, enabling richer behavioral modeling for applications in simulation, multi-agent coordination, and human-AI interaction.

title: Deep Learning-Based Tornado Detection Using Multi-Scale Spatiotemporal Fusion of Polarimetric Weather Radar Data
authors: Jiedi Chen
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: Tornadoes pose severe threats to life and property, yet operational detection systems suffer from high false alarm rates exceeding 70%. This work presents a novel deep learning framework for automated tornado detection using full-resolution WSR-88D Level II polarimetric radar observations. We introduce a hierarchical architecture integrating coordinate-aware polarimetric feature extraction, spatiotemporal encoding via U-Net and BiConvLSTM, and multi-scale supervision with focal loss. Evaluated on a decade-long dataset spanning diverse tornado intensities (EF0-EF5), our method achieves superior performance with critical success index exceeding 0.48 at 20-minute forecast horizons, substantially outperforming operational baselines while maintaining temporal consistency through learned attention mechanisms.

title: An Improved Genetic Algorithm-Based Method for Task Scheduling and Resource Allocation in Avionics Network
authors: Yuhang Luo, Yicheng Wu, Bo Xu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Communication (ICAIRC)
abstract: Addressing the challenge of task scheduling and resource configuration in airborne networks under multi-constraint coupled environments, this paper proposes an optimization framework based on an Improved Genetic Algorithm (IGA). This framework constructs a constraint model incorporating factors such as terminal computing resources, link bandwidth, and task period limitations. It designs a multiobjective function that comprehensively considers maximum completion time, terminal load balancing ratio, and resource utilization ratio. An improved genetic algorithm is introduced to achieve the collaborative optimization of task scheduling and resource configuration, thereby enhancing the overall system performance. Experimental results demonstrate that under two typical scenarios of low terminal load and high terminal load, the task scheduling scheme generated by the IGA outperforms non-optimized schemes in terms of terminal bandwidth utilization, terminal load balancing ratio, and resource utilization ratio. Furthermore, it surpasses schemes generated by traditional genetic algorithms and ant colony algorithms in aspects such as terminal load balancing ratio and resource utilization ratio, validating the effectiveness and superiority of the IGA-based method for optimizing task scheduling and resource configuration.

title: Design and Development of an Adaptive Respiratory Exercise Robot
authors: Sabir Saheel, Arshia Khan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Robotics and Artificial Intelligence (ICRAI)
abstract: While breathing techniques have been used for centuries, a modern method that incorporates robotics could improve their efficacy. We describe the creation of the Adaptive Respiratory Exercise System (ARES), a robot that engages in box breathing exercises, and the final iteration of the prior robot known as BREATH. Built with an ESP32 microprocessor, a speaker, and two opposite-facing linear actuators, the fully 3D-printed design has a minimalist spherical form that conceals its internal parts. During breathing, the robot expands to signal an inhale and contracts to signal an exhale, timed for 4 seconds to facilitate box breathing. ARES is compact and modular so it can be used on its own or placed inside various casings, depending on the target audience.

title: Blockchain-based Distributed Provenance and Data Integrity Assurance Research for Digital Heritage Archives
authors: Rui Li
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Information Science, Parallel and Distributed Systems (ISPDS)
abstract: The permanent, secure, and trustworthy preservation of digital heritage archives presents a significant challenge for cultural inheritance. Addressing core issues in traditional centralized management systems, such as data vulnerability to tampering, lack of provenance information, and fragile trust mechanisms, this paper proposes a Blockchain-based Distributed Digital Heritage Archive Provenance and Integrity Model (BDHAPI). The model adopts a “Blockchain + IPFS” collaborative architecture. It utilizes IPFS (InterPlanetary File System) for the distributed storage of archive data entities, resolving the blockchain’s storage bottleneck. Cryptographic hash functions are used to lock the archive content’s “digital fingerprint”. The core contribution lies in the design of a smart contract to construct a structured, immutable full life-cycle Provenance Event Chain (PE) on the blockchain, enabling the automated and trustless execution of archive registration, integrity verification, and provenance queries. To validate the model’s effectiveness, a comparative experiment was conducted on the Hyperledger Fabric consortium blockchain platform using the Europeana public digital heritage dataset. Experimental results show that, compared to a traditional Centralized Database (CDB) and a simplified blockchain solution (BC-Simple), the BDHAPI model has reasonable overhead in write and query latency but exhibits an absolute advantage in security. It achieves 100% detection of tampering with data, metadata, and provenance logs, providing a highly trustworthy data integrity assurance and a transparent, traceable chain of evidence for digital heritage archives.

title: TI-TNet: A Text-Image Fusion Network for Thyroid Nodule Segmentation
authors: Xingfang Ai, Zhihao Jin, Yifeng Zeng, Shaojun Zou
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: The typical initial symptom of thyroid cancer is a thyroid nodule (TN). The rapid and accurate automatic lesion/cancerous area segmentation algorithm is not only a means of auxiliary diagnosis for clinicians, but also can reduce the heavy workload to some extent. In this work, we proposed a text-image fusion network - TI-TNet, which using text information to aid thyroid nodule segmentation. To validate this multimodal fusion mechanism, we construct a visual-text dataset based on thyroid ultrasound images. To address interference from redundant text, we transform the text data into attribute triples with strong semantic correspondence. Similarly, to mitigate background noise in ultrasound images, we employ a dual-stage network within the segmentation framework. Within this network architecture, we investigate the impact of feature alignment, fusion mode (e.g., early or late fusion), fusion location, and training sample size on the performance of the multimodal fusion network. Related experiments prove that the proposed multimodal fusion network can improve the network segmentation ability by introducing text features.

title: Eco-Friendly Synthesis of Benzimidazole: Linking Green Chemistry with Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare
authors: Aniket Sanjay Chaudhari, P.K.P. Gaitry Chopra
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Benzimidazole and derivatives are favored scaffolds in medicinal chemistry as antimicrobials, anticancer agents, antivirals, antiparasitic and proton pump inhibitors. Despite their extensive use, traditional synthetic techniques have undesirable attributes that include a severe reaction condition, low yields and unsustainability. Conversely, synthetic techniques, such as green catalysis, nanotechnology, and microwave-assisted reactions legitimized recently offer eco-friendly and scalable approaches with high-yield, yield. The present review summarizes the knowledge presented in 21 articles (2000-2025) and outlines the reshaping of benzimidazole manufacturing solutions. Moreover, it highlights how the emerging methods enable the use of AI in healthcare, as it allows choosing a fast predictive approach to drug discovery based on rapidly and reproducibly generated and data-rich compound library.

title: Artificial Intelligence in the Assessment of Range of Motion for Musculoskeletal Conditions During Telerehabilitation
authors: Lajwanti Lalwani, Amit Bhayani
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence in physiotherapy helps in assessment, personalized treatment and also in assessing the progress of the musculoskeletal conditions. Assessment of Range of motion (ROM) of patient is important in various musculoskeletal conditions. The present study uses AI for assessment of ROM using pose detection and flexifyMe app softwares. 22 patients were enrolled and informed consent was obtained. Baseline assessment of ROM and after 1 5 session the ROM was measured and change was calculated. The study included 6-shoulder patients, 7- cervical patients, 6- low back patients and 3 knee patients. All patients showed improved ROM after 15 sessions. The study concluded that AI in the assessment of ROM for musculoskeletal conditions during tele rehabilitation is effective can be used for remote treatment.

title: Design of a Wide Dualband Circularly Polarized Rectangular Dielectric Resonator Antenna Using Deep Learning
authors: Xijiao Yang, Yuanchao Shi, Liangliang Cui, Bin Li
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: This paper introduces an optimization methodology for a dual-band, broadband circularly polarized rectangular dielectric resonator antenna (CPDRA), combining a Deep Residual Attention Network with the Non-dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm II (NSGA-II). The design process is organized into three stages: data preparation for network training and prediction, construction of the neural network architecture, and parameter optimization using NSGA-II. By integrating a multi-head attention mechanism and a residual structure, the network enhances its global modeling capacity and feature trade-off capability for key performance metrics such as impedance bandwidth and axial ratio bandwidth, thereby improving the accuracy and stability of multi-objective predictions. Once trained, the network can rapidly and accurately predict antenna performance from geometric parameters within seconds. To demonstrate the approach, a DRA was designed for the 2.5-4.5 GHz band, achieving impedance bandwidths of 24% and 5.54%, along with axial ratio bandwidths (AR < 3 dB) of 19.2% and 8.2%, respectively. In comparison to conventional methods, the proposed network enables efficient multi-objective design with a reduced simulation dataset, significantly accelerating the antenna optimization workflow.

title: Machine Learning in Drug Discovery and Development
authors: Narendra Pal Singh, Abhay Gangawr
publish: 2025 14th International Conference on System Modeling &amp; Advancement in Research Trends (SMART)
abstract: This is more important that how to reduce the human workload and enhance quality of life significantly as number of diseases are increase day to day. For this purpose, the current rise of machine learning in drug discovery and development has been playing a significant role. This article basically describes two main tasks in drug discovery and development. First one is target identification and hit generation to absorption, distribution, metabolism, excretion and toxicity (ADMET) prediction and clinical trial optimization. Second one is how ML models especially deep learning, graph neural networks and generative models are increasing timelines, improving hit quality and enabling data-driven decisions. This paper categorizes various machine learning methodologies, common datasets and benchmarks, representative applications such as virtual screening, de-novo design, retrosynthesis, ADMET and industrial successes. The discussion also covers specific persistent challenges such as data quality, interpretability, domain shift and regulatory concerns in drug discovery and development. The findings cover in this paper should help researchers, students, and the pharmaceutical industry learn more about machine learning and artificial intelligence in the context of drug discovery and development. They also show how machine learning techniques are widely used in pharmaceutical research, drug discovery and development and also point to a bright future for these technologies.

title: Optimal Scheduling of High-Altitude Multi-Energy Microgrid Clusters Based on Robust Optimization and Tiered Carbon Trading Mechanism
authors: Tao Zhang, Yifan Zhang, Xu Jia, Pengbo Ma, Dingwen Zhou, Ruijin Zhu
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: Under the “dual carbon” goals, the construction of low-carbon energy systems in high-altitude regions has gained widespread attention. To enhance the energy consumption and low-carbon economic operation of multi-microgrid systems in high-altitude areas, this paper proposes a coordinated scheduling method for multi-microgrids based on robust optimization and tiered carbon trading. Firstly, this paper constructs a combined heat and power (CHP) model integrating Power-to-Gas with Carbon Capture (P2G-CCS) and Power-to-Hydrogen with Vacuum Pressure Swing Adsorption (P2H-VPSA) systems, and develops a low-carbon operation scheduling model for multi-energy microgrid clusters, combined with the tiered carbon trading mechanism. Secondly, a robust optimization method is applied to reduce the risks brought by electricity price fluctuations. Finally, the Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM) is used for distributed solving to optimize micro grid trading volumes and ensure supply-demand balance. The simulation results show that this method significantly reduces the operational and overall energy costs of microgrids, with promising application prospects.

title: An Intelligent Strata Identification Method for Shield Tunneling Based on a State-Space Model and Dynamic Thresholding
authors: Wenqing Wu, Chao Zheng, Jinjin Zhang, Tianming Zhan
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: The efficiency and safety of shield tunnel construction depend heavily on an accurate assessment of the upcoming strata. Traditional methods for strata identification suffer from issues such as lag and high subjectivity. To achieve real-time and precise identification of complex strata during tunneling, this paper proposes an intelligent recognition method based on the State-Space Model (Mamba) and dynamic threshold determination. First, the method utilizes the real-time tunneling parameters of the shield machine and enhances the data by constructing physical-derived features and temporal fluctuation features. Secondly, to address the issue of strata sample imbalance, SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique) is employed to optimize the training data. Next, the novel Mambular classifier is used for model training, capturing the long-sequence dependencies between tunneling parameters. The most innovative aspect of this paper is the design of a post-processing method that decomposes the composite strata probabilities predicted by the model into individual strata probabilities, dynamically setting decision thresholds based on the frequency of occurrence of each strata in the training set. This approach effectively identifies composite strata formed by multiple geotechnical layers. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves excellent classification performance in an actual metro line 4 tunneling data set with a test accuracy of 92.14 %, validating the effectiveness and reliability of the method in engineering applications.

title: A Design of Wider Impedance Bandwidth with Spider-Parasitic Elements Patch Antenna for Ku-Band Applications
authors: Saritha Vanka, Gowri Sri Sannihitha Vaddi, Divya Sai Vittamsetti, Sindhura Ravuri, Alekhya Bandi, Vara Prasad Kudumu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: In this work, a novel microstrip patch antenna with spider-shaped parasitic elements integrated for improved impedance bandwidth for Ku-band applications is designed and analyzed. The suggested antenna arrangement greatly increases the impedance bandwidth while preserving a small form factor by manipulating the electromagnetic coupling effects of carefully placed parasitic structures. The antenna is appropriate for high-frequency wireless applications such as radar systems and satellite communication because it displays consistent radiation patterns and gain performance throughout the targeted Ku-band frequency range. The potential of spider-parasitic geometries in advanced antenna engineering is confirmed by the results of the design’s implementation and optimization using full-wave electromagnetic simulation tools.

title: MAHAD: Multi-Granularity Autoencoder for Hyperedge Anomaly Detection
authors: Kaihui Yang, Hui Feng, Bo Hu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Communication (ICAIRC)
abstract: Hypergraphs are widely used to model complex and high-order interactions in real-world systems, which have received increasing research attention recently in the context of artificial intelligence and data mining. Despite their growing importance, the problem of detecting anomalous hyperedges remains underexplored.
