There are two key challenges: (1) hypergraphs exhibit higher-order topological structures and highdimensional features more complex than ordinary graphs; (2) anomalies often arise from subtle abnormal behaviors at different granularities of constituent nodes, such as a small number of malicious individuals within a collaborative group, making them difficult to detect. To address these challenges, we propose an unsupervised Multi-Granularity Autoencoder for Hyperedge Anomaly Detection (MAHAD), a novel machine learning approach based on neural network modeling. Specifically, we introduce a hypergraph mask convolution module for adaptive fusion of node information at varying levels of detail, and employ a multi-granularity mask strategy with cross-granularity attention to capture diverse-scale anomaly hyperedge. Experiments on five real-world datasets show that our method outperforms existing approaches, demonstrating its effectiveness for anomaly detection in complex relational systems.

title: Data Exfiltration Detection System using Machine Learning
authors: B Harippriya, Simon Eng Soo Guan, Casey How Kiam Cheng, Yongqing Zhu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Service Operations and Logistics, and Informatics (SOLI)
abstract: Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) are struggling to manage cyberattacks, particularly against ransomware with data exfiltration capabilities. They often resort to paying the ransom in the hope of recovering their data, which indicates a reactive rather than proactive approach to cybersecurity. This study aims to address the problem through the development of a machine learning-based system for the detection of data exfiltration. By analysing real malware samples, the machine learning model together with feature selection methods and learning algorithms will accurately predict whether an instance of malware would exfiltrate data. Several state-of-the-art models were evaluated and out of these, the Random Forest model using an Embedded Method was the outright winner.

title: A Review of Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Based Approaches for Ergonomic Risk Assessment in Agriculture
authors: Pramod S. Mahale, Rajendra Rewatkar, K.T.V. Reddy, Pradnyawant M. Gote
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Work-related musculoskeletal disorders (WMSDs) remain one of the most significant occupational health risks in agriculture, where repetitive, physically demanding tasks such as planting, weeding, and harvesting are still widely performed manually. Traditional ergonomic risk assessment methods, including RULA, REBA, OWAS, and biomechanical modeling, have provided valuable insights but are often limited by subjectivity, static posture evaluation, and lack of scalability. Recent advances in artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning (ML), computer vision, and Internet of Things (IoT) technologies are reshaping this field by enabling automated, real-time, and predictive assessment of ergonomic risks. Hybrid and IoT enabled systems integrate multiple modalities and offer digital dashboards for remote monitoring, while predictive models based on deep learning and reinforcement learning allow early detection of WMSDs before symptom onset. Despite their promise, these approaches face challenges related to data availability, sensor calibration, worker acceptance, privacy concerns, and the lack of regulatory standards. This review synthesizes current methods and applications, identifies key challenges, and highlights future directions, including AR/VR based ergonomic training, digital twins, federated learning, and explainable AI, to advance safe and sustainable agricultural labor practices.

title: Clustering of High-Dimensional Uncertain Data Based on Probability Distributions
authors: Jing Gao, Jing Wan
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Information Science, Parallel and Distributed Systems (ISPDS)
abstract: In order to address the problem of clustering high dimensional uncertain data in discrete domains, and to improve clustering efficiency, this paper proposes a method for reducing high-dimensional uncertain data to low-dimensional uncertain data. The method aims to preserve the data characteristics in the high-dimensional space while ensuring that the topological structure of the low-dimensional space remains consistent with the high-dimensional space. Specifically, a Discrete Domain Highdimensional Uncertain Data Dimensionality Reduction Algorithm (DDHUDR) is proposed, as well as a Probability Distributionbased Uncertain Data Clustering Algorithm in Discrete Domain (PDUCDD). Theoretical research and experimental results demonstrate that the proposed algorithms achieve higher efficiency.

authors: Feridun Talat, Jim Basilakis, Quang Vinh Nguyen

title: The Coralscapes Dataset: Semantic Scene Understanding in Coral Reefs
authors: Jonathan Sauder, Viktor Domazetoski, Guilhem Banc-Prandi, Gabriela Perna, Anders Meibom, Devis Tuia
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Coral reefs are declining worldwide due to climate change and local stressors. To inform effective conservation or restoration, monitoring at the highest possible spatial and temporal resolution is necessary. Conventional coral reef surveying methods are limited in scalability due to their reliance on expert labor time, motivating the use of computer vision tools to automate the identification and abundance estimation of live corals from images. However, the design and evaluation of such tools has been impeded by the lack of large high quality datasets. We release the Coralscapes dataset, the first general-purpose dense semantic segmentation dataset for coral reefs, covering 2075 images, 39 benthic classes, and 174k segmentation masks annotated by experts. Coralscapes has a similar scope and the same structure as the widely used Cityscapes dataset for urban scene segmentation, allowing benchmarking of semantic segmentation models in a new challenging domain which requires expert knowledge to annotate. We benchmark a wide range of semantic segmentation models, and find that transfer learning from Coralscapes to existing smaller datasets consistently leads to state-of-the-art performance. Coralscapes will catalyze research on efficient, scalable, and standardized coral reef surveying methods based on computer vision, and holds the potential to streamline the development of underwater ecological robotics.

title: A Low-Cost GNSS/UWB Integrated Positioning Method Based on Non-Stationary Noise Suppression
authors: Kaiting Xie, Zhandong Wu, Zhaoguo Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: To address the limitations of insufficient non-stationary noise suppression in low-cost positioning systems in dynamic environments, this paper proposes a robust positioning method based on the fusion of the Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) and Ultra-Wideband (UWB). First, a spatiotem-poral joint alignment framework is established by employing a sliding-window time synchronization strategy (SW-TSS) together with an improved Procrustes analysis, achieving sub-millisecond synchronization accuracy and a 47.78% improvement in spatial alignment accuracy. Second, a non-stationary noise suppression algorithm is developed, enhancing trajectory smoothness metrics by more than 80%, and effectively mitigating data jumping issues. Finally, an improved adaptive extended Kalman filter (IA-EKF) is designed, attaining a positioning accuracy of 2.84m in mixed line-of-sight (LOS)/non-line-of-sight (NLOS) scenarios. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves strong robustness in regions where GNSS carrier-to-noise ratios as low as 10-20dBHz and under UWB/NLOS conditions.

title: TechRxiv: Share Your Preprint Research With the World!
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems

title: DrivingGaussian++: Towards Realistic Reconstruction and Editable Simulation for Surrounding Dynamic Driving Scenes
authors: Yajiao Xiong, Xiaoyu Zhou, Yongtao Wang, Deqing Sun, Ming-Hsuan Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: We present DrivingGaussian++, an efficient and effective framework for realistic reconstruction and controllable editing of surrounding dynamic autonomous driving scenes. DrivingGaussian++ models the static background with incremental 3D Gaussians and reconstructs moving objects with a composite dynamic Gaussian graph, ensuring accurate positions and occlusions. By integrating a LiDAR prior, it achieves detailed and consistent scene reconstruction, outperforming existing methods in dynamic scene reconstruction and photorealistic surround-view synthesis. DrivingGaussian++ supports training-free controllable editing for dynamic driving scenes, including texture modification, weather simulation, and object manipulation, leveraging multi-view images and depth priors. By integrating large language models (LLMs) and controllable editing, our method can automatically generate dynamic object motion trajectories and enhance their realism during the optimization process. DrivingGaussian++ demonstrates consistent and realistic editing results and generates dynamic multi-view driving scenarios, while significantly enhancing scene diversity.

title: A Survey of Water Pipeline Leak Detection using Acoustic Sensing
authors: Lixin Tu, Rakiba Rayhana, Ling Bai, Jiatong Ling, Zheng Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Water transmission and distribution infrastructures are essential assets within urban communities and crucial for maintaining a reliable and efficient water supply. The aging of water mains can lead to water loss, environmental contamination, and service disruptions. Acoustic-based leak detection methods have become a key approach because they can detect small leaks and are cost-effective. This paper reviews both traditional and state-of-the-art acoustic detection techniques, focusing on the integration of machine learning approaches. Machine learning-based approaches have shown promising accuracy and efficiency in recent years. The survey covers several key areas, including the characterization of common leak types, the deployment of acoustic sensors, the general framework for acoustic leak detection processes (highlighting the role of conventional machine learning and deep learning in processing acoustic signals for leak detection), and the research challenges in this field. This article presents an overview of recent advances in acoustic leak detection technologies, providing a guide to select suitable approaches based on data availability and research requirements. It also identifies prospective research areas for water pipeline inspection studies.

title: A Linearization of DFT Spectrum for Precision Power Measurement in Presence of Interharmonics
authors: Jian Liu, Wei Zhao, Jianting Zhao, Shisong Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: The presence of interharmonics in power systems can lead to asynchronous sampling, a phenomenon further aggravated by shifts in the fundamental frequency, which significantly degrades the accuracy of power measurements. Under such asynchronous conditions, interharmonics lose orthogonality with the fundamental and harmonic components, giving rise to additional power components. To address these challenges, this paper introduces a linearization algorithm based on DFT spectrum analysis for precise power measurement in systems containing interharmonics. The proposed approach constructs a system of linear equations from the DFT spectrum and solves it through efficient matrix operations, enabling accurate extraction of interharmonic components near the fundamental and harmonic frequencies (with a frequency interval ≥1 Hz). This allows for precise measurement of power across the fundamental, harmonic, interharmonic, and cross-power bands, as well as total power. Test results demonstrate that the proposed method accurately computes various power components under diverse conditions— including varying interharmonic/fundamental/harmonic intervals, fundamental frequency deviations, and noise. Compared to existing methods such as fast Fourier transform (FFT), Windowed interpolation FFT, and Matrix pencil-Singular value decomposition, the proposed technique reduces estimation error by several times to multiple folds and exhibits improved robustness, while maintaining a computational time of only 7 ms for processing 10-power-line-cycle (200 ms) data.

title: Golden Section Search-based GNSS Multipath Mitigation for Enhanced Urban Positioning
authors: Xin Qi, Bing Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Multipath has long been recognized as a major source of error in Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) urban positioning. Since no external assistance is required, baseband signal processing is a preferred solution for multipath mitigation. Although many multipath mitigation techniques at the baseband signal processing level have been proposed, the case where the power of the reflected signals is greater than that of the direct-path (DP) signal has received little attention. Moreover, the mitigation for short-delay multipath remains a challenge. To address this gap, this paper analyzes the characteristics of the composite autocorrelation function (ACF) with multiple reflected signals and considers the case where the power of the reflected signals is greater than the power of the DP signal. Based on this analysis, a code discrimination technique is proposed that utilizes multiple rounds of golden section (GS) search to directly estimate the code phase of the DP signal. Simulation results show that the proposed algorithm can effectively mitigate the multipath effect even in cases where the power of the reflected signals is greater than that of the DP signal. On Spirent short-delay multipath datasets, the proposed algorithm reduces 3D positioning root mean square error (RMSE) by over 22% compared to the narrow correlator, confirming its superior capability for mitigating short-delay multipath. For real urban datasets, the proposed algorithm outperforms both the narrow correlator and the classical multipath estimating delay-lock loop (MEDLL), achieving average reductions of 17.8% and 36.8% in 3D positioning RMSE respectively, which validates its robustness and practical utility in complex urban environments.

title: Robust Multimodal Sensor Fusion using Variational Autoencoder
authors: Parashara Kodati, Ranjith Chiplunkar, Biao Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Industrial systems rely on data from diverse modalities, such as visual, thermal, and other process sensors, for effective monitoring and control. While traditional sensor fusion is largely limited to single-modal multi-sensor fusion, multimodal fusion has proven to be a successful approach of combining information from multiple sensor modalities, providing a reliable understanding of the process and enhancing performance in subsequent downstream tasks. However, integrating such heterogeneous modalities is a challenge, especially under the conditions of noisy or missing data, which is quite prevalent in the real world. In this article, we propose a data-driven framework for multimodal sensor fusion based on a Variational Autoencoder (VAE) architecture. The proposed model jointly encodes image-based and numerical sensor inputs into a shared latent representation, enabling an improved classification accuracy using the extracted latent vectors from the trained model. To better capture cross-modal dependencies and achieve superior latent separability, we relax the common assumption of diagonal covariance in both the posterior and prior distributions. The joint posterior is modeled with a full covariance matrix parameterized via Cholesky decomposition, while the joint prior is structured using QR-based parameterization. We evaluate the applicability of this framework on the task of classification using a gas detection dataset combining thermal camera images and gas sensor data.

title: VNiScan-Fruit: A Non-Invasive Visible-Near Infrared Sensing System for Soluble Solids Content Estimation in Fruits
authors: Lu Wang, Qiqi Wang, Kaixin Chen, Haiyan Hu, Usman Saleh Toro, Yueling Che, Kaishun Wu, Qian Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Estimating the soluble solids content (SSC) in fruits is essential for meeting consumer expectations, ensuring the quality of processed products, and minimizing food waste. Current methods often rely on destructive sampling, expensive equipment, and complex procedures, which limit their practicality. This paper presents VNiScan-Fruit, a non-invasive, low-cost, and easy-to-use optical sensing system that utilizes visible-near-infrared (Vis-NIR) spectroscopy to estimate SSC by analyzing the interaction of light with fruit tissues to generate characteristic absorption spectra. Our approach diverges from traditional high-precision spectrometers, employing commercial LEDs and photodetectors (PD) to construct the optical sensing unit. We design and implement innovative ring-shaped rubber enclosure, interference elimination algorithms, and reconstruction strategy to extract low-dimensional reflectance spectral features from various fruits. These features are then processed using a specially designed nonlinear regression model, incorporating sucrose values obtained from a commercial refractometer to estimate SSC accurately. We tested VNiScan-Fruit on a range of fruits with diverse peel characteristics, demonstrating its ability to penetrate exocarps and effectively analyze fruits with rough surfaces and delicate tissues. The system achieved normalized mean absolute errors (NMAE) of 8%, imperceptible to consumers in sweetness difference, and remained stable under varying lighting and temperature conditions. Our findings highlight the potential of VNiScan-Fruit as a practical tool for non-destructive fruit quality assessment.

title: A High- Q Coupled Dual-Beam Piezoelectric MEMS Resonator for Frequency Control Applications
authors: Bo Wu, Quan Yuan, Jiaming Wang, Haopeng Xu, Hangbing Xiao, Duli Yu
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: To mitigate the substantial degradation of the quality factor (Q factor) in traditional piezoelectric microelectromechanical systems (MEMS) resonators, primarily due to anchor loss and mode competition, this study introduces a coupled dual-beam structure based on aluminum nitride (AlN) for piezoelectric resonators. By jointly optimizing the geometric configuration and material stacking of the coupled dual-beam, and exploiting the mechanical coupling effect in the bending mode, effective suppression of anchor loss and energy localization was achieved. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed resonator attains a Q factor of 1479.17 at a resonant frequency of 301.74 kHz. The device has a temperature coefficient of frequency (TCF) of -27.67 ppm/°C, exhibiting good frequency-temperature linearity. In terms of the manufacturing process, the critical dimensional error of the structure is less than 1 μm, while maintaining compatibility with CMOS processes, ensuring high-precision fabrication and integration. This study validates the potential of the coupled dual-beam structure in improving the Q factor of MEMS resonators, laying a solid foundation for advancing device miniaturization and multifunctional integration.

title: DFPR-BEV: Cross-modal Knowledge Distillation of Decouple Features and Projection Regions for Multi-Camera 3D Object Detection
authors: Jiaxin Liu, Wei Liu, Pengyu Wang, Yongming Li, Jun Hu, Shuai Cheng, Wenxing Yang
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Multi-camera 3D object detection receives extensive attention in autonomous driving because of its advantage in efficiency and cost-effectiveness. However, a performance gap still exists between camera-based detector and LiDAR-based detector. Because camera-based detector cannot extract precise depth and geometric information from the input image data, resulting in obvious projection deviation when transforming features from the perspective view to the bird’s eye view (BEV). We propose a cross-modal knowledge distillation framework (DFPR-BEV) to address this problem, making the camera-based student detector to improve its feature representation by imitating the trained LiDAR-based teacher detector during training. To prevent semantic information from being harmed by directly aligning features during distillation by modal variation, we improve the student model to decouple point clouds feature distillation and image projection position correction. A heatmap-based projection correction method is proposed to correct image feature projection deviation and eliminate conflicting features. A Gaussian mixture region-based method is introduced for comprehensive geometry and depth transmission of point clouds features. Finally, the detection performance is improved by effectively fusing the two distilled features. Extensive experiments on nuScenes benchmark illustrate that DFPR-BEV can significantly improve the performance of the student model, outperforming current distillation methods and other camera-based detectors with equal or lower computational budget. Notably, on the nuScenes val set after distillation, BEVDet’s NDS and mAP are improved by 4.9% and 4.2%, respectively, BEVDet4D’s inference speed is 5× faster than BEVFormer-S with the same mAP and higher NDS.

title: Multi-Level Online Group Pruning for Deep Semantic Segmentation: Balancing Efficiency and Class Fairness
authors: Yan Fei, Yaocheng Zhong, Jiayu Wang, Huang Shita, Xiaodong Wang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This study targets Encoder-Decoder architectures acceleration for semantic segmentation. Although deep neural networks have shown superior performance in image segmentation tasks, their substantial model size and high computational complexity limit their application in real-time processing and resource-constrained devices. To tackle this issue, pruning techniques have been developed to compact Encoder-Decoder networks. However, most current pruning methods are designed for classification tasks, rendering them challenging to achieve satisfactory results on fine-grained demands of segmentation tasks. Additionally, these methods only consider the filters’ global average contribution to each class, which can compromise model sensitivity to minority classes. To solve this issue, we propose a multi-level online group pruning method. Our method identifies and preserves the most discriminative filters for critical minority classes, ensuring that the pruned network can still accurately segment these underrepresented categories. Consequently, it fosters a more balanced and fair segmentation performance across all classes.We evaluated our method on two popular image segmentation datasets, i.e., Camvid and Cityscapes. After pruning 93% of parameters in trendy Encoder-Decoder networks, our method achieves noteworthy IoU gains for the minority classes, e.g., 6.5% for Pole on Camvid and 4.7% for Fence on Cityscapes, over state-of-the-art online pruning methods, while still maintaining comparable or superior performance for majority classes.

title: ELAS-FG: Efficient Lightweight Authentication Scheme in Factor Graph-based Communication for VANET_supp1-3665634.pdf
authors: Jayaprakash Kar
publish: N/A
abstract: Vehicular Ad-Hoc Networks (VANETs) enable secure, real-time communication among vehicles and roadside infrastructure, improving traffic safety and system efficiency. However, VANETs face significant security and performance challenges due to their dynamic topology, real-time constraints, and resource limitations. This paper presents a novel efficient lightweight authentication scheme in certificateless(ELAS-FG) setting for VANETs, utilizing Factor Graph-based (FG) communication models to optimize efficiency and scalability. The proposed scheme integrates conditional probability distributions and message-passing algorithms from factor graph theory with cryptographic primitives to reduce computational and communication overhead. Performance analysis demonstrates that our method achieves robust security guarantees, including mutual authentication, integrity, and resistance against replay and impersonation attacks, while maintaining low latency and minimal resource consumption. Comparative analysis with existing schemes highlights the superior efficiency and practicality of our approach for real-world VANET environments.

title: Calculation and Analysis of Motor Unbalanced Electromagnetic Force with or without Eccentricity Considering Thermal Factors Related to Multi-field Coupling Effect for In-wheel Electric Vehicle Applications
authors: Di Tan, Feihong Wang, Pengyu Fu, Wenzhe Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: Distributed electric vehicles driven by in-wheel motors(IWMs) integrate the drive motors highly within the wheels. Road roughness excitation, structural deformation, etc. can all cause eccentricity of the motor. This will cause the generation of unbalanced electromagnetic force(UEF), which not only intensifies the IWM vibration but also further affects the vehicle's dynamics. Many efforts have been devoted to investigate the influence of eccentricity on UEF. However, current research mostly focuses on this issue from the perspective of electromagnetic fields, ignoring the thermal factors(TFs, including thermal deformation and the impact of temperature on material properties) as well as the multi-physics field coupling effects existing in the IWM, which are crucial for eccentricity and UEF. This can lead to significant deviations between the calculated results and the actual situation. Aiming at above issues, a 15kW IWM is taken as the research object. A TD analysis model of the IWM is first established considering the coupling effects of electromagnetic, temperature, flow, and structural field. The validity of the developed model is verified through experiments, and the TD of the IWM is calculated by using the established model. Based on this, by reconfiguring the deformed motor structure, the quantitative calculation and analysis are carried out on the UEF under eccentricity and non-eccentricity conditions, considering the influence of one type of TF alone and the combined influence of two types of TFs. The influence laws of the TFs on the UEF, as well as the influence laws of the IWM eccentricity on the UEF under the effect of the TFs, are obtained. Furthermore, the influence levels of two types of TFs on the UEF of IWMs in both eccentric and non-eccentric states are studied and evaluated.

title: Cover
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)

title: A Machine Learning-Based Framework for Bias Correction of Doppler Weather Radar Observations
authors: Vaibhav Tyagi, Saurabh Das
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Radar constant miscalibration is one of the major sources of uncertainty in the radar-derived rainfall products. The traditional bias correction techniques often struggle to account for the non-stationary and nonlinear nature of bias. This study proposes a novel machine learning-based framework using the XGBoost algorithm to model reflectivity bias as a function of ground radar (GR) and space radar (SR) reflectivity differences, along with radar geometrical parameters (range, azimuth, and elevation). Furthermore, a strategy for near real-time bias correction is proposed based on an ensemble approach that combines an offline-pretrained model with an adaptive online learning component, incrementally updating the output as new data becomes available. This allows the model to adapt to evolving bias patterns over time. The results indicate that the proposed technique consistently outperforms the traditional iterative method. The results point towards its potential in reducing bias in near real-time for improved quantitative precipitation estimation (QPE) and other applications.

title: Accelerating the Max-Cut problems via distributed Ising machine solvers
authors: Xing-yu Wu, Yi-Xin Zhang, Chen-rui Fan, Chuan Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Quantum Engineering
abstract: The Ising machine, as a quantum-inspired computing system, can be used to efficiently solve combinatorial optimization problems. Ongoing studies have positioned it to potentially surpass the performance limitations of traditional computers. However, such Ising machines also suffer from scalability as the solution quality becomes sub-optimal when the problem size increases. In this work, we propose an Ising-based framework that uses a distributed Ising algorithm to find the near-optimal solution for large-scale Max-Cut problems through parallel processing via graph segmentation and reassembly. We selected the coherent Ising machine as the hardware solver and successfully found the solution for a 2000-node instance utilizing 20 computational bits on the Gset standard dataset. Additionally, we investigated the key factors influencing the system's performance and discovered that setting the number of reorganizations to one results in higher accuracy. Building on this configuration, we utilized a 100-bit CIM hardware platform (CPQC-1) to solve instances with varying numbers of nodes. The experimental results agree well with the theoretical simulations, confirming the practical feasibility of our method. Moreover, compared to existing Ising-based solvers, our algorithm significantly reduces computational resource requirements while achieving superior performance on large-scale combinatorial optimization problems, highlighting its effectiveness on quantum-inspired hardware.

title: A 10-GHz GaN MMIC Self-Synchronous Rectifier With a GaAs MMIC Tunable Gate Termination
authors: Alexandra Montgomery, Ariana Kazemi, Laila Marzall, Zoya Popović
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: A 10-GHz self-synchronous MMIC power rectifier (PR) is designed and fabricated in the WIN Semiconductors NP12 120-nm GaN-on-SiC process. When the gate is terminated with a load-pull tuner, the rectifier demonstrates a peak RF-DC conversion efficiency of 61% with 33-dBm input RF power at 10.25 GHz. To implement the tuner function on-chip, an X-band MMIC tunable load is designed in the WIN Semiconductors PQG3-0C 150-nm GaAs process and hybridly integrated in the gate circuit of the rectifier. A maximum conversion efficiency of 59% with 32-dBm input RF power at 10.5 GHz is measured when the rectifier MMIC is connected to the tunable load MMIC.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Journal on Miniaturization for Air and Space Systems

title: Is It Certainly a Deepfake? Reliability Analysis in Detection & Generation Ecosystem
authors: Neslihan Kose, Anthony Rhodes, Umur Aybars Çiftçi, İlke Demir
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: As generative models are advancing in quality and quantity for creating synthetic content, deepfakes begin to cause online mistrust. Deepfake detectors are proposed to counter this effect, however, misuse of detectors claiming fake content as real or vice versa further fuels this misinformation problem. We present the first comprehensive uncertainty analysis of deepfake detectors, systematically investigating how generative artifacts influence prediction confidence. As reflected in detectors' responses, deepfake generators also contribute to this uncertainty as their generative residues vary, so we cross the uncertainty analysis of deepfake detectors and generators. Based on our observations, the uncertainty manifold holds enough consistent information to leverage uncertainty for deepfake source detection. Our approach leverages Bayesian Neural Networks and Monte Carlo dropout to quantify both aleatoric and epistemic uncertainties across diverse detector architectures. We evaluate uncertainty on two datasets with nine generators, with four blind and two biological detectors, compare different uncertainty methods, explore region- and pixel-based uncertainty, and conduct ablation studies. We conduct and analyze binary real/fake, multi-class real/fake, source detection, and leave-one-out experiments between the generator/detector combinations to share their generalization capability, model calibration, uncertainty, and robustness against adversarial attacks. We further introduce uncertainty maps that localize prediction confidence at the pixel level, revealing distinct patterns correlated with generator-specific artifacts. Our analysis provides critical insights for deploying reliable deepfake detection systems and establishes uncertainty quantification as a fundamental requirement for trustworthy synthetic media detection.

title: K-StyleLoRA: Information-Guided Image Generation via Selective Feature Learning
authors: Soyoung Lee, Hyoungseo Cho, Myungjoo Kang, YoungJoon Yoo
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Despite remarkable advances in image generation, existing diffusion models struggle to capture diverse cultural aesthetics. While Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) enables efficient fine-tuning, conventional approaches lack semantic awareness. They apply uniform adaptations across all features, leading to suboptimal cultural representation. To address these limitations, we introduce K-StyleLoRA, a novel framework that leverages CLIP's cross-modal capabilities for cultural image generation. Our approach introduces two key contributions. First, CLIP-Guided Information Gating dynamically modulates LoRA adaptations based on cultural relevance scores, selectively enhancing relevant features while suppressing irrelevant ones. Second, Cultural Semantic Loss provides additional semantic guidance by optimizing CLIP-based similarity to cultural concepts. Extensive experiments on Korean traditional culture show superior cultural fidelity while maintaining generation quality and diversity. In particular, K-StyleLoRA demonstrates exceptional cultural transfer capability on generic prompts that require implicit cultural understanding. Our method achieves a Cultural Similarity Score of 0.274, representing a 9.6% improvement over the vanilla SDXL baseline (0.250). Our framework establishes semantic-aware adaptation as a powerful paradigm for cultural representation. This scalable approach can be extended to diverse cultural contexts and generation tasks beyond Korean aesthetics.

title: Probing the Representational Power of Sparse Autoencoders in Vision Models
authors: Matthew L. Olson, Musashi Hinck, Neale Ratzlaff, Changbai Li, Phillip Howard, Vasudev Lal, Shao-Yen Tseng
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) have emerged as a popular tool for interpreting the hidden states of large language models (LLMs). By learning to reconstruct activations from a sparse bottleneck layer, SAEs discover interpretable features from the high-dimensional internal representations of LLMs. Despite their popularity with language models, SAEs remain understudied in the visual domain. In this work, we provide an extensive evaluation the representational power of SAEs for vision models using a broad range of image-based tasks. Our experimental results demon-strate that SAE features are semantically meaningful, im-prove out-of-distribution generalization, and enable con-trollable generation across three vision model architectures: vision embedding models, multi-modal LMMs and diffusion models. In vision embedding models, we find that learned SAE features can be used for OOD detection and provide evidence that they recover the ontological structure of the underlying model. For diffusion models, we demon-strate that SAEs enable semantic steering through text en-coder manipulation and develop an automated pipeline for discovering human-interpretable attributes. Finally, we conduct exploratory experiments on multi-modal LLMs, finding evidence that SAE features reveal shared represen-tations across vision and language modalities. Our study provides a foundation for SAE evaluation in vision models, highlighting their strong potential improving interpretabil-ity, generalization, and steerability in the visual domain.

title: Model as a Game: On Numerical and Spatial Consistency for Generative Games
authors: Jingye Chen, Yuzhong Zhao, Yupan Huang, Lei Cui, Li Dong, Tengchao Lv, Qifeng Chen, Furu Wei
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Recent advances in generative models have significantly impacted game generation. However, despite producing high-quality graphics and adequately receiving player input, existing models often fail to maintain fundamental game properties such as numerical and spatial consistency. Numerical consistency ensures gameplay mechanics correctly reflect score changes and other quantitative elements, while spatial consistency prevents jarring scene transitions, providing seamless player experiences. In this paper, we revisit the paradigm of generative games to explore what truly constitutes a Model as a Game (MaaG) with a well-developed mechanism. We begin with an empirical study on “Traveler”, a 2D game created by an LLM featuring minimalist rules yet challenging generative models in maintaining consistency. Based on the DiT architecture, we design two specialized modules: (1) a numerical module that integrates a LogicNet to determine event triggers, with calculations processed externally as conditions for image generation; and (2) a spatial module that maintains a map of explored areas, retrieving location-specific information during generation and linking new observations to ensure continuity. Experiments across three games demonstrate that our integrated modules significantly enhance performance on consistency metrics compared to baselines, while incurring minimal time overhead during inference. We will release our code and models to facilitate future research in this area, with code and samples in the supplementary material.

title: Prompt-Guided Spatial Understanding with RGB-D Transformers for Fine-Grained Object Relation Reasoning
authors: Tanner W. Muturi, Blessing A. Kyem, Joshua K. Asamoah, Neema J. Owor, Richard Dyzinela, Andrews Danyo, Yaw Adu-Gyamfi, Armstrong Aboah
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Spatial reasoning in large-scale 3D environments such as warehouses remains a significant challenge for vision-language systems due to scene clutter, occlusions, and the need for precise spatial understanding. Existing models often struggle with generalization in such settings, as they rely heavily on local appearance and lack explicit spatial grounding. In this work, we introduce a dedicated spatial reasoning framework for the Physical AI Spatial Intelligence Warehouse dataset introduced in the Track 3 2025 AI City Challenge. Our approach enhances spatial comprehension by embedding mask dimensions in the form of bounding box coordinates directly into the input prompts, enabling the model to reason over object geometry and layout. We fine-tune the framework across four question categories namely: Distance Estimation, Object Counting, Multi-choice Grounding, and Spatial Relation Inference using task-specific supervision. To further improve consistency with the evaluation system, normalized answers are appended to the GPT response within the training set. Our comprehensive pipeline achieves a final score of 73.0606, placing 4th overall on the public leaderboard. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of structured prompt enrichment and targeted optimization in advancing spatial reasoning for real-world industrial environments.

title: AI vs. Human Moderators: A Comparative Evaluation of Multimodal LLMs in Content Moderation for Brand Safety
authors: Adi Levi, Or Levi, Sardhendu Mishra, Jonathan Morra
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: As the volume of video content online grows exponentially, the demand for moderation of unsafe videos has sur-passed human capabilities, posing both operational and mental health challenges. While recent studies demon-strated the merits of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) in various video understanding tasks, their application to multimodal content moderation, a domain that requires nuanced understanding of both visual and textual cues, remains relatively underexplored. In this work, we benchmark the capabilities of MLLMs in brand safety classification, a critical subset of content moderation for safe-guarding advertising integrity. To this end, we introduce a novel, multimodal and multilingual dataset, meticulously labeled by professional reviewers in a multitude of risk categories. Through a detailed comparative analysis, we demonstrate the effectiveness of MLLMs such as Gemini, GPT, and Llama in multimodal brand safety, and evaluate their accuracy and cost efficiency compared to professional human reviewers. Furthermore, we present an in-depth discussion shedding light on limitations of MLLMs and failure cases. We are releasing our dataset alongside this paper to facilitate future research on effective and responsible brand safety and content moderation.

title: Embedding Font Impression Word Tags Based on Co-occurrence
authors: Yugo Kubota, Seiichi Uchida
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Different font styles (i.e., font shapes) convey distinct im-pressions, indicating a close relationship between font shapes and word tags describing those impressions. This paper proposes a novel embedding method for impression tags that leverages these shape-impression relationships. For instance, our method assigns similar vectors to impres-sion tags that frequently co-occur in order to represent im-pressions of fonts, whereas standard word embedding meth-ods (e.g., BERT and CLIP) yield very different vectors. This property is particularly useful for impression-based font generation and font retrieval. Technically, we construct a graph whose nodes represent impression tags and whose edges encode co-occurrence relationships. Then, we apply spectral embedding to obtain the impression vectors for each tag. We compare our method with BERT and CLIP in qualitative and quantitative evaluations, demonstrating that our approach performs better in impression-guided font generation.

title: Smart ANN Based UPFC Controller for Improving Transient Stability of Power System
authors: Pravin Khandare, Prashant Jagtap
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Electricity remains a cornerstone of industrial progress, with rising demand from both residential and industrial sectors necessitating large-scale power generation and transmission. However, efficiently transmitting high volumes of electricity poses technical and economic challenges. Conventional protective devices require scaling to handle increased loads, resulting in higher operational costs. Adaptive AC Transmission Technologies have emerged as a promising explanation to enhance power system efficiency and reliability This research work examines the concert of the Comprehensive Power Flow Control Device, a vital FACTS device, under fault conditions. A Line-to-earthing error is introduced to evaluate the structure's dynamic response MATLAB/SIMULINK simulations are used to compare the effectiveness of dual control strategies: a predictable PI Control System and a Fuzzy Logic Enhanced PI Controller. The results specify that the fuzzy logic-enhanced Comprehensive Power Flow Control Device offers superior performance, with faster response and better voltage stability. This underscores the value of intelligent control in modern power systems.

title: Path Optimization of Unmanned Vehicles Based on Penalty A* Algorithm in Plateau Terrain
authors: Xiaofeng Ji, Zhongyuan Zhang, Zhenyu Yang, Yi Liu
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: Path planning for unmanned vehicles is crucial for enhancing transportation efficiency, but research on real-time path planning in high-altitude terrain is still insufficient. This paper addresses this issue and proposes an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{A}^{*}$</tex> algorithm path planning model based on penalty mechanism, in combination with the real-time path status. First, analyze the geospatial data and extract the features of elevation and slope; Secondly, a multiobjective evaluation index including energy consumption, slope and path length is constructed, and the selection of large slope grids is suppressed through the penalty function. Finally, the greedy algorithm is adopted for local optimization, and the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{A}^{*}$</tex> algorithm and dynamic penalty mechanism are integrated to achieve adaptive path search and obstacle avoidance, so as to improve the search efficiency. Simulation experiments in the complex terrain of the plateau show that this method can adaptively generate the optimal path and achieve real-time path judgment in the terrain change area. Compared with the method without dynamic penalty mechanism, the transportation safety index has increased by 7.84 %, verifying the effectiveness of the model.

title: Fake News Detection Using Improved Machine Learning Approaches
authors: Deepak Parashar, Rahul Joshi, Kshem Dikshit, Deepak Kumar, Gouranga Mandal, Nilesh Bahadure
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: The content-based text features machine learning method of detecting fake news is highly useful. It consists of effective text preprocessing. Next, the textual data are converted to numeric information employing Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF) vectorization. The classic automated approaches, such as the Random Forest (RF) and the Gradient enhancer based, were employed and demonstrated a great level of accuracy in identifying fake and real news. The algorithm is computationally efficient and scalable. The study manly focuses on the significance of descriptors algorithms and classic models in obtaining reliable results without the high computational overhead of advanced high dimension data-based approaches. Nevertheless, additional approaches, including BERT and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and social graph-based features can be incorporated into future research to increase its accuracy and generalizability and obtain relational and contextual cues.

title: Performance Analysis of Energy-Saving and Emission-Reduction in Diesel-Electric Hybrid Marine Propulsion Systems
authors: Guojie Cao
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: This study aims to design a hybrid power propulsion system for ships based on diesel engines and lithium batteries, and to evaluate its performance in terms of fuel consumption, carbon emissions, and power demand. The research method involves establishing a hybrid power system model and comparing it with traditional diesel engine-driven ship power systems under two typical operating conditions. The analysis focuses on energy distribution, fuel consumption levels, and carbon emissions under different operating conditions, while introducing the EEOI (Energy Efficiency Operational Indicator) to evaluate the operational energy efficiency and carbon emission intensity of ships under various conditions. The research results indicate that hybrid-powered ships outperform traditional diesel engine-driven ships in both key indicators of energy saving and emission reduction, demonstrating significant advantages in energy conservation and emission reduction.

title: Hierarchical Multi-Modal Fusion for Roadside VRU Detection: Method Complementarity Under Sparse Label Constraints
authors: Chuheng Wei, Ziyan Zhang, Haishan Liu, Guoyuan Wu, Matthew J. Barth
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Accurate detection of Vulnerable Road Users (VRUs) by roadside sensors poses significant challenges due to sparse labeled data and multimodal sensor fusion complexity. This paper proposes a hierarchical multimodal fusion framework that integrates traditional 3D object detection methods with deep learning-based approaches. Our method combines the robustness of conventional detection pipelines with neural network adaptability through a novel complementarity strategy. The framework employs DAB-DETR for 2D detection, traditional 3D clustering algorithms, and PointPillars architecture, unified through confidence-based fusion. Experimental evaluation on the Intersection Safety Challenge (ISC) dataset demonstrates the effectiveness of our approach with overall mAP@0.5 of 49.12% compared to 36% for conventional methods and 15.99% for deep learning approaches. Notable improvements include vehicle detection (46.09% vs. 39.12%) and VRU detection, especially for children (30.71% vs. 3.61%). Our hierarchical fusion strategy successfully leverages complementary strengths of traditional classification accuracy and deep learning localization precision. The solution based on this detection method won an award in the ISC Stage 1B competition, validating its effectiveness for data-constrained road-side VRU detection scenarios.

title: A Retrieval-Augmented Generation Method Based on Intent Analysis for Explainable Speech Emotion Recognition
authors: Pilita A. Amahan
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Data Science and Information Technology (DSIT)
abstract: This study introduced a method that recognizes emotions from speech using wavelet compression to clean audio signals and preserve key emotional features. The study improves the accuracy and reduces noise in fusing pitch, MFCC, and wavelet data. Results validated by BERT-based model classify emotions such as anger, sadness, happiness, fear, and neutral achieving strong performance of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 4 \%}$</tex> accuracy, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 1 \%}$</tex> happy, 89% for angry, 88% for sad, and 90%, and 90% for neutral. Compression results show 80% to 87%, and the average processing time per sample is between 1.1 and 1.5 seconds, supporting real-time use. Future study, human experts may be included in the loop of the framework to review and validate outputs, helping align predictions with human perception to understand, and may be useful for education, research, and diagnostic applications. Overall, the study's framework offers an accurate and transparent way to detect emotions in speech.

title: Multiple-Layer Waveguide Terahertz Reconfigurable Integration Platform
authors: Zhongxia Simon He, Chenyi Liu, Biao Ding, Peng Wu, Weihua Yu, Ziqiao Zhou
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: The fabrication of terahertz (THz) waveguides demands exceptionally high tolerance, posing significant challenges to scalable manufacturing. To address this, various electromagnetic bandgap (EBG) structures have been explored for their potential to mitigate fabrication sensitivity. Among these, the integration of higher symmetry configurations emerges as a compelling strategy, offering both enhanced manufacturability and improved electromagnetic performance. In this study, we introduce the Multiple-Layer Waveguide (MLW) architecture, which leverages symmetry-induced robustness to reduce processing costs by up to two orders of magnitude without compromising functional integrity. Building on the theoretical framework of Higher Symmetry Electromagnetic Bandgap (HSEBG), we present a novel low-loss THz interconnection technology that is both reconfigurable and fabrication-friendly. This advancement culminates in the proposal of the THz Reconfigurable Integration Platform (TRIP), a versatile solution poised to accelerate the deployment of next-generation THz systems.

title: Design of Networked Digital Twins Production Line Based on Industrial Robot
authors: Yuanjiang Liao, Shouyun Cai, Michael Basin, Umair Javaid, Xiaoxiao Mi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Industrial production lines are composed of different types of control systems. There are differences in data formats between the systems. How to combine data with digital twin models to achieve real-time updates and feedback is a problem that needs to be solved. A networked digital twins production line based on industrial robot has been designed. A new automatic winding changing structure has been used, and the constant tension and constant speed control systems for the winding flying shear equipment has been designed. The system uses a redundant network structure and industrial wireless network, which can real-time monitoring and debugging of various faults on the production line by operators. Finally, automatic labeling and outbound operations for packaged products were achieved, significantly improving production efficiency and product quality.

title: BDCHS: A Blockchain-Enhanced Framework for Cloud Security—Evaluation Current Strategies and Future Innovations
authors: Ritu Dagar, Shilpa Mahajan, Preeti Mahajan
publish: 2025 14th International Conference on System Modeling &amp; Advancement in Research Trends (SMART)
abstract: This paper examines why there is an increasing threat of data breaches and continued security vulnerabilities in cloud systems. Existing cryptography solutions do not have scalability, efficiency, and quantum robustness in IoT-edge environments. The paper proposes to apply a cryptographic curve hash signature (BDCHS) that is supported by a Blockchain mechanism to enhance the security of employee personal data. This is method whereby it relies on a systematic approach which involves the registration, verification, submission of contracts, storage and retrieval of data and keeping a watch over transactions. It does this with smart contracts and a blockchain that only certain people can access. Performance evaluation of the experimental results shows that the proposed BDCHS model, as compared to the traditional cryptographic approaches (i.e., ECC, RSA and DH): Reduced the encryption time in the range of 7% 26%, improved the decryption time up to 73%, increased the throughput up to 22%, and diminished the transaction delay by over 99% to related conventional methods. These results demonstrate the soundness, scalability, and privacy protection of BDCHS in secure employee service in modern enterprise.

title: AI-Driven Claims Adjudication: Optimizing Healthcare Systems with Machine Learning and Deep Neural Networks
authors: Damodhara Reddy Palavali, Suneetha Pothireddy, Dinesh Kumar Elumalai, Madhusudan Nagaraja
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: Healthcare claims adjudication is an important administrative process that affects the accuracy and efficiency of reimbursements in healthcare systems and prevents fraud. Growing volumes of claims, challenging billing codes, undocumented clinical records, and sophisticated fraud schemes present weaknesses in rule-based and manual adjudication. This study introduces an AI-based claims adjudication system that combines transformer-based natural language processing (NLP), ensemble machine learning, and a two-step fraud detection pipeline. The system examines the structured attributes of claims, utilization, profile of provider behavior, and contextual embeddings of clinical text to automatically perform adjudication with great accuracy and transparency. Claim classification is performed using a weighted combination of XGBoost, Random Forest, and neural network classifiers, and fraud detection is performed using a hybrid model of unsupervised anomaly detection and supervised learning. On large-scale healthcare data, experimental analysis reveals 94.7% classification accuracy, 67 percent processing time reduction, and 37 percent better fraud detection results than rule-based systems. The interpretability, regulatory compliance, computational complexity, and deployment considerations discussed in this study make the framework a scalable platform for next-generation healthcare claims processing.

title: Study on the Influence of Lamination Parameters of Civil Aircraft Carbon Fiber Composites on Electromagnetic Shielding Characteristics
authors: Zhebo Wang, Yonggen Wang
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: The lamination parameters of carbon fiber composites (CFC), including the number of plies, ply orientation, and stacking sequence, significantly influence their electromagnetic shielding characteristics. By analyzing the reflection and transmission behaviors under different polarization directions, their electromagnetic response patterns are revealed. Investigating the impact of the number of plies on the shielding effectiveness clarifies the enhancement of shielding performance with increasing ply count. Furthermore, simulation analyses of ply orientation and stacking sequence indicate that the superposition of multiple composite layers with different orientations can mitigate anisotropy, resulting in macroscopic properties approximating those of an isotropic homogeneous medium. This provides a basis for the equivalent electromagnetic modeling of CFC.

title: Prompt4Trust: A Reinforcement Learning Prompt Augmentation Framework for Clinically-Aligned Confidence Calibration in Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Anita Kriz, Elizabeth Laura Janes, Xing Shen, Tal Arbel
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) hold considerable promise for applications in healthcare. However, their deployment in safety-critical settings is hindered by two key limitations: (i) sensitivity to prompt design, and (ii) a tendency to generate incorrect responses with high confidence. As clinicians may rely on a model's stated confidence to gauge the reliability of its predictions, it is especially important that when a model expresses high confidence, it is also highly accurate. We introduce Prompt4Trust, the first reinforcement learning (RL) framework for prompt augmentation targeting confidence calibration in MLLMs. A lightweight LLM is trained to produce context-aware auxiliary prompts that guide a downstream task MLLM to generate responses in which the expressed confidence more accurately reflects predictive accuracy. Unlike conventional calibration techniques, Prompt4Trust specifically prioritizes aspects of calibration most critical for safe and trustworthy clinical decisionmaking. Beyond improvements driven by this clinically motivated calibration objective, our proposed method also improves task accuracy, achieving state-of-the-art medical visual question answering (VQA) performance on the PMC-VQA benchmark, which is composed of multiple-choice questions spanning diverse medical imaging modalities. Moreover, our framework trained with a small downstream task MLLM showed promising zero-shot generalization to larger MLLMs in our experiments, suggesting the potential for scalable calibration without the associated computational costs. This work demonstrates the potential of automated yet human-aligned prompt engineering for improving the the trustworthiness of MLLMs in safety critical settings. Our codebase can be found at https://github.com/xingbpshen/prompt4trust.

title: Refractive Scene Camera Pose Estimation via Inverting Underwater Neural Radiance Fields
authors: Zhixin Zhang, Xiaoqiang Zhang, Xiaoxia Li, Zhiyuan Chang, Li He
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: The accuracy of camera pose estimation is crucial for various robotics applications. While the technique of pose estimation in single refractive medium (SRM), for example, the air, is relatively mature, pose estimation in scenes with multiple refractive media (MRM) still suffers from problems of low accuracy due the ray refraction. Since in the MRM scene, the ray path from a 3D scene point to its corresponding 2D image pixel is typically not a straight line as studied in most previous NeRF work. As a result, the constraints such as epipolar geometry are invalidated. In this paper, a novel camera pose estimation method for MRM scenes is proposed based on the inverse optimization of the camera pose on an underwater Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF). Firstly, to accurately model the refracted rays in the MRM scene, a cascade Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) module is designed to subsequently estimate the distance from the ray origin to the refractive surface and the surface normal, which could avoid singular value issues caused by inaccurate camera poses. The Snell's law is then applied to geometrically solve the refracted ray direction. Secondly, an underwater NeRF is trained based on a collection of refracted rays of images from very sparse cameras with known poses. The NeRF photometric loss is used to optimize the above cascade MLP, together with the NeRF MLP. Finally, based on the pre-trained network parameters, the unknown or inaccurate poses are iteratively optimized on the SE(3) manifold. Extensive experimental results on both public and self-build datasets demonstrate that our method achieves superior pose estimation accuracy in refractive scenes. Moreover, the proposed method can also be useful for selfsupervised NeRF training in underwater scenes with inputs of inaccurate camera poses.

title: A Block Sparsity-Based Method for Target Detection by Synthetic Aperture Interferometric Radiometer
authors: Jiale Min, Dong Zhu, Fei Hu
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: Synthetic aperture interferometric radiometer (SAIR) has emerged as a powerful tool for target detection, owing to its capability for instantaneous observation in lowvisibility environments. Existing detection methods employed in SAIR are image feature-based approaches, which demonstrate significant performance degradation under low signal-to-clutter ratio (SCR) conditions. In this article, we propose a novel target detection method that simultaneously exploits spatial sparsity and structural clustering characteristics in SAIR. The framework reformulates detection problem as a block-sparsity optimization model by partitioning the observation scene into spatial blocks and minimizing the number of active blocks. Simulated and real-measured experiments validate the effectiveness of proposed target detection method in SAIR. Specifically, in low-SCR scenario, proposed method achieves reliable detection performance.

title: Role of Artificial Intelligence in Newborn Screening: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Anurag Chakraborty, Archana Dhok, Ashish Anjankar
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is the capacity of computer systems to perform tasks typically associated with human cognition, including learning, reasoning, problem-solving, perception, and decision-making. The tools and software that allow computers to sense their surroundings and employ intelligence and learning to act in ways that maximise the likelihood that they will accomplish predetermined goals are developed and studied in this field of computer science study. Newborn screening (NBS) is a public health initiative that involves evaluating newborns quickly after birth for diseases that can be treated but are not yet clinically noticeable. The objective is to detect newborns who are susceptible to these disorders early enough to validate the diagnosis and offer treatment that will change the disease's clinical trajectory and stop or lessen its clinical symptoms. Artificial intelligence (AI) has become a potent tool to improve the effectiveness, precision, and reach of NBS programs due to the growing complexity and volume of data produced by sophisticated biochemical, genetic, and metabolomic testing. This paper highlights how machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) models might transform the early diagnosis and management of neonatal illnesses by examining the uses, advantages, drawbacks, and future potential of AI in newborn screening.

title: DeepColor: A CNN-Based Skin Tone Detection and Personalized Color Recommendation System
authors: Lakshmi Sharma, Devidas Thosar, Barkha Kumari, Sakshi Nikamline, Laxmi Aher, Saif Sayyad
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: The paper presents DeepColor, a Deep Learning based automated skin tone classification and personalized color recommendation system in Cosmetics and Fashion Domain. By using a Custom Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architecture, the system classifies skintone from the facial images of the User and also implemented Color Recommendation which suggest the “Best” color palette to the user considering the detected skintone. The system is trained and tested on the UTKFace dataset, Where the system achieved 81.12 % accuracy in categorizing skin tones into five distinct categories: Ivory, Sand, Honey, Caramel, and Espresso. The five distinct categories ensures inclusivity by including the Diverse skintone which led to a scalable and more accurate prediction. The Custom Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) system implemented face detection using Harr Cascades, more advanced image processing techniques and improved CNN Model with batch normalization and dropout layers. The final experimental results depicted the system's performance across different ethnicities and lighting conditions, achieving precision of 0.81 and recall of 0.80 on test data.

title: Posture Alert System: Design, Implementation and Evaluation
authors: Sanskruti Gayki, Rajendra Rewatkar, Pradnyawant Gote, Ujwalla H. Gawande, Jay Thakare, Gaurav Mahakalkar
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: The majority of people sit at their laptops or desktops and do not realise that their musculoskeletal health is getting hurt because of poor posture. Back pain, neck strain, and lethargy may be experienced in case of slouching, tilting the head, and bending over with time. A posture warning system would help in providing users with real-time warnings to correct bad posture as it can recognize bad posture. In this paper, the author is talking about a cheap wearable posture alarm device that was made out of ESP32 and MPU-6050 sensors. The system measures angles of trunk tilt, real-time sensor data and provides buzzer or vibration signals whenever bad posture is maintained. Apilot study demonstrates that the system is effective in detecting slouching postures, reducing the discomfort of users, and is useful with students, office employees, and elderly people.

title: Applications of the Directional Coupler with Arbitrary Phase Difference Slope for Doherty Power Amplifier
authors: Yufei Pan, Wanjun Yang
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: The flexible control of phase characteristics is a critical requirement for advancing modern radio frequency (RF) components. Directional couplers, owing to their inherent ability to provide specific phase relationships between output ports, present themselves as a promising candidate for sophisticated power amplifier feeding networks. This paper systematically investigates different methodologies for controlling the phase difference slope (PDS) in directional couplers. Furthermore, the discussion is extended to demonstrate how these techniques can be leveraged to overcome bandwidth and efficiency limitations in conventional Doherty power amplifiers (DPAs).

title: Design and Development of a Smart Street Light Controller
authors: Joy Sarkar, Prasanjit Ghosh Pranta, Soumya Chandra Saha, Sangita Sahana, Biswanath Roy
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th Conference on Applied Signal Processing (ASPCON)
abstract: Street lighting constitutes a major share of electrical energy consumption, particularly when operated continuously at full brightness. This paper proposes a smart and automated streetlight control system designed to enhance energy efficiency by dynamically adjusting lumen output based on ambient conditions and real-time traffic movement. The system dims streetlights during periods of inactivity and restores full brightness upon detecting motion, thereby reducing unnecessary power usage and minimizing wear on lighting components. It utilizes a Light Dependent Resistor (LDR) for light detection and Infrared (IR) sensors for vehicular motion sensing. An Arduino Uno microcontroller serves as the system's central controller, enabling fully automated, maintenance-free operation. A prototype was developed and tested under simulated nighttime conditions, demonstrating effective performance, cost-efficiency, and adaptability. The proposed solution is well-suited for smart cities, campuses, parking lots, and other outdoor environments seeking sustainable lighting infrastructure.

title: Simulation and Analysis of the Interaction in a Dual-Band Gyro-TWT
authors: Keyu Pan, Xiaofeng Li, Qixiang Zhao
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: In this paper, a dual-band dielectric-loaded gyrotron-traveling-wave tube interaction circuit is designed and optimized, with the low band operating in TE<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf> mode in the Ku-band and the high band operating in TE<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">01</inf> mode in the Ka-band. Combining the dispersion equation, the dispersion characteristics of the waveguide are analyzed. Meanwhile, the dielectric-loaded waveguide structure is simulated using the three-dimensional electromagnetic simulation software CST. The PIC simulation results show that under the electron beam conditions of 75 kV and 10 A, the peak output power is achieved at 132.6 kW and 219.8 kW in Ku-band and Ka-band respectively, with saturated gains of 42.2 dB and 44.4 dB, and 3-dB bandwidth of 1.6 GHz and 1.5 GHz.

title: A Compact SIW-Based Millimeter-Wave Self-Diplexing Slot Antenna with Low Cross-Polarization for K-Band and 5G Applications
authors: S. Tejaswi, V. Sai Prasad, Rusan Kumar Barik
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: The focus of this paper is on the design and analysis of a compact millimeter-wave self-diplexing slot antenna designed for K-band and 5G applications. The proposed antenna has a rectangular substrate-integrated waveguide geometry with two slots which define two mushroom-shaped radiating patches. These patches demonstrate two distinct frequency bands when excited by 50 Ω microstrip lines. These bands are tuned by altering design parameters of the slots and radiating patches. To enhance the isolation between the ports at the center of the SIW cavity a single metallic via is placed. The radiation function, equivalent circuit model, and parametric analysis are presented in detailed. Finally, a compact self-diplexing slot antenna operating at 22 and 27 GHz is realized and analyzed. A compact footprint of a suggested antenna has of $0.56\lambda _g^2$ and an isolation better than 24 dB. It exhibits realized gain exceeding 4 dBi, efficiency > 80%, and cross-polarization < -40 dB, which makes this antenna a potential device for K-band and 5G millimeter-wave applications.

title: The Future Works Differently: Exploring Gen-Z’s Compensation Priorities in the UAE
authors: Nasser Alharthi, Malak Arnaout, Sarah Alghoul, Madheya Alkhyeli, Iffat S. Chaudhry
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Social Networks Analysis, Management and Security (SNAMS)
abstract: This study investigates the compensation preferences of Generation Z in the United Arab Emirates (UAE), a demographic that is rapidly becoming a dominant force in the workforce. Using a structured quantitative survey, data was collected from 365 respondents born between 1997 and 2012 to examine their salary expectations, benefit priorities, and trade-off tendencies. While competitive salary remains the top priority, 89% of participants indicated a strong preference for health insurance, 72% favored flexible working hours, and 52% were willing to accept a lower-than-expected salary in exchange for career growth opportunities. The study results reflect a nuanced shift from traditional financial incentives toward personal well-being, autonomy, and value alignment. Gender and employment status also influenced preferences, with female respondents placing greater importance on work-life balance. The findings underscore that Generation Z is not solely financially motivated but also driven by holistic, value-based considerations. This research provides actionable insights for HR professionals and employers seeking to attract and retain Gen Z talent within the UAE’s unique, tax-free yet high-cost labor market.

title: Analysis and Optimization of the Bottleneck in Concurrent I/O Writing for Multiple Nodes in the WRF Model
authors: Haowen Zhang, Qingbo Yuan, Linyang Li, Yuzhuo Kang
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Information Science, Parallel and Distributed Systems (ISPDS)
abstract: As the computational scale of meteorological simulations continues to expand, I/O performance has become a key bottleneck constraining thei overall efficiency. This paper focuses on the performance issues of the WRF (Weather Research and Forecasting) model when using PNetCDF parallel I/O. By combining system call tracing and I/O request analysis, it identifies that a large number of random small blocks and misaligned write operations are the main factors affecting write performance. To address this,this paper proposes two optimization strategies: one is astriping aligned writing method based on process collaboration to solve the problem of misalignment between aggregator data and file system stripes; the other is a fixed aggregate block size strategy to improve data concatenation efficiency and stripe hit rate. Furthermore, an adaptive I/O optimization framework is designed to dynamically identify I/O patterns and match the optimal strategy. Experiments conducted on a parallel file system show that the proposed optimization scheme can reduce the write time of wrfout files by nearly 85% and increase bandwidth utilization by about 6 times in a 16 - node scale, effectively alleviating the parallel write bottleneck and significantly improving the overall simulation efficiency of the WRF model. The optimization strategies have good generality and can be applied to more I/O-intensive scientific computing scenarios.

title: An Efficient Implementation of DGEMM on the Latest Shenwei Processor
authors: Junhui Liu, Junmin Wu
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Information Science, Parallel and Distributed Systems (ISPDS)
abstract: BLAS (Basic Linear Algebra Subprograms), as a core mathematical library in high-performance computing, underpins critical workloads in deep learning and scientific computing. While vendor-optimized BLAS implementations exist for various architectures, existing DGEMM (Double-precision General Matrix Multiply) implementations on the Sunway OceanLight platform—based on the SW26010-Pro processor—fail to fully exploit its computational potential. To address this, this paper presents an efficient DGEMM implementation tailored to the SW26010-Pro architecture. The key contributions include: (1) a thread-level parallel DGEMM algorithm deployed within a single core group; and (2) a novel diagonal HDTBP (Hybrid Double- and Triple-Buffering Prefetching) strategy that effectively hides memory-to-LDM data transfer latency while significantly reducing LDM space overhead. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed implementation achieves up to 91.27% of the processor’s peak theoretical performance.

title: Visual Servo Control of Robotic Arm Based on Reinforcement Learning
authors: Guoqing Zhong, Jian Hu, Jianyong Yao, Shenqing Ding, Yibiao Chen, Mingyi Li
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: To improve the accuracy, convergence speed, and stability of visual servoing control systems, this paper proposes an intelligent control strategy integrating reinforcement learning. By framing parameter optimization as a sequential decision-making problem, the method dynamically adjusts servo gains using reinforcement learning, with control parameter increments as actions and a comprehensive performance index as the objective. An improved controller combining proportional-integral-derivative (PID) and sliding mode control (SMC) is developed for an Eye-in-Hand system. The gain adjustment is formulated as a multilevel decision process and solved using the action-dependent heuristic dynamic programming (ADHDP) algorithm to optimize the balance between speed and stability. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method improves both dynamic performance and feature point visibility.

title: AI-Driven Comparative Analysis of Echo Intensity and Velocity-SNR Relationships for Dual- and Single-Polarization Radars under Heavy Precipitation
authors: Qiao Liang, Ye Yong, Qian Tiao, Lian Ping, Zhang Yang, Fan Rui
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: This study conducts a comparative analysis of a strong precipitation event using data from the dual-polarization Doppler weather radar in Baoqing and the single-polarization radar (Z9085) in Jiansanjiang., Heilongjiang Province. Beyond traditional radar performance evaluation., this work incorporates intelligent data analysis strategies to explore the relationships among radar reflectivity factor (dBZ)., velocity., and signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). The goal is to improve data quality assessment and to support AI-based meteorological applications. The results indicate that the Baoqing dual-polarization radar outperforms the Jiansanjiang radar in detecting strong echoes., especially when reflectivity exceeds 45 dBZ. Polarimetric parameters (ZDR., CC., and KDP) were analyzed with emphasis on their stability under varying SNR levels.
