The findings reveal that intelligent quality control strategies can be guided by SNR thresholds: when SNR < 10 dB., noise causes large measurement errors., while SNR > 15 dB ensures more stable and reliable parameter retrievals. Such insights provide a foundation for integrating radar data into machine learning frameworks for precipitation estimation., intelligent data fusion., and decision-support systems in meteorological services.

title: Single- and Dual-Broadband End-Fire Filtennas with High Front-to-Back Ratios
authors: Yahui Liu, Min Xiang, Chun Liang, Dajiang Li, Zhiyuan Chen, Kun-Zhi Hu
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: This paper presents two types of single-layer end-fire filtennas with enhanced front-to-back ratio (FIB), achieving single- and dual-broadband operations respectively. The broadband configuration incorporates five functional sections: a parallel rectangular double stubs (PRDS) feeder, dual driven elements, parasitic branches, director elements, and a concave ground plane. The dual driven elements naturally generate a lower-band realized gain null, while the parasitic patches create an upper-band realized gain null. By loading extended parasitic strips onto the broadband structure, a dual-band antenna is realized, which produces an additional realized gain null between the two operating frequency ranges. The optimized ground plane and directors collectively improve the FIB ratio performance. Measured results align well with simulations, showing that the broadband prototype achieves an overlapping bandwidth <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\vert \mathrm{S}_{11}\vert \leq-10$</tex> dB and FIB ratio ≥ 15 dB) of 54.3%, while the dual-band prototype attains 33.6% and 15.5% for its two bands. Both prototypes exhibit good bandpass filtering performances.

title: DFT AI: Machine Learning-Guided Test Point Insertion for Pre-Silicon Debug and Post-Silicon Diagnosis in Complex VLSI Systems
authors: Deepika Bhatia
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: The rising complexity of modern System-on-Chip (SoC) designs has made it increasingly important to deploy intelligent Design-for-Testability (DFT) strategies that support both pre-silicon debug and post-silicon fault diagnosis. Conventional test point insertion (TPI) techniques are typically driven by static heuristics or ATPG-based analyses, and often struggle to scale in the presence of diverse microarchitectures, tight timing budgets, and strict area constraints. This paper introduces DFT AI, a machine learning–guided framework that automates TPI using data-driven reasoning and structural learning. The approach combines gradient-boosted decision trees with graph neural networks (GNNs) to estimate signal importance from structural, dynamic, and contextual features extracted from synthesized RTL designs. A constraint-aware selector ensures that only high-impact, timing-safe test points are chosen. We evaluate DFT AI on ITC’99 and OpenCores benchmark suites and observe up to 28% improvement in observability gain and a 27% reduction in diagnostic ambiguity over state-of-the-art methods, with modest area penalty and significantly reduced runtime. The framework generalizes well across heterogeneous designs and provides interpretability via SHAP-based signal analysis, offering a scalable and trustworthy solution for lifecycle-wide DFT automation.

title: Dual-Approach CNN Framework for Brain Tumor Detection Using MRI Images
authors: K. Prasanthi, Kottu Sangeeth Kumar, Nagalingam Phani Rohith, Adaka Jagapathi Babu, Pothi Reddy Pavan Kumar Reddy, Samyuktha Pusarla
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: One of the critical illness problems that are being experienced in the world and early and accurate diagnosis is the most crucial in treatment are brain tumors. The study hypothesizes a twin-type deep-learning brain tumor detection system on MRI screening images, which is intended to classify brain tumor tissues and one through transfer learning Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). The tumor and the non-tumor scans which were referred to as MRI scans were introduced in high numbers so that, the model would be used in applying to unknown information. CNN was trained as train on scratch; retraining an already trained network to the classification task was performed by the transfer learning approach. The evaluation of the experimental results received (97%) on higher values (high) of accuracy and high values of precision and recall i.e. they are reliable and balanced. The comparative analysis of the two approaches suggests that the existing trade-offs between model development in a linear fashion and adaption of pre-trained architecture. Since the suggested framework suggests, several deep learning tactics can be combined to achieve the aim of improving diagnostic accuracy, assist radiologists with their initial diagnosis, and, lastly, achieve improved patient outcomes.

title: Cross-Modal Mamba Fusion for Multispectral Object Detection
authors: Zelong Shi, Weiming Liu
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: Cross-modal fusion has proven effective for enhancing object detection in challenging environments by integrating complementary cues from visible and thermal imagery. To exploit this advantage, we propose a Mamba-based multispectral detection framework that combines efficient sequence modeling with adaptive feature interaction. A Global-Local Mamba Interaction (GLMI) module enables both global context understanding and fine-grained local perception through a dual-scale scanning strategy, improving the detection of multi-scale targets. Furthermore, a Channel-aware Mamba Fusion (CMF) module introduces an adaptive cross-modal attention mechanism to refine channel-wise interactions and strengthen feature consistency between modalities. Evaluations on three benchmark datasets-FLIR, LLVIP, and M<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>FD-verify the effectiveness of our model in maintaining stable and accurate detection under diverse scene conditions.

title: A Review on Deep Neural Network Approaches for Early Autism Spectrum Disorder Diagnosis: Current Trends, Challenges and a Proposed Framework
authors: Ashwini Rampure, Namdev Sawant, Dhanashri Bhosale
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is a complicated neural development condition identified by a diverse variety of clinical features, including obstacles in social interaction, behavior, and sensory processing. Early assessment of ASD is crucial, as it really help quality of life for affected people. Previously researchers have worked on ML and deep learning methods for the identification of ASD, but many studies only look at a few types of models or use small datasets that focus on very specific areas. Because of this, their results might not work well for other situations or be very reliable. Moreover, there is a lack of comprehensive comparative analysis across different deep neural models that can effectively handle diverse forms of input data such as behavioral patterns, sensory responses, and developmental markers. Motivated by this need, our study presents a deep learning-based model for catching autism early. Planned models include CNN-1D, MLP, LSTM, and DNN. These architectures employ different learning mechanisms and structural designs to capture and interpret complex, nonlinear patterns in ASD-related data. Our key contribution lies in systematically comparing these models on benchmark datasets available from repositories such as Kaggle, with a focus on evaluating accuracy, robustness, and broader applicability.

title: Unsupervised Domain Adaptation via Content Alignment for Hippocampus Segmentation
authors: Hoda Kalabizadeh, Ludovica Griffanti, Pak-Hei Yeung, Ana I. L. Namburete, Nicola K. Dinsdale, Konstantinos Kamnitsas
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Deep learning models for medical image segmentation often struggle when deployed across different datasets due to domain shifts - variations in both image appearance, known as style, and population-dependent anatomical char-acteristics, referred to as content. This paper presents a novel unsupervised domain adaptation framework that di-rectly addresses domain shifts encountered in cross-domain hippocampus segmentation from MRI, with specific empha-sis on content variations. Our approach combines efficient style harmonisation through z-normalisation with a bidi-rectional deformable image registration (DIR) strategy. The DIR network is jointly trained with segmentation and dis-criminator networks to guide the registration with respect to a region of interest and generate anatomically plausible transformations that align source images to the target domain. We validate our approach through comprehensive evaluations on both a synthetic dataset using Morpho-MNIST (for controlled validation of core principles) and three MRI hippocampus datasets representing populations with varying degrees of atrophy. Across all experiments, our method outperforms existing baselines. For hippocam-pus segmentation, when transferring from young, healthy populations to clinical dementia patients, our framework achieves up to 15% relative improvement in Dice score compared to standard augmentation methods, with the largest gains observed in scenarios with substantial content shift. These results highlight the efficacy of our approach for accurate hippocampus segmentation across diverse populations.

title: GraphGAN-Based Fault Detection and Location for Complex Power Grids
authors: Jiahua Liu, Huizhong Shi, Yuhui Chen, Chengyue Yang, Mingyu Ma, Yanping Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy Power and Electrical Technology (CEPET)
abstract: With the development of modern power systems, especially smart grids and microgrids, the topological structure and operation mode of power grids have become increasingly complex, making the problem of fault detection and location more intricate. Traditional fault detection methods often lack efficiency and accuracy when dealing with data from power grids with complex structures and diverse fault types. This paper proposes a GraphGAN-based method for fault detection and location in power grids. GraphGAN is a new deep learning model that combines Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) and Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs). It can effectively model the complex topological structure of power grids, extract relational information between nodes and edges, and enhance the robustness of fault detection through data augmentation. Through simulation experiments, this paper verifies the accuracy and real-time performance of the proposed method in power grid fault detection and location, especially its application under complex power grid topologies.

title: Predicting Emotional Well-Being of Undergraduate Students Using Machine Learning Approaches
authors: Satish N Bhadane, Kuraparthi TV Reddy, Pradeep Patil
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: This paper investigates the emotional well-being status of 719 undergraduate students through 10 questionnaires co-relate with psychology and comprising negatively phrased items. Emotional well-being plays an important role in academic activities, social connect, and long-term mental well-being. The status of emotional well-being among various undergraduate students is a critical determinant of academic activities, personal development, and long-term state of mental health. The present paper applies machine learning methods to predict students' emotional well-being using questionnaire dataset based on a Likert scale. Since all items were negatively worded, responses were reverse-scored to compute overall emotional well-being indices. Both regression and classification models were implemented to predict continuous well-being scores and categorize students into low, medium, and high emotional wellbeing groups. Additionally, unsupervised learning approaches such as K-Means, Hierarchical Clustering, and DBSCAN were applied to discover hidden patterns in student responses. Results indicate that Random Forest Regression achieved the highest predictive accuracy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{1}$</tex>), while Logistic Regression provided robust classification (Accuracy = 0.99). Clustering analysis revealed the presence of compact groups of students sharing similar well-being profiles. These findings suggest that data-driven approaches can provide early insights into students' emotional health, enabling universities to design targeted interventions.

title: Real-Time Machine Learning Analysis During Accentuated Eccentric Jumps and Ladder Drills to Predict Sprint and Change of Direction Performance in Futsal Players
authors: Darpan Chaudhari, Sharath Hullumani, Swapnil U. Ramteke
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: This pilot study explores the use of real time machine learning (Xsens MVN Link) and timing gates for objectively monitoring and enhancing sprint and change-of-direction (COD) performance in futsal players. A parallel-group randomized controlled trial (RCT) design was employed, with participants randomly assigned to either an experimental group (Accentuated Eccentric Loading [AEL] jump and ladder training) or a control group (ladder training only). Sprint and agility performance were assessed using dual-beam timing gates, providing millisecond-level accuracy in timing measurements, while kinematic data were captured using 17 real-time machine learninginertial measurement units (IMUs) integrated into a Lycra suit. The findings indicated that the experimental group, which incorporated AEL countermovement jumps (CMJ) and drop jumps, showed superior improvements in both sprint performance (mean change: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-\mathbf{0. 1 5} \mathrm{s}, \mathrm{p}=0.001$</tex>) and agility (mean change: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-0.25 ~\mathrm{s}, \mathrm{p}=0.001$</tex>) compared to the control group. The use of Xsens IMUs provided detailed kinematic data, allowing for the analysis of stride length, stride frequency, and joint angular velocities during sprinting and COD maneuvers. This study highlights the feasibility and accuracy of using integrated real-time machine learningtechnology and timing gates for assessing athletic performance. The results suggest that AEL-based training, combined with advanced performance monitoring, can offer valuable insights into neuromuscular adaptations in futsal players. However, the small sample size and short intervention period warrant caution in interpreting these findings, and future studies with larger cohorts and longer interventions are recommended to confirm these preliminary results.

title: MBIST++: An Adaptive March Algorithm Generator for Memory Test Coverage Enhancement in Post-Silicon Validation
authors: Deepika Bhatia
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: As embedded memories continue to dominate System-on-Chip (SoC) designs, their testing and validation have become critical bottlenecks for post-silicon reliability. Conventional Memory Built-In Self Test (MBIST) architectures typically rely on static March algorithms which, although effective for classical fault models, struggle to expose complex or emergent defects introduced by advanced technology nodes and layout-dependent anomalies. This paper proposes MBIST++, a dynamic and hardware-efficient framework that generates adaptive March test sequences guided by runtime fault profiling. In contrast to traditional approaches, MBIST++ embeds an on-chip evolutionary engine that synthesizes high-coverage test patterns by learning from post-silicon fault signatures in real time. The architecture couples a microcoded execution engine with a feedback-driven generator and fault classification module, enabling responsive and targeted validation of embedded memory blocks. Experimental results on industrial-grade memory macros show that MBIST++ achieves up to 96.2% fault coverage, outperforming March SS and machine-learning-enhanced BIST methods, while preserving competitive test latency and incurring only modest area overhead. These findings suggest that MBIST++ offers a scalable path toward post-silicon resilience and adaptive memory test automation for emerging semiconductor platforms.

title: Enhancing Argument Structure Extraction with Effective Fusion of Contextual Information
authors: Mingyang Qiao, Yanliang Zhang
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing (MLNLP)
abstract: Argument Structure Extraction (ASE) is a key task in natural language processing, identifying arguments and their support or attack relationships among textual propositions. Extensive research has shown that contextual information plays a crucial role in ASE. Although advancements in pre-trained language model techniques in recent years, most approaches still treat the sentences before and after the target proposition separately, ignoring the interaction between sentences, which makes it difficult to effectively integrate contextual information, and limits the model's ability to recognize argument relation in complex contexts. To tackle these issues, this study introduces an ASE model that effectively fuses contextual information (FCASE). The model takes the sentences before and after the target proposition as bidirectional inputs, so that the vector contains semantic information from both directions, thereby obtaining richer semantic representations. When modelling the relationship between the target proposition and other propositions, a more complicated fusion mechanism is utilized to increase relation prediction precision. We systematically assessed the model across five publicly available datasets, and performed comparative experiments with various baseline methods. The results demonstrate that the proposed method surpasses baseline models in performance metrics.

title: Food Stiffness Identification and Adaptive Constant-Force Feeding Control for Assistive Feeding Robots
authors: Haoqi Huang, Yitao Chen, Weiming Wu, Binshan Hu
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: To address the autonomous feeding assistance needs of people with disabilities and older adults, this study develops an assistive feeding robotic platform integrating multimodal perception and proposes a method for food impedance identification and biomimetic strategy planning based on active interaction forces. The approach enables high-accuracy classification of foods with different hardness. Using motion-capture techniques to analyze eating data from non-disabled adult participants, we establish a mapping model between food stiffness and key motion parameters, allowing the robot to adaptively select biomimetic feeding strategies. Finally, through an adaptive impedance controller based on active disturbance rejection control (ADRC), constant-force operation is achieved, maintaining conformal contact with the plate throughout the food-acquisition process.

title: Study on the Dynamic Assessment Method for Flood-Related Risks of Power Grid Equipment Based on Real-Time Data
authors: Yufeng Tai, Guanghui Wu, Changwen Li, Junbo Liu, Zhenyu Li, Chenxi Du, Wenrui Han, Jialong Wu, Qinqin Deng, Ziyan Ou
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: Aiming at the limitations of traditional flood risk assessment methods—such as relying on static zoning and using fixed model parameters that fail to capture real-time risks—this study proposes a dynamic assessment method for flood-related risks of power grid equipment based on real-time data. First, multi-source data are integrated to establish a basic database, and this study adopts GPU-accelerated coupling of 1D and 2D hydrodynamic models to simulate hazard-causing factors under different flood frequencies. Subsequently, real-time data from the “space-air-ground integrated platform” are accessed to establish a dynamic correction mechanism for the core parameters of the model, and iterative optimization of parameters is achieved through deviation comparison between simulation results and real-time data. Finally, the Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP) is integrated to quantify the risk levels of power grid equipment, and dynamic risk zoning is output via a visualization platform. Taking the Lalin River Basin as the experimental area, the results show that the proposed method realizes the dynamic updating and accurate quantification of flood-related risks for power grid equipment, which can provide a scientific basis for power grid flood control planning and emergency repair during disasters.

title: Design and Implementation of a Self-Powered Wireless Water Temperature Measurement and Control System
authors: Wei Yang, Qianyu Zhang, Xuanke Tong, Zhengxuan Wei
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: Traditional water temperature monitoring and control systems suffer from temperature lag due to the temperature measurement point being located inside the water heater, and remote temperature measurement devices face power supply difficulties. This research designs and implements a self-powered wireless water temperature monitoring and control system, solving these problems through a distributed architecture. The system's temperature measurement end utilizes a hydroelectric turbine to generate electricity, integrating a DS18B20 high-precision sensor and an nRF24L01+ wireless module to collect and transmit temperature data in real time. The temperature control end adjusts the hot and cold water ratio based on a PID algorithm, achieving closed-loop control via wireless communication. Experiments show that the system achieves a temperature measurement accuracy of 0.1°C and a temperature control accuracy of 0.5°C, with a power consumption of only 50mW at the temperature measurement end. This scheme provides an efficient and scalable reference for the power supply and communication design of embedded monitoring and control systems.

title: SCP-Boost: A Data-Driven Multi-Model Framework for Protein Identification in Single-Cell Proteomics
authors: Zeyao Chen
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Data Science and Information Technology (DSIT)
abstract: Quantification of multiplexed single-cell proteomes by liquid chromatography tandem mass spectrometry (LCMS/MS) has greatly improved proteome coverage. However, low protein abundance still limits protein identification from single-cell samples. To address this, a data-driven framework that integrates multiple independent models is proposed to enhance single-cell proteome identification. ElasticNet is used for robust retention time prediction, enabling more accurate peptide alignment, while a model combining Mamba Block and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory captures complex ionization patterns and sequential dependencies. To further improve accuracy, a lightweight model is incorporated to predict reliable collision cross-section values, addressing the lack of collision cross-section data. In addition, a novel combination of reinforcement learning for adaptive feature selection and Light Gradient Boosting Machine for re-scoring, which together identify the most informative features, reduce redundancy, and optimize the evaluation of peptide-spectrum matches. With a false discovery rate controlled at 0.01, the framework delivers high-confidence peptide and protein identification. The experimental results show that each module contributes positively to performance. This lightweight and efficient framework is well suited for advancing single-cell proteomics and holds strong potential for future biological research applications.

title: A Online Monitoring Method for Novel Power System Node Inertia Based on Gradient Descent
authors: Qingyu Ji, Muyang Liu, Genzhu Wu, Peiyi Wang
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: As the proportion of new energy sources continues to increase, the rotational inertia of power systems has significantly decreased, posing severe challenges to system frequency stability. To address the difficulty in accurately identifying inertia in quasi-steady-state scenarios, this paper proposes a real-time node inertia monitoring method based on gradient descent. This method uses the second derivative of system frequency and active power deviation as inputs, establishing an error feedback model to track node inertia parameters. Subsequently, an improved WSCC-9 model is employed for simulation testing. Simulation results demonstrate that this method achieves relatively accurate node inertia monitoring under small disturbance conditions.

title: Triple-Notched UWB Monopole Antenna Incorporating Swastika Structures and a Closed π Shaped Slot
authors: V.Venkata Rao, Venkata Lakshmaiah Y, T. Hemalatha, J Bhavani
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel ultra-wideband monopole antenna featuring triple-notched properties. Initially, rejection of the 4.07–5.47 GHz (1.08 GHz) band is achieved through a closed π slot embedded within the radiating patch. Subsequently, two additional notched frequencies, suitable for S-band applications at 5.41–7.41 GHz (2.00 GHz) and C-band at 10.31–14.93 GHz (4.62 GHz), are introduced using swastika slots on a partial ground plane. Fabricated on an FR4 substrate, the antenna boasts compact dimensions of 32 × 35 × 1.5 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>, covering a frequency band from 2.65 GHz to 11.11 GHz. Notably, the aerial demonstrates effective notch management capabili- ties, suppressing unwanted frequencies. The paper meticulously evaluates the antenna’s performance characteristics, including radiation patterns, surface current distributions, and rejection bands

title: A Novel Hybrid SMC and MPC Method for Improved Trajectory Tracking of Robotic Arm
authors: Daixing Lu, Yingjie Gao, Junjie Lu
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid control strategy that integrates Sliding Mode Control (SMC) and Model Predictive Control (MPC) to improve the trajectory tracking accuracy of robotic arms in micro-automobile production lines. A six-degree-of-freedom Mecharm270 robotic arm model is developed using the Denavit-Hartenberg (D-H) method. An adaptive switching mechanism dynamically selects between SMC and MPC based on real-time tracking errors. The SMC ensures a rapid response for small errors, while the MPC predicts and compensates for larger deviations. Additionally, an adaptive hysteresis design is implemented to enhance stability by reducing the frequency of switching between the controllers. Simulation results obtained using Matlab/Simulink demonstrate that the hybrid controller achieves a tracking error rate of 9.05 % and a response time of 0.05 s, outperforming both traditional SMC and MPC approaches. The proposed method significantly enhances precision, robustness, and real-time performance, making it well-suited for high-precision automation applications.

title: Full-Range Arbitrary Complex-Amplitude Manipulation for Holographic Metasurface
authors: Jinqi Zhang, Yuxin Wang, Yijia Zhao, Wenmei Zhang, Rongcao Yang
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: In this paper, a transmissive metasurface element achieving full-range and arbitrary complex-amplitude manipulation is proposed for circularly polarized (CP) waves. It relies solely on controlling the orientation angles of two identical anisotropic layers to achieve arbitrary amplitude and phase modulation without complex parameter optimization. Based on this element, two holographic metasurfaces are designed and simulated, achieving the peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) of 19.19 dB and 18.05 dB, which shows excellent agreement with theoretical results and demonstrates its applicability in imaging and wireless communication systems.

title: Securing RIS-Assisted NOMA Network With Optimized Resource Allocation and Known Jamming Under Single and Multiple Eavesdropping Scenarios
authors: Alla Prudhvi Sai, V Narasimha Nayak, Polasi Phani Kumar, Swarna Balaji, Kollu Kartheek
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: This paper investigates the physical layer security (PLS) of a Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS)–assisted non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) network, focusing on some conditions such as the involvement of single and multiple eavesdroppers. To improve security, a known jammer is strategically placed near the eavesdroppers. This study analyzes secrecy performance under Rayleigh fading conditions, considering both perfect and imperfect Successive Interference Cancellation (SIC). Furthermore, optimal power allocation is also employed to improve secrecy rates. Monte Carlo simulations confirm that the proposed approach significantly improves security compared to conventional schemes without jamming or optimal resource allocation.

title: Artificial Intelligence for the Development and Customization of Hemostatic Biomaterials in Otorhinolaryngology
authors: Harshil Dobariya, Shraddha Jain, Chandraveer Suryavansh
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Bleeding control in otorhinolaryngology (ENT) is challenging due to the anatomically intricate, vascular, and functionally sensitive nature of the head and neck regions. Hemostatic biomaterials are artificial materials that facilitate quick and secure blood coagulation and are valuable tools in ENT practice. Hemostasis is required during and after various nose, anterior skull base, ear and lateral skull base surgeries. Artificial intelligence advances are profoundly impacting design, customisation, and clinical application of these biomaterials. This review discusses the complex role that AI will play in creating hemostatic biomaterials specific to ENT applications. It sets the stage for AI- driven modelling methods for reproducing material behaviour, such as finite element simulations and supervised learning algorithms, achieving prediction accuracies up to 92 % in clotting efficacy classification. It highlights using convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and random forest models for high-throughput screening and formulation optimisation, reducing trial- and-error cycles by nearly 60%. Anatomy-based customisation based on 3D segmentation of CT/MRI scans allows for personalised biomaterial shaping with spatial precision under 1 mm. The review also explores intelligent deployment system integration using sensor- augmented dressings and real-time biosignal processing through AI-enabled microcontrollers, achieving bleeding detection sensitivities above 95%. The review outlines new trends in AI-augmented biomaterials, such as peptide-based hydrogels, chitosan composites, and sensor-integrated dressings supported by recent literature. Through the integration of knowledge in material science, clinical practice, and computational intelligence, this review highlights the breakthrough potential of AI to provide next-generation hemostatic therapies for safer, more effective, and personalised ENT medicine.

title: Who's Asking? Investigating Bias Through the Lens of Disability-Framed Queries in LLMs
authors: Vishnu Hari, Kalpana Panda, Srikant Panda, Amit Agarwal, Hitesh Laxmichand Patel
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) routinely infer users' demographic traits from phrasing alone, which can result in biased responses, even when no explicit demographic information is provided. The role of disability cues in shaping these inferences remains largely uncharted. Thus, we present the first systematic audit of disability-conditioned demographic bias across eight state-of-the-art instruction-tuned LLMs ranging from 3B to 72B parameters. Using a balanced template corpus that pairs nine disability categories with six real-world business domains, we prompt each model to predict five demographic attributes - gender, socioeconomic status, education, cultural background, and locality - under both neutral and disability-aware conditions. Across a varied set of prompts, models deliver a definitive demographic guess in up to 97% of cases, exposing a strong tendency to make arbitrary inferences with no clear justification. Disability context heavily shifts predicted attribute distributions, and domain context can further amplify these deviations. We observe that larger models are simultaneously more sensitive to disability cues and more prone to biased reasoning, indicating that scale alone does not mitigate stereotype amplification. Our findings reveal persistent intersections between ableism and other demographic stereotypes, pinpointing critical blind spots in current alignment strategies. We release our evaluation framework and results to encourage disability-inclusive benchmarking and recommend integrating abstention calibration and counterfactual fine-tuning to curb unwarranted demographic inference. Code and data will be released on acceptance. Content Warning: This paper reproduces ableist and demographic stereotypes solely for research purposes. Readers may find this language offensive or distressing.

title: Style Composition Within Distinct LoRA Modules for Traditional Art
authors: Jaehyun Lee, Wonhark Park, Wonsik Shin, Hyunho Lee, Hyoung Min Na, Nojun Kwak
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Diffusion-based text-to-image models have achieved remarkable results in synthesizing diverse images from text prompts and can capture specific artistic styles via style personalization. However, their entangled latent space and lack of smooth interpolation make it difficult to apply distinct painting techniques in a controlled, regional manner, often causing one style to dominate. To overcome this, we propose a zero-shot diffusion pipeline that naturally blends multiple styles by performing style composition on the denoised latents predicted during the flow-matching denoising process of separately trained, style-specialized models. We leverage the fact that lower-noise latents carry stronger stylistic information and fuse them across heterogeneous diffusion pipelines using spatial masks, enabling precise, region-specific style control. This mechanism preserves the fidelity of each individual style while allowing user-guided mixing. Furthermore, to ensure structural coherence across different models, we incorporate depth-map conditioning via ControlNet into the diffusion framework. Qualitative and quantitative experiments demonstrate that our method successfully achieves region-specific style mixing according to the given masks.

title: Multi-Task Learning for Joint Action and Gesture Recognition
authors: Konstantinos Spathis, Nikolaos Kardaris, Petros Maragos
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Real-world applications focused on interpreting human behavior often require multiple computer vision tasks to be addressed simultaneously. Multitask learning typically achieves this by jointly training a single deep neural network to learn shared representations, providing efficiency and improving generalization. Although action and gesture recognition are closely related tasks, since they focus on body and hand movements, current state-of-the-art methods handle them separately. In this paper, we show that employing a multi-task learning paradigm for action and gesture recognition results in more efficient, robust and generalizable visual representations, by leveraging the synergies between these tasks. Experiments on multiple action and gesture datasets demonstrate that handling actions and gestures in a single architecture can achieve better performance for both tasks in comparison to their single-task learning variants.

title: Comparative Analysis of FANET Routing Strategies: A Comprehensive NS-3 Based Study
authors: Rashi, Archana Das, Pawan Kumar, Kamal Kumar Gola
publish: 2025 1st IEEE Uttar Pradesh Section Women in Engineering International Conference on Electrical Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPWIECON)
abstract: The performance of Flying Ad-Hoc Networks (FANETs) in dynamic environments is critically dependent on the employed routing strategy. This paper presents a comprehensive evaluation of proactive, reactive, and hybrid routing approaches to identify the most suitable framework for high-mobility aerial networks. Using NS-3, we analyzed representative protocols from each category-including proactive link-state and table-driven methods, reactive source-routing and distance-vector methods, and a hybrid zone-based method-under varying network densities. Performance was benchmarked against key metrics including end-to-end delay, packet delivery ratio, throughput, jitter, and routing overhead. The results conclusively show that the reactive source-routing strategy delivered superior performance, maintaining consistently high reliability and throughput with minimal delay. In contrast, proactive strategies suffered from excessive control overhead, which led to poor scalability and negligible data throughput. The reactive distance-vector approach exhibited severe latency degradation at scale, while the hybrid zone-based protocol offered stable but intermediate performance. This study concludes that a reactive, source-routing strategy is the most robust and efficient for ensuring reliable data communication in highly mobile FANETs, providing critical guidance for designing effective communication systems for UAV-based applications.

title: Adaptive Grasp Control Framework: Integrating Slip Detection Network with Constrained Force Optimization for Dexterous Manipulation
authors: Hongjun Ma, Weichang Li, Zhihua Yang, Che Hou
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Achieving human-like dexterity in robotic manipulation requires seamless integration of tactile perception and adaptive control strategies. This work presents a novel control framework integrating data-driven slip detection with constrained force optimization for four-fingered robotic manipulation using the Paxini Dexhand platform. We develop a comprehensive force balance constraint algorithm employing friction cone projections and optimization objective <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V(\mathbf{f})$</tex> for real-time grasp force regulation. A compact slip detection network utilizing dual-domain feature extraction and channel attention mechanisms achieves high-precision slip identification, providing reliable tactile feedback for force control. The systematic integration strategy translates slip detection results into concrete force regulation actions, realizing a closed-loop system from reactive detection to proactive control. Experimental validation demonstrates detection latencies under <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0 m s}$</tex> with slip detection accuracy exceeding 95 %, adaptively adjusting contact forces in real-time during dynamic manipulation tasks, reducing average gripping forces by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$35-45 \%$</tex> compared to conservative approaches while handling objects from delicate items to heavy industrial components.

title: Text-Aligned Radar-Based Sign Language Recognition for Healthcare Communication
authors: Raffaele Mineo, Amelia Sorrenti, Gaia Caligiore, Federica Proietto Salanitri, Giovanni Bellitto, Senya Polikovsky, Sabina Fontana, Egidio Ragonese, Concetto Spampinato, Simone Palazzo
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Sign language recognition (SLR) aims to bridge the communication gap between signers and non-signers through automated interpretation of signs. While most existing methods rely on RGB or depth video, we explore radar signals as a privacy-preserving alternative. We introduce TRACE (Text-Radar Alignment via Contrastive Embedding), a novel radar-based framework for SLR. TRACE extracts spatiotemporal embeddings from Range-Doppler Map (RDM) sequences using a 3D convolutional neural network (CNN). During training, it employs prompt-conditioned contrastive learning to align radar features with soft-prompted class embeddings from a frozen text encoder. At inference time, TRACE assigns to each radar sequence the label whose textual embedding is most similar to the visual representation. We evaluate our method on a radar-based Italian Sign Language (LIS) dataset, showing that it outperforms state-of-the-art baselines under standard classification metrics. The results and the ablation study confirm that joint training enhances embedding quality by guiding the CNN toward semantically meaningful features through text alignment, while learnable soft prompts yield more suitable textual representations.

title: AI-Driven Delta Robot System for Automated Loose Fruitlet Collection
authors: Amirullah Hamzah, Farhan Mustafa, Bayhaqi Jailani, Hasmarina Hasan, Zulaikha Kadim
publish: 2025 International Conference on Robotics and Artificial Intelligence (ICRAI)
abstract: Loose fruitlet collection in oil palm plantations remains a labor-intensive process that contributes to significant yield losses when fruitlets are left uncollected. This work presents an AI-driven delta robot system that capable of real-time detection, localization, and collection of loose fruitlets. A monocular camera captures tray images, which are rectified using homography and processed with a convolutional detector trained on a curated oil palm fruitlets dataset. Detected centroids are transformed into robot coordinates through a zone-based calibration scheme, enabling precise targeting within the robot’s workspace. The delta robot executes a simplified pick-and-place sequence with fixed vertical states for approach and retraction, combined with a vacuum end-effector ensure reliable pickup. A verification stage provides an accurate fruitlet counting and failure handling. The system operates entirely on an embedded platform, achieving detection to actuation latencies under 0.5s and pick cycles within 2s. Experimental evaluation demonstrates practical accuracy more than 85% accuracy, robustness under variable lighting, and throughput exceeding 50 fruitlets per minute, indicating a strong potential for deployment in plantation environments.

title: Supporting First Grade Students' Number Sense Using Game-Based Learning of MathPlay
authors: Zetra Hainul Putra, Jesi Alexander Alim, Eni Mulyani, Difa Zarrar Hafizah, Indah Haryati, Numala
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: The purpose of this study was to support first-grade students' number sense using MathPlay as a game-based learning method. This study used classroom action research, with 24 first-grade students working in small groups of 6 students. Data were collected through observations and field notes regarding students' learning activities and interactions with MathPlay. The findings of the study indicate that the application of MathPlay in addition and subtraction learning supports students' number understanding and interest in learning mathematics. The implication of this study is that game-based learning supported by MathPlay provides opportunities for students' early number understanding engagement and development

title: Enhancing Cognitive Behavioural Insights Using Neural Networks: A Deep Learning Approach to Psychological Pattern Recognition
authors: Sumayah Mittal, Pinki Sagar, Santosh Kumar Srivastava, Manoj Kumar Mahto
publish: 2025 1st IEEE Uttar Pradesh Section Women in Engineering International Conference on Electrical Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPWIECON)
abstract: Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT) is still considered a trademark in psychological interventions aimed at changing maladaptive thought patterns to alter emotional and behavioural responses. However, it is rendered subjective by its input with possible risk of the therapist, making it cumbersome if not impossible to scale. Thus, we present a novel interpretable deep learning framework that connects CBT theory with neural architectures to automate the recognition of natural language cognitive distortions, emotional states, and schema-driven thought patterns. The system leverages Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) networks and attention-based Transformer encoders to model thought-emotion-behaviour loops in therapeutic conversations. The classification layers are trained to detect clinically relevant distortions, such as catastrophizing, personalization, and dichotomous thinking, and attention heatmaps provide linguistic interpretability. Evaluation on psychologically annotated corpora demonstrated very high fidelity in detecting distortion and emotion. This offers a theoretical and computational means forward toward AI-based augmentation for psychotherapy, with applications in semi-automating CBT, clinical decision support, and early detection for psychological risk.

title: Beyond Appearance: Geometric Cues for Robust Video Instance Segmentation
authors: Quanzhu Niu, Yikang Zhou, Shihao Chen, Tao Zhang, Shunping Ji
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Video Instance Segmentation (VIS) fundamentally struggles with pervasive challenges including object occlusions, motion blur, and appearance variations during temporal association. To overcome these limitations, this work introduces geometric awareness to enhance VIS robustness by strategically leveraging monocular depth estimation. We systematically investigate three distinct integration paradigms. Expanding Depth Channel (EDC) method concatenates the depth map as input channel to segmentation networks; Sharing ViT (SV) designs a uniform ViT backbone, shared between depth estimation and segmentation branches; Depth Supervision (DS) makes use of depth prediction as an auxiliary training guide for feature learning. Though DS exhibits limited effectiveness, benchmark evaluations demonstrate that EDC and SV significantly enhance the robustness of VIS. When with Swin-L backbone, our EDC method gets 56.2 AP, which sets a new state-of-the-art result on OVIS benchmark. This work conclusively establishes depth cues as critical enablers for robust video understanding.

title: Deep Learning for Kidney Disease Prediction: A Review, Gap Analysis, and Proposed Model
authors: Manjusha Zipare, Namdev Sawant, Punam Gavali
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) is a critical healthcare challenge affecting people globally, as it advances without noticeable symptoms during the early stages, often going undetected until significant kidney damage has occurred. Timely identification of CKD is vital to improving treatment outcomes and preventing severe complications. This paper provides a review of recent developments in deep learning approaches applied to CKD prediction. It begins by highlighting significance of early diagnosis and the contributions of deep learning to medical practice. This paper presets an extensive survey of existing literature, summarizing important findings and advancements. Different machine learning and deep learning algorithms employed for CKD prediction are evaluated based on their performance, advantages, and limitations. Additionally, a conceptual approach for implementing deep learning models tailored for CKD prediction is described. The paper concludes by highlighting upcoming research guidelines in addition with emphasizing the importance of ongoing innovation to strengthen accuracy and accessibility of automated kidney disease detection systems.

title: Mass Estimation Using Supervised Learning with a Tactile Robotic Hand
authors: Manikandan Cheruvary, Nathan F. Lepora, Max Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Robotic Computing and Communication (RoboticCC)
abstract: In this paper, we demonstrate how tactile and proprioceptive sensing in dexterous robotic hands can be leveraged to directly estimate the mass of a manipulated object. Mass estimation is a fundamental cognitive aspect of human dexterity and plays an important role in enabling robots to handle objects effectively. To this end, we propose a supervised-learning method for mass estimation using tactile and proprioceptive sensor data. We carefully collect large-scale simulated dexterous manipulation data and train a Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) to predict the mass of the manipulated object. A comparison is made with mass estimation using static grasp data, showing the importance of dynamic manipulation for estimation of object properties using deep learning approaches. We transfer the learned model to the real system and evaluate the performance of the approach through in-hand manipulation on the Allegro Hand and show that the method accurately estimates the object's mass during manipulation. The method enables mass estimation during object rotation without requiring any additional measurement setup.

title: Securing TOTP-Based Authentication in Online Social Systems via Entropy Augmentation
authors: Francesco Buccafurri, Vincenzo De Angelis, Sara Lazzaro, Carmen Licciardi
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Social Networks Analysis, Management and Security (SNAMS)
abstract: In online social systems, authentication plays a crucial role in ensuring the trustworthiness and integrity of interactions between users and services. Social engineering attacks and malware pose significant threats to the security of the authentication process, enabling impersonation and identity theft. Two-factor authentication is a widely used mechanism to strengthen authentication and reduce the impact of the human factor on the security of online interactions. Often, two-factor authentication leverages Time-based One-Time Passwords (TOTP), a standardized mechanism that allows the client and the server to autonomously compute a one-time password (OTP) based on a shared secret and the current time slot. Unfortunately, TOTP is vulnerable to forward replay attacks, whereby an attacker can pre-compute future valid OTPs and use them for malicious purposes. In this paper, we propose an enhanced TOTP that includes external entropy to fully prevent forward replay attacks. The solution offers a practical and deployable improvement over the current TOTP standard, addressing a known vulnerability with minimal implementation overhead.

title: Enclave Driven Tokenization: Reducing PCI DSS Scope in Cloud Native Checkout Systems
authors: Rajgopal Devabhaktuni, Gopalakrishnan Venkatasubbu
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: The shift of ecommerce platforms to cloud-native architectures (that are based on microservices and containers) has greatly made PCI DSS compliance more complex. The compliance scope of these systems is often extremely wide as their components are generally distributed and the scope includes almost all parts of such system, they have high audit costs and present more security risks. In this paper, we introduce a new design that uses confidential computing with secure enclaves to realize data tokenization. In this model, sensitive cardholder data is handled only within a hardware-enforced secure environment removing the exposure of raw PAN values out to the cloud native application. This strategy allows you to lock PCI DSS scope down into that enclave, and in essence removes compliance responsibilities from the application scale. A synthetic dataset of 410 transaction instances was used to replicate a live checkout setting for an online shopping platform. The system was implemented as a Python microservices application orchestrated using Kubernetes and executed utilizing AWS Nitro Enclaves as the confidential computing technology. It shows how the scope of coverage is cut down from the whole application stack to a minimal service. This approach is a practical, high-assurance path toward simplifying PCI DSS compliance in complex, modern cloud environments.

title: GFR-CAM: Gram-Schmidt Feature Reduction for Hierarchical Class Activation Maps
authors: Kaveh Safavigerdini, Bahram Yaghooti, Amir Erfan Zareei Shams Abadi, Kannappan Palaniappan
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Deep learning models have achieved remarkable success in computer vision tasks, yet their decision-making processes remain largely opaque, limiting their adoption especially in safety-critical applications. While Class Activation Maps (CAMs) have emerged as a prominent solution for visual ex-planation, existing methods suffer from a fundamental limi-tation: they produce single, consolidated explanations leading to “explanatory tunnel vision.” Current CAM methods fail to capture the rich, multi-faceted reasoning that underlies model predictions, particularly in complex scenes with multiple objects or intricate visual relationships. We introduce the Gram-Schmidt Feature Reduction Class Acti-vation Map (GFR-CAM), a novel gradient-free framework that overcomes this limitation through hierarchical feature decomposition that provides a more holistic view of the ar-chitecture's explanatory power. Unlike existing feature re-duction methods that rely on Principal Component Analysis (PCA) and generate a single dominant explanation, GFR-CAM leverages Gram-Schmidt orthogonalization to system-atically extract a sequence of orthogonal, information rich components from model feature maps. The subsequent or-thogonal components are shown to be meaningful expla-nations - not mere noise, that decomposes single objects into semantic parts and systematically disentangles multi-object scenes to identify co-existing entities. We show that GFR-CAM on ResNet-50 and Swin Transformer architec-tures across ImageNet and PASCAL VOC datasets achieves competitive performance with state-of-the-art methods.

title: Design of User Growth and Data-Driven Operational Decision-Making Architecture for Highway Service Areas
authors: Pan Zhang, Jie Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Service Operations and Logistics, and Informatics (SOLI)
abstract: In response to the lack of basis and difficulty in measuring the effectiveness of decision-making in the operation of highway service areas, this paper proposes a user growth model in the context of highway service areas, as well as a data-driven architecture for highway service area operation decision-making. The aim is to achieve revenue growth and service improvement in service areas through a systematic strategy of data-driven product iteration. Its design includes modules such as service area user growth model design, data collection, indicator analysis, strategy generation, service outreach, and effectiveness evaluation. The service area user growth model defines five stages of the user lifecycle for the service area; The data collection module obtains real-time traffic, facility usage, and consumption data in the service area; The indicator analysis module defines key operational indicators and data reference ranges for each stage based on the user lifecycle, providing quantitative measurement standards for subsequent modules; The strategy generation module generates corresponding optimization solutions based on real-time operational indicators and improvement goals; The service outreach module releases solutions through information display terminals; The effect evaluation module monitors and evaluates the effectiveness of optimization plans by tracking changes in operational indicators. This architecture can effectively respond to the operational decisions of complex scenarios in highway service areas. It can provide a set of universal operational indicators evaluation standards and data-driven product iteration strategies for service areas with different locations, customer groups, and service facilities, enabling highway service areas to continuously achieve revenue growth and service improvement through iterative closed-loop. This study provides a new solution for the intelligent operation of service areas.

title: A Low-Cost Sensing Glove for Robust and Dexterous Human-Robot Interaction
authors: Dongfu Yin, Run Yang, Lei Xie, F. Richard Yu, Ji Chen, Bing Dai
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Data gloves are key to intuitive human-robot interaction (HRI), but existing solutions are often limited by high cost or performance trade-offs, while vision-based alternatives lack robustness. To address these challenges, we present a low-cost, high-performance flexible glove with five customfabricated CNTs/PDMS piezoresistive sensors, coupled with a software framework for high-accuracy, 1D-CNN-based gesture recognition and a Learning from Demonstration approach for dexterous grasping. Experiments demonstrate our system achieves 99.2% accuracy on 32 gestures and maintains robust control where vision-based methods fail under complete visual occlusion. Furthermore, the learning framework enables a robot to grasp fragile objects, such as an egg, with 98% success by preventing deformation. This work provides a validated pathway for developing low-cost, intelligent, and reliable systems for advanced HRI.

title: Codebase Aware Generative Agents for the SDLC: Automating Documentation, Dependency Analysis and Test Generation
authors: Vatsal Kishorbhai Mavani
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: Autonomous agents are becoming common in software development, but they often struggle to maintain context in large, changing codebases. To solve this, we propose CA-SAF (Codebase-Aware Self-Adapting Framework), a multi-agent system designed to fix this architectural "blindness" by actively retrieving precise code details. Our core innovation is ZSDM (Zero-Shot Dependency Mapping). This hybrid method combines AST parsing with strict LLM extraction to map every entity and relationship into a queryable Knowledge Graph. By using structured code summaries, we achieved roughly 80% data compression, ensuring the context remains efficient. We benchmarked the system against GPT-4o, LLaMA 3.1, and Claude 3 Opus. We found that Claude 3 Opus, with its 200,000 - token window, slightly outperformed the others (capped at 128,000) on complex, non-local mapping tasks. Over a six-month operational study, using CA-SAF led to a measurable drop in both Cycle Time and Lead Time for maintenance work. Crucially, the framework used this codebase awareness to balance speed with quality, actively reducing the number of critical bugs that high-speed LLM generation often introduces.

title: Application of Generative AI in Animation Character Creation: A Study on Style Transfer Design*
authors: Xue Li, Yu Xiong
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: With the rapid development of generative AI technology, animation character creation is transitioning from traditional manual design to a “human-machine collaboration” model, where style transfer an essential application scenario, enables efficient conversion of diverse animation styles. This study focuses on the application of generative AI in animation character style transfer. First, it clarifies the principles of core technologies (GANs, Diffusion Models) and defines the compositional elements of animation character styles as well as style transfer design objectives. Then, it elaborates on specific application paths from three dimensions: data preparation, model selection (CycleGAN, Stable Diffusion), and process implementation. Practical cases validate the technology’ s feasibility and creative efficiency gains. Finally, optimization strategies (e.g., model fine-tuning for precise control, human-machine collaborative copyright compliance) are proposed. This study provides valuable reference for advancing the animation industry toward efficiency and personalization.

title: Development and Implementation of a Digital Twin for Power Plants: Enhancing Maintenance and Operational Efficiency
authors: Guneet Bhatia, Nagarjun, Jason Voelker
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: The advent of Industry 4.0 has ushered in a new era of digital transformation, where the integration of advanced technologies, such as digital twins, plays a pivotal role in optimizing industrial operations. This research paper presents the development and implementation of a comprehensive digital twin for industrial power plants, aimed at enhancing maintenance strategies and improving operational efficiency. The proposed framework includes the design and visualization of the mechanical and operational characteristics of the power plants, seamless data acquisition and integration, and the application of cutting-edge AI algorithms for condition monitoring, anomaly detection, and failure prediction. The digital twin's prescriptive maintenance capabilities leverage optimization techniques and “what-if” scenario analysis to provide actionable recommendations for operational improvements. The effectiveness of the digital twin is demonstrated through case studies in real-world industrial settings, showcasing its ability to accurately detect anomalies, predict imminent failures, and optimize maintenance schedules, ultimately leading to significant cost savings and improved equipment reliability. The findings of this study highlight the transformative potential of digital twin technology in the realm of industrial asset management, paving the way for widespread adoption and adaptation to various types of industrial equipment. The insights gained from this research will serve as a valuable resource for both academia and industry professionals in advancing the field of digital twins and their applications in the context of Industry 4.0.

title: A Predictive Framework for Employment Forecasting Through Sectoral Stock Prediction and Portfolio Optimization
authors: Deekshanya U, Sunitha R., Smrithi Warrier, Sreeja Kochuvila
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: In the modern era of data-driven decision-making, accurately forecasting employment trends is essential for policymakers and businesses. Traditional forecasting approaches often fail to incorporate real-time financial indicators, such as stock market performance, which frequently act as early signals of sector-wise hiring patterns. This study proposes a hybrid framework that integrates portfolio optimization with machine learning–based stock prediction to forecast sectoral employment trends. Using historical stock data from the S&P 100 (2015–2024) and government labor statistics, key financial indicators such as returns, volatility, and Sharpe ratio were extracted and correlated with employment variations using supervised learning techniques. XGBoost regression and Random Forest classification models were employed for stock forecasting, while portfolio construction was guided by Efficient Frontier theory via PyPortfolioOpt. Experimental results indicate a strong association between optimized portfolios and positive employment growth in the IT, Healthcare, and Consumer Goods sectors. The proposed integrated model facilitates real-time forecasting of labor demand using financial signals, offering strategic insights for investors, businesses, and workforce planning agencies.

title: TAGS: 3D Tumor-Adaptive Guidance for SAM
authors: Sirui Li, Linkai Peng, Zheyuan Zhang, Gorkem Durak, Ulas Bagci
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Foundation models (FMs) such as CLIP and SAM have recently shown great promise in image segmentation tasks, yet their adaptation to 3D medical imaging-particularly for pathology detection/segmentation-remains underexplored. A critical challenge arises from the domain gap between natural images and medical volumes: existing FMs, pre-trained on 2D data, struggle to capture 3D anatomical context, limiting their utility in clinical applications like tumor segmentation. To address this, we propose an adaptation framework called TAGS: Tumor Adaptive Guidance for SAM, which unlocks 2D FMs for 3D medical tasks through multi-prompt fusion. By preserving most of the pre-trained weights, our approach enhances SAM's spatial feature extraction using CLIP's semantic insights and anatomy-specific prompts. Extensive experiments on three open-source tumor segmentation datasets prove that our model surpasses the state-of-the-art medical image segmentation models (+46.88% over nnUNet), interactive segmentation frameworks, and other established medical FMs, including SAM-Med2D, SAM-Med3D, SegVol, Universal, 3D-Adapter, and SAM-B (at least +13% over them). This highlights the robustness and adaptability of our proposed framework across diverse medical segmentation tasks. Our code and model are available at: https://github.com/sirileeee/TAGS.

title: SSFM: A Selective Spatial-Frequency Module for Enhancing the Detection Effect of UAVs
authors: Kaixuan Wu, Kunlin Zou, Haitao Zhao, Zirui Guo
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are widely deployed across multiple domains, and unregulated drone operations pose significant risks. This paper addresses the issues of receptive field matching and background interference by proposing the Selective Spatial-Frequency Module (SSFM). Through branch convolutions, dynamic weight selection, and a dual-domain coordination mechanism between spatial and frequency domains, SSFM enhances UAV detection performance. Experimental results demonstrate that SSFM achieves significant improvements over various baseline models.

title: Synergistic Optimization of Smart Grids for Renewable Energy Resilience Under Extreme Weather
authors: Zhe Wang, Da Xie
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: The increasing integration of renewable energy sources, while crucial for the energy transition, poses significant challenges to power system resilience in the face of increasingly frequent and intense extreme weather events. This paper focuses on enhancing the resilience of renewable-dominated power systems against such threats through the enabling capabilities of smart grids. A comprehensive resilience enhancement framework, spanning pre-disaster preparedness, in-disaster response, and post-disaster restoration, is proposed. Central to this framework is a “source-grid-load-storage” collaboration optimization mathematical model, formulated to minimize weighted load shedding during extreme events by leveraging smart grid functionalities such as advanced sensing, predictive analytics, and coordinated control. A case study simulating a typhoon event on a modified IEEE 33-node distribution system is conducted. Results demonstrate that the proposed smart grid strategy dramatically outperforms conventional operation methods, reducing total load loss by 85.7%, increasing the power supply guarantee rate for critical loads to 99.8%, and shortening the average system restoration time by 81.5%. Furthermore, the superior performance of the smart grid scheme across key resilience metrics-including restoration speed, critical load protection, and energy utilization-is quantified via a radar chart analysis. The study concludes that smart grids, through synergistic optimization and active control, are pivotal in transforming power systems from being vulnerable to being resilient against extreme weather.

title: Siamese Network-Based Infrared Dim and Small Object Tracking with Multi-Scale Fusion and Re-Detection Mechanism
authors: Wei Song, Shize Zhang, Mengyao Cui, Yiyun Wang, Kaiyuan Wang, Xiaofeng Lu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Communication (ICAIRC)
abstract: Infrared Dim and Small Object Tracking holds significant application value in fields such as military reconnaissance and UAV surveillance. However, due to characteristics such as limited pixel coverage and weak radiation intensity, traditional tracking methods often struggle to achieve satisfactory performance. To address challenges including low contrast, frequent occlusion, rapid motion, and scarce appearance information in infrared dim and small object tracking, this paper proposes the SiamFR tracker. The proposed approach introduces three key innovations: First, an Infrared Feature Fusion module is designed, integrating a channel attention mechanism and spatial gating technology. The channel attention mechanism highlights critical object features, while spatial gating effectively suppresses background clutter interference. Second, a re-detection mechanism is introduced, which constructs a trajectory memory bank, expands the search area, and matches new trajectories. This enhances tracking performance while ensuring the quality of the tracking trajectory. Finally, a dynamic template update strategy is proposed, which controls update quality based on dual thresholds of confidence and IoU, and employs an adaptive learning rate to balance template stability and adaptability. Experimental results demonstrate that SiamFR achieves improvements in both success rate and precision across four datasets. Notably, it attains a 4% increase in EAO on the VOT2016-TIR dataset, validating the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed method.

title: Improving U-Net Confidence on TEM Image Data with L2-Regularization, Transfer Learning, and Deep Fine-Tuning
authors: Aiden Ochoa, Xinyuan Xu, Xing Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: With ever-increasing data volumes, it is essential to develop automated approaches for identifying nanoscale defects in transmission electron microscopy (TEM) images. However, compared to features in conventional photographs, nanoscale defects in TEM images exhibit far greater variation due to the complex contrast mechanisms and intricate defect structures. These challenges often result in much less labelled data and higher rates of annotation errors, posing significant obstacles to improving machine learning model performance for TEM image analysis. To address these limitations, we examined transferring learning by leveraging large, pre-trained models used for natural images. We demonstrated that by using the pre-trained encoder and L2-regularization, semantically complex features are ignored in favor of simpler, more reliable cues, substantially improving the model performance. However, this improvement cannot be captured by conventional evaluation metrics such as F1-score, which can be skewed by human annotation errors treated as “ground truth”. Instead, we introduced novel evaluation metrics that are independent of the annotation accuracy. Using grain boundary detection in UO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> as a case study, we found that our approach led to a 64% increase in the total number of grains detected, which acts a robust and holistic measure of model performance on the TEM dataset used in this work. Finally, we showed that model self-confidence is only achieved through transfer learning and fine-tuning of very deep layers.

title: MedGuardian: AI-Driven Smart Trolley for Patient Assistance
authors: M. Neela Harish, Asha M, Ashruthi V, Dhivya Mangai G
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: The present invention relates to an AI-powered autonomous smart trolley system, Med-Guardian, designed to support nurses and healthcare staff by automating routine medicine delivery tasks in hospitals and care facilities. The system combines AI-based navigation, patient identification, scheduled dispensing, and optional vitals monitoring to ensure timely, accurate, and contactless medication administration. Med-Guardian is capable of autonomously following nurses during rounds or navigating independently to patient beds based on preloaded schedules. Using QR or RFID scanning, the system verifies patient identity before dispensing medication through a servo-controlled compartmental mechanism. The trolley supports gesture and voice-based interaction for hands-free control and can transmit emergency alerts or health data via a mobile or web-based platform. By reducing the manual burden on healthcare workers, improving accuracy, and enabling real-time responsiveness, this invention significantly enhances operational efficiency and patient safety in clinical environments.

title: Optimization of Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) Solver Performance Using Parallel Computing Techniques
authors: Xipeng Huang
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Information Science, Parallel and Distributed Systems (ISPDS)
abstract: The paper is a detailed study of the art of optimization of performance using advanced parallel computing methods in Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) solver. The paper empirically compares parallelization plans such as domain decomposition plans, hybrid MPI-OpenMp plans, and GPU acceleration on a variety of computing platforms. The performance improvements in benchmarking a three standard test cases, such as lid-driven cavity flow, flow around a circular cylinder, and backward-facing step show significant improvements without numerical accuracy. Optimized parallelization is found to incur improvements of $65.5 \times$ on 128 processors and 89% parallel efficiency at 32 processors. GPU acceleration provides acceleration factors of 16-21 times at diverse complexities of problems with 12-16 times of energy savings. Mixed MPI+OpenMP systems are faster (618x) than pure MPI systems and save 34-41 percent of memory by avoiding data replication. Analysis of algorithms specific to the method shows that multigrid methods achieve higher parallel efficiency, 89.2%, than traditional Krylov methods, 72.5%. Scalability bottleneck is communication overhead that eats up to 34 per cent of total execution time at high processor counts. Extensive numerical verification of preservation of solution accuracy with a maximum relative error less than 0.035% and RMS error less than $2 \times 10^{-7}$, as compared to serial baselines, is demonstrated to indicate that the gains in performance are realized without deterioration of the numerical fidelity. These results would be useful in designing effective CFD solvers that are optimized to run on current heterogeneous computing systems.

title: High-Threshold Precipitation Forecasting Model for Adverse Conditions Based on Improved PredRNN Framework
authors: Zhihao Wen, Yuchao Xia, Yongkang Zhao, Jijing Cai, Bingtao Zhu, Hailin Feng, Thippa Reddy Gadekallu, Kai Fang
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: Accurate forecasting of high-intensity precipitation is crucial for disaster prevention and mitigation. Although deep learning-based radar extrapolation techniques have become mainstream, their performance in practice is challenged by two major bottlenecks. First, data are often corrupted by interference in severe weather conditions. Second, models generally produce blurry predictions for the core of high-intensity precipitation. To address these challenges, this paper proposes the Spatiotemporal Filtering and Multi-Threshold PredRNN (SFMT_RNN) frame-work. This framework is composed of three components: a Spatiotemporal Filtering Module (SFM), the PredRNN network as the predictive backbone, and a Multi-Threshold Joint Supervision (MTJS) module. Specifically, the SFM is an innovative hybrid architecture of U-Net and ConvGRU, designed to repair and enhance the input radar sequence prior to prediction. PredRNN serves as the core predictive network, responsible for learning the spatiotemporal evolution patterns from this enhanced sequence to generate future forecasts. The MTJS is a multi-task learning module that assists the main regression task by establishing parallel classification branches. This forces the model to learn more discriminative feature representations for high-intensity precipitation events, thereby effectively resolving the issue of blurry predictions. Experiments on the large-scale public dataset, CIKM AnalytiCup 2017, demonstrate the superiority of the proposed framework. Compared with the baseline model, SFMT_RNN achieves improvements of 13.61%, 13.81%, and 15.90% on the key metrics of CSI, HSS, and POD, respectively.

title: Analysis of NDVI and NDMI Indices for Remotely Sensed Images for Groundwater Zone Identification
authors: Amit A. Gudadhe, K.T.V. Reddy, Minal A. Dewade, Prashant G. Ghulaxe, Pranita P. Deshmukh
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Aerial photography was a primary method of remote sensing in the past. Aerial photography is used to pinpoint a location, capture, and track changes to the surface of the earth. By analysing photographs, it is also likely to identify deeper soil based on the features of the top loose dirt. Remote sensing has shifted to using sensor technologies based on satellites, since aerial photography has some limitations. As lot of changes has been gone in the environment, early prediction of these changes has become imperious.
