Due to such changes naturally in the environment, effect has been seen in the Groundwater, lakes, forests, borders, irrigation, roads, and buildings. Remotely sensed images can be analysed for various indices for further prediction of the groundwater zones. This paper focus on analysis of NDVI and NDMI indices for remotely sensed images for groundwater zone identification in the Wardha district.

title: CLASP: Adaptive Spectral Clustering for Unsupervised Per-Image Segmentation
authors: Max Curie, Paulo da Costa
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: We introduce CLASP (Clustering via Adaptive Spectral Processing), a lightweight framework for unsu-pervised image segmentation that operates without any labeled data or fine-tuning. CLASP first extracts per-patch features using a self-supervised ViT encoder (DINO); then, it builds an affinity matrix and applies spectral clustering. To avoid manual tuning, we select the segment count automatically with a eigengap-silhouette search, and we sharpen the boundaries with a fully connected DenseCRF. Despite its simplicity and training-free nature, CLASP attains competitive mIoU and pixel-accuracy on COCO-Stuff and ADE20K, matching recent unsupervised baselines. The zero-training design makes CLASP a strong, easily reproducible baseline for large unannotated corpora-especially common in digital advertising and marketing workflows such as brand-safety screening, creative asset curation, and social-media content moderation.

title: Research on Medical Cloud-Edge Collaborative Scheduling Method Based on MADDPG Integrating JIME and MACE
authors: Hongfei Du, Zhiyou Yang, Siying Wang
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: To address the complexity and dynamics of task scheduling in medical cloud-edge collaborative environments, this paper proposes a reinforcement learning scheduling framework based on Multi-Agent Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (MAD-DPG) that incorporates Joint Intrinsic Motivation Exploration (JIME) and Multi-Agent Contrastive Exploration (MACE). The framework enhances resource scheduling efficiency and latency control in continuous state spaces by improving policy exploration capabilities and inter-agent coordination. Specifically, we design a multi-agent scheduling model with a two-stage tandem queue, utilizing JIME to promote diversified exploration among agents and employing MACE to strengthen agent collaboration and credit assignment, effectively mitigating local optima in policy convergence. Experiments conducted in real-world medical task scheduling scenarios compare three algorithms: MADDPG, MADDPG+JIME, and MADDPG+MACE. The results demonstrate that the proposed hybrid mechanism significantly improves task completion rates and resource utilization efficiency while reducing latency violation probabilities, validating the superiority and practical value of our approach for medical cloud-edge collaborative scheduling.

title: A Noise-Robust Deep Learning Framework for Mechanical Fault Diagnosis Based on STFT and Attention Mechanism
authors: Haitao Xu
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: A deep learning framework for mechanical fault diagnosis is proposed, combining Signal Preprocessing and 2D Reconstruction, Temporal Variable Denoise (TVD), and Temporal Attention Fusion Module (TAFM). The STFT-based preprocessing enhances time-frequency feature extraction, while the TVD and TAFM modules improve noise suppression and feature fusion. Experimental results show that the proposed model achieves higher accuracy and robustness than FFT-based and ablated versions, especially under low SNR conditions, demonstrating its effectiveness for intelligent fault diagnosis in noisy industrial environments.

title: Differential Antenna Array Based on Equal Power Division Feed Network
authors: Sheng Yang, Kerui Du, Qinyun Yang, Longqiang Ding, Hongyu Dai, Junfeng Chen, Bian Wu
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: In this paper, a new differential quasi-Yagi antenna array is proposed for 5G dual-band (3300–3600 MHz and 4800–5000 MHz) wireless communication systems. The antenna array adopts four differential power dividers for power feeding, combined with double-layer Wilkinson power dividers and slotted metal ground structure, to achieve efficient differential signal distribution and common-mode noise suppression. Through the C-type open resonant ring (SRR) design, the antenna exhibits good radiation characteristics and frequency selectivity in the dual frequency band. The simulation and measured results show that the differential mode reflection coefficient of the power divider is less than −14 dB, the insertion loss is between 0.9-1.6 dB, the port isolation degree is greater than 27 dB, and the commonmode rejection ratio is more than 28 dB in the 3.3-5 GHz frequency band. The return loss of the antenna array in the target frequency band is less than −10 dB, the gain is between 8.1 and 10.7 dBi, and the common-mode signal is almost fully reflected, showing excellent anti-interference performance. This study provides a compact and superior solution for highfrequency differential antenna systems, which is suitable for 5G base stations, millimeter-wave communication, and MIMO systems.

title: Study on Electricity Price Optimization of Rural New Energy-Charging and Storage Coordinated System Driven by Stackelberg Leader-Follower Game
authors: Lin Shi, Bingkang Li, Zhifei Cai, Jing Hu, Huiru Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy Power and Electrical Technology (CEPET)
abstract: To tackle insufficient absorption of rural distributed new energy and difficult multi-subject interest balance, this paper constructs a non-cooperative Stackelberg Leader-Follower Game model with village-level energy storage station operators as leaders and load aggregators as followers to optimize the electricity price mechanism for rural new energy-charging and storage coordination. It first clarifies the interaction relationships and role positioning of the power grid, village-level energy storage station operators, and load aggregators, and then establishes the objective functions and constraint conditions for the leader and the follower—covering key aspects like electricity price, power balance, and energy storage state of charge. On this basis, it designs mixed-integer linear programming to solve the leader’s pricing model and proposes a dual-mutation differential evolution algorithm to address the follower’s multi-objective operation optimization problem. Finally, real rural scenario case simulations verify that the proposed model and algorithm can simultaneously increase energy storage operators’ revenue, reduce load aggregators’ costs, and improve new energy absorption rate, providing theoretical support and practical reference for electricity price mechanism design in rural new energy-charging-storage coordinated development.

title: Generative AI-Driven Acceleration of Drug Repurposing and Pharmaceutical Development
authors: Sakshi C. Wankhede, Anil M. Pethe
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Generative AI is really shaking things up in drug development and repurposing. Because it helps researchers find and make better therapeutic candidates faster. These AI systems have smart machine learning models that look at tons of biomedical info like molecular structures and clinical trial data. This helps them guess new ways drugs might work and spot new uses for drugs already out there. This AI speed boost cuts down on the time and money it usually takes to create drugs. It also makes things better for finding targets. Plus the design and testing of drugs become easier. Using AI lets you explore chemical possibilities with data, find hidden connections, and deal with things like missing info and complicated biology. As these methods get better, they could create amazing chances for personalized medicine, give more treatment choices, and change how drug companies handle caring for patients and coming up with new ideas.

title: Dynamic Path Pruning-Based Hierarchical Multi-Label Text Classification Model
authors: Wenhao Fu, Yaru Sun, Huacheng Li
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing (MLNLP)
abstract: To address the limitations of current hierarchical multi-label text classification models in processing hierarchical multi-label samples and relationship mining, we introduce a novel hierarchical multi-label text classification model incorporating dynamic path pruning. Our approach significantly improves fine-grained label recognition through innovative dynamic path pruning techniques. The model capitalizes on the comprehensive semantic understanding and robust fault tolerance of large language models to effectively combine hierarchical multi-label structures with domain-specific semantic features, thereby enhancing the representation of relationships between domain texts and their corresponding label systems. Furthermore, we integrate graph neural networks to process encoded information linkages and hierarchical relational patterns, successfully unifying domain knowledge with label dependency relationships within the hierarchical framework. An attention mechanism is implemented to optimize label feature processing. Comprehensive evaluations demonstrate our model's superior performance over state-of-theart alternatives on both WOS and RCV1-V2 datasets, along with significantly improved robustness against data perturbations.

title: MAYA: Multi-Attack Yielding Augmentation for Unified Face Attack Detection
authors: Taehoon Kim, Jongwook Choi, Seungjin Jung, Jongwon Choi
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Face anti-spoofing remains a critical challenge as both physical and digital attack techniques continue to evolve. While prior methods often address either physical or digital threats in isolation, real-world attack scenarios require unified models capable of handling a broad range of spoofing attack strategies. In this work, we propose MAYA (Multi-Attack Yielding Augmentation), a unified face attack detection framework that generates subtype-specific pseudo-attacks during training. MAYA simulates eight distinct attack variants—including both physical (e.g., print, cutouts, replay) and digital (e.g., attribute-edit, face-swap, adversarial attack) threats—based on a fine-grained taxonomy of attack subtypes. To further enhance generalization, we introduce an attack-wise contrastive loss that enforces semantic consistency by aligning the features of samples sharing the same attack. Extensive experiments on the UniAttackData+ benchmark—the official dataset of the ICCV 2025 Face Anti-Spoofing Workshop Challenge—demonstrate the effectiveness of our method. Ablation studies further show that both the attack-wise contrastive loss and subtype-aware pseudo-attacks contribute to improved generalization across diverse attack types.

title: Doc2GraphQA: Document to Knowledge Graph Question Answering System
authors: R. Rajadurai, V. Naveen, M. Sakthivel, R. Asha, E. Naveen Kumar, P. Eshwar
publish: 2025 14th International Conference on System Modeling &amp; Advancement in Research Trends (SMART)
abstract: Doc2GraphQA is fundamentally a single framework that converts the extremely complex, multi-format documents into knowledge graphs that are semantically more meaningful in that you can perform fancy Q&A, aggregation, and predictive reasoning on big LLMs. It consists of three key components: (1) a Document Processor which reads PDFs, converts them to Markdown, disaggregates them into token-aware block, and adds metadata by use of a sequential hierarchical system that causes the LLM to generate summaries, roles, and relations. (2) a Knowledge Graph Builder which extracts entities and relations out of messy or semi-structured data, labels them with metadata like role, confidence, and source, and embeds the nodes in a hybrid symbolic-neural manner, allowing them to be accessed in such a manner. (3) a Retriever and Multi-Agent QA Engine that combines Cypher graph queries with a vector search that allows multi-hop reasoning, summarizing, and predictive inferences by specialized LLM agents. It is made to work with fixed and open-schema documents and is allegedly efficient and clear to analyze documents and solve complex tasks in the form of question and answer.

title: Tensor Program Superoptimization through Cost-Guided Symbolic Program Synthesis
authors: Alexander Brauckmann, Aarsh Chaube, José W. De Souza Magalhães, Elizabeth Polgreen, Michael F. P. O’Boyle
publish: 2026 IEEE/ACM International Symposium on Code Generation and Optimization (CGO)
abstract: Modern tensor compiler frameworks like JAX and PyTorch accelerate numerical programs by compiling or mapping Domain-Specific Language (DSL) code to efficient executables. However, they rely on a fixed set of transformation rules and heuristics, which means they can miss profitable optimization opportunities. This leaves significant optimization potential unused for programs that fall outside these fixed patterns.This paper presents STENSO, a tensor DSL program superoptimizer that discovers such missing rewrites. STENSO’s core is a symbolic program synthesis based search algorithm that systematically explores the space of equivalent programs. By combining symbolic execution and sketch-based program synthesis, it generates equivalent candidate implementations. To make the search computationally tractable, STENSO further integrates a cost model with a branch-and-bound algorithm scheme. This effectively prunes the search space, arriving at optimal solutions in a reasonable time.We evaluate STENSO on over 30 benchmarks. The discovered programs achieve geometric mean speedups of 3.8x over NumPy and 1.6x over state-of-the-art compilers like JAX and PyTorch-Inductor. These results underscore the limitations of heuristic-based compilation and demonstrate STENSO’s effectiveness in finding such optimizations automatically.

title: HelmetDet: An Efficient Multi-Scale Detection Framework for Electric Vehicle Helmet Wearing
authors: Hanchao Huo
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Communication (ICAIRC)
abstract: In urban traffic safety management, whether electric vehicle riders wear helmets directly affects accident protection and personal safety. However, the subtle visual difference between wearing and non-wearing states, along with complex backgrounds and lighting variations, makes accurate detection challenging. To address this, we propose HelmetDet, an efficient helmet-wearing detection framework based on YOLOv12. It integrates a Multi-level Context-Aware Fusion (MCAF) module to combine global and local features, an Adaptive Attention Feature Enhancer (AAFE) to focus on key regions, and an Edge-Guided Small-object Optimizer (EGSO) to improve small-object recognition. Experiments on a helmet-wearing dataset show that HelmetDet achieves 94.2% mAP with high real-time performance, outperforming existing detection models.

title: LLM-Powered Autonomous Security Agents for Next-Generation Cyber Defence
authors: Tanvir H. Sardar, Bishwajeet Pandey, Laura Aldasheva
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: The paper proposes a new system design of rolling out Large Language Model (LLM)-driven autonomous security agents in enterprise cybersecurity. Cyber threats are becoming more complex and security operations centres (SOCs) are being overwhelmed by alerts, which is why traditional Security Orchestration, Automation, and Response (SOAR) systems based on those originally relying on rules are no longer able to cope with new attack patterns. We introduce SENTINEL (Security-Enabled Neural Threat Intelligence and Learning), an architecture, which uses LLMs as the reasoning engine to handle autonomous threat investigation, incident triage, and coordinated autonomous response. Our framework deals with such issues as reliability of agents, resistance to adversarial manipulation, and reasonable limits of human control. A three-environment experimental assessment shows that SENTINEL can be used to classify threats with an accuracy of 94.2% and is able to minimize the mean time to respond (MTTR) by 73-percent and detect 89-percent of new attack measures that could not be detected by traditional systems. We also present a formal verification scheme on how to ensure that agent behavior does not exceed specified safety limits so as to allow reliable autonomous behavior in the production security setting.

title: Dual-Trap Ultra-Wideband Filter Using Stepped Butterfly T-Type Resonator
authors: Mingming Gao, Maoyuan Wei, Hanci Jiang, Ke Li, Xinyue Zhang, Yuanjie Zhang
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: This paper presents an ultra-wideband filter with dual notch bands designed to mitigate interference from national satellite X-band signals in ultra-wideband communication systems. The device employs a defected ground structure (DGS) integrated with a stepped butterfly T-type multi-mode resonator to achieve wideband transmission. Dual stopbands in the 7.42-7.49 GHz and 8.35-8.43 GHz ranges are realized through an E-type resonator and a symmetrical double-concave resonator coupled with a symmetric linked-line structure. Simulation results demonstrate a 105% fractional bandwidth with a 4.16-11.30 GHz passband. Notch centers at 7.46 GHz and 8.40 GHz exhibit >30 dB rejection depths. The compact layout achieves dimensions suitable for miniaturized system applications.

title: Advanced GPS Navigation Systems for Intelligent Transportation: Reliability Analysis and Cost Optimization
authors: Sadiya Naaz, Gotte Ranjith Kumar, Meera Sharma, Mayank Pant
publish: 2025 14th International Conference on System Modeling &amp; Advancement in Research Trends (SMART)
abstract: System performance can be evaluated based on detailed reliability assessment with the use of path tracing method (PTM). The reliability of the system will be obtained from the individual critical components which include the probability of failure, system signature reliability, expected life, and the cost optimization. The Barlow-Proschan index is used to assess how the system behaves under varying states. Reliability assessments that are performed by multiple reliability analysis methods provide a basis for comparison of results to confirm the previous findings and identify weakness in a product or system. Relevant preliminary research into the field of intelligent transportation systems (ITS) includes the evaluation of a navigation system. In the long run, the study supports the development of ITS, and enhances methods for administering traffic by demonstrating the benefits of the system and evaluating the reliability of the system.

title: A Novel Ring Type Quasi-Elliptic Bandstop Filter with Multi-Transmission Zeros and Controllable Equal Ripple Performance
authors: Yi Wang, Peng Chen, Chang Xu, Jian Ma, Xiaolong Wang, Chun-ping Chen, Zhewang Ma
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: This letter presents a bandstop filter (BSF) with novel ring type structure. It consists of two parts to realize high attenuation rate, where, the upper part is a T-shaped structure, while the lower part includes two pairs of reversed coupled lines (CLs) and one transmission line with cascaded connection. By analyzing the performance of the proposed BSF provides the seventh-order quasi-elliptic response, which including eight reflection zeros (RZs) and five transmission zeros (TZs) with equal ripple response in the stopband and passband, respectively. Furthermore, the ripple level of the stopband and passband can be controlled at the same time. For verification, an experimental circuit with a center frequency of 2 GHz was designed, and the measured results were in good agreement with the electromagnetic simulation results.

title: A Motion Planning Method for Tube Plate Quadruped Robot Based on Target-Oriented D* Search
authors: Biying Xu, Yujie Lang, Qianqian Chen, Lanbing Yin, Qiang Li
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Online motion planning for the tube-plate quadruped robot (Spibot) presents significant challenges due to the complex and irregular distribution of tube holes, their large number, and frequent obstructions within the steam generator's tube plate. Existing planning algorithms, when applied to Spibot—with its intricate mechanical structure and high mobility—often result in low planning efficiency, excessive path length, frequent turns, and degraded motion performance. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a hierarchical online motion planning framework based on the Target-oriented D* (TOS-D*) search algorithm, designed to generate high-quality motion strategies for Spibot in real time. At the offline planning stage, a base accessibility evaluation method is developed, and the parent node update process is redefined to enhance computational efficiency. Furthermore, a refined node cost function and a variable search direction mechanism are introduced to optimize path quality and expansion. At the online planning stage, a foot-point planning method is proposed to control robot motion. When encountering previously unknown or obstructed tube holes, the system promptly re-evaluates the accessibility of subsequent base nodes and re-plans the foot-point or overall path accordingly. Experimental results verify that the proposed algorithm achieves superior pathfinding speed and path quality. Compared with the D* Lite algorithm, it reduces path length by 0.06%, turning frequency by 91.31 %, and motion steps by 36.38%. The method demonstrates strong adaptability to various tube plate configurations, effectively satisfying the requirements of online motion planning while ensuring safe and reliable navigation to target positions.

title: SYM3D: Canonicalizing Triplanes via Symmetry for Single-View 3D Learning
authors: Jing Yang, Kyle Fogarty, Fangcheng Zhong, Cengiz Oztireli
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Triplane is an effective 3D representation capable of generating high-quality 3D assets from 2D images by synthesizing rich details from multiple viewing directions. However, it relies heavily on multi-view images with camera annotations to achieve sufficient detail across all perspectives. The limited availability of calibrated multi-view image datasets, especially when compared to single-view images, has left triplane representation significantly under-constrained. In this work, we introduce SYM3D, which harnesses the common reflectional symmetry found in natural and man-made objects to regulate triplane learning. However, symmetry alone does not resolve the ambiguity in each plane's focus within the 3D space without camera annotations. To address this, we incorporate a view-aware spatial attention mechanism that aligns each plane of the triplane with a consistent focus in 3D space. We evaluate SYM3D on the triplane representation across various frameworks (3D-GAN and diffusion), tasks (reconstruction, unconditional generation, and text-to-3D), and datasets, including synthetic (ShapeNet Chairs, Cars, and Airplanes) and real-world data (ABO-Chair). The results demonstrate the effectiveness of integrating symmetry regularization. More details can be found at our project page: https://jingyang2017.github.io/sym3d.github.io/.

title: Digital Prototype of 16-Channel W-Band TR
authors: Ning Yang, Jingwei Zhou, Xiaolin Qi, Guodong Su, Xiao Li, Yuanhao Shan, Li Hong, Jun Liu, Zhiqiang Wang
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: This paper presents a W-band 16-channel T/R circuit model. The circuit model is constructed using physically up-conversion and down-conversion mixers, RF switch circuits, low-noise amplifiers, driver amplifiers, power amplifiers, digitally controlled attenuator, and digitally controlled phase shifter. When compared with measured data, the simulation curves closely match the actual data plots, demonstrating excellent agreement. This validation highlights the model's potential as a valuable tool for advancing research into large-scale, complex networks.

title: UAE’s Public Policy Agenda in the Digital Debates: Insights from Social Networks Analysis
authors: Noura Naqbi, Najia Ketbi, Khalaf Tahat, Dina Tahat, Abdulaziz Altawil, Ahmed Mansoori
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Social Networks Analysis, Management and Security (SNAMS)
abstract: The study aims to analyze the impact of Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed’s Twitter account (@MohamedBinZayed) in shaping digital discussions and directing public agendas across social media, in addition to studying the structure of the digital network and the impact of these discussions on public opinion in the UAE. The study used the social network analysis methodology using the NodeXL Pro tool on a sample of 5,585 nodes and 17,829 edges collected during February 2025. The results showed that @MohamedBinZayed’s digital network is large and has dense and mostly unidirectional interactions, with the Sheikh’s account appearing as a major source of interactions. The digital discussions focus on three main axes: Gulf and Arab relations, international relations, and local issues related to education and community service. By analyzing the hashtags, it was found that the public pays great attention to political, social, and diplomatic issues related to the UAE. Hashtags such as “President of State” and “Mother of the Emirates Medal” were also highlighted, reflecting the UAE’s prominent leadership role in regional and international politics. The study shows how political leadership can use platforms such as Twitter to influence public debates and shape public opinion.

title: Unlocking Python Multithreading Capabilities using OpenMP-Based Programming with OMP4Py
authors: César Piñeiro, Juan C. Pichel
publish: 2026 IEEE/ACM International Symposium on Code Generation and Optimization (CGO)
abstract: Python exhibits inferior performance relative to traditional high performance computing (HPC) languages such as C, C++, and Fortran. This performance gap is largely due to Python’s interpreted nature and the Global Interpreter Lock (GIL), which restricts multithreading efficiency. However, the introduction of a GIL-free variant in the Python interpreter opens the door to more effective exploitation of multithreading parallelism in Python. Based on this important new feature, we introduce OMP4Py with the aim of bringing OpenMP’s familiar directive-based parallelization paradigm to Python. Its dual-runtime architecture design combines the benefits of a pure Python implementation with the performance and low-level capabilities required to maximize efficiency in compute-intensive tasks. In this way, OMP4Py offers both full Python support and the high performance required by HPC workloads.

title: Research on Automated Extraction Methods for External Flow Field Data of Hypersonic Vehicles
authors: Yi Zhe, Jiangting Li, Qian Ma, Baili Duan
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: This paper presents an Automated Zoning Method (AZM) for efficient extraction of external flow field data of hypersonic vehicles. Built upon the Inhomogeneity Zonal Medium Model (IZMM), the proposed approach automatically partitions and samples three-dimensional plasma flow fields of hypersonic vehicles. The sampled data are employed in Physical Optics (PO) to calculate the Radar Cross Section (RCS) of plasma-sheathed vehicles. Comparative results with metallic targets verify the accuracy and reliability of the method. The AZM significantly reduces post-processing time in large-scale flow field simulations, thereby enhancing the efficiency of electromagnetic scattering analysis of hypersonic vehicles.

title: AMS-YOLO: An Improved Object Detection Algorithm for UAV Aerial Images
authors: Yu Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Communication (ICAIRC)
abstract: To address the issues of low detection accuracy and severe missed detection when targets are dense in current UAV aerial images, this paper proposes an improved aerial target detection algorithm, AMS-YOLO, based on YOLOv8n. Firstly, the C2f module is improved using depthwise convolutions and a multibranch structure, which reduces the number of model parameters and computational complexity while enhancing the model's feature extraction capability. Secondly, a multi-scale feature fusion module (MSCA) is designed by leveraging multi-scale dilated depthwise convolutions and integrating a channel attention module. Finally, a dedicated small target detection layer is added based on the original detection layers to improve the detection accuracy of dense small targets. Experiments on the VisDrone2019 dataset show that the proposed AMS-YOLO model achieves increases of 3.1%, 5.4%, and 5.4% in precision, recall, and mean average precision (mAP) respectively compared to YOLOv8n. Additionally, compared with other classical object detection methods such as RetinaNet, it exhibits improvements in both detection accuracy and speed.

title: Research on Transient Current Suppression in VSC-HVDC System for Offshore Wind Power Transmission
authors: Linzhen Fan, Siqi Chen, Yiming Zhang, Kuan Zheng, Qichen Chen, Mingze Gang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy Power and Electrical Technology (CEPET)
abstract: 2 GW-level offshore wind power transmission technology utilizing VSC-HVDC has emerged as a significant solution for future offshore wind power evacuation. This paper establishes an electromagnetic transient model for 2 GW-level offshore wind power VSC-HVDC transmission system, conducts simulations and mechanistic analysis of the system’s transient currents, and proposes suppression measures. The findings indicate that a ground fault on the valve side of the bridge arm reactor constitutes the most severe condition for transient currents, potentially causing the bridge arm current to exceed the withstand capability of the IGBT devices. Appropriately increasing the inductance value of the bridge arm reactor, in conjunction with the installation of current-limiting reactors, can effectively suppress the rate of rise of the bridge arm current during transients, thereby mitigating the transient current. The results of this study can provide valuable references for the engineering of 2 GW -level offshore wind power VSC-HVDC transmission systems.

title: StableGAN: A Hybrid Approach to Mitigate Mode Collapse and Vanishing Gradients
authors: Shubham Das, Anurag De, Gautam Pal, Sahadeb Shit, Arindam Dey, Yogananda Patnaik
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: The Generative Adversarial Networks have been emerging as one of the most powerful tools for image synthesis, also they often face critical training issues and instabilities such as mode collapse and vanishing gradients. These issues lead to convergence and reduce diversity in generated outputs. This work deals with StableGAN, a hybrid approach that integrates Wasserstein GAN with Gradient Penalty (WGAN-GP), improved gradient Penalty Computation, architectural improvements and learning rate scheduling to achieve more suitable training dynamics. Optimisation is done with adaptive Adam configurations, and regularization techniques are used to maintain Lipschitz continuity without excessive weight clipping. Experimental evaluation is performed on the CIFAR-10 dataset, for demonstration of improved quality of images, diversity and convergence speed compared to baseline WGAN-GP Models. Qualitative Matrices, visual samples and training loss curves validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach in mitigating mode collapse and vanishing gradients. This study also highlights the scalability of StableGAN for broad generative tasks while preserving computation efficiency.

title: A General Framework for Manipulators Combining Vision and Force in Feature Space with Barrier Lyapunov Function
authors: Dongrui Wang, Lei Ma, Jianfei Lin, Wenru Lu, Yutao Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: A general framework for integrating visual and force control in feature space is presented in this paper to enhance robotic manipulation in unstructured environments. By incorporating visual and force sensing, the proposed system ensures precise and reliable performance, offering higher safety. The framework seamlessly accommodates scenarios with or without contact forces, improving flexibility and applicability. To optimize transient and steady-state performance, tan-type barrier Lyapunov functions are employed, effectively constraining image feature errors within prescribed boundaries. This approach enhances the dynamic response and stability of the visual servoing system. Experimental validation demonstrates that the proposed algorithm significantly improves transient behavior, operational safety, and task success rates, highlighting its effectiveness in real-world applications.

title: Multi-Layered Security Framework for Financial AI Solutions - PISA
authors: Aruun K. Kumar, George Belsian, Santosh Srinivasan, Giridhar Shyam Sankararaman
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) is rapidly transforming organizations, enabling intelligent customer interactions, automated analysis, and agentic decision workflows. At the same time, GenAI introduces a new class of probabilistic and semantic vulnerabilities like prompt manipulation, hallucinations, sensitive-information leakage, and unauthorized agentic actions, that traditional cybersecurity controls are not designed to mitigate. These risks are particularly acute in financial services, a sector widely recognized as part of national critical infrastructure, where security failures can trigger immediate financial loss, regulatory non-compliance, and systemic instability. This paper introduces PISA, a four-layer security framework designed specifically to protect GenAI applications in financial institutions. PISA integrates prompt-layer defense, information-boundary controls, semantic verification, and agent-authorization safeguards into a unified defense-in-depth model. We present a reference architecture implementing PISA, along with an adversarial evaluation across high-risk financial use cases. The results demonstrate substantial reductions in prompt-injection success, hallucination rates, and unauthorized action attempts compared to baseline GenAI systems. This research provides financial institutions with a practical and sector-aligned methodology for deploying secure, trustworthy, and regulation-aware GenAI systems.

title: Multimodal and Multi-task Fusion for Spatial Reasoning
authors: Van-Minh Dang, Thanh-Sach Le
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have made significant progress, yet they still struggle with fine- grained spatial understanding at the region level, especially in complex environments such as warehouses, due to limited spatial perception and reliance on raw input data. Existing methods often represent region information textually, overburdening the LLM's reasoning and failing to fully exploit depth information for detailed spatial relationships. To address this, we propose an enhanced SpatialRGPT architecture that integrates a novel Region Enhancer module to strengthen interaction and encoding between RGB and depth features at the region level. We also incorporate a specialized Region Classifier to perform direct object classification on specific regions, reducing the load on the LLM and improving accuracy. Our multi-stage training paradigm effectively fine-tunes these new modules while leveraging transfer learning. Experiments on the PhysicalAI Spatial Intelligence Warehouse dataset (AI City Challenge 2025, Track 3) show substantial improvements on spatial reasoning tasks over the baseline. The overall performance consistently improves across training phases, validating the effectiveness of our enhanced region representations, specialized classification module, and the staged training procedure.

title: Prefix-Tuning for Large Language Models Enhanced with Hierarchical Graph Semantics in CI/CD Workflow Generation
authors: Qipeng Yue, Yong Liu, Wenya Fu, Shuai Guo, Yanli Kong, Chengyao Li
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Information Science, Parallel and Distributed Systems (ISPDS)
abstract: In recent years, the automation of continuous integration and deployment in software engineering has been continuously improving. However, the hierarchical complexity of workflows and cross-framework dependencies still limit their efficiency and reliability. Large language models (LLMs) offer new insights into workflow generation, but traditional serialized generation methods struggle to model hierarchical structures and cross-node constraints, often leading to syntactic errors and semantic mismatches. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a hierarchical graph semantic-enhanced prefix finetuning method for workflow generation. First, structural features of workflows are extracted through Syntax Trees (ASTs), forming data representations for fine-tuning. Next, a Transformer is used for semantic mapping and feature encoding, establishing a unified representation of structure and semantics. Finally, a hierarchical graph neural network generates prefix vectors and injects them into the attention layers of a large language model, enabling parameter-efficient, structure-guided generation. Additionally, to avoid sequence bottlenecks and long-dependency attenuation, a hierarchical attention mechanism is employed to enhance the modeling capability of the graph neural network, ensuring structural consistency and semantic integrity throughout the decoding process. Experimental results show that this method effectively alleviates issues of structural and semantic inconsistency, demonstrating its effectiveness and universality in workflow generation tasks.

title: HRIT: A Human-Readable Framework for Phishing URL Detection using Large Language Models
authors: Tanya Yadav, Mohammad Masum
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: Phishing URL detection remains a persistent cybersecurity challenge due to rapidly evolving adversarial tactics and the inherent limitations of rule-based and traditional machine learning approaches. Although Large Language Models offer promising capabilities in contextual reasoning and natural-language explanation, their effective use for URL-based threat detection is constrained by a semantic mismatch between numeric security features and language-model reasoning. This paper introduces HRIT (Human-Readable Indicator Transformation), a lightweight and model-agnostic framework that transforms structured URL security features into semantically meaningful, human-readable indicators optimized for LLM inference. HRIT enables LLMs to reason about phishing intent using interpretable descriptors derived from statistically validated, dataset-driven, and industry-informed thresholds, without requiring model fine-tuning or retraining. We evaluate HRIT on a balanced phishing URL dataset containing 11,430 samples and compare its performance against a strong Random Forest baseline and multiple LLMs under zero-shot prompting. Experimental results show that HRIT improves detection sensitivity, improving recall and F1-score while maintaining low inference cost and latency. These findings demonstrate that effective phishing URL detection can be achieved through semantic feature transformation rather than complex prompting or model adaptation.

title: Multimodal Semantic-Similarity Gate for Real-Time Optimized Deepfake Detection
authors: Yousung Yeon, JunHo Yoon, Chang Choi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: With recent advances in deep learning-based generative artificial intelligence (AI), highly realistic synthetic videos and audio have rapidly proliferated and been increasingly misused, intensifying the need for reliable deepfake detection. Accordingly, growing attention has been given to multimodal AI-based deepfake detection methods. These systems typically analyze the global context of video and audio information using attention mechanisms to detect whether the content is synthetic. However, attention-based multimodal fusion does not distinguish the manipulation status of each modality; therefore, the computational cost increases as the video and audio sequences become longer. Although frame selection can reduce computation, it often causes global structural information loss and omits certain important sequence information. This study proposes a multimodal semantic-similarity gate (MSG)-based deepfake detection framework, which reduces computational cost while preserving the entire video–audio sequence information. The MSG adopts a two-stage backbone to process common sequences of video and audio modalities, comprising a front stage that determines the semantic similarity between modalities and a back stage that controls hard/soft attention. Experimental results show that the proposed MSG maintains an F1-score difference within 0.0001–0.0106 compared with baseline models, while reducing GFLOPs by 10.59–28.07% and per-sample inference latency by 3.35–41.96%. These results demonstrate improved computational efficiency and support real-time deepfake detection.

title: Research on the Bending Performance of a Double-layer Tenon–Mortise Modularized Conductor (TMMC)
authors: Bin Zhao, Jinxing Zheng, Yuan Cheng, Zihan Qian, Chuang Lu, Ming Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: The previously reported Tenon–Mortise Modularized Conductor (TMMC) achieved a critical current (I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</sub>) of 13.69 kA under self-field at 77 K, exhibiting low AC losses. To optimize the bending performance, a double-layer sleeve-assembled TMMC was developed. By adjusting the configuration of the tape stacks, the in-plane bending of the superconducting tapes is reduced, and the flow channels for coolant are increased. A three-dimensional finite element model was established to analyze the strain distribution during the bending process. The influence of the bending radius and the friction coefficient on the average and maximum axial strain of the tapes was analyzed. Furthermore, a 2-meter-long prototype TMMC was fabricated for bending experiments, where the I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</sub> of the outermost superconducting tapes were measured under different bending radii. The results show that after bending from 400 mm to 300 mm, significant I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</sub> degradation was observed in the measured tapes, with the maximum degradation reaching 14.17%. Simulation results reveal that at 300 mm, the peak axial strain in several tapes exceeds the irreversible strain limit of 0.4%, which is consistent with the experimental observations. Consequently, the critical bending radius of prototype TMMC is identified to be between 400 mm and 300 mm, confirming that inclined stacks are more susceptible to strain-induced degradation.

title: Track-Before-Estimate for Low-Elevation Targets in the Presence of Multipath
authors: Hao Zhang, Keyi Li, Yuguan Hou, Gongjian Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: The presence of multipath effect leads to significant errors in elevation angle measurements, resulting in performance degradation in subsequent tracking. In this paper, a track-before estimate method is proposed to eliminate the effect of multipath on low-elevation target tracking under specular reflection conditions when the sea surface is smooth relative to the radar wavelength. Based on the understanding that multipath errors are not random noise but definitive measurements arising from multipath propagation, the monopulse ratio is employed rather than the elevation angle as the measurement in the tracking process to provide Cartesian state estimates. Under the specular reflection assumption, an accurate measurement equation is established according to the geometric relationship between the target and the reflection point to describe the relationship between the monopulse ratio and target height. This enables the transformation from the traditional track-after-estimate strategy to track-before-estimate. Under the circumstances that the re f lection coefficient is unknown and time-varying, the coefficient can be estimated by incorporating it into the state vector, or alternatively, the wave height can be incorporated into the state vector and estimated instead, indirectly providing an estimate of the reflection coefficient. The unscented Kalman filter is employed to extract Cartesian states and the sea surface reflection coefficient from monopulse ratio measurements. To deal with the filter initialization issue that one monopulse ratio measurement may correspond to multiple initial height estimates, a multiple subfilter strategy is proposed based on the static multiple model framework, where each subfilter corresponds to one possible height estimate. The bisection method is adopted to find out all the possible height estimates from multiple height intervals divided according to the transmitted frequency. Numerical simulations are conducted to evaluate the effectiveness and stability of the proposed track-before-estimate method. Benefiting from the effective utilization of the monopulse ratio measurement in the presence of multipath, superior estimation performance is achieved.

title: Writer Identification by Processing Hyperspectral Images using Singular Value Decomposition and Linear Discriminant Analysis
authors: Muhammad Moin Qasim, Saeed Mian Qaisar, Khurram Khurshid, Muhammad Nadeem
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Writer identification is a critical task in forensic document analysis, requiring precise methods to differentiate individual handwriting samples. This study presents a framework leveraging hyperspectral imaging (HSI) for writer identification, employing Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) to reconstruct denoised hyperspectral cubes, which are then classified using Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA) and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN). Optimal SVD components are selected via analysis of variance (ANOVA), and pen-based blind testing is conducted under multiple training–testing ink mixtures. The framework is evaluated on the UWA Writing Ink Hyperspectral Image (WIHSI) dataset (33 bands, 400–720 nm, seven writers, multiple blue and black inks) and the iVision Handwritten Hyperspectral Image Dataset (HHID) (149 bands, 512 × 650 pixels, 54 writers, with five selected blue pens and one sample per pen) across four ink mixing configurations: 4:1, 3:2, 2:3, and 1:4. On the iVision HHID dataset, SVD-LDA achieves near-perfect accuracy of 99.99% for the 4:1, 3:2, and 2:3 configurations, and 97%for the 1:4 configuration, outperforming SVD-CNN. On the UWA WIHSI dataset, with limited training and black ink excluded in the 1:4 configuration, SVD-LDA attains 97% accuracy, while SVD-CNN drops to 86.12% with higher training data, SVD-CNN slightly outperforms SVD-LDA. Overall, SVD-LDA demonstrates stability under limited training, SVD-CNN excels with abundant data, and SVD reconstruction enhances spectral discriminability, especially for noisy datasets like iVision HHID. These results confirm the robustness, effectiveness, and practical applicability of the proposed framework for forensic writer identification.

title: A Framework for Encrypted Traffic Classification with Decoupled yet Aligned Information Representation
authors: Wei Lin, Eric B. Blancaflor
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The extensive deployment of end-to-end encryption renders traditional network traffic analysis methods ineffective, necessitating the development of new advanced deep models with high classification performance. Combination models of CNNs and Transformers have shown efficacy by acquiring both local and global feature extraction capabilities. Existing hybrid architectures often adopt heuristic fusion operations (e.g., direct feature concatenation or hand-crafted cross-branch feature exchange), without explicitly encouraging complementarity between heterogeneous representations, which may result in redundant features and suboptimal performance. This paper introduces DAIR-MTC, a new model for Decoupled but Aligned Information Representation in Multi-Task Classification. DAIR-MTC innovates by splitting the feature space of each branch (1D-CNN and Transformer) into two disjoint subspaces: a shared space and a private space. We introduce a dual information-theoretic goal to structure this representation. First, a contrastive learning loss is employed to enforce alignment of shared features, compelling the two branches to agree on the intrinsic, modality-invariant characteristics of the traffic. Second, a mutual information minimization loss is employed to enforce decoupling of private features, compelling each branch to extract distinctive, complementary information. This ‘‘consensus and specificity’’ framework creates a highly efficient and robust feature representation. Final classification is done on an aggregation of the aligned common features and the diversified private features. Experiments on large scales on benchmark ISCX VPN-nonVPN and CICIDS2017 datasets indicate that DAIR-MTC performs better than the baseline MTC model and numerous other state-of-the-art approaches by a significant margin on a range of classification tasks.

title: SCMF-Net: Sparse Self-Attention Driven Cross-Modal Fusion for Robust Detection in Complex Road Scenes
authors: Yunze He, Yousheng Hao, Mengying Qian, Baoyuan Deng, Lilian Zhang, Liang Cheng, Yaonan Wang
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: This paper introduces SCMF-Net (Sparse Cross-Modal Fusion Network), a lightweight multimodal perception framework designed to enhance representation quality and inference efficiency while minimizing computational overhead. To address the sparsity and irregular distribution of LiDAR point clouds, an intensity-aware depth encoding strategy is proposed to enhance the structural cues in the depth modality. Additionally, a dual-branch backbone is employed to further strengthen feature extraction. Building upon this, FFLSA (Feature Fusion Local Self-Attention) is introduced to enable efficient cross-modal fusion. FFLSA leverages Self-Attention Clustering (SAC) to identify salient cross-modal regions, and Self-Attention Fusion and Purification (SAFP) to refine feature aggregation and reduce redundancy, forming an effective region-selection–refined fusion mechanism. Additionally, a Cross-Modal Feature Fusion Module (FFM) is proposed, which jointly models spatial and channel attention to enable adaptive RGB-depth interaction with fine-grained weighting. Extensive experiments on the KITTI dataset and a custom RGB–LiDAR benchmark under challenging conditions (fog, low light, and overexposed) validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach. SCMF-Net achieves 79.3% mAP on our dataset and 88.3% on KITTI, surpassing current state-of-the-art detection methods.

title: Fuzzy Matching Data Sharing with Equality Test for Internet of Medical Things
authors: Xiaokun Zheng, Yinghui Zhang, Yong Yu, Qiuxia Zhao, Dong Zheng
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: The contemporary healthcare ecosystem is being radically reshaped by the burgeoning deployment of Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) technologies; however, this ubiquity inevitably expands the attack surface regarding data confidentiality and user privacy. To mitigate the risk of sensitive physiological data leakage, this paper introduces a novel cryptographic primitive: Fuzzy Matching Data Sharing with Equality Test (FMDS-ET), optimized for the constraints of IoMT environments. Distinct from traditional approaches, our construction enforces a bilateral fine-grained access control mechanism. Specifically, decryption is authorized if and only if the cardinality of the intersection between the attribute sets and access policies of both the transmitter and the recipient surpasses a predefined threshold. This design not only bolsters the resilience of mutual authentication but also facilitates secure data dissemination and ciphertext equality test. Furthermore, it provides a viable solution for privacy-preserving data classification and record linkage. We provide a formal definition of the system syntax and prove its semantic security in the random oracle model. Comprehensive performance evaluations confirm that the proposed scheme maintains low computational overhead, rendering it highly feasible for resource-constrained medical sensors.

title: Optimizing System Performance for Autonomous Recovery in AI-Native Networks Using Large Language Models
authors: Liming Huang, Yulei Wu, Dimitra Simeonidou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Future networks are expected to be AI-native, where AI is no longer an external tool but an integral element of the network infrastructure, with a complete AI lifecycle management. This lifecycle spans data collection, model training, inference, and evaluation, and must be coordinated alongside network infrastructure and application performance to maintain reliable end-to-end system behavior. However, such deep integration introduces new challenges, particularly in managing performance degradation caused by complex dependencies across lifecycle components in the system level. Traditional optimization methods, which isolate AI components as well as their interactions with networks and applications, fail to provide system-level assurance in AI-native environments. To address this, we propose a system-level performance optimization framework based on Large Language Models (LLMs) as autonomous reasoning agents within an Agentic AI paradigm. Our approach introduces a two-phase learning strategy: (1) fine-tuning the LLM with knowledge from AI solutions, networks, and applications to enable autonomous identification of performance-affecting factors, especially performance bottlenecks; and (2) real-world refinement using interaction-based learning and a reward-driven alignment mechanism that ties LLM decisions to application performance improvements. By embedding embodied intelligence into the network operation, the framework enables adaptive and lifecycle-aware performance recovery. We validate our method on a real-time 3D reconstruction task within an AI-native network, achieving a 94.5% performance recovery rate with minimal correction latency. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of LLM-powered agentic reasoning and adaptation in maintaining system performance and mitigating performance degradation in complex AI-native networking scenarios.

title: Policy-Bound, Verifier-Pluggable Smart Contract Framework for Auditable Healthcare Analytics
authors: Muhammad Saeed Javed, Ali Hennache, Muhammad Imran, Saddam Hussain Abbasi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Healthcare analytics involving sensitive patient data demand robust statistical safeguards alongside verifiable compliance mechanisms that allow regulators to independently reconstruct query operations, applied policies, and reported outcomes without accessing raw medical information.We present a governance-first, upgradeable on-chain framework that anchors complete request lifecycles to public blockchain using keccak256 cryptographic commitments. This approach delineates a process wherein researchers submit structured queries, a controller captures active policy states, and hospitals provide result digests. A comprehensive and immutable event trail for this entire process is maintained on the Ethereum Sepolia blockchain. External auditors can then reconstruct complete timelines and verify consistent binding between requests, policies, and outcomes. The proposed modular verifier interface, currently implemented via configurable MockVerifier, maintains stable Application Binary Interface compatibility for future production verifier integration while validating end-to-end governance. We demonstrate the framework’s practicality through a detailed diabetes prevalence analytics case study. We provide detailed gas consumption profiles, request latency measurements, and observable failure modes that demonstrate how governance rules translate into enforceable reversions. The architecture maintains minimal on-chain state with Layer-2 readiness, offering immediate regulatory accountability while preserving straightforward upgrade paths to advanced cryptographic components like zk verifiers (Groth16), verifiable differential privacy through VRF-seeded randomness, content-addressed artifact storage, and versioned policy management. This approach effectively separates initial deployment feasibility from computationally intensive cryptography while delivering immediately actionable, externally verifiable evidence of policy compliance.

title: A Self-Updating Hybrid Meta-Learning Framework for IoT Traffic Classification
authors: Rishul Arora, A. Anjali, Adarsh Kumar, Vedant Kadam, Om Jee Pandey, Hong-Ning Dai
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Accurate classification of encrypted IoT traffic remains challenging due to evolving applications and distribution shifts. This work presents a self-updating hybrid meta-learning framework that integrates Bayesian Neural Networks (BNN) for uncertainty-aware update triggering with a Random Forest meta-classifier for robust decision fusion. The proposed design improves scalability and interpretability through feature-importance analysis and lightweight ensemble learning. Prediction instability is quantified using the Hellinger distance, avoiding normalization overhead and enabling an adaptive familiarity score via a tunable parameter α. Experimental results on encrypted traffic datasets demonstrate significant gains in reliability, achieving up to 95.7% accuracy and 0.95 macro-F1, and effective selective retraining under distribution shifts.

title: Generation of High-Coverage Traffic Scenarios for Efficient Simulation Testing of Automated Driving Systems_supp1-3641393.pdf
authors: Penghui Li
publish: N/A
abstract: Virtual simulation testing is crucial for ensuring automated vehicles safety, which offers low cost and good repeatability. The key is to test in various virtual driving scenarios, but often fails to strike a balance between scenario coverage and test efficiency. To address this issue, we propose a scenario generation method based on a Genetic Algorithm optimized Hamiltonian Monte Carlo sampling approach. Specifically, a Markov chain is constructed converging to the joint probability density distribution function of scenario parameters. By defining a Hamiltonian function with a potential energy term related to the posterior distribution and a kinetic energy term, the sampling process moves efficiently towards high probability regions and achieves faster convergence to the target distribution. Moreover, Jensen-Shannon divergence between generated samples and raw data is proposed to evaluate the scenario coverage, and used as the objective function in Genetic Algorithm to optimize the algorithm parameters. The proposed method is validated by lead vehicle deceleration scenarios generation, where an S-shaped deceleration model is proposed to parameterize the scenario and 22,343 segments extracted from a naturalistic driving dataset are used to calibrate the scenario parameters. Subsequently, the joint probability distribution density function of scenario parameters fitted by a gaussian mixture model is imported into our proposed method. As the results, 1,649 concrete lead vehicle deceleration scenarios are generated with a coverage of 99.14% for the dataset. Compared with the previous Markov Chain Monte Carlo sampling method, our method achieved 9 times higher coverage with 28 times fewer scenarios.

title: Occupancy Grid Mapping with Uncertain LiDAR Data
authors: Yuguang Liu, Marko Radanovic, Krista A. Ehinger, Kourosh Khoshelham
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: High-definition (HD) maps are essential for autonomous vehicles. A promising approach to automated generation of HD maps is occupancy grid mapping using vehicle-borne light detection and ranging (LiDAR) data. Existing occupancy grid mapping methods do not integrate the LiDAR pose uncertainty caused by signal blockage and multipath effect of Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS) into the geometric inverse sensor model (ISM) update. This results in systematic and random errors in the generated maps. To address the problem, this article presents an asymmetric Gaussian Bell ISM that minimizes the systematic and random errors caused by the pose uncertainty in occupancy grid mapping. Our proposed asymmetric Gaussian Bell ISM is evaluated on both a real indoor LiDAR dataset and a small-scale outdoor LiDAR dataset with two designed evaluation metrics. The results demonstrate that the proposed ISM effectively reduces the systematic error in both environments. Specifically, our ISM achieves the lowest systematic error of 7.5 cm and 5.1 cm for the indoor and outdoor datasets with lidar pose uncertainties of 20 cm and 40 cm, respectively, producing more accurate occupancy grid maps compared to five existing ISMs and indicating its potential for HD map-related applications.

title: Maximizing Sum Rate by Joint Sensing and Communication Scheduling for IRS-Assisted Multi-User Downlink Wireless Communications
authors: Shiyi Gu, Kunyi Xie, Lei Feng, Jiacheng Wang, Dusit Niyato, Celimuge Wu, Yu Zhou, Evangelos K. Markakis, Shahid Mumtaz
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Millimeter-wave (mmWave) technology has emerged as a promising approach to deliver ultra-high data rates for Automated Guided Vehicles (AGVs), thereby supporting critical Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) applications such as path planning. However, its narrow directional beams are prone to blockages and difficult to align with a moving AGV, leading to beam misalignment errors and subsequent capacity degradation. By integrating sensing and communication (ISAC), mmWave can sense and extract environment information and AGV location from echo signals to optimize beamforming, thereby mitigating misalignment effects. However, frequent sensing consumes time resources, reducing data transmission time. Hence this paper proposes a multiple Intelligent Reflecting Surface (IRS)-assisted ISAC framework with a location sensing activation mechanism to balance communication performance and sensing overhead. Within this framework, multiple semi-passive segmented IRSs are deployed to simultaneously support location sensing and data transmission for multiple AGVs by allocating sub-IRS arrays independently. Moreover, we formulate a fairness-aware system rate optimization problem. We then propose an ISAC resource allocation algorithm based on heterogeneous agents proximal policy optimization (HAPPO) to jointly optimize the activation factor for location sensing and the allocation of limited wireless communication resources. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves centimeter-level localization accuracy, delivers superior communication performance with balanced multi-AGV fairness, and operates with low inference latency suitable for real-time industrial ISAC applications.

title: MINNs: MNA Informed Neural Networks for Fast Transient Simulation of Nonlinear Transmission Lines Subject to Parametric Uncertainty
authors: Dyuti Basu, Aakanksha Verma, Avirup Dasgupta, Sourajeet Roy
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: In this paper, a new artificial neural network (ANN) formulation is presented for the transient simulation of nonlinear transmission lines (NLTLs) in the presence of parametric uncertainty. The key attribute of the proposed formulation is that it minimizes a loss function expressed as the sum of the residuals of the modified nodal analysis (MNA) equations and the initial conditions describing NLTLs instead of the classical data-driven loss function. The resultant ANNs are referred to as MNA informed neural networks (MINNs). MINNs exhibit a completely data-free and highly efficient training unlike conventional ANNs that are trained on large datasets extracted from time and memory-intensive SPICE simulations of NLTLs. Furthermore, by virtue of its more fundamental MNA-based training, MINNs are more accurate than conventional ANNs when predicting NLTL responses for parameter values lying outside the training range. In this paper, additional techniques such as dynamic weighting and multistep multi-fidelity training are developed to mitigate the inherently slow convergence of MINNs. Overall, MINNs are demonstrated to enable highly accurate and efficient Monte Carlo driven uncertainty quantification of NLTLs that is not possible using conventional ANNs or brute force SPICE simulations.

title: Edge-Oriented Events-to-Video Reconstruction for High-Speed Scenarios via Knowledge Distillation
authors: Jian Liu, Jingyu Song, Can Gao, Lei Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Event cameras provide high temporal resolution, high dynamic range, and low power consumption, making them ideal for high-speed robotic applications such as autonomous driving and aerial navigation. Events-to-video reconstruction aims to generate dense, photorealistic frames from sparse, asynchronous event streams. However, event data are globally sparse but locally bursty in fast-motion scenarios. Naive processing of such data is prone to noise in inactive regions, while excessive focus on dense bursts overlooks sparse global cues, leading to incomplete structures and degraded textures. Moreover, existing reconstruction methods are generally parameter-heavy and computationally demanding, limiting real-time deployment on resource-constrained edge devices. To address these challenges, we propose a lightweight events-to-video reconstruction framework optimized for edge devices. Specifically, a score-guided sparse adaptation module evaluates local event density to enhance salient regions and improve feature quality under sparse inputs. Additionally, a multi-level knowledge distillation strategy transfers reconstruction capability from a high-capacity teacher network to a compact student network, substantially reducing parameters with only minor compromise in visual fidelity. Implemented on the NVIDIA Jetson Orin NX SUPER, the framework achieves an effective balance between reconstruction accuracy, latency, and resource usage. Extensive experiments on a simulated dataset and four high-speed, high-dynamic-range, real-world datasets demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms existing approaches in both image quality and inference time. The results indicate its potential for real-time event-based vision on resource-constrained edge devices.

title: An Early Conflict Resolution Mechanism for Blockchain-Based Delay-Sensitive IoT Networks
authors: Aditya Pathak, Irfan Al-Anbagi, Howard J. Hamilton
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: Blockchain technology, particularly Hyperledger Fabric (HLF), has emerged as a promising solution to enhance security and privacy in various domains, including Internet of Things (IoT) networks. Conflicting transactions in a HLF-based IoT network occur when multiple transactions attempt to modify the same asset or data concurrently. Conflicting transactions can lead to data inconsistencies, because the network may be unable to determine the correct order or the most preferred valid transaction. Existing conflict resolution mechanisms in HLF-based IoT networks often introduce considerable transaction latency, detect and resolve conflicting transactions in the late stages of the transaction lifecycle (ordering and validation), or require significant changes to the underlying HLF blockchain platform. To overcome these limitations, we propose an Early Conflict Resolution (ECR) mechanism that detects and resolves conflicts during the endorsement stage. The ECR mechanism uses a local cache (Sync.Map) and a dependency graph to efficiently detect conflicts by analyzing the Read-Sets (RS) and Write-Sets (WS) of transactions. ECR resolves conflicts in the detected conflicting transactions through transaction reordering or sequential processing. It also executes non-conflicting transactions in parallel to speed their processing. Our results show that the ECR mechanism improves transaction latency and the success rate for varying conflict rates, block sizes, and IoT devices compared to existing mechanisms.

title: A Smart Ice Monitoring System for Photovoltaic Applications based on Capacitive Graphene Sensors
authors: Luca Tari, Gabriele Cavaliere, Sarah Sibilia, Antonio Maffucci, Luigi Ferrigno
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: This work presents an innovative smart system for monitoring ice formation on photovoltaic systems (PV) based on capacitive sensors made of graphene nanoplatelet (GNP) nanocomposites and a low-cost conditioning circuit. At the heart of the methodological approach is integrating a multi-gap sensor configuration, designed to maximise sensitivity to dielectric variations, and an oscillator circuit that converts capacitance variations into frequency signals, ensuring robust and noise-free transmission even in extreme environments. The sensor design includes selecting innovative materials and an optimised electrode configuration. The metrological characterisation of the system shows a sensitivity down to almost 6 Hz/pF and a resolution of less than about 1.3 pF, confirming the system’s excellent performance in variable environmental conditions and ensuring high accuracy in ice detection. Experimental tests with ice thicknesses of up to 10 mm showed perfect alignment with theoretical models. In addition, data analysis using machine learning techniques produced accurate results in classifying the presence of ice, with an accuracy of over 95% and remarkable robustness even in the presence of noise. The results confirm the system’s high performance and versatility for PV, energy and aerospace applications, proposing a smart and energy-efficient solution for ice management in PV systems.

title: A Novel Multi-layer Task-centric and Data Quality Framework for Autonomous Driving
authors: Yuhan Zhou, Haihua Chen, Kewei Sha
publish: IEEE Internet Computing
abstract: The next-generation autonomous vehicles (AVs), embedded with frequent real-time decision-making, will rely heavily on massive multisource and multimodal data. In real-world settings, the data quality (DQ) of different sources and modalities varies due to unexpected environmental factors or sensor issues. However, both researchers and practitioners in the AV field overwhelmingly concentrate on models/algorithms while undervaluing the DQ. To improve AV’s functionality, efficiency, and trustworthiness, this paper proposes a novel task-centric and data quality vase framework, which consists of five layers: data, DQ, task, application, and goal. The proposed framework aims to map DQ with task requirements and performance goals. This paper opens up a range of critical but unexplored challenges at the intersection of DQ, task orchestration, and performance-oriented system development in AVs. It is expected to guide the AV community toward building more adaptive, explainable, and resilient AVs that respond intelligently to dynamic environments and heterogeneous data streams.

title: Gravity Gradient Field Transformation by Enhanced Physics-Guided Multi-task Network
authors: Yu Zhang, Zhengwei Xu, Minghao Xian, Michael S. Zhdanov, Xuben Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: The transformation of the gravity field into the gravity gradient data is an important topic in geophysical research, playing a key role in mineral boundary delineation and geological structure interpretation. However, traditional wavenumber-domain and spatial-domain transformation methods are often susceptible to noise and boundary effects, thereby limiting the accuracy, stability, and adaptability of the transformed gravity gradient data in complex geological settings. In this study, we introduce a new approach to the solution of this problem based on multi-task learning U-Net deep Neural Network. First, we propose an innovative approach to construct geological density model datasets using probabilistic constraints. Next, we utilize the Physics-Guided Multi-Task (PGMT) Network, a multi-layer CNN module, to enable the transition from single-task to multi-task outputs, capturing interdependencies among gravity gradient components. Finally, we incorporate the Laplace equation into the network architecture as a physical constraint, ensuring the three gravity gradient components adhere to the solid physical relationships. Numerical studies show that this network effectively alleviates noise and boundary effects. The determination coefficient (R²) is above 0.98, significantly reducing the root mean square error (RMSE), thereby greatly improving the prediction accuracy and robustness. After verification using the total tensor gradient gravity data of the Nordkapp Basin in Norway, the average error between the predicted gravity gradient data and the on-site measurement results is less than about 1%. These results demonstrate that the PGMT Network provides high precision and reliability in transforming low-order gravity data into high-order gravity gradient data, confirming its effectiveness for gravity field transformations.

title: Robust Multi-View Extrinsic Calibration with Limited Co-observations via Sparse Graph Reprojection and Target-Driven Loop Closure
authors: Guanhua Chen, Fengdong Chen, Guodong Liu, Yueyue Han, Kejun Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Extrinsic calibration of multi-view camera systems typically uses limited local simultaneous co-observations to achieve global optimization. Conventional approaches face two key shortcomings: when local simultaneous co-observations involve multiple viewpoints, the inability to exploit co-observation relationships leads to modeling gaps and decreased optimization stability; when explicit loop-closure constraints are insufficient, the effectiveness of global optimization is limited, resulting in reduced overall calibration accuracy. To address these issues, this paper presents a sparse graph reprojection-based extrinsic calibration method for multi-camera systems. The method first performs local optimization based on the hand–eye calibration principle, followed by global optimization by integrating a pose graph with bundle adjustment. By uniformly modeling the sparse simultaneous co-observation relationships in local measurements and concurrently establishing reprojection constraints within the sparse pose graph, overall robustness increases. Additionally, a target pair reuse mechanism is introduced to explicitly compensate for missing loop-closure constraints in chain-like configurations, ensuring globally consistent solutions through graph optimization. Simulation experiments evaluated the calibration accuracy and stability of the proposed method for all cameras, while real-world tests assessed its calibration performance for the camera without loop closures, demonstrating its feasibility and effectiveness.

title: Maximum Correntropy Criterion-Based Robust Fuzzy Adaptive Unscented Kalman Filter for Target Tracking with Model Mismatches
authors: Yunyi Yang, Guoguang Wen, Yidi Wang, Yunhe Meng, Tingwen Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper investigates the robust state estimation problem for non-cooperative target tracking to overcome the limitations of conventional tracking algorithms under model uncertainty and noise uncertainty. Although some existing algorithms employ the maximum correntropy criterion (MCC) to handle non-Gaussian noise, most of them generally assume precise state-space models and neglect the effect of uncertain noise parameters as well. To deal with this limitation, a robust fuzzy adaptive MCC-based unscented Kalman filter (UKF) algorithm is proposed, which enhances robustness through the simultaneous state and unknown input estimation (SSUIE), and adjusts the noise covariance via a fuzzy inference system (FIS) adaptively. First, an unknown input (UI) estimator is integrated into the MCC-based UKF to tackle the challenge of model uncertainty, thereby eliminating the reliance on a precise state-space model. Then, an FIS is employed to adjust noise covariance, which explicitly accounts for uncertain noise statistics, improving adaptability in challenging conditions. Notably, the FIS-based adjustment is a more efficient and flexible method compared to the interacting multiple model (IMM) and variational Bayesian (VB) methods, as it avoids the need for precise prior knowledge and high computational complexity. Finally, the simulation results demonstrate that our proposed algorithm can enable effective tracking of non-cooperative targets, even during orbital maneuvers and under uncertain noise conditions. This work provides an adaptive solution for enhancing estimation robustness against uncertain target behaviors and dynamic environments, with potential applications in autonomous systems and security defense systems.

title: Describe Anything Model for Visual Question Answering on Text-Rich Images
authors: Yen-Linh Vu, Dinh-Thang Duong, Truong-Binh Duong, Anh-Khoi Nguyen, Thanh-Huy Nguyen, Le Thien Phuc Nguyen, Jianhua Xing, Xingjian Li, Tianyang Wang, Ulas Bagci, Min Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Recent progress has been made in region-aware vision-language modeling, particularly with the emergence of the Describe Anything Model (DAM). DAM is capable of generating detailed descriptions of any specific image areas or objects without the need for additional localized image-text alignment supervision. We hypothesize that such region-level descriptive capability is beneficial for the task of Visual Question Answering (VQA), especially in challenging scenarios involving images with dense text.
