In such settings, the fine-grained extraction of textual information is crucial to producing correct answers. Motivated by this, we introduce DAM-QA, a framework with a tailored evaluation protocol, developed to investigate and harness the region-aware capabilities from DAM for the text-rich VQA problem that requires reasoning over text-based information within images. DAM-QA incorporates a mechanism that aggregates answers from multiple regional views of image content, enabling more effective identification of evidence that may be tied to text-related elements. Experiments on six VQA benchmarks show that our approach consistently outperforms the baseline DAM, with a notable 7+ point gain on DocVQA. DAM-QA also achieves the best over-all performance among region-aware models with fewer parameters, significantly narrowing the gap with strong generalist VLMs. These results highlight the potential of DAM-like models for text-rich and broader VQA tasks when paired with efficient usage and integration strategies. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/Linvyl/DAM-QA.git.

title: Learning Generalizable Diabetic Retinopathy Grading by Decoupled State Space Decoding
authors: Jingjun Yi, Qi Bi, Hao Zheng, Haolan Zhan, Wei Ji, Huimin Huang, Yuexiang Li, Xian Wu, Yefeng Zheng
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is a leading cause of vision loss. The rapid advancement of deep learning has significantly propelled the development of automated DR grading methods. However, retinal images are typically collected from diverse clinical centers using equipments from various vendors, leading to domain shifts. As a result, the pre-trained model for DR diagnosis has to handle the unseen retinal images when doing inference. In this paper, we propose Decoupled State Space Decoding (DSSD), a selective state space-based model designed to enhance the generalization capability of DR grading methods to unseen domains. Leveraging the selective scan mechanism to encode local patches relevant to lesions and the recurrent modeling to capture long-range dependencies, DSSD aims to mitigate cross-domain style variations through both channel-wise and sample-wise decoupling. Additionally, it introduces an implicit constraint between shallower and deeper state embeddings to ensure stable recurrent modeling for grade-level predictions. Experiments conducted under both leave-one-domain-out and extreme-single-domain-generalization settings demonstrate its state-of-the-art performance.

title: LSSGen: Leveraging Latent Space Scaling in Flow and Diffusion for Efficient Text to Image Generation
authors: Jyun-Ze Tang, Chih-Fan Hsu, Jeng-Lin Li, Ming-Ching Chang, Wei-Chao Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Flow matching and diffusion models have shown impressive results in text-to-image generation, producing photorealistic images through an iterative denoising process. A common strategy to speed up synthesis is to perform early denoising at lower resolutions. However, traditional methods that downscale and upscale in pixel space often introduce artifacts and distortions. These issues arise when the upscaled images are re-encoded into the latent space, leading to degraded final image quality. To address this, we propose Latent Space Scaling Generation (LSSGen), a framework that performs resolution scaling directly in the latent space using a lightweight latent upsampler. Without altering the Transformer or U-Net architecture, LSSGen improves both efficiency and visual quality while supporting flexible multi-resolution generation. Our comprehensive evaluation covering text-image alignment and perceptual quality shows that LSSGen significantly outperforms conventional scaling approaches. When generating 1024<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>images at similar speeds, it achieves up to 246% TOPIQ score improvement. Code is available at: https://github.cominventec-ai-center/LSSGen.

title: SCRAMBLe: Enhancing Multimodal LLM Compositionality with Synthetic Preference Data
authors: Samarth Mishra, Kate Saenko, Venkatesh Saligrama
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Compositionality, or correctly recognizing scenes as compositions of atomic visual concepts, remains difficult for multimodal large language models (MLLMs). Even state of the art MLLMs such as GPT-4o can make mistakes in distinguishing compositions like “dog chasing cat” vs “cat chasing dog”. While on Winoground, a benchmark for measuring such reasoning, MLLMs have made signif-icant progress, they are still far from a human's performance. We show that compositional reasoning in these models can be improved by elucidating such concepts via data, where a model is trained to prefer the correct caption for an image over a close but incorrect one. We introduce SCRAMBLe: Synthetic Compositional Reasoning Augmentation of MLLMs with Binary preference Learning, an approach for preference tuning open-weight MLLMs on synthetic preference data generated in a fully automated manner from existing image-caption data. SCRAMBLe holistically improves these MLLMs' compositional reasoning capabilities which we can see through significant improvements across multiple vision language composition-ality benchmarks, as well as smaller but significant improvements on general question answering tasks. As a sneak peek, SCRAMBLe tuned Molmo-7B model improves on Winoground from 49.5% to 54.8% (best reported to date), while improving by 1% on more general visual question answering tasks. Code for SCRAMBLe along with tuned models and our synthetic training dataset is available at https://github.com/samarth4149/SCRAMBLe.

title: Space-Time Optimisations for Early Fault-Tolerant Quantum Computation
authors: Sanaa Sharma, Prakash Murali
publish: 2026 IEEE/ACM International Symposium on Code Generation and Optimization (CGO)
abstract: Fault-tolerance is the future of quantum computing, ensuring error-corrected quantum computation that can be used for practical applications. Resource requirements for fault-tolerant quantum computing (FTQC) are daunting, and hence, compilation techniques must be designed to ensure resource efficiency. There is a growing need for compilation strategies tailored to the early FTQC regime, which refers to the first generation of fault-tolerant machines operating under stringent resource constraints of fewer physical qubits and limited distillation capacity. Present-day compilation techniques are largely focused on overprovisioning of routing paths and make liberal assumptions regarding the availability of distillation factories. Our work develops compilation techniques that are tailored to the needs of early FTQC systems, including distillation-adaptive qubit layouts and routing techniques. In particular, we show that simple greedy heuristics are extremely effective for this problem, offering significant reduction in the number of qubits compared to prior works. Our techniques offer results with an average overhead of 1.2× in execution time for a 53 % reduction in qubits against the theoretical lower bounds. As the industry develops early FTQC systems with tens to hundreds of logical qubits over the coming years, our work has the potential to be widely useful for optimising program executions.

title: Early and Accessible ADHD Screening: A Design Proposal Integrating the Digital EDAH Test with Computer Vision
authors: Jair Stephano Rodriguez Calla, David Joaquín Niño Suarez, Patricia Daniela Reyes Silva
publish: 2025 Fifth International Conference on Information Systems and Software Technologies (ICI2ST)
abstract: Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) is one of the most common neurodevelopmental disorders in childhood, significantly affecting school performance, emotional self-regulation, and social interaction. Despite its high prevalence, many educational institutions lack accessible tools for early detection. This paper presents a solution proposal based on a web application that integrates an adapted digital behavioral test (EDAH) with a computer vision system for automated analysis of attentional variables. The architecture includes five key components: a child-friendly web interface, a structured backend layer, an intelligent diagnostic module, a relational storage system, and a camera-based visual analysis subsystem.As an initial feasibility test, a set of machine learning models (Random Forest, XGBoost, SVM) will be trained on a balanced dataset, with the expectation of achieving promising performance goals under controlled conditions. These performance expectations, although encouraging, represent an idealized baseline and should be validated in real contexts with more diverse and unbalanced data.The proposal demonstrates potential as a scalable and replicable alternative for school environments with limited resources, enabling broader access to reliable and non-invasive screening processes. Lines of future work are proposed to clinically validate the system and strengthen the capabilities of the visual module.

title: Hoecken-D Hand: A Novel Robotic Hand for Linear Parallel Pinching and Self-Adaptive Grasping
authors: Wentao Guo, Wenzeng Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: This paper presents the Hoecken-D Hand, an underactuated robotic gripper that combines a modified Hoecken linkage with a differential spring mechanism to achieve both linear parallel pinching and a mid-stroke transition to adaptive envelope. The original Hoecken linkage is reconfigured by replacing one member with differential links, preserving straight-line guidance while enabling contact-triggered reconfiguration without additional actuators. A double-parallelogram arrangement maintains fingertip parallelism during conventional pinching, whereas the differential mechanism allows one finger to wrap inward upon encountering an obstacle, improving stability on irregular or thin objects. The mechanism can be driven by a single linear actuator, minimizing complexity and cost; in our prototype, each finger is driven by its own linear actuator for simplicity. We perform kinematic modeling and force analysis to characterize grasp performance, including simulated grasping forces and spring-opening behavior under varying geometric parameters. The design was prototyped using PLA-based 3D printing, achieving a linear pinching span of approximately 200 mm. Preliminary tests demonstrate reliable grasping in both modes across a wide range of object geometries, highlighting the Hoecken-D Hand as a compact, adaptable, and cost-effective solution for manipulation in unstructured environments.

title: Quantum-Inspired Deep Reinforcement Learning for Solving the Traveling Salesman Problem
authors: Qianqian Liu, Jiahong Rui, Yue Ruan
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: The Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP), a well-known NP-hard optimization problem, has been widely studied for decades due to its broad practical applicability. In recent years, deep learning methods have shown promise in solving TSP; however, existing approaches often struggle to extract informative edge features between cities, which limits solution quality. To address this, we propose a Quantum-Inspired Deep Learning (QIDL) model that enhances node representations through quantum-inspired embedding, integrates Euclidean distance into the Transformer to strengthen spatial reasoning, and employs a quantum-inspired pointer network to replace greedy decoding with global optimization search. Experimental results demonstrate that QIDL outperforms traditional pointer networks, achieving faster convergence, improved stability, and a 1.76% reduction in tour length, with strong performance on medium-scale TSP instances. These results highlight the potential of QIDL to open new avenues for quantum-inspired learning in combinatorial optimization.

title: Multimodal AI-Based Robot Autonomous Navigation and Decision Optimization in Complex Dynamic Environments
authors: Gangzheng Zhang, Bocheng Shi, Menglu Xuan, Junlei Chen
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: Traditional single-modal navigation pipelines suffer from illumination variance, occlusions, and poor generalization under highly dynamic scenarios. We propose a four-stage closed-loop framework- “multimodal perception, feature fusion, navigation modeling, decision optimization”-to boost the autonomous mobility of service robots. First, a Multimodal Transformer (MM-Transformer) is designed to align visual, LiDAR, and IMU features through spatio-temporal cross-attention and adaptive weighting. Second, a hybrid “geometric + semantic” dynamic map is updated at 10 Hz while a novel Dynamic-RRT* planner fuses kinodynamic and social constraints. Third, a multi-objective Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) policy is trained to trade off localization accuracy, path efficiency, and energy cost. Extensive experiments on a TurtleBot4 platform are conducted in indoor corridors (high pedestrian density) and outdoor campus roads (mixed traffic). Compared with the baseline LOAM+EKF fusion, our method reduces Absolute Trajectory Error / Relative Pose Error by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5} \boldsymbol{\%} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{3 0} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex>, improves dynamic obstacle avoidance success rate by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5} \boldsymbol{\%} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{2 0} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex>, and yields 20% <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex> higher composite decision score while keeping decision latency below 100 ms. The proposed framework offers a deployable and cost-effective solution for robust robot navigation in real-world dynamic environments.

title: A 0-D Iron-Chromium Flow Battery Model with Parameter Identification: Incorporating Species Crossover and Overpotential Analysis
authors: Yujia Zhang, Qiang Ye
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: Identifying parameters governing the efficiency and capacity evolution of iron-chromium flow batteries (ICFBs) is crucial for performance evaluation and analysis. This study develops a zero-dimensional (0-D) dynamic ICFB model framework based on mass conservation within the mixed ironchromium electrolyte and electrochemical reactions overpotentials. The model explicitly integrates key mechanisms causing electrolyte imbalances: species crossover, the parasitic hydrogen evolution reaction (HER), and oxygen intrusion, providing a comprehensive framework for analyzing capacity fade. Using experimental data, we employ the particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm to identify parameters related to species crossover, reaction overpotentials, and electrolyte state. We characterize the resulting parameter distributions using kernel density estimation and confidence intervals, enabling a quantitative assessment of identification reliability. Global sensitivity analysis reveals the influence of individual parameters on cell voltage, efficiency, and capacity evolution. This analysis highlights the significant sensitivity of electrolyte utilization to Fe ion crossover diffusion coefficients, mainly due to Fe accumulation in the posolyte. This enrichment is intensified by either higher <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{F e}^{2+}$</tex> or lower <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Fe}^{3+}$</tex> diffusivity. Furthermore, we decompose the contributions of different overpotentials at various current densities. The mass transfer overpotential in the positive electrode surges significantly at the end of charging. Conversely, discharge terminates as concentration polarization loss in the negative electrode rises abruptly. At high state-of-charge (SOC), parasitic HER is aggravated by both the increased hydrogen ion concentration and the decreased <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{C r}^{3+}/ \mathrm{C r}^{2 +}$</tex> equilibrium potential in the negolyte.

title: Simulation Verification and Optimized Design of Thermal Runaway Suppression Strategies for Power Battery Modules
authors: Lu Ban, Mingjia Wang, Luying Chen, Yiyong Han, Jingsong Pan, Qicong Nong, Dongqi Qin, Wei Liang
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: With the rapid development of new energy automobile industry, thermal runaway of power battery has become the core problem restricting module safety, and the thermal runaway suppression strategy needs theoretical support urgently. In this paper, LFP48H4G6 prismatic lithium iron phosphate battery module in Ningde era is taken as the research object. Based on ANSYS FLUENT, a thermal runaway simulation model coupled with single equation and NREL four equations is established, and the “phased fusion” strategy is adopted to simulate the reaction at different temperature stages. The simulation of internal short circuit scenario shows that cell 1 heats up first, and the module is completely out of control at 530 seconds (temperature 200.73 - 1108.77°C), forming a chain reaction;Csei, Cne, Ce decrease with temperature rise, Csei=0 (530 seconds) can be used as the core warning threshold. The model and method accurately capture the thermal runaway law, provide theoretical and technical support for the design, optimization and threshold setting of suppression strategies, and have engineering value.

title: Design of a Four-Port MIMO Antenna with High-Isolation for 5G Wireless Communication
authors: Nanyue Li, Yanhong Xu, Jingtao Zeng
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: A wideband MIMO antenna with high isolation for 5G n77 (3.7-4.2GHz), n79 (4.4-5.0GHz), and n256 (5.0-5.9GHz) is proposed. The antenna is formed by rotating a basic slot antenna counterclockwise by 90°, 180°, and 270°, respectively. Initially, the principle of current cancellation is utilized to realize high isolation. In the sequel, a rectangular ring and a cross-shaped structure are loaded in the center of the four slot elements to further enhance the isolation of the four-port MIMO antenna. Simulated results show that the proposed antenna achieves an isolation of better than 23dB and operates at 3.62-6.00GHz. Moreover, it exhibits stable gain, and excellent total efficiency (>80%). It should be emphasized that all the radiation patterns presented in this letter have been virtually undistorted, which is beneficial for practical applications utilizing MIMO technology.

title: A Low-Profile Ultra-Wideband Dual-Polarization Base Station Antenna for UHF
authors: Wei-Xin Yang, Zhi-Hong Tu, Jin-Jin Lin, Zi-Qi Feng, Fu-Chang Chen
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: A ±45° dual-polarized ultra-wideband base station antenna with stable radiation pattern characteristics is proposed in this paper. The antenna consists of two dipole pairs, a balun, four square resonators, parasitic patches, and a ground plane. The two dipole pairs are cross-arranged to achieve ± 45° polarization. Bending the dipole arms reduces the overall antenna size. Four square resonators are placed around the dipole arms to introduce new resonance modes, thereby expanding the antenna's operating bandwidth. The parasitic patches above the dipole will further enhances the operating bandwidth while, together with the bottom box-shaped ground plane, helps maintain stable radiation patterns. The proposed antenna operates over a bandwidth of 295–775 MHz(90%), maintaining stable gain and half-power beamwidth throughout the entire operating range.

title: Machine Learning Integration in Loan Decision Systems: Enhancing Kafka-Based Workflows with Predictive Analytics
authors: Jigar Mahendrabhai Solanki
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: Financial technology requires low-latency, high-throughput systems to make decisions for mission-critical applications such as dynamic credit scoring and fraud detection. The Apache Kafka streaming infrastructure can handle throughputs up to one million events per second with a latency of five milliseconds, by powering Event-Driven Architectures. This architectural challenge arises because Machine Learning models are much more accurate than traditional statistical methods. Python and require remote interface models are usually trained in a polyglot environment via REST or gRPC microservices. So, making the final transaction latency higher than the authorized time of fifty milliseconds. Such as, this polyglot bottleneck can be partially alleviated by natively deploying models inside the Kafka Streams Java Virtual Machine, as described in the hybrid architecture here. Use the Open Neural Network Exchange Runtime Java API because it allows safe inference execution within the same process. This significantly reduces inter-process communication and network delays, reducing model runtime from milliseconds to microseconds and bringing the total credit decision delay in steady-state scenarios well within very stringent financial Service Level Agreements. Therefore, the use of the Kafka Streams Processor API implementation for control and performance monitoring, which is proven to be strong and fast, is well-positioned for the future of highly automated lending.

title: The Design of Ku-Band Kilowatt-Level High-Efficiency Traveling Wave Tube
authors: Wenyu Li, Wenjie Yu, Yan Shen
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: This paper presents the design of a Ku-band, kilowatt-level, high-efficiency traveling-wave tube (TWT). With the development of next-generation electronic technology, the demand for higher power continues grows quickly. They must provide unprecedented efficiency, power, and reliability to meet new functions. The proposed design targets an output power of 1000 W with an overall efficiency of 50%. Critical components, including the electron gun, slow-wave structure, and multi-stage depressed collector, are carefully designed and optimized. Numerical simulations performed with MTSS and Opera validate the expected performance. Furthermore, thermal analysis under various temperature conditions demonstrates that the TWT maintains stable operation with 1000 W heat dissipation, thereby fulfilling the requirements of next generation applications.

title: Study on Electromagnetic Field of Autonomous Electric Vehicle Under Different Operating Conditions
authors: Rongxia Duan, Nan Li, Tingwei Jia, Zhuoqiao Ji
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: This paper illustrates the study on electromagnetic field of autonomous electric vehicle under different operating conditions. By means of actual measurement utilizing seven Nadar ELT-400 magnetic field exposure level meters, the magnetic field inside the autonomous electric vehicle under different operating conditions is achieved and compared. The reported results are helpful for establishing electromagnetic compatibility standards on autonomous electric vehicles.

title: A Robust Model Predictive Control Framework for Perturbed Discrete-Time Input-Affine Nonlinear Systems
authors: Jiali Xu, Yonghua Jiang, Siyu Liu
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: In this paper, we propose a robust model predictive control framework-disturbance-observer-based homothetic tube model predictive control (D-HTMPC)-for perturbed discrete-time input-affine nonlinear systems to mitigate disturbances and enhance robustness. Firstly, a discrete-time nonlinear disturbance observer is designed to estimate the disturbances. Then, a compensation mechanism is developed based on the space decomposition method and estimation information to compensate for the matched component of the disturbance. Finally, the homothetic tube model predictive control optimization problem is formulated by transforming the system into a set-valued form and designing appropriate constraints to obtain an optimal control input for handling the remaining disturbances. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is demonstrated through simulations.

title: Iterative Binary Training
authors: Vitor da Silva, Rosana Tomás, Fernando Cores, Francesc Giné
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: In this work, we present Iterative Binary Training, an effective training strategy designed to improve face anti-spoofing systems, especially when dealing with imbalanced datasets. Instead of treating all spoofing attacks at once, our method starts by training a binary classifier to distinguish bonafide faces from the most frequent spoofing type. Then, step by step, it adds new spoofing classes—moving from the most common to the rarest— and thus, the model gradually learns to handle a wider variety of attacks. This method encourages the model to first focus on dominant spoofing patterns and later adapt to more challenging, less frequent attacks, reducing overfitting and improving generalization. We tested it using four deep learning models, including ViT-B/16, ViT-B/32, ResNeXt-101, and ResNet-50. All showed good performance with our method, with ResNeXt-101 standing out as the top performer. Our approach does not rely on extra data, additional modalities, or ensembling techniques. Instead, it builds on standard tools like class-balanced loss functions and pretrained backbones, making it easy to reproduce and deploy. The results suggest that Iterative Binary Training offers a promising direction for enhancing FAS systems in real-world scenarios.

title: Vision Transformer-based OCT Retinal Disease Classification
authors: Haiyu Qin, Yang Wang
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Information Science, Parallel and Distributed Systems (ISPDS)
abstract: Manual interpretation of OCT images requires extensive expertise and is time-consuming, limiting clinical accessibility. This paper presents a novel automated approach for OCT retinal disease classification using Vision Transformer (ViT) enhanced with Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM). Our method leverages transformer self-attention to capture long range dependencies while incorporating spatial and channel attention for improved feature representation. We evaluate our approach on the OCT2017 dataset with 84,484 images across four categories: CNV, DME, Drusen, and Normal retina. The proposed ViT-CBAM model achieves superior performance with 97.8% accuracy, outperforming state-of-the-art CNN and baseline transformer models. Ablation studies demonstrate CBAM integration provides 2.3% accuracy improvement. The model exhibits excellent computational efficiency, making it suitable for real-time clinical deployment. Beyond accuracy improvements, the proposed pipeline maintains reproducibility with clearly defined preprocessing, stratified validation split, and open benchmark protocols, and offers strong interpretability through attention map analysis, which highlights clinically meaningful regions and may facilitate decision support and triage in high-volume ophthalmic screening in real-world workflows and resource-constrained settings globally.

title: Research on High-Power 48 V to 12 V DC-DC Converters for UPS Systems in Renewable Energy Power Station
authors: Zongshang Li, Yongqi Guo, Yong Chang, Ce Wang, Jiaxuan Shen, Lin Wang
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: To address the demand for powering critical <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2 V}$</tex> loads in Uninterruptible Power Supply (UPS) systems within renewable energy power stations, this paper investigates a high-efficiency, high-reliability 5kW high-power 48V to 12V DC-DC converter. Through comparative analysis of non-isolated topologies, a two-phase interleaved parallel buck topology is selected as the core structure to reduce output current ripple and component stress. This paper elaborates on the parameter calculation, component selection, application of synchronous rectification technology, and the PLECS-based simulation modeling process. It focuses on analyzing the key role of parallel MOSFET structures in reducing conduction losses and improving efficiency, along with thermal design and economic analysis.

title: Application Validation of 28-bit Programmable Data Strobe Parallel-to-Serial Converter
authors: Duan wei
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: In order to validate the application ability of Parallel-to-Serial Converter in different harsh environments. This paper proposes a full-function verification scheme for one kind of Parallel-to-Serial Converter, carries out the hardware design of the verification board, and finally verifies the application ability of a Converter device through 9 categories of project tests such as electrical performance, working stability, thermal and mechanical environment. The test results demonstrate the validation effectiveness.

title: A Compact Co-Circularly Polarized Millimeter-Wave Antenna with Dual C-Shaped Resonators Decoupling for In-Band Full-Duplex Systems
authors: Yaqi Liu, Yukai Xu, Zixu Weng, Di Wu
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: This paper presents a compact co-circularly polarized millimeter-wave antenna for in-band full-duplex systems. The design significantly improves isolation between the transmitting (TX) and receiving (RX) ports by incorporating two C-shaped resonators between the antenna elements, which maintain a center-to-center spacing of only a quarter-wavelength. The two C-shaped resonators are implemented by metallized vias and traces, which can resonate when the TX antenna is excited, thereby effectively suppressing coupling interference to the RX antenna. A prototype with overall dimensions of 0.74λ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf> × 0.29λ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf> × 0.07λ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf> has been designed and evaluated. Simulation results demonstrate that the port isolation exceeds 25 dB across the 28.05-29.05 GHz band, reaching a maximum value of 73 dB.

title: A Strategy-Based Approach for Bipedal Locomotion Without Prescribed Contact Sequences
authors: Yansong Bai, Junlei Zhu, Jingwei Peng, Shengzhen Huang, Jiahao Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Humanoid robots are faced with high dimensional and complex contact problems during motion, which makes realtime control challenging and limits the robot's capability. This paper proposes a strategy based control to realize real-time control and there is no need to give time sequence of contact. By removing the inequality constraint of the contact force in whole body control and using iteration process inspired by the concept of active set, we achieve adjustment of the contact modes by optimization. Indirect control of contact breaking and recovery through specifying high-level tasks enables a variety of motions that require contact mode changes, and for example, jumping is considered as the control of angular momentum and velocity of center of mass, while walking is regarded as an event-triggered oscillation. Simulations show that jumping and walking can be realized with our proposed simple strategies, and that humanlike walking is achieved without dedicated strategy design that imitates human.

title: The Escalator Problem: Identifying Implicit Motion Blindness in AI for Accessibility
authors: Xiantao Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) hold immense promise as assistive technologies for the blind and visually impaired (BVI) community. However, we identify a critical failure mode that undermines their trustworthiness in real-world applications. We introduce the Escalator Problem—the inability of state-of-the-art models to perceive an escalator's direction of travel—as a canonical example of a deeper limitation we term Implicit Motion Blindness. This blindness stems from the dominant frame-sampling paradigm in video understanding, which, by treating videos as discrete sequences of static images, fundamentally struggles to perceive continuous, low-signal motion. As a position paper, our contribution is not a new model but rather to: (I) formally articulate this blind spot, (II) analyze its implications for user trust, and (III) issue a call to action. We advocate for a paradigm shift from purely semantic recognition towards robust physical perception and urge the development of new, human-centered benchmarks that prioritize safety, reliability, and the genuine needs of users in dynamic environments.

title: Optimizing Customer Engagement through IoT Data Integration in CRM Ecosystems
authors: Sathish Kumar Velayudam
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: This research aims to illustrate what actually takes place with the data that is collected from IoT devices as it flows into Customer Relationship Management (CRM) systems, showing how this integration transforms the way businesses reach and engage customers. Traditional CRM systems lean too heavily on historical transaction data and static demographic attributes, limiting their ability to interpret real-time behavioral context. Integrating live streams – such as usage traces, performance variations, and device-level error logs – closes this latency gap by enabling immediate, context-aware business actions. Using a synthetically generated dataset of 455 smart-home user transactions, this study demonstrates how dense, high-velocity IoT data can be used to reveal behavioral patterns that remain invisible in conventional CRM environments. Although the technical pipeline is straightforward - data preprocessing using Python; the structured SQL queries, and Tableau visualizations mapping device activity to customer engagement – the data itself is rich, containing signals of genuine user behavior. Real-time data enables both informed decision-making as well as hyper-personalization. Empirical results show higher retention, lower churn and faster support resolution when CRM systems respond the moment an IoT signal is generated – whether due to malfunction, usage anomalies, or performance degradation. The objective extends beyond automation for its own sake; the focus is on optimal timing— engaging customers precisely when intervention is most valuable. The key finding demonstrates that when IoT and CRM operate synergistically, they create a feedback loop capable of generating value at increasing speed. Data velocity translates into enhanced trust, relevance, and ultimately customer loyalty. This research illustrates how customer engagement scales effectively in continuously connected environments.

title: A Fusion Approach of Wavelet Denoising and Mutual Verification for Improving Sampled Signal Reliability
authors: Yelin Zhuo, Zihao Zhang, Quan Xu, Tiantian Cai, Yukai Chen, Shijie Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy Power and Electrical Technology (CEPET)
abstract: Noise interference and abnormal sampling values exist in power system control device signal sampling. This paper proposed a method to enhance device data acquisition reliability. The method combines improved wavelet threshold denoising and a cross-verification mechanism. It first adopts an improved threshold function to overcome the discontinuity and constant bias of traditional functions. The method then introduces a crossverification mechanism. This mechanism validates and fuses denoised signals while balancing accuracy and efficiency. Simulation results showed that the improved threshold function effectively filtered noise while preserving signal features. The cross-verification mechanism significantly improved fused signal quality. Compared to single-channel denoised signals, the fused signal achieved a $\mathbf{5 \%}$ - $\mathbf{1 8 \%}$ SNR increase and a $\mathbf{9 \%}$-38% RMSE reduction. The method effectively enhances power system control device data acquisition reliability.

title: SOCot: Find Shorter Optimal Chain of Thought Using DPO Training
authors: Kaiyi Wang, Chen Dong, Feiran Yuan
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing (MLNLP)
abstract: Reasoning is a fundamental ability of large language models (LLMs), and many studies have focused on enhancing the model's reasoning ability by generating long chain of thought (CoT). However, the longer intermediate reasoning steps result in longer output sequences, which increases the computational cost of the inference and limits the model's applicability in delay-sensitive practical scenarios. In this paper, we propose SOCoT, a reinforcement learning (RL) optimization method based on direct preference optimization (DPO) for training LLM to reduce the length of CoT without affecting its reasoning ability. SOCoT uses on-policy sampling to obtain pairwise training data and provides real-time preferences based on output length and correctness. Penalties are also imposed for incorrect output. We conduct sufficient experiments to evaluate the effectiveness and efficiency of SOCoT and results show it outperforms the existing state-of-the-art methods. In addition, SOCoT can be applied directly to any LLM and reduces the output length while maintaining model accuracy.

title: Lightweight Humanoid Locomotion with Minimal Sensing: IRSE X1's Design and Reinforcement Learning Control
authors: Yuanpeng Wang, Shilong Sun, Haodong Huang, Chiyao Li, Yihui Li
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: This paper presents IRSE X1, a lightweight humanoid robot designed for robust blind locomotion using only proprioceptive sensing. IRSE X1 stands 1.2 meters tall, weighs 12 kilograms, and features a minimalistic design combining aluminum sheet metal, machined parts, and 3D-printed components. It utilizes an inertial measurement unit (IMU) as its sole sensor, eliminating reliance on exteroceptive devices such as LiDAR or depth cameras. Locomotion policies are trained via reinforcement learning in Isaac Gym and transferred to MuJoCo for high-fidelity validation, achieving stable walking across challenging terrains, including slopes, stairs, and uneven ground. The robot's electrical and mechanical architecture prioritizes simplicity, cost-effectiveness, and maintainability, with all components (except the power board) sourced off-the-shelf. Experimental results demonstrate that IRSE X1 achieves reliable blind walking while significantly lowering weight, system complexity, and development barriers compared to conventional humanoid platforms. This work highlights a pathway toward affordable, robust, and adaptable humanoid robots for diverse real-world environments.

title: Economic Impact of AI Adoption in Educational SMEs: A Sectoral Analysis of EdTech Implementation in Emerging Markets
authors: Shweta Sabu, Amruta Mahalle
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Educational institutions in emerging markets encounter substantial challenges when adopting artificial intelligence technologies, primarily due to resource constraints, inadequate infrastructure, and context-specific pedagogical requirements. This research examines the economic implications of AI adoption within small and medium educational enterprises (SMEs) across developing economies, with particular emphasis on educational technology applications and their financial sustainability. This study addresses four critical questions: First, what are the investment requirements for educational SMEs implementing AI-powered learning platforms and administrative systems? Second, how do financial returns vary across different educational AI applications, including personalized learning systems, intelligent tutoring platforms, and automated assessment tools? Third, which specific educational AI technologies demonstrate the most favorable economic outcomes for resource-constrained institutions? Fourth, what sector-specific barriers hinder successful AI adoption among schools, training centers, and educational service providers? Employing secondary data analysis and a comprehensive review of educational technology literature, this research examines <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A I$</tex> adoption patterns specifically within educational contexts. The study analyzes investment flows, return trajectories, and implementation challenges using established frameworks for educational technology assessment, while considering critical factors including student outcomes, teacher productivity, and institutional efficiency. This research contributes valuable insights for educational administrators, EdTech developers, and policymakers engaged in developing educational technology frameworks for emerging markets, thereby providing evidence-based guidance for strategic AI investment decisions.

title: A Plug-and-Play Approach for Robust Image Editing in Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
authors: Hyunwook Jo, Jiseung Maeng, Jun Hyung Park, Namhyuk Ahn, In Kyu Park
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: With the advancement of diffusion models, a wide range of image editing techniques have also been developed. To support these, various inversion methods have been introduced to preserve the original content. However, these inversion methods often exhibit instability, often failing to reconstruct certain images, particularly when applied to high-resolution diffusion models equipped with deep U-Nets. To address this issue, we propose a novel plug-and-play RLI (Residual Linear Interpolation) method. During the forward process, the method operates within the self-attention mechanism and performs an interpolation between the attention values before and after the computation. This interpolation mitigates abrupt changes in the attention map, thereby enabling smoother transitions in spatial representations and reducing unintended distortions of the original content. Our method is compatible with various existing diffusion model variants, inversion techniques, and image editing approaches. In particular, it provides a significant solution to the reconstruction failure observed when using Null-text Inversion with SDXL, where the null-text optimization does not converge properly. In addition, we demonstrate that, when combined with diverse inversion methods and image editing methods across multiple diffusion models, our approach achieves superior preservation of the original content, both quantitatively and qualitatively, without compromising the existing editing performance. The code is available at https://github.com/ugiugi0823/ICCVW-RLI

title: Image Servo Control of Ultrasound Scanning Robot for Hemorrhage Examination
authors: Ziqi Zhou, Weihao Ma, Jinhang Li, Pak Kin Wong, Peng Zhang, Liang Yao, Baoliang Zhao, Yuanyuan Yang, Ying Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Ultrasound-guided therapy has advantages such as real-time performance, safety, and no radiation in the treatment of trauma like visceral bleeding. However, the image quality of the target area is often affected by factors such as tissue interference and probe posture, which in turn limits the accuracy of positioning and treatment. To improve the quality of image acquisition and the efficiency of target area recognition,this paper proposes a master-slave ultrasound scanning robot system. The robot can complete the preliminary positioning of the lesion and pose adjustment based on image feedback. The system integrates an image quality assessment algorithm for vascular areas, achieving real-time scoring to assist in the recognition of potential targets. In terms of control strategy, by integrating image scoring and force feedback information, a closed-loop exploration controller based on Bayesian optimization is proposed, enabling the robotic arm to obtain lesion trajectories within a limited sampling range and significantly enhancing the visibility and coverage efficiency of the target area. This method provides key support for subsequent high-precision trauma identification and treatment, and has a promising clinical application prospect.

title: An Ultra-Wideband Switched-Capacitor Circulator with Clock Boosting Technique Based on a 55 nm CMOS Process
authors: Guang Zhu, Yuxuan Zhu, Yuanrui Li, Bingjun Tang, Li Geng
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: Based on an eight-way switched-capacitor network architecture, a clock-boosting linearity enhancement technique is proposed for the circulator design. The proposed circulator is implemented in a 55 nm CMOS process, occupying an area of 1 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, including pads. Simulation results demonstrate that the circulator achieves isolation >18 dB, insertion losses (ILs) ranging from 3.7 dB to 4.8 dB, and matching <-12 dB over an ultra-wideband operating frequency range from DC to 1 GHz. By incorporating the clock-boosting technique, the circulator exhibits an input 1dB compression point (IP<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1dB</inf>) exceeding 10.86 dBm with a power consumption of 104 mW.

title: AI-Driven Data Architecture: Building Intelligent Analytics Platforms with Azure and Python
authors: Suresh Pairu Subramanyam
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: An energy-focused company undergoing a transformation of its digital core developed out of necessity for them to depart from standard post-mortem Business Intelligence. This paper presents an AI-driven data architecture as the enabling tool for intelligent analytics in the Oil & Gas Industry. The framework is built on the scalability of Azure's end-to-end pipeline, combined with the flexibility of the Python Data Science Ecosystem to enable predictive and prescriptive analytics. It includes a detailed description of the multi-layered Medallion Architecture for Data Ingestion, Data Cleansing, and Data Consumption, and addresses some of the critical issues regarding data fragmentation, quality, and trust. The document will show the utility of this framework by providing a review of the data-driven applications that provide the greatest opportunity for positive impact when implemented using AI/ML; specifically, these applications are Predictive Maintenance, Operations Optimization, and Reservoir Characterization. Additionally, this document introduces strategic mitigation techniques for problems related to Data Drift and other critical Data-related issues through the use of Transfer Learning and MLOps. Thus, this document should be used as a reference for implementing AI technologies and to build a cultural foundation for developing a robust data-driven culture that will ensure the long-term viability and sustainability of intelligent energy systems.

title: Decoding Neurodevelopmental Signatures of Autism from EEG Using Deep Learning
authors: Abhyuday Venkatesh, Yashvi Kumar, Maninder Kumar, Unnatti Khariwal, Harmanjot Kaur, Simranjit Kaur, Sachin Kansal
publish: 2025 1st IEEE Uttar Pradesh Section Women in Engineering International Conference on Electrical Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPWIECON)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is a complex neurodevelopmental condition where scalable biomarkers remain limited. Electroencephalography (EEG), with its high temporal resolution and portability, offers a promising foundation for computational analysis. This work benchmarks deep and handcrafted EEG pipelines on paediatric data from 34 children, using strict leave-one-subject-out (LOSO) validation to prevent data leakage. While many models achieved inflated epoch- level accuracies of 80-90%, subject-level performance dropped sharply, often nearing chance. Supervised Transformers reached 60-78% accuracy across sites but underperformed Random Forests, which paired with spectral and entropy features achieved the most consistent subject-level accuracy 71%. Self-supervised pretraining partially closed the transformer gap, improving calibration but not surpassing RF. These findings highlight the importance of rigorous validation and suggest that physiologically informed, interpretable features may currently offer more robust support for ASD assessment than purely deep models, while also clarifying both the potential and current limitations of EEG- based pipelines.

title: Development of an Attentive Listening Robot Using Human-Human Conversation Data Weighted by Interaction Scores
authors: Haruka Oshiyama, Takahiro Shibasaki, Yutaka Nakamura, Yuya Okadome
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Although nodding is one of the fundamental functions in human-human communication, appropriate nodding for fluent interaction with a communication robot is not achieved. If data from human-human interactions can be directly used to predict the decision of when to nod, it could enhance the development of more natural conversational robot systems. In this study, we have developed an attentive listening robot system based on a surrogate dataset with synchrony levels during conversations. We use gathered human-human interaction data, referred to as the surrogate dataset, as a substitute for human-robot interaction data to predict the robot's nodding behavior. In the sampling process of surrogate data, we consider the level of synchrony calculated in human-human interactions by a self-supervised learning approach. We applied our proposed approach to the task of attentive listening and participants subjectively evaluate systems. The results indicated that participants tend to evaluate a system with a lower synchrony level more favorably than one with a higher score.

title: Automated Classification of Moisture Content in Wood Chips Using UWB Radio Transmission Signals
authors: Patrik Ottosson, T. Sunil Kumar, Daniel Ranta, Niclas Björsell, Daniel Rönnow
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: Accurately identifying moisture content in wood chips is crucial for optimizing energy production. This paper presents an automated classification approach using ultrawideband (UWB) radio transmission data. We collect data from 1,923 samples across four power plants and extract seven key features based on zero-crossings and amplitude information. To enhance classification performance, we apply Chi-square feature selection to identify the most significant features. These selected features are then fed into a classifier to determine moisture content levels. Our approach achieves a classification accuracy of 85.26%, demonstrating the effectiveness of the extracted features and the proposed methodology.

title: One-Dimensional Particle-Field Simulation Code for Single-Extractor Klystrons Optimization
authors: Duo. Li, Zijing Zhang, Yucheng Liu, Weihao Liu
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: Output efficiency of a klystron is strongly determined by the beam bunching quality, and the size optimize of the extractor. In this work, an automated optimization framework for single-extractor S-band klystrons is developed using the original one-dimensional particle-field interaction code. The Particle Swarm Optimization algorithm maximizes output power by tuning cavity gap width, external quality factor, resonant frequency, and position, while energy-conservation and divergence-coefficient criteria ensure physically valid solutions. Simulations show that under small beam currents, optimized output efficiency (~70%) consistent with 3D particle-in-cell (PIC) simulation results. For kA current operation, the framework provides reliable operational ranges, with PIC simulations yielding ~50% efficiency. The method efficiently guides cavity design, offering a robust and physically consistent tool for further multi-cavity optimization of klystrons.

title: A Keypoint-Based Method for Precise Object Size Measurement Using Improved YOLO11-Pose
authors: Yihuan Zhao, Jun Jin, Xiaodong Wang, Fei Ye, Ziyin Li
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: This study proposes an enhanced RT-YOLO model based on the improved YOLO11-Pose framework. Traditional methods such as manual inspection and conventional machine vision suffer from low efficiency and poor environmental robustness. While the baseline model YOLO11-Pose excels in keypoint detection and size measurement, it still faces limitations including high computational demands and insufficient accuracy for small object detection. To address these issues, we implement two core improvements: First, we replace the original backbone network with the RCS-OSA structure to achieve model lightweighting, reducing both parameter count and computational load. Second, we adopt the TADDH to enhance feature extraction capabilities through task decoupling and dynamic routing mechanisms, particularly improving small object keypoint localization accuracy. Experimental results on a self-built industrial dataset demonstrate that RT-YOLO achieves 10.07% reduction in parameters and 7.46% reduction in computational cost, while delivering 88.1% mAP50 and 49.4% mAP50-95 for keypoint detection—outperforming the baseline model. Visual analysis reveals RT-YOLO's superior adaptability and robustness in complex scenarios, effectively balancing real-time performance and accuracy, providing a viable solution for intelligent industrial dimensional measurement.

title: Large-Scale Constellation on-Orbit Software Update Protocol
authors: Xinyue Hou
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Communication (ICAIRC)
abstract: On-board software distribution is pivotal for sustaining satellite system functionality but faces three core challenges: inherently constrained bandwidth in satellite communications severely limits data transmission efficiency; long propagation distances and complex signal processing induce substantial latency, disrupting interactive transmission; and coordinating satellites with diverse operational states causes significant synchronization issues, endangering constellation-wide data consistency. To tackle these problems, this paper proposes a multistage fountain code broadcasting mechanism fully implemented at the application layer, which optimizes satellite data delivery efficiency by leveraging the rateless transmission and redundant symbol generation properties of fountain codes. Experimental results confirm its effectiveness: it achieves high bandwidth utilization by minimizing redundant data overhead, ensures complete software integrity via built-in verification, and avoids modifying underlying protocols-greatly enhancing compatibility with existing satellite systems-thus providing a practical solution to alleviate bottlenecks in satellite software distribution.

title: AIM 2025 Challenge on Robust Offline Video Super-Resolution: Dataset, Methods and Results
authors: Nikolai Karetin, Ivan Molodetskikh, Dmitry Vatolin, Radu Timofte, Yixin Yang, Junyang Chen, Jiangxin Dong, Jinshan Pan, Zhihao Liu, Lishen Qu, Shihao Zhou, Jufeng Yang, Yuxuan Jiang, Siyue Teng, Chengxi Zeng, Fan Zhang, David Bull, Qi Tang, Jie Liu, Jie Tang, Gangshan Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: This paper presents the AIM 2025 Challenge on Robust Offline Video Super-Resolution, the first challenge focusing on 4× upscaling of heavily degraded 270p videos to high-quality 1080p sequences. The challenge addresses the practical problem of enhancing low-quality video content while suppressing noise, blur, and compression artifacts under realistic hardware constraints. We introduce a comprehensive benchmark consisting of 30 diverse video clips spanning camera-shot and animated content, along with a novel synthetic degradation pipeline that ensures reproducible results. Our evaluation methodology employs subjective pairwise comparisons conducted through crowdsourcing. The challenge attracted significant participation and established new baselines for robust video super-resolution in challenging real-world scenarios.

title: GXMalDetect: A Hybrid GA–XGBoost Architecture for Malware Detection Using Static Image Features
authors: Abdul Kareem Uddin Mohd, Md Habibur Rahman, Md Wahidur Rahman, Avdesh Mishra, Tarek Mahmud, Maleq Khan
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: Malware is increasing in both volume and sophistication, and evasion strategies such as polymorphism, packing, and obfuscation reduce the effectiveness of signature-based detection. To enable accurate yet deployable malware classification, this paper proposes a lightweight hybrid framework that integrates Genetic Algorithm (GA)-based feature selection with an Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) classifier, aiming to reduce computational overhead while improving interpretability. Experiments are conducted on the Malimg benchmark, which contains 9,339 grayscale malware images from 25 families. We first extract a compact set of handcrafted static image features capturing statistical descriptors, texture/GLCM relationships, and morphological (shape) properties. The dataset is split using stratified 80:20 sampling to preserve class distributions, and GA optimization is performed only on the training split to avoid data leakage. Using cross-validated performance with a sparsity-aware fitness objective, GA converges to nine informative features spanning statistical (variance, energy, entropy, skewness, kurtosis), texture/GLCM (dissimilarity, homogeneity, correlation), and shape (compactness) descriptors. Trained on this subset, XGBoost achieves 99.30% test accuracy, 99.32% precision, 99.30% recall, 99.30% F1-score, and an MCC of 0.9918. ROC and precision– recall analyses further indicate near-perfect class separability. These results show that carefully selected handcrafted features coupled with gradient boosting can match or exceed heavier deep learning pipelines, providing a practical, fast, and interpretable solution for real-world malware classification.

title: Vibration and Noise Mechanism Analysis and Optimization of Synchronous Reluctance Motors
authors: Chaofan Liu, Aiyuan Wang
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: With the continuous development in the field of electric machines, the Synchronous Reluctance Motor (SynRM) has recently emerged as a research hotspot due to its advantages such as absence of rare-earth permanent magnets, no rotor winding copper losses, simple structure, and high cost-effectiveness. However, the discontinuous variation of reluctance caused by its multi-layer flux barrier rotor leads to significant torque ripple and vibration noise issues, which considerably restrict its competitiveness and widespread adoption in drive applications. Given the direct impact of electromagnetic design and rotor topology on noise and vibration characteristics, this paper focuses on the structural optimization of the SynRM. Through rotor topology refinement, the vibration and noise are effectively mitigated to a certain extent, providing theoretical support for enhancing the overall performance and operational reliability of the motor.

title: Fuzzy Method for Cluster Tomato Picking with a Robot
authors: YiFang Chen, Qin Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: In the context of intelligent agricultural production, cluster-picking robots for cluster tomatoes have become a research hotspot due to their high efficiency and non-destructive operation. However, factors such as unstructured field environments, individual differences among fruit clusters, and the stochastic distribution of plants severely restrict improvements in picking efficiency. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a fuzzy picking method specifically designed for cluster tomatoes. Leveraging the botanical growth characteristics of the crop, the method first estimates the spatial orientation of each cluster by analyzing the geometric relationship between the regions of interest (ROIs) of the peduncle and the fruits. Based on this orientation, an optimal picking pose is pre-computed and employed to accomplish clamping and cutting tasks for peduncles exhibiting a wide range of natural postures within a predefined workspace. Subsequently, auxiliary picking points are introduced to reduce the sensitivity to positional error of the primary picking point, thereby ensuring reliable cutting performance across diverse cluster attitudes. Extensive field experiments conducted under natural greenhouse conditions validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach. Experimental results demonstrate that the fuzzy picking strategy eliminates the need for acquiring high-precision pose estimates of picking targets, reducing image-processing latency significantly. By automatically selecting the end-effector's picking pose according to the cluster orientation, unnecessary pose adjustments during the picking cycle are avoided, lowering the risk of mechanical interference and increasing overall efficiency. The system achieved an effective picking success rate of 99.1%, with an average cycle time of 7.98 s from fruit recognition to completion of clamping and cutting. Compared with state-of-the-art cluster tomato harvesting technologies, the success rate improved by 14% and the picking time was shortened by 46%, satisfying the real-time requirements of robotic harvesting and substantially increasing operational throughput. The presented work provides a robust theoretical foundation and practical technical support for high-efficiency, continuous-fruit harvesting in complex, unstructured environments.

title: FreqCross: A Multi-Modal Frequency-Spatial Fusion Network for Robust Detection of Stable Diffusion 3.5 Generated Images
authors: Guang Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: The rapid advancement of diffusion models, particularly Stable Diffusion 3.5, has enabled the generation of highly photorealistic synthetic images that pose significant challenges to existing detection methods. This paper presents FreqCross, a novel multi-modal fusion network that combines spatial RGB features, frequency domain artifacts, and radial energy distribution patterns to achieve robust detection of AI-generated images. Our approach leverages a three-branch architecture: (1) a ResNet-18 backbone for spatial feature extraction, (2) a lightweight CNN for processing 2D FFT magnitude spectra, and (3) a multi-layer perceptron for analyzing radial energy profiles. We introduce a novel radial energy distribution analysis that captures characteristic frequency artifacts inherent in diffusion-generated images, and fuse it with spatial and spectral cues via simple feature concatenation followed by a compact classification head. Extensive experiments on a dataset of 10,000 paired real (MS-COCO) and synthetic (Stable Diffusion 3.5) images demonstrate that FreqCross achieves 97.8% accuracy, outperforming state-of-the-art baselines by 5.2%. The frequency analysis further reveals that synthetic images exhibit distinct spectral signatures in the 0.1-0.4 normalised frequency range, providing theoretical foundation for our approach. Code and pre-trained models are publicly available to facilitate reproducible research.

title: Is JPEG AI Going to Change Image Forensics?
authors: Edoardo Daniele Cannas, Sara Mandelli, Nataša Popović, Ayman Alkhateeb, Alessandro Gnutti, Paolo Bestagini, Stefano Tubaro
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: In this paper, we investigate the counter-forensic effects of the new JPEG AI standard based on neural image compression, focusing on two critical areas: synthetic image detection and image splicing localization. Neural image compression leverages advanced neural network algorithms to achieve higher compression rates while maintaining image quality. However, it introduces artifacts that closely resemble those generated by image synthesis techniques and image splicing pipelines, complicating the work of researchers when discriminating pristine from manipulated content. We comprehensively analyze JPEG AI's counter-forensic effects through extensive experiments on several state-of-the-art detectors and datasets. Our results demonstrate a reduction in the performance of leading forensic detectors when analyzing content processed through JPEG AI. By exposing the vulnerabilities of the available forensic tools we aim to raise the urgent need for multimedia forensics researchers to include JPEG AI images in their experimental setups and develop robust forensic techniques to distinguish between neural compression artifacts and actual manipulations.

title: Dynamic Temporal Gating Networks for Cross-Modal Valence-Arousal Estimation
authors: Yubeen Lee, Sangeun Lee, Chaewon Park, Junyeop Cha, Eunil Park
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Valence-arousal estimation in multimodal emotion recognition struggles to capture dynamic emotional transitions and maintain temporal coherence across audio-visual modalities. To address this challenge, we introduce TAGF, a Time-aware Gated Fusion framework for multimodal emotion recognition. The TAGF adaptively modulates recursive attention outputs by learning temporal importance patterns through a BiLSTM-based gating mechanism. This approach enables the selective integration of cross-modal features across multiple recursive steps, emphasizing emotionally salient moments while suppressing temporal noise. By incorporating temporal awareness into the recursive fusion process, TAGF effectively captures the sequential evolution of emotional expressions and the complex interplay between the modalities. Experimental results on the Aff-Wild2 dataset demonstrate that TAGF achieves competitive performance compared to existing recursive attention-based models in the 9th Affective Behavior Analysis in-the-Wild Competition. Furthermore, TAGF exhibits superior robustness under cross-modal misalignment conditions and maintains a stable inference performance in real-world scenarios characterized by frequent dynamic emotional transitions. The code is available at https://github.com/leeyubin10/9th-ABAW.git.

title: HMVAE: Brain-Inspired Hierarchical Memory for Detail-Aware VAE Reconstruction
authors: Lei Xu, Kaiyuan Liu, Bin Xie, Ji-Song Guan, Hong Xie
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: As a form of deep generative model, the Variational Autoencoder (VAE) exhibits strong performance in image reconstruction and generation. However, VAEs often struggle to capture top-down image details, leading to blurry output. Drawing inspiration from hierarchical information processing in the human brain memory, we propose a Hierarchical Memory-augmented VAE (HMVAE), which integrates an external memory module into the decoder to enhance detail preservation. We evaluated HMVAE on MNIST, Fashion-MNIST, CIFAR-10, and CelebA (64×64) in reconstruction tasks. Compared to baselines for VAE and MEMVAE, HMVAE consistently achieves lower FID scores and demonstrates improved robustness under noisy inputs. Furthermore, visualization of memory slot activations reveals interpretable input-adaptive attention to different image regions, supporting the effectiveness of our hierarchical memory design. These findings highlight the potential of biologically inspired memory mechanisms to improve detail modeling in VAE-based generative frameworks.

title: Emergent Cooperative Pursuit-Evasion Strategies for USV Swarms via Memory-Augmented Reinforcement Learning
authors: Han Feng, Haokun Zhu, Yiyun Zhao, Fanbiao Li, Weihua Gui
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: The cooperative pursuit-evasion game is a critical benchmark for the collaborative intelligence of Unmanned Surface Vehicle (USV) swarms. However, conventional deep reinforcement learning methods often struggle with the partial observability of the maritime environment and fail to produce sophisticated, emergent cooperative tactics. To address these challenges, we propose a memory-augmented multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) framework based on the Multi-Agent Proximal Policy Optimization (MAPPO) algorithm. First, we integrate a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network to enable agents to process temporal information, overcoming the limitations of short-sighted decision-making in partially observable settings. Second, we introduce a novel sector-based encirclement reward function designed to explicitly incentivize the swarm to form strategic, multi-directional envelopments rather than simple myopic pursuit. Comprehensive simulation studies demonstrate that our proposed framework significantly outperforms standard MARL baselines in both task success rate and capture efficiency. Crucially, the trained swarm exhibits advanced emergent strategies, such as adaptive flanking and dynamic encirclement, offering a robust and effective solution for the multi-agent pursuit-evasion problem.

title: Parkinson's Disease Detection From Voice Features Using CNN-LSTM with LIME-Based Explainability
authors: A Hidayath Abdulla, Baiju B V, Nived Krishna Mullanarambath, Johan Muttapally Jose, Sudeep Sunil, Adithya Manoj Nair
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Parkinson's Disease (PD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder that has a major impact on a patient's day-to-day life. An early diagnosis of the disease still remains a big challenge, as the symptoms of this disease overlap with other neurological conditions. In this project, a robust, explainable system for PD detection using voice recordings is proposed in this study. This method integrates traditional machine learning models with a deep learning structure in the form of a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and Long Short-Term Memory network (LSTM). The UCI Parkinson's dataset which is available in the public domain was preprocessed and normalized for training. Out of the models tested, the highest classification accuracy was achieved by CNN-LSTM hybrid, performing better than all the other classifiers. To ensure model transparency, Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME) was employed, providing insights into feature-level contributions for predictions. This collaboration between deep learning and interpretable AI could be the start of a promising path ahead for a valid and interpretable PD diagnosis that bridges accuracy and clinical trustworthiness.

title: Modeling and Experimental Verification of an Inflated Tube Robot with External Shaping Joint
authors: Wei Gong, Kun Guo, Haibo Gao, Jian Chen, Tianyi Cheng, Baolin Tian, Haitao Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: A novel inflated robot was designed for exploration in narrow and long spaces. The robot merges the stiffness of rigid robots with the flexibility of soft robots, showing great potential for various applications. To address the significant modeling challenges arising from discontinuous curvature deformations in rigid-flexible coupled robots, we have developed a kinematic model that integrates the piecewise constant curvature framework with Timoshenko beam theory, while explicitly accounting for the pronounced pressure follower force effects. Then, the complex mappings from the driven space to the task space was established via a linear constitutive law. Finally, a multi-sensor fusion framework was designed for parameter identification and model verification. Experimental results show that the robot exhibits large-curvature deformation and growth capability, with the proposed model showing strong agreement with measured data. This work provides important technical support for force control and broad applications of rigid-flexible coupled robots.

title: Bionic Compliant Design and Flying Compliant Control of a BioCM-based CMM End-Effector
authors: Ruizhou Wang, Binghao Wang, Hua Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: The measurement accuracy of complex curved-surface workpieces depends on the compliant performance of the structure and controller of coordinate measuring machines (CMMs). A bionic compliant mechanism (BioCM) and flying compliant controller are proposed for the CMM end-effector in this paper. A kangaroo-inspired compliant mechanism was designed to improve the magnification. A flea-inspired flexure hinge was added to decrease the stress. The static and dynamic properties of the BioCM were analyzed. The compliant controller was designed for the BioCM and piezoelectric actuator (PEA). The machine-electrical model of the PEA - BioCM was established. Equipped with capacitive sensors and force sensors inside the BioCM, a dual-closed-loop compliant controller of the PEA-BioCM was developed. The BioCM was fabricated and tested. The proposed bionic compliant structure and flying compliant control of a BioCM-based end-effector contribute to developing high-accuracy CMMs.

title: Oculus: Hierarchical Face Spoof Detection via Frequency-Enhanced Vision Transformers with Group-Aware Classification and Post-Fusion Attention ICCV Proceedings
authors: Vincent Whannou de Dravo, Kiran Raja, Mohammed Bouzidi, Limamou Gueye, Jon Yngve Hardeberg
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: This paper presents Oculus, our submission to the 6th Face Anti-Spoofing Challenge on Unified Physical-Digital Attacks Detection (ICCV Workshop 2025), where we propose a novel Hierarchical Vision Transformer (ViT)-based architecture for face anti-spoofing. Our method jointly addresses binary live/spoof classification, attack type identification, attack group prediction in a unified hierarchical framework. The proposed architecture leverages a ViT backbone fused with a frequency-domain branch, enhanced by a Central Difference Convolution (CDC) frontend and a Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) residual block to capture subtle spoofing cues. The model first predicts the coarse attack group before conditionally classifying the specific attack type within the predicted group. In parallel, a binary live/spoof decision is also produced, enabling the model to benefit from hierarchical supervision. Input images might be preprocessed via MTCNN face detection and then use FFT frequency-transformed. The final fused features are regularized using dropout and optimized with binary cross-entropy and softmax losses in a multi-task setting. On the challenge validation set, our Hierarchical ViT architecture achieved an ACER under 7%, while ResNet-based variants achieved ACERs below 5%. Our best model reported an official ACER of 20.14% on the competition leaderboard. These results confirm the effectiveness of our multi-branch, hierarchical ViT framework for robust face anti-spoofing under both physical and digital attack scenarios. Our training setting is available at https://github.com/de20ce/oculus.

title: ATR-FTIR Spectroscopy Integrated with Machine Learning: A New Method for Classification of Two Similar Gynecological Disorders
authors: Imon Mitra, Abhijit Saha, Pratip Chakraborty, Sunita Sharma, Koel Chaudhury
publish: 2025 1st IEEE Uttar Pradesh Section Women in Engineering International Conference on Electrical Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPWIECON)
abstract: Adenomyosis and endometriosis are two similar benign uterine diseases exhibiting symptoms of chronic pain, heavy menstrual bleeding and infertility. Both these conditions are common in the Indian population and affect the quality of life in women considerably. Adenomyosis remains under-explored as compared to endometriosis; yet this condition is associated with poor fertility outcome. We hypothesize that Fourier transform infrared (FTIR) spectral peaks corresponding to the biochemical signatures of adenomyosis and endometriosis may be used to generate a robust classification model for distinguishing between the two diseases. Herein, we have applied multivariate statistical methods and machine learning algorithms on FTIR spectroscopic data generated from endometrial tissue of adenomyosis, endometriosis along with controls. The supervised multivariate model, principal component analysis - linear discriminant analysis (PCA-LDA) showed good separation between the groups. Further, Support Vector Machine (SVM) demonstrated the best performance and outperformed other models in discriminating between adenomyosis and endometriosis with 90.3% accuracy and area under the curve (AUC) value of 0.96.

title: Enhancing Identity Preservation in Portrait Generation via Reward Optimization
authors: Yang Liu, Hongyu Zang, Chao Xu, Baigui Sun, Shan Luo
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Recent advancements in tailored image synthesis have high-lighted the remarkable capabilities of pre-trained text-to-image frameworks in encapsulating individual identity traits from a collection of portrait photographs. However, these solutions may not accurately reflect the key characteristics of the input, leading to a loss of essential identity traits. To alleviate this issue, our study introduces a new framework for personalized portrait generation. This framework leverages reward optimization to refine the generation process, integrating a face recognition model into the reward function.
