It assesses the similarity between user-provided images and synthetic portraits to determine rewards. We utilize a pathwise estimator for gradient estimation, employing the Gumbel-Softmax technique to fulfill the differentiability requirement and incorporating a KL divergence regularizer to mitigate the risk of overfitting on reward. Our showcases indicate a marked improvement in preserving human identity in the generated portraits.

title: Efficient Depth- and Spatially-Varying Image Simulation for Defocus Deblur
authors: Xinge Yang, Chuong Nguyen, Wenbin Wang, Kaizhang Kang, Wolfgang Heidrich, Xiaoxing Li
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Modern cameras with large apertures often suffer from a shallow depth of field, resulting in blurry images of objects outside the focal plane. This limitation is particularly problematic for fixed-focus cameras, such as those used in smart glasses, where adding autofocus mechanisms is challenging due to form factor and power constraints. Due to unmatched optical aberrations and defocus properties unique to each camera system, deep learning models trained on existing open-source datasets often face domain gaps and do not perform well in real-world settings. In this paper, we propose an efficient and scalable dataset synthesis approach that does not rely on fine-tuning with real-world data. Our method simultaneously models depth-dependent defocus and spatially varying optical aberrations, addressing both computational complexity and the scarcity of highquality RGB-D datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that a network trained on our low resolution synthetic images generalizes effectively to high resolution (12MP) real-world images across diverse scenes.

title: ViT-FIQA: Assessing Face Image Quality using Vision Transformers
authors: Andrea Atzori, Fadi Boutros, Naser Damer
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Face Image Quality Assessment (FIQA) aims to predict the utility of a face image for face recognition (FR) systems. State-of-the-art FIQA methods mainly rely on convolutional neural networks (CNNs), leaving the potential of Vision Transformer (ViT) architectures underexplored. This work proposes ViT-FIQA, a novel approach that extends standard ViT backbones, originally optimized for FR, through a learnable quality token designed to predict a scalar utility score for any given face image. The learnable quality token is concatenated with the standard image patch tokens, and the whole sequence is processed via global self-attention by the ViT encoders to aggregate contextual information across all patches. At the output of the backbone, ViT-FIQA branches into two heads: (1) the patch tokens are passed through a fully connected layer to learn discriminative face representations via a margin-penalty softmax loss, and (2) the quality token is fed into a regression head to learn to predict the face sample's utility. Extensive experiments on challenging benchmarks and several FR models, including both CNN- and ViT-based architectures, demonstrate that ViT-FIQA consistently achieves top-tier performance. These results underscore the effectiveness of transformer-based architectures in modeling face image utility and highlight the potential of ViTs as a scalable foundation for future FIQA research https://cutt.ly/irHlzXUC.

title: EF-YOLOv11: An Accurate Ceramic Tile Defect Detector via Edge-Guided Context and Adaptive Feature Fusion
authors: Guisheng Zhang, Ting Wang, Hongxiao Gan, Yan Jiang
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: The ceramic manufacturing industry has created significant challenges for automated quality control, and manual testing has become harder to meet modern production needs. Small defects can cause large economic losses, so robust automated defect detection is crucial for keeping product quality stable. But traditional algorithms have limits, especially when finding small and multi-scale defects. To fix these problems, this study introduces EF-YOLOv11, an improved version of the YOLOv11 model. The model has two main parts: a large-core edge fusion module that uses global context to guide local edge feature extraction, which helps detect defects of different sizes; and a multi-scale adaptive fusion network that links the high-resolution P2 layer with an adaptive weighting system to keep key details for small defect detection. When tested on a custom industrial dataset, EF-YOLOv11 reached an average accuracy of 85.88% at an IoU threshold of 0.5, which was 2.76% higher than the baseline YOLOv11.

title: Planar Antenna with Two Symmetrical Rectangular Rings for X & Ku Band Applications
authors: Drashti Khokhar, Praveen Kumar Chakravarti
publish: 2025 1st IEEE Uttar Pradesh Section Women in Engineering International Conference on Electrical Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPWIECON)
abstract: A planar antenna with two symmetrical rectangular rings is presented for X and Ku band. A rectangular-shaped patch forms the antenna, utilizing a microstrip feed line. The antenna uses a DGS, which is achieved by two symmetrical rectangles and one rectangle from the top notch combined with two symmetrical rings. The proposed antenna dimensions are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$21 \text{mm }\times 18\text{ mm }\times 1.6\text{ mm}$</tex>. An FR4 epoxy, characterized by a thickness of 1.6mm and a dielectric constant of 4, is used as a substrate for the proposed structure. The proposed structure resonates at frequencies of 11.83, 12.87 and 14.87 GHz, respectively. The proposed antenna is simulated using software HFSS.

title: Deep Spatial-Temporal Attention Transformer with Hierarchical Reconciliation for Multi-Granularity Electricity Sales Forecasting
authors: Qing Xu, Zhengying Liu, Wei Zhang, Xinying Wang, Weiwu Li, Tianshou Li
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: Accurate electricity sales forecasting is essential for electricity market planning, grid dispatching, and policy design. Yet existing methods often neglect inter-regional spatial correlations, multi-timescale feature fusion, and hierarchical consistency. To overcome these limitations, this study proposes a multi-granularity forecasting model that integrates spatiotemporal attention with hierarchical consistency constraints across four levels: provincial annual, city-level annual, city-level monthly, and city-category monthly. The model employs an improved Transformer for cross-scale feature extraction, captures spatial dependencies and consumption-structure differences through spatiotemporal attention, and enforces strict top-down and bottom-up consistency. Using empirical data from 2019-2024, the model achieves a MAPE of 6.38% at the finest level, outperforming the second-best benchmark by 20.4% and reducing inconsistency errors by 97.0%. Ablation results confirm that hierarchical architecture and spatiotemporal attention are the primary contributors to accuracy and consistency gains. The proposed approach offers precise and interpretable support for multi-level load forecasting and market-oriented power system operations.

title: Dopamine-Inspired Neuro-Modulated Radiomic Pipeline for Computed-Tomography-Driven Prediction of Immunotherapy Response in Metastatic Cancer Treatment
authors: Francesco Rundo, Massimo Orazio Spata, Carmelo Pino, Sebastiano Battiato
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Immuno-oncology has redefined cancer treatment paradigms, yielding high survival advantages and markedly lower systemic toxicity than conventional cytotoxic regimens. However, clinical uptake is hampered because the molecular underpinnings that govern patient-specific benefit remain only partially disclosed. The aim of this work is to propose an innovative approach that correlates the diagnostic imaging (CT-scans) that cancer patients undergo with the related response to immunotherapy treatment. To close this knowledge-to-clinical gap, the authors propose an end-to-end pipeline that sequentially localizes CT-scan cancer lesions via an enhanced Attention-based YOLO detector, amplifies spatio-temporal variance with ad-hoc non-linear system, distills features through an innovative encoder and finally refines them using a Dopamine-inspired neuro-modulatory attention layer whose output yields class probabilities through a fully connected soft-max head. Validation on a cohort of metastatic urothelial carcinoma confirmed a balanced accuracy of roughly 96%, substantiating the framework's promise as a bedside decision-support tool.

title: Assessing the Quality of Soccer Shots from Single-Camera Video with Vision-Language Models and Motion Features
authors: Filip Noworolnik, Joanna Jaworek-Korjakowska
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Automated quality assessment (AQA) of football striking technique typically relies on wearable inertial units or multi-camera marker-based systems that impose substantial logistical overhead. We propose a single-camera, vision-language pipeline that combines raw video with engineered 3D kinematic features and leverages a Vision-Language Model (VLM) to generate expert-style feed-back and scalar ratings. A monocular side view recording of each shot is first processed with RTMPose-3D [9] and smoothed with a 1-Euro filter [6]. Nine domain-informed features spanning the approach phase, plant mechanics, segmental coordination, and post-contact stability are automatically extracted and, together with the video clip, form a multimodal prompt to the VLM. Experiments on 70 shots (50 adults, 20 youth) labeled by UEFA B-licensed coaches demonstrate that the joint video and feature prompt achieves an MAE of 0.61 ± 0.03 with the ground truth, out-performing the video-only and feature-only variants by 0.34 and 0.24, respectively. Ablations reveal that down-sampling the frame rate to 30 FPS produces minimal quality degradation, and that 1-Euro temporal filtering slightly reduces the rating error.

title: Research on Multimodal Intelligent Decision-Making Technology for Complex Power Operation Processes
authors: Chao Zhang, Heyi Zhang, Shuo Zhang, Yana Zhu, Jiarong Xie, Xiaofeng Wu
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: With the expansion of power systems and the advancement of intelligent technologies, power operation processes have become increasingly complex and high-risk. Traditional single-modal decision-making methods struggle to process multi-source heterogeneous information effectively. This paper systematically studies multimodal intelligent decision-making technologies for complex power operation processes, analyzes process structures and data types, and clarifies the theoretical foundation of multi-source information fusion. A deep learning-based approach for multimodal feature fusion and joint modeling is proposed, alongside the design of an adaptive intelligent decision model enhanced with reinforcement learning to improve decision-making capabilities. Simulation experiments in typical substation scenarios show that the multimodal fusion model significantly outperforms traditional methods in decision accuracy, response speed, and anomaly detection, increasing operational efficiency by 43.2 %. This study provides theoretical and technical support for intelligent upgrades in power operation processes.

title: GWOGA-Based Path Planning for Mobile Robots in Obstacle Environments
authors: Juntao Zhang, Zizhuo Lu
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: Mobile robot path planning in cluttered environments is a challenging task that requires a careful balance between global exploration and local exploitation. To address this, we propose GWOGA, a hybrid meta-heuristic algorithm that integrates the Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO) into the framework of the Genetic Algorithm (GA). Unlike traditional GA, which relies on random selection, GWOGA leverages the hierarchical leadership of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha, \beta$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\delta$</tex> wolves to guide the population toward promising regions. This mechanism enhances both convergence speed and solution quality. A multi-objective fitness function is designed to optimize path length, smoothness, and collision avoidance simultaneously. Experimental simulations are conducted on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20 \times 20$</tex> grids with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 \%}$</tex> obstacle density. Results demonstrate that GWOGA reduces path length by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 \%}$</tex> and achieves a final fitness value approximately 11.1% higher than that of the standard GA. The proposed algorithm shows strong performance in static obstacle environment, making it a promising solution for real-world mobile robot applications.

title: Grok-Powered Analysis of Project-Based-Learning Student Feedback
authors: Cecilia T. Gumanoy
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Data Science and Information Technology (DSIT)
abstract: This paper presents a compact, reproducible workflow that uses a large language model to convert multilingual project-based learning reflections into actionable evidence for instructors and administrators. A corpus of 5,894 anonymized comments was compiled in English, Tagalog, and Taglish; for the deterministic one-shot analysis reported here, 866 comments fit the token/character budget and were processed after normalization and deduplication, with each comment treated as a single, equally weighted unit. Using Grok with structured JSON outputs and deterministic decoding, the pipeline performs internal translation for reasoning, assigns a sentiment label to each comment, and aggregates corpus-level themes with short representative quotes. Results show a predominantly neutral profile (456 comments), alongside 312 positive and 98 negative comments. The most frequent positive signals highlight teamwork and collaboration, successful project completion, hands-on learning, growing confidence, and memorable experiences. The most frequent negative signals indicate time-management pressure, technical errors, cooperation gaps, frustration following failures, and resource limitations. The method remains audit-friendly through schema validation and simple checks, is easy to rerun by section or term, and preserves student voice while scaling beyond manual coding. Course- and program-level recommendations target the observed frictions and reinforce strengths surfaced by the analysis.

title: Analyzing Closed-Loop Training Techniques for Realistic Traffic Agent Models in Autonomous Highway Driving Simulations
authors: Matthias Bitzer, Reinis Cimurs, Benjamin Coors, Johannes Goth, Sebastian Ziesche, Philipp Geiger, Maximilian Naumann
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Simulation plays a crucial role in the rapid development and safe deployment of autonomous vehicles. Realistic traffic agent models are indispensable for bridging the gap between simulation and the real world. Many existing approaches for imitating human behavior are based on learning from demonstration. However, these approaches are often constrained by focusing on individual training strategies. Therefore, to foster a broader understanding of realistic traffic agent modeling, in this paper, we provide an extensive comparative analysis of different training principles, with a focus on closed-loop methods for highway driving simulation. We experimentally compare (i) open-loop vs. closed-loop multi-agent training, (ii) generative adversarial vs. deterministic supervised training, (iii) the impact of reinforcement losses, and (iv) the impact of training along-side log-replayed agents to identify suitable training techniques for realistic agent modeling. Furthermore, we identify promising combinations of different closed-loop training methods.

title: A Method for Detecting Surface Defects in Steel Based on an Improved YOLOv10 Algorithm
authors: Xin-Le Zhang, Dao-Fu Zhou, Xiu-Jun Wang, Xu-Dong Hong, Lan-Jun Li, Tao Tao
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: Efficient detection of steel surface defects is recognized as essential for ensuring product quality and supporting intelligent industrial production. In this work, an improved You Only Look Once version 10 nano (YOLOv10n) model was proposed, in which the Efficient Channel Attention (ECA) mechanism and the Mobile Inverted Bottleneck Convolution (MBConv) structure were integrated to enhance detection accuracy and computational efficiency. The proposed model yielded an 8.3-percentage-point increase in mean average precision at 0.5 (mAP@0.5), while reducing the number of parameters and floating-point operations (GFLOPs) by 28.7% and 35.4%, respectively, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed improvements.

title: Research on the Early Fault Identification of Transmission Lines Based on Partial Discharge
authors: Cheng Wu, Yuhong Wu, Jinwei Lin, Mingjie Xu, Tao Ji
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: To enhance early fault identification in transmission lines, this paper proposes an intelligent diagnostic model integrating Gradient Boosted Decision Tree (GBDT) and Multi-layer Perceptron (MLP), termed GBMLP. The model employs a hierarchical architecture where GBDT captures multiscale features of partial discharge signals, MLP establishes complex fault pattern correlations through deep nonlinear mapping, and a self-attention mechanism dynamically fuses complementary features to mitigate high-dimensional data interference. Experimental results demonstrate 100% diagnostic accuracy for 12 fault categories in 230kV PSCAD simulations, 99.24% accuracy on public datasets, and performance above 97% under extreme sample distributions, confirming the model's practical application potential for intelligent fault identification and early warning in transmission lines.

title: RetailAction: Dataset for Multi-View Spatio-Temporal Localization of Human-Object Interactions in Retail
authors: Davide Mazzini, Alberto Raimondi, Bruno Abbate, Daniel Fischetti, David M Woollard
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: We introduce RetailAction, a novel dataset designed for multi-view spatio-temporal localization of human-object interactions in retail stores. Existing datasets either provide video-level action classification only (without spatio-temporal localization), or, when such annotations are present, they are limited in scale and not specific to the retail sector, often lacking real-world store data. RetailAction addresses these limitations by focusing on interactions between actual customers and store products, captured from multiple top-view cameras in 10 different real-world convenience stores. The dataset consists of 21,000 samples, each containing two synchronized videos with a total duration of 41 hours. In addition to the videos, the dataset includes annotations detailing precise interaction points for both views, temporal ranges, and action categories for each interaction. In this paper, we describe the data collection process and we provide an analysis of the dataset's statistics. We also present a baseline model for spatio-temporal localization of interaction points and compare different state-of-the-art backbones. Finally, we present a novel set of evaluation metrics tailored to this use case. RetailAction aims to facilitate research on fine-grained action recognition and localization, offering a valuable resource for developing advanced retail analytics applications.

title: Numerical Simulation of SCR Catalytic Denitrification Based on Heat Storage Function
authors: Yujie Zhao, Ninghao Shi, Wei Bai, Yifeng Chen
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: Selective Catalytic Reduction (SCR) technology is a highly typical method for treating flue gas NOx. However, to avoid the issue of unstable NOx emission control caused by instantaneous fluctuations in flue gas temperature, additional equipment and cost investments are required. In view of this, this paper proposes a coupled SCR denitrification process based on thermal storage, and investigates the thermal storage characteristics, temperature distribution, and NOx removal effects under two thermal storage solid structures through numerical simulation. The results reveal that significant temperature gradient variations are observed along the height direction in both the thermal storage solid and fluid regions. As the thermal storage period extends, the differences in the flow area of the thermal storage solid have a substantial impact on the total thermal storage capacity and outlet temperature. On the other hand, when the inlet wetted perimeter is small and the catalytic area increases, the outlet NOx concentration significantly decreases (to 35 mg/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>), ensuring that the NOx emission concentration remains stably within the compliance limit.

title: Optimum Design and Stiffness Evaluation of a 3-DoF Parallel Module for a Hybrid Machining Robot
authors: Shihan Lin, Fugui Xie, Zenghui Xie, Xin-Jun Liu, Jialin Feng, Fei Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: To address the need for processing small-scale features on large flat thin-walled components, a hybrid machining robot with 3R(Ps)-3UPS parallel module is designed. In this processing scenario, the parallel module is required to have high dynamic performance and stiffness. To meet these requirements, this paper carries out the kinematic optimum design of the module. To avoid the computational inefficiency and unintuitive expression caused by high-dimensional parameter design spaces, the geometric parameters of the module are divided into two groups and optimized respectively. Based on the motion/force transmission and constraint performance indices, the global design indices are defined, guiding the selection of the dimensionless parameters. Then, the scale factors are decided to meet the required workspace of the module, and the dimensional geometric parameters are calculated. To evaluate the stiffness of the optimized module, an analytical stiffness model is established by modeling the deformation and the stiffness of the chains. Finally, the stiffness is investigated within the workspace, showing that the module exhibits ideal stiffness performance. The work in this paper not only lays the foundation for the further development of the 3R(Ps)-3UPS module and the hybrid machining robot, but also proposes a novel approach to optimize multiple dimensional parameters of parallel mechanisms with passive chains.

title: Automated Advertising Campaign Generation from Minimal Prompts: An AI-Powered Multi-Component Approach
authors: Md Abdul Hannan, Shamsuddin Ahmed, Md Radion Kabir Badhon, Md Shariar Kabir
publish: 2025 International Conference on Robotics and Artificial Intelligence (ICRAI)
abstract: Manual advertising campaign creation is often resource-intensive, requiring specialized expertise in content design, targeting, and platform optimization. This paper presents EventAIC, an AI-driven system that autonomously generates complete advertising campaigns using minimal input specifically, a product type and an event context. EventAIC integrates large language models (GPT-4.1) and diffusion-based image generation models within a three-stage pipeline for text creation, visual synthesis, and automated evaluation. Experimental validation with 100 campaigns across 10 product categories and 10 event types preliminary that EventAIC achieves a mean overall quality score of 7.92/10 (SD=0.38) with an average generation time of 5.82 seconds per campaign at a cost of $0.0039 per campaign. Exploratory statistical analysis reveals that event type significantly impacts campaign quality (F=3.81, p=0.0004), while model configuration and product type show no significant effects. These preliminary findings indicate that EventAIC can produce campaigns of comparable quality to human-generated ones while significantly reducing creation time from weeks to seconds. This study contributes a practical end-to-end architecture for automated advertising and validates the feasibility of minimalinput campaign generation.

title: Enhanced Tea Leaf Disease Classification Using Transfer Learning and Continuous Layer with Attention Mechanism
authors: Gaurav Kr. Rajput, Dampy Singh, Pragya Siddhi, Gunjan Chhabra, Shobhit Prajapati, Anuradha Brijwal
publish: 2025 14th International Conference on System Modeling &amp; Advancement in Research Trends (SMART)
abstract: In the rapid growth of precision agriculture requires scalable, smart, and highly accurate plant disease identification precisely in high value crop like tea. In order to develop the accuracy and efficiency of tea leaf disease classification, this research study suggests a novel deep learning framework. It inculcates transfer learning with a specifically developed Continuous Convolutional Layer with an combined attention mechanism. Our framework utilized Gaussian basis-driven convolution to acclimatise to various shapes of illness. In contrast to traditional CNN-based models, attention also emphases on the most significant areas linked to illness in leaf images. The dataset teaLeafBD has 5276 images categorized in seven classes. While one is healthy leaves and others six have diverse illness. The proposed model has been trained on this dataset. Image normalization, strategic up sampling, stratified data partitioning has been utilized to enhanced model generalization and class imbalance reduction. A well-known function called VariationalLoss utilized to penalize sudden changes in space that helped to improve learning. Apart from, it helps to improve the model's accuracy. Our model performance on test data shows momentous performance. Model has got test accuracy is 91.19 % and validation accuracy is 91.63 %. This fusion framework not only progresses the area of plant disease detection but also inaugurates the foundation for deployable AI systems in the field of agriculture that can perceptively systematize early disease discovery, yield protection and maintainable farming methods.

title: Find First, Track Next: Decoupling Identification and Propagation in Referring Video Object Segmentation
authors: Suhwan Cho, Seunghoon Lee, Minhyeok Lee, Jungho Lee, Sangyoun Lee
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Referring video object segmentation aims to segment and track a target object in a video using a natural language prompt. Existing methods typically fuse visual and textual features in a highly entangled manner, processing multi-modal information together to generate per-frame masks. However, this approach often struggles with ambiguous target identification, particularly in scenes with multiple similar objects, and fails to ensure consistent mask propagation across frames. To address these limitations, we introduce FindTrack, an efficient decoupled framework that separates target identification from mask propagation. FindTrack first adaptively selects a key frame by balancing segmentation confidence and vision-text alignment, establishing a robust reference for the target object. This reference is then utilized by a dedicated propagation module to track and segment the object across the entire video. By decoupling these processes, FindTrack effectively reduces ambiguities in target association and enhances segmentation consistency. Find-Track significantly outperforms all existing methods on public benchmarks, demonstrating its superiority.

title: Optimization Design of Inspection Robot for Granary Temperature and Humidity Based on Deep Learning
authors: Suoguo Wang, Jing He, Ge Guan, Tingting Liu
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: In the field of grain storage safety management, in order to solve the problems of complex dynamic interference and insufficient adaptability of traditional fixed path method in granary environmental temperature and humidity inspection, this study proposes an optimization design method of granary inspection robot based on deep Q network (DQN). Granary environment has the characteristics of large spatial structure, uneven distribution of temperature and humidity, change of grain pile shape and frequent operations in and out of the warehouse, and the traditional static monitoring network is difficult to achieve comprehensive coverage and real-time response. By integrating the perception ability of deep neural network and the decision-making mechanism of reinforcement learning, a closed-loop learning framework of “state perception-action decision-reward feedback” is constructed, which enables the robot to autonomously generate a patrol scheme that takes into account the optimal monitoring coverage, path efficiency and energy consumption. The experimental results show that compared with particle swarm optimization (PSO) and ant colony algorithm (ACO), the path length <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(55.8 ~\mathrm{m})$</tex> of the proposed method is reduced by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$19.7 \%-21$</tex>. 4 %, the running time <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(150.1 ~\text{ms})$</tex> is increased by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$21.1 \%-33.3 \%$</tex>, the probability of the optimal solution is 97.5 %, and the monitoring coverage rate is increased to 96 %. The energy consumption efficiency is optimized by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 6 \%} \boldsymbol{-} 20 \%$</tex>, which significantly improves the robustness and efficiency of inspection in the dynamic environment of granary, and provides reliable technical support for intelligent granary management.

title: Advancing Non-Invasive Scoliosis Detection Using Deep Learning
authors: Devanshu Ekhar, Sidhant Lohkar, Jeena Joseph, Saveri Dongre, Yogesh Golar
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Scoliosis is a complex spinal disorder characterized by an abnormal curvature of the spine, primarily affecting adolescents and often progressing without timely diagnosis. Early detection is crucial to prevent long-term complications, but conventional diagnostic methods rely heavily on expert interpretation of radiographs, which can be subjective and inaccessible in under-resourced areas. This research proposes a computer-aided diagnostic system using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to automatically detect scoliosis from grayscale X-ray images. The model was trained on a publicly available dataset comprising 259 labelled X-ray images, achieving a test accuracy of 78.85%. Although the dataset size and class imbalance (188 scoliosis vs. 71 normal) limit generalization, results indicate strong feasibility for early detection. The system was integrated into an interactive Streamlit web application, enabling real-time predictions while complementing radiologists' assessments rather than replacing them. The application emphasizes usability and accessibility, particularly for preliminary screening in rural clinics, schools, or telehealth services. Overall, this study demonstrates that deep learning models can offer a scalable, cost-effective, and reliable solution for assisting scoliosis diagnosis in both clinical and non-clinical environments.

title: Research on Performance Comparison of Different Python PDF Parsion Libraries in Analyzing Protection Setting Lists
authors: Han Yang, Zihao Wei, Ziyi Cheng, Minhao Zou, Fang Chen, Wei Luo, Yanyi Fu, Zhen Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy Power and Electrical Technology (CEPET)
abstract: Distribution automation technology is increasingly important in smart grids. Distribution terminals are key components of distribution automation systems. Accurate verification of protection settings helps enhance power supply reliability. The electronic versions of protection setting lists are typically stored in PDF format. Rapid and accurate parsing of protection setting list content represents a meaningful research challenge. This study selected five mainstream Python PDF parsing libraries for testing setting list content extraction. The five libraries are PyPDF2, Pdfplumber, Camelot, PDFMiner, and Tabula-py. Results demonstrate that for parsing setting sheet content, Pdfplumber shows advantages in both accuracy and speed, making it more suitable for this specific task.

title: A Wideband S/X-Band Shared-Aperture Antenna Based on Metasurface
authors: Xiangdong Sun, Shengyuan Luo, Yingsong Li, Adrian Bekasiewicz
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: This paper presents a wideband SIX-band shared-aperture antenna based on metasurface. In the X-band, a Fabry-Perot (F-P) antenna is realized utilizing the partial reflection characteristics of the metasurface, achieving high gain and wideband operation. In the S-band, efficient wideband radiation is achieved through the excitation of surface currents by a coupled feeding patch. The simulation results show that the proposed antenna achieves a 10 dB return loss bandwidth and a 3 dB gain bandwidth of 8.8-11 GHz (22.2%) in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{X-}$</tex> band, with a peak gain of 15.1 dBi at 10.3 GHz. In the S-band, a 10 dB return loss bandwidth and a 3 dB gain bandwidth of 2.2 to 3.1 GHz (33.96%) are obtained, with a maximum gain of 7.82 dBi at 2.5 GHz. The mutual coupling between the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{S}{-}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{X-}$</tex> band ports is less than -40 dB, confirming the effectiveness and isolation performance of the proposed design.

title: A Dual-task Prediction Model for Starlink Maritime Performance
authors: Richard Li, Md Muntasir Hossain, Xingya Liu, Ruhai Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: While LEO satellite systems have revolutionized broadband access for maritime industries, the dual challenges of high orbital mobility and dynamic weather lead to unpredictable signal instability. This prevents the maritime industry from effectively managing bandwidth. To address this issue, this work developed a short-term, dual-task prediction model to predict future downlink throughput and latency simultaneously using a 15-minute prediction horizon. The model is trained using real-world experimental data, which includes recent throughput history, network-layer indicators, and environmental variables. By utilizing a shared backbone for simultaneous downlink throughput and latency forecasting, this dual-task approach outperforms single-task models. Furthermore, its computational efficiency makes it ideal for edge deployment and real-time, application-aware decision-making.

title: Polarn: Polar BEV Representation with Unified Multi-Modal Fusion for 3D Detection
authors: Jiaxin Chen, Feng Gao, Niuniu Wang
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: Multimodal fusion is essential for autonomous driving perception, enabling more comprehensive environment under-standing and improving driving safety. However, conventional Cartesian BEV representations often suffer from feature misalignment across different sensor modalities, which limits the achievable trade-off between accuracy and efficiency. In this work, we propose a polar BEV-based multimodal fusion network that improves cross-modal alignment while maintaining computational efficiency. Specifically, we introduce PolarAware encoder and Polar-Aware Geometry Adapter (PGA) module in the lidar branch, and the polar coordinate transformation module in the camera branch to project surrounding-view image features into the polar BEV space. At the BEV level, we design the PolarCross Fusion (PCF) module to further enhance cross-modal consistency. Extensive experiments on the nuScenes dataset demonstrate that our approach consistently outperforms strong baselines, achieving 71.3% mAP at 8.9 FPS, while improving the detection accuracy of nearby objects.

title: Bridging the Black Box: Explainable Anomaly Detection for Critical Infrastructure Systems
authors: John DeLeon, William Mitchell, Shane Waldrop, Serena Reese, Tatum Beaugh, Amiran Fields, Erdogan Dogdu, Roya Choupani
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: Cyberattacks have become more frequent and complex, increasing the chance of malicious actors targeting infrastructure. Deep learning models have shown promise in being effective at detecting anomalies, but are generally uninterpretable. Our CIS framework seeks to incorporate Explainable AI (XAI) and knowledge graphs to make a more interpretable model that enhances the provided context of each detected anomaly, while balancing the predictive accuracy of deep learning models with the added XAI. It includes an ETL process that ingests network and sensor logs through a scalable pipeline powered by Spark, Kafka, TimescaleDB, and Neo4j graph databases. We deploy a graph neural network autoencoder for anomaly detection and an XAI framework, such as SHAP or GNNExplainer, to indicate the most influential features, nodes, and relationships from the graph.

title: A Fault-Tolerant State Estimation Algorithm for Unmanned Systems Based on Koopman Operator and Particle Filter Fusion
authors: Longxiang Zhang, Jiawei Liu, Le Ru, Yuze Wang, Yujie Wei, Chunye Chen
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: In complex environments, unmanned platform dynamic systems exhibit strong nonlinearity, high coupling characteristics, and susceptibility to interference, leading to potential sensor data drift or failures that significantly degrade state estimation performance <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">[1]</sup>. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a Koopman operator and particle filter fused state estimation algorithm (Koopman-PF) for unmanned systems. The algorithm leverages the Koopman operator to linearize complex nonlinear systems using raw datasets <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">[2]</sup>, while integrating particle filtering's probabilistic reasoning framework. This dual approach reduces particle filtering's dependency on precise nonlinear system modeling while achieving robust estimation under non-Gaussian noise. An adaptive measurement noise update mechanism is introduced to enhance dynamic fault tolerance under sensor failure conditions. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm maintains rapid convergence and high-precision estimation despite sensor drift and faults. Comparative analysis with Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) validates the algorithm's superiority in effectiveness and estimation accuracy.

title: Research on Finite Element Simulation of Wireless Power Transmission
authors: Menghan Wang
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: To address the critical influence of the design of coupling coils on the transmission efficiency, power, and magnetic field leakage in radio power transmission systems, this paper conducts research using the finite element simulation method. Firstly, a three-dimensional model of a rectangular coupling coil is constructed, and core parameters such as self-inductance, mutual inductance, and magnetic flux density modulus are simulated and calculated. The influence laws of the number of coil turns and magnetic core structure on these parameters are analyzed. Secondly, the energy transmission characteristics of the system are evaluated based on the simulation results, and the coil losses and magnetic field leakage distribution are quantified to reveal the logical relationship between the coupling mechanism of parameters and system performance. Finally, the coil structure is optimized based on the simulation data, increasing the system transmission efficiency by 8 % to 12 % and reducing the magnetic field leakage by more than 15 %. The research shows that finite element simulation can precisely guide the design of coupling coils and provide reliable technical support for the performance optimization of WPT systems.

title: Enhancing Domain Diversity in Synthetic Data Face Recognition with Dataset Fusion
authors: Anjith George, Sébastien Marcel
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: While the accuracy of face recognition systems has improved significantly in recent years, the datasets used to train these models are often collected through web crawling without the explicit consent of users, raising ethical and privacy concerns. To address this, many recent approaches have explored the use of synthetic data for training face recognition models. However, these models typically underperform compared to those trained on real-world data. A common limitation is that a single generator model is often used to create the entire synthetic dataset, leading to model-specific artifacts that may cause overfitting to the generator's inherent biases and artifacts. In this work, we propose a solution by combining two state-of-the-art synthetic face datasets generated using architecturally distinct backbones. This fusion reduces model-specific artifacts, enhances diversity in pose, lighting, and demographics, and implicitly regularizes the face recognition model by emphasizing identity-relevant features. We evaluate the performance of models trained on this combined dataset using standard face recognition benchmarks and demonstrate that our approach achieves superior performance across many of these benchmarks.

title: ERP-Driven Sales Forecasting with AI Techniques: A Predictive Analytics Approach
authors: Gopikrishna Maddali, Saurabh A. Pahune, Mahi Ratan Reddy Deva, Pushpalika Chatterjee
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems are encircled by a robust sales forecast that will probably hold equal significance in corporate strategy formulation, inventory management, and resource maintenance. The author is mostly concerned with the use of different ML methods and predictive analytics in ERP systems in sales forecasting. The key subject of this work is to present a method of building a sound sales forecasting model with the use of ML. The authors utilize a dataset of real-life retail sales, which contains past data of transactions. An extensive data pre-processing pipeline is defined and includes the tasks of addressing the lack of data, feature normalization, and label encoding. The solution to sales prediction is a deep learning hybrid model, which applies both Gated Recurrent Units (GRU) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM). The efficacy of the model is assessed by applying standard ML techniques such as Support Vector Machine and Random Forest. The LSTMs with GRUs model with a R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 98.05, RMSE of 17.32 and an MAE of 13.27 outperforms all the other models with the experimental data. The findings demonstrate how a series of models founded on DL can enhance the degree of sales forecasting through the introduction of intricate time-related relationships. The implementation of the ML methods has significantly enhanced the sales prediction accuracy following the implementation of the ERP systems.

title: MedSAM-Guided Curriculum Learning for White Matter Tract Segmentation in Block Face Imaging of Fetal Brain
authors: Athira Kalladayil Shibu, Sriprabha Ramanarayanan, Vinoth Kanna, Jaikishan Jayakumar, Keerthi Ram, Mohanasankar Sivaprakasam
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Block Face Imaging (BFI) is a high-resolution snapshot acquired during histological sectioning that captures the exposed tissue surface using tissue autofluorescence of lipids and NADPH. BFI offers up to five times greater white-gray matter contrast than traditional imaging, allowing clear visualization of white matter(WM) tracts. To enable accurate segmentation of WM tracts, we introduce a MedSAM-guided curriculum learning that progressively improves performance by training on increasingly complex samples. This approach takes advantage of both the anatomical precision of BFI and the generalizability of the medical foundation models. The resulting segmentations are manually validated by expert neuroscientists to ensure anatomical accuracy. Our method achieves an average Dice score of 0.9297, evaluated across 230 BFI slices with a spatial resolution of approximately 60μm, offering significantly enhanced structural clarity over conventional MRI-based approaches (500μm resolution) and outperforming state-of-the-art segmentation techniques. These findings demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach in leveraging high-resolution BFI data for accurate WM tract segmentation, highlighting its potential as a complementary tool along-side conventional neuroimaging techniques.

title: A Generalized Two-Stage Approach to Motion Style Transfer
authors: Tong Guo, Zhiqian Xia, Feng Yu, Haifeng Xia, Siyu Xia
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: This paper addresses the generalized motion style transfer problem, where the main challenge lies in the inability to apply discrete semantic labels for effective constraints. A cross-dataset style transfer framework is introduced to address key limitations in existing motion synthesis methods. Unlike prior approaches that rely on paired data or discrete semantic labels, our model transfers style through AdaIN-based adaptive instance normalization to encode content motion and style motion separately. A dual-path training strategy that combines style transfer generation and self-reconstruction ensures content preservation. Then, the Dual-Stream Denoising Network (DSDN) integrates LSTM processing for dynamic correction and spatio-temporal autoencoders for static motion refinement, with an Adaptive Gating Fusion Unit (AGFU) that dynamically balances stride amplitude and joint angles. Innovations include cross-dataset transfer without predefined labels, raw-sequence LSTM processing for motion correction, and adaptive fusion architecture outperforming single-method approaches. Validated on the HumanML3D and Mocap datasets, our framework demonstrates significantly enhanced cross-dataset style transfer efficacy.

title: A Parallel Fault Diagnosis Framework Based on KNN-Graph Convolution and BiGRU with Global Attention
authors: Yue Fei, Kun Tong, Jun Huang, Yun-Quan Gao, Xi-Wen Qu, Tao Tao
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: The importance of fault diagnosis for safety and efficiency was enhanced by the intelligent transformation of industrial systems. To overcome the limitations of manual feature extraction in traditional methods, a novel parallel model was proposed. Graph structures were constructed from vibration signals using KNN, with GCN utilized to capture spatial features and a Bidirectional GRU with global attention employed to model long-term temporal dependencies. Effective multi-dimensional feature fusion was thereby enabled. Empirical evaluation on the CWRU bearing dataset demonstrated the model's superiority, achieving 96.63% accuracy in ten-class fault diagnosis and exhibiting strong robustness and generalization.

title: A Raspberry Pi Based Approach to Skin Cancer Detection Through Transfer Learning
authors: A V S Deepika, V. Ravindranath Chowdary, P. Pavan Aditya, Ch. Pavani, G. Siva Narayana, V. Prasanthi
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: The most-deadly type of skin cancer, melanoma, is distinguished by its high death rate and quick spread. Melanocytes are usually the source of it, and it frequently manifests as an asymmetrical, multi colored mole that is larger than 6 mm. For better treatment results and patient survival, early detection is essential. This study presents a portable, low- cost melanoma detection system based on the Vision Transformer (ViT) architecture and Raspberry Pi. High resolution pictures of skin lesions are taken by a camera module attached to the Raspberry Pi, and these images are subsequently preprocessed to improve their diagnostic value. A pretrained ViT model uses transfer learning to ascertain if the lesions are harmless or malignant. The system is ideal for use in remote locations, clinics, or even homes because it supports offline, real-time detection using Raspberry Pie's Edge processing options. According to this study, deep learning-based clinical equipment that is accurate can be made available with inexpensive, low-power hardware without sacrificing functionality.

title: CSG-Fusion: Consistent Sparse-View Gaussian Splatting via Matching-based Fusion
authors: Yan Xia, Wenbo Ji, Weirong Chen, Daniel Cremers
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Recent developments in Gaussian splatting have enabled high-fidelity 3D reconstruction from multi-view images, but pixel-aligned methods such as MASt3R often produce redundant primitives and inconsistent geometry under few-view settings. We propose CSG-Fusion, a feed-forward framework that mindfully integrates pixel-aligned pointmap to reduce redundant primitives and produce compact and consistent 3D structures. Our approach leverages a matching prior with spatial thresholds to prune overlapping Gaussians, forming a coherent base 3D model, and then applies a mask-based feature aggregation module to merge local features and improve photometric consistency with fewer primitives. To enforce cross-view agreement after fusion, we further incorporate context-view supervision to align appearance and geometry across perspectives. Experiments on the large-scale ScanNet++ and object-level DTU benchmarks demonstrate both the efficiency and generalization of our method. Compared to the leading pose-known and pose-free approaches, our method achieves higher rendering quality with substantially fewer Gaussians.

title: Research on a Lightweight Object Detection Algorithm for Chaotian Peppers Based on YOLOv11s-PSN Network
authors: Jianning Shen, Xiaobin Zhang
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: Chaotian pepper is a common pepper variety in our country, due to its diverse morphological characteristics, there are obvious interference problems such as overlapping of multiple plants in complex farmland environment, and the traditional object detection method has certain challenges in its identification and mobile device deployment. To solve this problem, a YOLOv11s-PSN pep-per picking recognition and localization model based on YOLOv11s is constructed, which adds a P2/4 small object detection layer on the basis of the original P3-P5 detection layer, and captures the 4×4 pixel-level tiny fruit features through 160×160 feature maps to obtain Yolov11-P2. SPD-Conv is used to optimize the backbone network structure of YOLOv11s to reduce the number of model parameters, and then the NWD loss function is introduced to solve the problem that the model cannot effectively provide gradient in the face of occluding small targets, dense scene gradient oscillation, and cold start in the early stage of training. By comparing the identification and positioning models of pepper picking and positioning such as YOLO-Z, TPH-YOLO, YOLOv8s, and YOLOv11s, the comparison results showed that the YOLOv11s-PSN model significantly improved the comprehensive performance of pepper fruit detection: the global average accuracy (mAP50) increased from 88.01% to 88.98%, and the mAP50 of the key category Chili reached 97.6%. The model parameters were reduced from the original 9,413,961 to 8,495,836, providing a high-precision and lightweight real-time detection solution for agricultural pepper picking.

title: Information Security & Engineering
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 14th International Conference on System Modeling &amp; Advancement in Research Trends (SMART)

title: CoSense: Bridging Real-Time Performance and Fine-Grained Detail in mmWave Sensing
authors: Long Fan, Lei Xie, Shiyuan Ma, Jingyi Ning, Chuyu Wang, Wenhui Zhou, Jun Luo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Millimeter-wave (mmWave) radar offers significant potential for fine-grained sensing, yet transitioning from controlled laboratory environments to dynamic real-world applications remains challenging. Existing methods face a dichotomy: real-time point clouds sacrifice crucial signal details needed for sophisticated tasks, whereas information-rich raw data sensing imposes prohibitive transmission and computation overheads, often limiting analysis to offline settings and hindering real-time viability. To this end, we present CoSense, a real-time edge-end collaborative sensing system built on commodity mmWave radar (end) and edge intelligence. We first introduce a novel dual-stream data acquisition mechanism via realizing radar driver-level interfaces, enabling simultaneous transmission of point clouds and raw data. To bridge the fidelity-latency trade-off, we implement an adaptive transmission strategy via firmware modifications, selectively forwarding raw data segments (corresponding to regions of interest identified in the point cloud) for detailed fine-grained analysis, while continuously delivering point clouds for low-latency coarse-grained sensing and control loops. Furthermore, we incorporate closed-loop feedback beamforming, dynamically steering the radar beam based on real-time tracking to counteract motion-induced misalignment and enhance signal fidelity. Extensive evaluations under dynamic conditions demonstrate that CoSense successfully achieves real-time fine-grained sensing with high fidelity and manageable overhead.

title: An Adaptively Sampled HFM Pulsed Radar for Energy-Efficient Multi-Target Sensing
authors: Saira Rafique, Başak Ak Özbakiş, Hüseyin Arslan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: The next generation of wireless networks necessitates high-resolution and accurate sensing across diverse operational conditions. Pulse compression radars assist in achieving this goal at the cost of expensive high-rate analog-to-digital converters (ADCs). Applying a uniform Nyquist sampling rate for all scenarios leads to unnecessary energy consumption, particularly when targets are well-separated and do not require fine-grained resolution. To address this challenge, in this paper, a novel energy-efficient sensing framework for multi-target scenarios is introduced, employing adaptively sampled hyperbolic frequency modulated (HFM) pulsed radar. The system intelligently adjusts its fast-time sampling rate in accordance with a motion-separation metric that quantifies range and velocity differences between targets. Depending on the scene-complexity, it selects an appropriate sampling factor, with updates guided by a confidence metric and periodic resets to the Nyquist rate to ensure consistent long-term performance. The HFM pulses exhibiting non-linear time-frequency signature are exploited for inherent resilience to aliasing, enabling reliable range and velocity estimation under sub-Nyquist sampling. To evaluate the performance, closed-form Cramér-Rao lower bounds for range and velocity estimation are derived, that incorporates the impact of noise folding on the effective signal-to-noise ratio. Simulation results reveal that reduced sampling rates lead to lower energy consumption while preserving estimation accuracy and maintaining reliable detection performance for well-separated targets.

title: Development of an Eight-channel Wearable Olfactory Display for Virtual Reality
authors: Zhe Zou, Takamichi Nakamoto, Hsueh Han Wu, Kelvin Cheng, Sungho Lee, Shoichi Hasegawa
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: The olfactory display is a tool capable of generating essential olfactory feedback, and when implemented in a wearable form integrated with virtual reality (VR) hardware, it can significantly enhance immersion. However, current olfactory displays suffer from insufficiencies including limited scent generation range, inevitable latency before perception, the persistence and diffusion of previously emitted odors, and imprecise intensity control. We present an eight-channel wearable olfactory display that mounts directly on a head-mounted display (HMD) and delivers scents with minimal latency. Through precise calibration of the microfluidic channels, the system achieves nanoliter-scale liquid delivery control, generating accessible odor concentrations ranging within 1000 ppm. An active air-cleaning mechanism effectively prevents the lingering odor. Leveraging the intrinsic advantages of the wearable form and rapid odor generation technology, the system achieves a perception latency of just 3.17 s. Finally, to address heat accumulation from onboard electronics, an active thermal convection structure was implemented. This design suppresses temperature rise by 48 %, enabling its stable, long-term continuous operation. When integrated into a VR headset, the system produces a smell-enhanced environment that significantly increases subjective presence and vividness. This platform provides a compact, low-latency solution for olfactory augmentation in immersive media and human-computer interaction research.

title: Task and Interrupt Packing for Efficient CPU Utilization
authors: Ikuo Otani, Shogo Saito, Kei Fujimoto
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: With technological advancements, scale-out approaches utilizing many Central Processing Unit (CPU) cores on general-purpose servers have become common to enhance performance. To reduce initial costs and lower operational costs, efficient CPU utilization with as few cores as possible is required. However, standard schedulers, such as the one in the Linux kernel, do not achieve this. This is because one of the goals of standard schedulers is to allocate execution time fairly among tasks and they tend to assign cores to tasks abundantly. Therefore, in this paper, we verify the hypothesis that CPU utilization efficiency can be optimized by packing tasks and interrupts onto as few cores as possible while considering latency requirements. Specifically, to improve CPU utilization efficiency, we propose and implement a method that combines two approaches on different timescales: task and interrupt packing to minimize the number of CPU cores required, and load-balancing to avoid excessive wait times based on CPU contention states to reduce task latency. The proposed method differs from existing techniques in two key aspects: First, it incorporates interrupts, traditionally treated separately from tasks, into packing calculations based on estimated interrupt processing time. Second, it proactively avoids delay impacts by using the kernel’s task queue length, rather than directly observing task processing time. Performance evaluation demonstrated the feasibility of the proposed method. It packed tasks and simulated interrupts onto a single core while distributing load to another core when task queue length is long. Furthermore, compared to simply packing tasks onto the minimum number of cores, the proposed method successfully reduced maximum latency by 36% (12% under conditions with simulated interrupts). These results showed that it is feasible to efficiently utilize the CPU by packing tasks and interrupts onto as few cores as possible while considering latency requirements.

title: Novelty Detection in Event Surveillance Documents
authors: Edmond Menya, Roberto Interdonato, Dickson Owuor, Mathieu Roche
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Event Based Surveillance (EBS) monitors online sources such as broadcast, print, web news and generates early warning and response (EWAR) signals for use in disaster mitigation. These online sources provide a dynamic data source allowing for potential real-time EBS updates. However, in dealing with news articles, fragmented information exists in varied sources and redundant information are known to overburden EBS. In this study we propose a Large Language Model based approach that filters out redundancies while learning novel information from event centric online news corpora. We study this novelty task for events covering animal health, food security and climate change surveillance domains. Our approach focuses on features integrating spatio-temporal information (such as location and date of event) and thematic information (such as the name of disease, food insecurity triggers, climate change magnitude).We characterize novelty as presence of new and additional information (e.g., a newly mentioned disease name or additional location information) as distinguished from duplicate (e.g., an already seen disease name) and missing (expected but absent) information. To this regard, our approach proposes fine-grained classification of novelty in event surveillance and language modeling adoption with a multi-class classification objective to learn classifying of event information. Our LLM adoption strategy proposes question-based prompts whose extracted answers map to predefined feature types (e.g., location, date, name of disease) in order to enrich our classifier. In our empirical studies, we present comparative analysis with respect to language models and large language models for State-Of-The-Art performance in the event novelty classification task. Our findings demonstrates the ability of cross-domain novelty classification with our model EpidGPT (few-shot) achieving F<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sub>% scores of 82.3, 85.49 and 88.97 in animal health, food security and climate change domains while finetuned EpidGPT achieves F<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sub>% scores of 96.02, 86.0 and 88.45 on each respectively domains.

title: Classification of Hypoxic Ischemic Encephalopathy (HIE) in Neonates Using ECG and a Transformer-Based Model_supp1-3662271.pdf
authors: KIMIA REZAEI
publish: N/A

title: Contactless System for Sleep Prediction in Drivers
authors: Sara Groppo, Michele Guagnano, Valeria Serchi, Gabriel Beltrao, Luigi Pugliese, Massimo Violante
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Drowsy driving contributes to 10–30% of all vehicle crashes, making it a major road safety concern. Driver Monitoring Systems (DMS) aim to assess driver alertness and are typically categorized into vehicle-, behavior-, and physiology-based approaches. While physiology-based systems offer the highest accuracy, most of them rely on costly and intrusive contact-based cardiac sensors. This study demonstrates, for the first time, the feasibility of predicting driver sleep events using a fully contactless, physiology-based approach that analyzes breathing patterns in real time. Data were collected using a short-range 60 GHz standalone automotive radar in a driving-seat mockup. Crucially, sleep events were objectively validated for the first time in this context using polysomnography (PSG) data reviewed by a medical expert, following American Academy of Sleep Medicine (AASM) guidelines for the Maintenance of Wakefulness Test (MWT) — marking a departure from previous reliance on subjective behavioral observations. The proposed heuristic algorithm achieved 85% overall accuracy, with 100% (95% CI 29%–100%) specificity and 80% (95% CI 44%–97%) sensitivity. This work presents a validated, non-intrusive solution for sleep event prediction in drivers, underscoring its potential for enhancing road safety through practical, clinically supported DMS technologies.

title: Suppressing Hexagonal Defects in Ex Situ Remote Plasma Dry Etch Processing
authors: Kangjian Cheng, Jingyan Huang, Wen Siang Lew
publish: IEEE Transactions on Semiconductor Manufacturing
abstract: Hexagonal silicon oxide defects induced by an ex-situ remote plasma dry etch pre-clean process (SiCoNi<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TM</sup>) were observed at the NPN emitter step of a 40 nm BiCMOS device, leading to high defect density and device yield loss. In this work, a systematic investigation was performed to identify the root cause and formation mechanism of these defects. Comprehensive characterization and experiments reveal that fluorosilicate byproducts trapped in the frontside silicon nitride layer interact with moisture absorbed in the backside TEOS-based silicon oxide during FOUP storage, leading to localized silicon oxide crystallization. Although aggressive annealing can eliminate the defects, it deteriorates the doping profile and device performance. In contrast, removal of the wafer backside TEOS layer effectively suppresses defect formation without compromising device performance. This study provides practical insights into SiCoNi-induced defect formation and proposes compatible mitigation strategies for remote plasma-assisted dry clean processes in advanced technologies.

title: Channeling Fairness: Class Imbalance-Aware Skin Disease Recognition via Fair Channel Enhancement Module
authors: Joseph Thomas Año, Arren Matthew C. Antioquia
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Skin disease classification presents significant challenges due to class imbalance, low inter-class variability, and high intra-class variation present in most clinical image datasets. To address these issues, we propose Fair Channel Enhancement (FCE), a novel module that improves fine-grained feature representation without requiring additional annotations nor complex architectures. FCE allocates feature channels proportionally based on class frequency, which ensures fairer representation of underrepresented classes. FCE is coupled with CutMix augmentation and label smoothing to enhance model robustness and generalization. Extensive experiments on three dermatology benchmark datasets, including SD-128, SD-198, and SD-260, demonstrate that our approach achieves up to a 7.13% accuracy improvement over baseline models and outperforms state-of-the-art methods by a significant margin. FCE also boosts the average accuracy of both low- and high-frequency classes by up to 8.60% and 10.48%, respectively. Furthermore, our method generalizes effectively to other medical image datasets, including ISIC 2018 and Hyper-Kvasir, and performs well on smaller dataset subsets. These results highlight FCE as a simple and effective solution for imbalanced classification problems.

title: Active Thermal Control for EV Motor Drives under Stall and Low-Frequency Conditions Using Additional Current Degrees of Freedom
authors: Mingjin Hu, Wei Liu, Zekai Lyu, Zhenghao Li, Shuangxia Niu, Kwok Tong Chau
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: Power semiconductor devices in electric vehicle (EV) traction motor drives experience significant thermal stress under stall and low-frequency, high-torque conditions. This stress, primarily due to high peak phase currents, leading to excessive junction temperatures and large temperature fluctuations, can compromise reliability and necessitate conservative current derating. This paper proposes a novel active thermal control (ATC) strategy to mitigate these challenges by exploiting the additional current degrees of freedom inherent in open-ended winding (OEW) and multiphase star-connected winding (SCW) motor configurations. By strategically injecting harmonic currents that do not contribute to average torque, the proposed method minimizes the peak instantaneous current across all phases. Theoretical analysis shows that the proposed method can reduce the peak phase current by 7.2%-19.6% for various configurations. This reduction in peak current directly translates into effective thermal management, leading to lower peak junction temperatures and reduced temperature fluctuations in the power switches. An optimization problem is formulated to determine the optimal harmonic current injection for thermal stress reduction, and practical online implementation is discussed. Experimental results on a test rig configurable for three-phase OEW, five-phase SCW, and asymmetrical six-phase SCW drives validate the thermal benefits of the proposed approach.

title: Disturbance-Adaptive Data-Driven Predictive Control: Trading Comfort Violation for Savings in Building Climate Control
authors: Jicheng Shi, Christophe Salzmann, Colin N. Jones
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: Model predictive control (MPC) has demonstrated significant potential in improving energy efficiency in building climate control, outperforming traditional controllers commonly used in modern building management systems (BMSs). Among MPC variants, data-driven predictive control (DPC) offers the advantage of modeling building dynamics directly from data, thereby substantially reducing commissioning efforts. However, inevitable model uncertainties and measurement noise can result in comfort violations, even with dedicated MPC setups. This article introduces a disturbance-adaptive DPC (DAD-DPC) framework that ensures asymptotic satisfaction of predefined violation bounds without knowing the uncertainty and noise distributions. The framework employs a data-driven pipeline based on Willems’ Fundamental Lemma and conformal prediction for application in building climate control. The proposed DAD-DPC framework was validated through four building cases using the high-fidelity building optimization testing framework (BOPTEST) simulation platform and an occupied campus building, Polydome. DAD-DPC successfully regulated the average comfort violations to meet predefined bounds. Notably, the 5% violation DAD-DPC setup achieved 30.1%/11.2%/27.1%/20.5% energy savings compared to default controllers across four cases. These results demonstrate the framework’s effectiveness in balancing energy consumption and comfort violations, offering a practical solution for building climate control applications.

title: A Systematic Framework for Data Quality Assessment in Industrial Process Monitoring
authors: Liang Cao, Jianping Su, Yixiu Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics
abstract: High-quality data is the cornerstone of reliable machine learning in industrial process monitoring, yet industrial datasets frequently suffer from complex degradations such as sensor failures, variable operating conditions, and label noise. Existing quality assessment methods typically rely on aggregate statistical metrics, failing to capture sample-level nuances or the interplay between data characteristics and model learning dynamics. To address these shortcomings, we propose a comprehensive, hierarchical framework that systematically quantifies data quality at both dataset and sample levels. Our approach integrates three fundamental quality dimensions: completeness, consistency, and diversity. The framework further combines information-theoretic measures with gradient-based importance estimation to deliver unified and interpretable quality assessments. Experimental evaluations on five synthetic datasets simulating industrial scenarios demonstrate that our quality-guided data cleaning strategies significantly enhance downstream model performance. The framework further provides robust quality tracking in online monitoring scenarios, validating the necessity of multi-scale quality assessment in data-centric industrial applications.

title: CNN-based Transistor Modeling under Self-Heating Effects
authors: Tarek Mohamed, Hussam Amrouch
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computers
abstract: Due to the steady shrinking of technology node sizes, Self-Heating Effect (SHE) has become at the forefront of reliability and longevity concerns. Without careful consideration of the increased temperatures, reliability effects such as aging will be underestimated, putting the transistor and circuit at risk. SHE challenges and concerns are all amplified at cryogenic temperatures, as at low temperatures, multiple physical changes take effect, like the significant decrease of thermal conductivity, carriers’ mobility increase, and lower heat dissipation due to the low ambient temperature. The study of SHE at cryogenic temperatures is especially important in applications like quantum computing and space electronics. To accurately anticipate the SHE on the transistor, physics-based transistor and semiconductor simulation tools, Technology CAD (TCAD), are used. TCAD tools offer accurate, physics-based transistor SHE results, which designers can use to make informed decisions. However, TCAD tools can only deliver accurate SHE results after a manual, time-intensive calibration process. This calibration can only be done for operating temperatures and transistor configurations with existing experimental data. The reliance on experimental data for calibration and the constant need for recalibration restrict the scope of the design process to only a limited subset of configurations. In this work, we propose the first data-driven surrogate Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)-based TCAD thermal model for SHE thermal profile and channel hotspot prediction. Our approach enables designers to sweep a huge amount of different operating temperatures without the dependency on available experimental data or the constant need for model recalibration, while achieving a thermal profile prediction accuracy of 99.91%, a thermal profile extrapolation accuracy of 96.88%, hotspot prediction average error of 0.127%, and hotspot extrapolation average error of 1.65%. All results are compared to calibrated TCAD thermal model simulation results. Our model also alleviates the high computational cost and low throughput associated with TCAD tools by offering a time speedup of more than 13 000 x for both prediction and extrapolation tasks.

title: IEEE Transactions on Education Publication Information
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Education

title: Designing and Evaluating Live Migration of Confidential Virtual Machines for Commodity Arm CCA-based Platforms
authors: Fang-Jie Yang, Tse-Wei Lin, Shih-Wei Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computers
abstract: Confidential virtual machines (CVMs) are increasingly deployed to protect users’ code and data in use against attackers with hypervisor privileges. Arm has recently introduced hardware extensions, the Confidential Compute Architecture (CCA), to support CVMs. However, Arm CCA does not support live migration, a crucial virtualization feature. This work presents the first design of live migration support for Arm CCA. The design aims to reuse functionality for migrating regular VMs from commodity hypervisors to simplify development efforts while preserving the protection of CVMs. We prototype the design to support de facto mechanisms, including pre-copy and post-copy, to migrate CVMs on Arm CCA. Given that no CCA hardware is currently available, we created a performance modeling framework, pCCA that mimics a CCA environment over an off-the-shelf Arm server hardware, enabling the evaluation of CCA-based CVM live migration performance for the first time.

title: Information provision strategy of retailers under consumer reference-dependent behavior_supp1-3666760.pdf
authors: Tingting Li
publish: N/A
abstract: Due to the considerable valuation uncertainty in online shopping, consumers often return products after purchase. To reduce consumer returns, retailers provide detailed product information, referred to as uncertainty-reducing (UR) information, to mitigate consumers' valuation uncertainty. Even so, consumers cannot fully ascertain their true valuations before purchase. Furthermore, they often discover that the actual utility after purchase deviates from their pre-purchase expectations, leading to perceived losses or gains, which is referred to as reference-dependence. This study considers a monopolistic online retailer selling products to consumers with valuation uncertainty. The retailer can provide UR information to consumers who receive an imperfect signal (good or bad) indicating their true valuation types. We examine the retailer's optimal information provision strategy for rational and reference-dependent consumers, respectively. Furthermore, we analyze the interplay between the retailer's provision of UR information and the consumers' reference-dependent behavior. The results show that, for rational consumers, the retailer can always provide information, and higher information accuracy is more advantageous. However, with reference-dependent behavior, even if providing information is optimal, overly accurate information may harm the retailer's interests. Additionally, providing overly accurate information can exacerbate the return behavior of reference dependent consumers. Moreover, we analyze consumer surplus under the retailer's optimal information provision strategy and find that the strategy of providing UR information and offering full market coverage can lead to a win-win outcome for the retailer and the consumers. To ensure analytical rigor, we extend the analysis by considering a positive return hassle cost of consumers and demonstrate that all key insights remain qualitatively robust.

title: ICRAI 2025 TOC
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Robotics and Artificial Intelligence (ICRAI)

title: Beyond Heat Dissipation: Optimizing Diffusion Models in Frequency Domain
