However, there are bottlenecks in the carbon footprint accounting of electrical equipment, such as data silos, low data credibility, and broken full chain traceability, which make it difficult to support full life cycle carbon management. Therefore, this paper proposes a method for the integration of identification and resolution system and blockchain technology. This paper takes an 500KV power transformer as an example, a unique identification code is assigned to the transformer product through second-Level node of identification and resolution, connecting upstream and downstream supplier data, and using blockchain technology to ensure that carbon data is tamper proof and trustworthy, achieving accurate accounting of product carbon footprint.

title: Generative AI for IoT Security: A Review of Attack Detection, Prevention, and Secure Data Transmission Mechanisms
authors: Kanchan Vishalkumar Wankhade, Aditi Sharma
publish: 2025 1st IEEE Uttar Pradesh Section Women in Engineering International Conference on Electrical Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPWIECON)
abstract: The Internet of Things' (IoT) explosive expansion has created both enormous benefits and serious cybersecurity dangers. Among them, distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) assaults continue to be a maj or worry since hackers frequently take advantage of weak IoT devices to interfere with networks. These developing risks are posing a growing threat to traditional security solutions, particularly because IoT devices have limited resources, making advanced protection challenging to install. Generative AI has shown promise as a way to close this gap. Beyond the limits of traditional methods, techniques like Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) and Variational Autoencoders (VAEs) provide more accurate anomaly detection and proactive threat mitigation. With a focus on identifying and stopping threats while guaranteeing secure data transmission, this study explores how generative AI might improve IoT security. IoT networks can strengthen their defences against complex and widespread cyberattacks by utilising these cutting-edge models.

title: Conformal Field Injection for Accurate FDTD Simulation of Off-Grid Planar Transmission Line Structures : (Invited Paper)
authors: Andreas Lauer, Thorsten Liebig, David Schäfer, Winfried Simon, Andreas Wien
publish: 2024 IEEE MTT-S International Conference on Numerical Electromagnetic and Multiphysics Modeling and Optimization (NEMO)
abstract: To improve simulation accuracy in RF and microwave planar structures, a method using a conformal map to calculate FDTD parameters around electrode edges is presented. Its superior efficiency is shown in convergence studies of simple and advanced microwave structures. As electrode edges are easily identified in complex 3D layout data, the method is well suited for commercial FDTD simulators and has been implemented in EMPIRE XPU™ [1].

title: Multi-Frame RGB-Based Category-Level Object Pose Estimation
authors: Yucong Wang, Xiangrui Zeng, Zhouping Yin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: In open-world robot manipulation and navigation, the robots need to interact with objects that they have never seen before, and knowing the pose of these objects may be critical to some tasks. This letter proposes a method for RGBbased pose estimation that leverages multi-frame information for unseen objects from a known category. For objects within the category, latent features are learned to infer a 3D representation and an interpretable pose estimation optimization stage is developed. An efficient viewpoint sampling and position optimization process are designed to initialize the pose estimation based on neural radiance field (NeRF), addressing the large variations in object poses. The pose estimation can be iteratively optimized based on the loss between rendered images and camera images, without requiring any depth information or point cloud supervision. The confidence of observing the target object from multiple frames is considered with Kalman filtering to further improve the accuracy of pose estimation. The proposed approach is evaluated on a publicly available 3D annotated dataset as well as the data we collect on a robotic arm. The results show that the approach achieves promising pose estimation accuracy and exhibits strong generalization capabilities.

title: Multi-Channel Impedance Measurement Method Based on Data Acquisition Card
authors: Xinyu Li, Pan Xu, Zhibin Zhang, Junwei Zhou, Željka Lučev Vasić, Mario Cifrek, Yueming Gao
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: Multi-channel impedance measurement technology enhances the monitoring of dynamic changes in complex biological tissues, such as the muscle system, by simultaneously acquiring and processing impedance signals from multiple spatial locations. This enables accurate muscle function evaluation and real-time human-machine interaction. However, existing systems face challenges, such as signal crosstalk, sampling clock drift, and limited applicability. To address these, this paper proposes an anti-interference dynamic muscle impedance measurement method based on the Data Acquisition Card (DAC) platform. The method uses interleaved excitation signals to suppress crosstalk and an adaptive segmentation algorithm to compensate for clock drift, ensuring high-precision impedance demodulation. System validation results show a high degree of consistency with commercial impedance analyzers, with a root mean square error (RMSE) below 1% and a time resolution of 250 Hz. Dual-channel consistency analysis shows all points clustered around the y=x line, with a maximum deviation of 0.534 Ω, confirming high channel consistency. The method successfully resolves crosstalk and drift issues, offering a reliable solution for high-precision dynamic impedance monitoring and potential wearable device applications.

title: Few-Shot Vision-Language Reasoning for Satellite Imagery via Verifiable Rewards
authors: Aybora Köksal, A. Aydin Alatan
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Recent advances in large language and vision-language models have enabled strong reasoning capabilities, yet they remain impractical for specialized domains like remote sensing, where annotated data is scarce and expensive. We present the first few-shot reinforcement learning with veri-fiable reward (RLVR) framework for satellite imagery that eliminates the need for caption supervision-relying solely on lightweight, rule-based binary or IoU-based rewards. Adapting the “1-shot RLVR” paradigm from language models to vision-language models, we employ policy-gradient optimization with as few as one curated example to align model outputs for satellite reasoning tasks. Comprehensive experiments across multiple remote sensing benchmarks-including classification, visual question answering, and grounding-show that even a single example yields substantial improvements over the base model. Scaling to 128 examples matches or exceeds models trained on thousands of annotated samples. While the extreme one-shot setting can induce mild, task-specific overfitting, our approach consistently demonstrates robust generalization and efficiency across diverse tasks. Further, we find that prompt design and loss weighting significantly influence training stability and final accuracy. Our method enables cost-effective and data-efficient development of domain-specialist vision-language reasoning models, offering a pragmatic recipe for data-scarce fields: start from a compact VLM, curate a handful of reward-checkable cases, and train via RLVR. Our model, training code and dataset will be at https://github.com/aybora/FewShotReasoning.

title: Attitudes of Jordanian journalists towards the Cybercrime Law and its Impact on Freedom of Opinion and Expression
authors: Amer Khaled Ahmad, Mahmoud Yousef Al-Aboud, Abd Allah Sultan Aljalabneh, Tasnim Koufi, Saeed Khalfan Alshamsi
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Social Networks Analysis, Management and Security (SNAMS)
abstract: This study examines Jordanian journalists’ attitudes toward the 2023 Cybercrime Law and its perceived impact on freedom of expression as well as on professional practice. This study employed a filed survey using a questionnaire distributed on a sample of 300 journalists that were selected using a simple random sampling technique. The study finds that respondents report a high level of awareness and familiarity with the 2023 law. The study results indicated that the law is perceived to exert an impact on freedom of opinion and expression, as many respondents view it as misaligned with calls to broaden freedoms. Participants in the study further perceive that the law affects journalistic work by increasing legal exposure and imposing sizeable penalties and fines. The analysis reveals a positive association between journalists’ level of knowledge about the 2023 Cybercrime Law and the negativity of their attitudes toward it.

title: Evaluating Variance in Visual Question Answering Benchmarks
authors: Nikitha SR
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have emerged as powerful tools for visual question answering (VQA), enabling reasoning and contextual understanding across visual and textual modalities. Despite their advancements, the evaluation of MLLMs on VQA benchmarks often relies on point estimates, overlooking the significant variance in performance caused by factors such as stochastic model outputs, training seed sensitivity, and hyperparameter configurations. This paper critically examines these issues by analyzing variance across 14 widely used VQA benchmarks, covering diverse tasks such as visual reasoning, text understanding, and commonsense reasoning. We systematically study the impact of training seed, framework non-determinism, model scale, and extended instruction finetuning on performance variability. Additionally, we explore Cloze-style evaluation as an alternate assessment strategy, studying its effectiveness in reducing stochasticity and improving reliability across benchmarks. Our findings highlight the limitations of current evaluation practices and advocate for variance-aware methodologies to foster more robust and reliable development of MLLMs.

title: WLIP-WT Model Based MPC-VMC Hierarchical Control for Wheeled Biped Robots
authors: Haoyang Ren, Runchen Xu, Yihang Wang, Yutong Jiang, Zhen Han, Yu Wang, Wei Gao, Shiwu Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Wheeled bipedal robots (WBRs) encounter significant dynamic coupling among the torso, wheels, legs, and upper body during operation. Existing control strategies suffer from two limitations: ignoring the dynamic coupling between the torso and wheel-legs, and failing to meet real-time control requirements due to model complexity. To address these, this paper first proposes a wheeled linear inverted pendulum model with waist and torso (WLIP-WT), which abstracts the robot as a six-body system. Dynamic equations are derived via the Newton-Euler iteration method, with tailored state variables to better represent the non-minimum phase characteristics of the wheeled structure. Subsequently, a hierarchical decoupling control framework, MPC-VMC, is proposed. The upper layer utilizes model predictive control (MPC) for whole - body dynamics optimization, solving real-time torque distribution within the prediction horizon. The lower layer adopts virtual model control (VMC) to decompose leg thrust into joint torques, combining PID and feedforward compensation for accurate regulation of leg length and roll angle. Experiments show the proposed MPC-VMC controller effectively compensates for inertial disturbances from upper body movements, maintaining stability in rugged terrain and speed tracking tasks, resolving the traditional trade-off between real-time performance and accuracy.

title: Aerial Target Tracking: Benchmarking Algorithms on an Air-to-Air UAV Dataset
authors: Chengwei Li, Xiangyu Zhu, Yong Wang, Wenzhi Tang, Xuefeng Ren, Shunan Wu, Zhigang Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: This paper studies the problem of air-to-air visual tracking of quad-rotor unmanned aerial vehicle(UAV). This research is important for applications like security monitoring, logistics and emergency rescue. Current deep learning tracking algorithms have shown superior performance in various tracking tasks, on the purpose of exploring full potential of these methods for UAV tracking, we present a new dataset, named Drone2Drone, which consists of 58 video sequences containing over 110000 frames. Compared to other existing datasets, the proposed one features diverse environmental backgrounds, various shooting angles, and multiple lighting conditions. We implement a comprehensive training and evaluation process based on OSTrack tracking algorithms with the proposed datasets. The results indicate UAV tracking improvement and prove that tracker learns more robust class-level UAV information. Furthermore, we present an experimental evaluation of eight representative tracking algorithms, the result of which highlights some prominent challenges in the task of air-to-air UAV tracking and suggests possible directions for tracking algorithms improvement in the future. All the resources will be available before October.

title: Generative AI for Creating Interactive and Adaptive Educational Videos: A Pedagogical Framework
authors: Devanshi Timande, Utkarsha Pacharaney, Nishant Agade, Janvi Abhyankar, Nikhil Pardhi, Yashika Gaidhani
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) has rapidly expanded the capacity to produce instructional content at scale, yet most AI-generated educational videos remain static, pedagogically naive, and insufficiently adaptive to diverse learner needs. This paper introduces a pedagogically-aligned GenAI framework for the automated creation of interactive and adaptive educational videos. The framework integrates a Pedagogical Engine (mapping content to Bloom's taxonomy and instructional design), an Interactivity Module (automatic quiz generation and branching), and a Student Modeling System (real-time personalization and feedback), all orchestrated with a GenAI video generation pipeline and LMS interface. We implement a prototype pipeline that generates script → video → embedded assessments and evaluate it using controlled simulation scenarios (virtual classroom, educator panel review, and adaptive learner simulation). Simulated results indicate increased engagement, improved knowledge gain, and higher retention in adaptive, interactive videos versus static baselines. The paper contributes a practical design for pedagogy-aware GenAI video production, an implementation blueprint and toolset mapping, and an evaluation methodology with metrics for learning outcomes, engagement, and cognitive load. We conclude by discussing limitations of simulation-based validation and outlining a path for in-class pilots, multimodal analytics integration, and standardized evaluation protocols.

title: Survey of Deep Learning in Melanoma Detection
authors: Ali Al-Yousef, Rami Mohawesh, Moy’Awiah Al-Shannaq, Ahmad A. Saifan, Mohannad Alkhalaileh
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Social Networks Analysis, Management and Security (SNAMS)
abstract: Melanoma is the most dangerous type of skin cancer, and it is also the most deadly type of skin cancer in the world. Finding the condition early greatly improves the chances of people living longer. Dermoscopy and biopsy are common ways to find skin problems, but they are expensive, take a lot of time, and depend greatly on the skills of skilled dermatologists. Deep learning methods have been demonstrated to be good at automating the identification of melanoma by analyzing dermoscopic photos, which makes the process more accurate and scalable. This article looks at the progress that has been made in deep learning methods for diagnosing melanoma, such as datasets, assessment measures, obstacles, and possible future paths.

title: Customized CNN for Detecting Abnormalities in X-Ray and CT Scan Images
authors: Shruti, Deepak Painuli
publish: 2025 1st IEEE Uttar Pradesh Section Women in Engineering International Conference on Electrical Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPWIECON)
abstract: The identification of anomalies in medical imaging, including X-rays and CT scans, is essential for the prompt diagnosis and management of many disorders. Nonetheless, manual interpretation by radiologists may be laborious and susceptible to inaccuracies. This research presents a tailored Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architecture explicitly developed for the detection of anomalies in X-ray and CT scan pictures. The proposed model utilizes customized CNN with 4 different convolutional layers with activation units and maximum pooling for improved performance. Experimental findings on publicly accessible datasets indicate that the tailored CNN attains enhanced accuracy and resilience relative to current methodologies. The results indicate that the suggested approach may substantially assist radiologists in clinical environments, decreasing diagnostic duration and enhancing precision.

title: Adaptive Honeypots using Reinforcement Learning Algorithms DQN and DDQN in Ensemble Framework: A Systematic Literature Review
authors: Sohrab Farooq, Sadaf Hina, Sadaqat Rehman
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: The exponential growth of sophisticated cyber threats has necessitated the evolution from traditional static honeypots to intelligent, adaptive deception systems. This systematic literature review examines the integration of reinforcement learning algorithms, specifically Deep Q-Networks (DQN) and Double Deep Q-Networks (DDQN), within ensemble frameworks for adaptive honeypot systems. Following PRISMA 2020 guidelines, we analyzed 48 studies from 2015-2025, revealing significant performance improvements in adaptive honeypots employing reinforcement learning techniques. Our analysis demonstrates that DDQN-based adaptive honeypots achieve 95% detection accuracy with 7.2 minutes average engagement time, substantially outperforming traditional static honeypots (73% accuracy, 2.1 minutes engagement). Furthermore, proposed ensemble DDQN frameworks show potential for 97% detection accuracy with 4% false positive rates. Key research gaps identified include limited real-world deployments, lack of standardized evaluation frameworks, and insufficient integration of ensemble methods. This review contributes a comprehensive taxonomy of adaptive honeypot architectures, comparative analysis of reinforcement learning approaches, and identifies eight critical research directions for future work. The findings provide a foundation for developing next-generation cyber deception systems capable of autonomously adapting to evolving threat landscapes.

title: AAO-Grasp: Diffusion-Enhanced Object-Level Grasp All at Once
authors: Yiming Li, Hanchi Ren, Yue Yang, Xianghua Xie
publish: 2025 International Conference on Robotic Computing and Communication (RoboticCC)
abstract: 6-DoF grasping in cluttered scenes remains a challenging open problem. Most existing methods rely on RGB-D inputs or pre-defined 3D object models to extract geometric cues for grasp prediction. We propose a diffusion-enhanced 6-DoF grasp candidate generation framework that requires only a single monocular RGB image, without using any 3D priors or multi-view inputs. This enables grasp prediction from arbitrary and unseen viewpoints. Our method constructs a semantically guided diffusion-based scene template and adopts a coarse-tofine structure to resolve scale ambiguities at both the scene and object levels. Experiments on the GraspNet-1Billion benchmark demonstrate that our approach surpasses baseline performance and achieves competitive results on novel objects, validating its effectiveness and generalization capability.

title: Metaheuristic Optimization for Feature Reduction in Cerebrovascular Disease Detection: A Butterfly Algorithm Approach
authors: G S Pradeep Ghantasala, Anuradha Reddy, R Rajesh Sharma, Pellakuri Vidyullatha, Akey Sungheetha, U Ananthanagu
publish: 2025 1st IEEE Uttar Pradesh Section Women in Engineering International Conference on Electrical Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPWIECON)
abstract: Around the world, cerebrovascular illnesses—including stroke and aneurysms—remain a primary purpose of death and disability. High-dimensional clinical datasets can impair model overall performance and interpretability, regardless of the fact that early and correct prognosis is critical for successful management. The Butterfly Optimization Algorithm (BOA), a nature-inspired metaheuristic, is the foundation for feature reduction framework projected on the study to enhance the prognosis of cerebrovascular disorders. By the usage of BOA to medical and imaging datasets, the maximum pertinent features are found, reducing dimensionality without compromising diagnostic precision. Following feature selection, machine learning representations comprising Random Forests and Support Vector Machines were usage to categorise the features. Results from experiments display that in comparison to traditional techniques, the BOA substantially will increase classification accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity. In addition to reducing computational complexity, this technique improves diagnostic reliability. More effective computer-aided analysis techniques in cerebrovascular healthcare are made feasible through the study, which additionally indicates the promise of bio-inspired optimization for clinical data processing.

title: Privacy-Driven Cloud AI: Federated Learning and Zero-Trust for Secure Multi-Domain Collaboration
authors: Prashanthi Matam, Akshay Mittal, Karthik Pappu, VasanthRao Jadav
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: This paper introduces the privacy-preserving cloud AI system, GWO-FLNet, which is an arrangement of Federated Learning (FL), Zero-Trust architecture (ZTA), and the Gray Wolf Optimizer (GWO) that allows secure and high-accuracy interaction between distributed and multi-domain systems. The first goal is to maximize the precision of the models and preserve the data privacy when the datasets of clients are statistically heterogeneous and domain-specific. GWO-FLNet utilizes a CNN-BiLSTM hybrid setup, which models spatial-temporal regularities and trains the message layer of a client without communication of raw signals between that client and the server. In order to address inter-client differences and training imbalance, GWO adjusts the learning rate, the size of BiLSTM units, and aggregation weights, personalized by the client during the federated training process. Access control and strict authentication are imposed through ZTA principles over all clients. The experimental data show that the advancement in the performance is significant as opposed to traditional FL models, including FedAvg and FedProx: average Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC) increase of 7.8%, Intersection over Union (IoU) gain of 8.3%, and an increase in F1-score of 6.4%, besides 15% improvement in communication overhead. These results highlight the strength and scalability of the structure in situations with decentralized, nonuniform populations of data, which makes it highly appropriate for setups applicable in the real-time, privacy-conscious use of the cloud.

title: A 16-Element Broadband High-Gain Linearly-Polarized Array Antenna
authors: Nian Chen, Yong Mao Huang
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: This paper presents the design of an L-band, linearly polarized 4x4 phased array antenna. The design employs a stratified structural unit comprising a top-layer radiating patch, a dielectric substrate, and a ground plane. The overall structure is enclosed by an aluminum grid, and the array is constructed using an aperture-coupled feeding mechanism from the bottom layer. The top radiating patches feature rectangular dimensions with unequal length and width, and are excited via an offset feeding technique, where the feed point is horizontally displaced. Comprising 16 elements, the array maintains a low-profile characteristic. Simulation results indicate that a single antenna element achieves a peak gain of 6.3 dB within the L-band, while the 4x4 array demonstrates a significant gain enhancement of 16 dB, along with a bandwidth of 37 MHz (S11 < −10 dB). The design also exhibits stable linear polarization characteristics and a radiation pattern that meets coverage requirements. With its novel and practical structural approach, the proposed antenna satisfies the gain, bandwidth, and stability requirements for L-band applications, offering a viable solution for antenna selection in related fields, such as the transmission enhancement of the unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs).

title: Synthesizing Specialized Sparse Tensor Accelerators for FPGAs via High-Level Functional Abstractions
authors: Hamza Javed, Christophe Dubach
publish: 2026 IEEE/ACM International Symposium on Code Generation and Optimization (CGO)
abstract: Sparsity is inherent in many applications such as machine learning and graph analytics. However, achieving high efficiency in sparse computations requires specialized hardware accelerators like FPGAs, as traditional accelerators typically cater to dense data. While high level synthesis enables the automatic generation of FPGA-based accelerators, generic solutions produced via C-based synthesis flows often demand extensive development time, leading designers to prioritize broad applicability over fine-grained structural specialization. Consequently, these accelerators fail to fully exploit FPGA’s reconfigurablility, leaving substantial performance and efficiency gains untapped.This paper pushes the boundary by automatically generating specialized accelerators that match a given fixed sparse structure (e.g., in static graph analytics and pruned neural networks). It achieves this by leveraging functional abstractions within high level synthesis, an approach that has already proven effective in automating the generation of specialized dense tensor accelerator. Tensor shapes are encoded directly in the type system and specialized primitives for irregular data are introduced. Together, these innovations enable a concise specification of sparse accelerators and drive advanced optimizations—including dynamic partitioning and vector sharding—to produce hardware precisely tailored to the sparsity pattern of the underlying tensors.Compared to state-of-the-art generic accelerators (HiSparse, HiSpMV and GraphLily), the approach achieves up to a 2.8× improvement in bandwidth efficiency for sparse matrix computations and a 1.8× speedup on graph algorithms. Against the hls4ml neural network acceleration framework, it achieves up to a 1.8× improvement in throughput with a 4× reduction in resource usage, enabling scaling to larger networks. These results establish this approach as a flexible, powerful, and rapid solution for designing high-performance specialized sparse accelerators.

title: Modeling and Simulation of Battery Modules and Study of Thermal Runaway Characteristics Based on ANSYS Fluent
authors: Ji Gong, Luying Chen, Mingjia Wang, Lu Ban, Yi Fan, Zhaoquan Wu, Zheng Yu, Wei Tan, Wei Lang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy Power and Electrical Technology (CEPET)
abstract: In the context of the rapid development of the new energy vehicle industry, fire and explosion accidents caused by thermal runaway in power batteries occur frequently, seriously threatening personal and property safety and limiting industry progress. Over-discharge and internal short circuits are the core triggering factors. This study focuses on a ternary lithium battery and, based on ANSYS Fluent software, combines a single-equation thermal runaway model with the NREL four-equation thermal runaway model to conduct thermal runaway modeling and simulation under internal short circuit conditions for battery modules, with a focus on investigating the impact of internal short circuits on thermal runaway characteristics. The simulation used a single-layer battery model, dividing it into structures such as the positive electrode, negative electrode, cell, and busbar, and performing mesh division. Temperature, heat source distribution, and dimensionless concentration changes were monitored under room temperature of $26.84^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and a convection coefficient of 1. The results show that the thermal runaway temperature of the battery ranges from 219.65 to $678.37^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, with a maximum temperature difference of $458.72^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, and thermal runaway is triggered at 530 seconds. Heat sources are concentrated at both ends of the battery electrodes, starting around cell 3 and spreading with the propagation of heat. When the short circuit location is farther from the ends, thermal runaway is triggered earlier. The dimensionless concentrations Ce, Cne, and Csei decrease continuously with increasing temperature, and the reaction in cell 3 is initiated first due to the influence of the short-circuit region. This study provides critical data support and technical reference for revealing the thermal runaway mechanism of power batteries and optimizing safety protection design.

title: Cracking the Code of Spotify Hits: The Impact of Data Preprocessing and Machine Learning Models
authors: Vanita Jain, Rupal Gupta, Aayush Chauhan, Ekansh Dubey
publish: 2025 14th International Conference on System Modeling &amp; Advancement in Research Trends (SMART)
abstract: The prediction of commercial success of music, also known as “hit song science”, is a field of significant interest. Due to the high dimensionality of audio data and temporal drift in musical trends, it faces challenges. As a result, models trained on the data spanning decades often suffer from reduced accuracy, as the features evolve. This study investigates the impact of temporal data refinement and preprocessing in hit songs prediction, conducts a comparative analysis of four machine learning models (SVM, Random Forest, Logistic Regression and kNN). The analysis identifies the most effective way to predict modern hits, uses a dataset of over 40,000 tracks. Preprocessing the data with Principal Component Analysis (PCA) reducing the dimensionality from 15 to 13 features, retaining 99% variance. The analysis was performed on the complete dataset of 6 decades (1960s-2010s) and on a temporal dataset of last 3 decades (2000s-2010s). For full dataset, SVM achieved the highest accuracy of 77.77 % whereas, on the temporal dataset Random Forest outperformed with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 2. 8 4 \%}$</tex> accuracy, further increased to 83.17% by hyperparameter tuning. The results demonstrate the significance of addressing the temporal drift by training models on recent dataset, with Random Forest as the best model for this purpose.

title: TrafficInternVL: Understanding Traffic Scenarios with Vision-Language Models
authors: Hsiu-Fu Wu, Ya-Ting Yang, Yung-Ter Chen, I-Fan Chou
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Accurate description and analysis of traffic safety scenarios is essential for traffic safety assessment and the development of AI-driven traffic analysis systems. While recent vision-language models (VLMs) perform well on general benchmarks, they often fail to capture the complex spatial-temporal dynamics and causal reasoning required in safety-critical domains. To address this challenge, we propose TrafficInternVL, a structured fine-tuning frame-work for open-source VLMs, tailored to traffic safety description and analysis, with a focus on pedestrian-vehicle interactions. Our approach integrates keyframe-based global-local view construction, role-aware multimodal prompt design, representative sample selection, and joint QA-VQA supervision. By treating each question as an independent reasoning session, the model avoids reliance on dialogue history and achieves stable performance across diverse scenarios. Evaluated on the WTS dataset in the 2025 AI City Challenge Track 2, TrafficInternVL achieves the top official score (60.0393). The code will be released soon.

title: RG-Attn: Radian Glue Attention for Multi-Modal Multi-Agent Cooperative Perception
authors: Lantao Li, Kang Yang, Wenqi Zhang, Xiaoxue Wang, Chen Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Cooperative perception enhances autonomous driving by leveraging Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) communication for multi-agent sensor fusion. However, most existing methods rely on single-modal data sharing, limiting fusion performance-particularly in heterogeneous sensor settings involving both LiDAR and cameras across vehicles and roadside units (RSUs). To address this, we propose Radian Glue Attention (RG-Attn)-a lightweight and generalizable cross-modal fusion module that unifies intra-agent and inter-agent fusion via transformation-based coordinate alignment and a unified sampling/inversion strategy. RG-Attn efficiently aligns features through a radian-based attention constraint, operating column-wise on geometrically consistent regions to reduce overhead and preserve spatial coherence, thereby enabling accurate and robust fusion. Building upon RG-Attn, we propose three cooperative architectures. The first, Paint-To-Puzzle (PTP), prioritizes communication efficiency but assumes all agents have LiDAR, optionally paired with cameras. The second, Co-Sketching-Co-Coloring (CoS-CoCo), offers maximal flexibility, supporting any sensor setup (e.g., LiDAR-only, camera-only, or both) and enabling strong cross-modal generalization for real-world deployment. The third, Pyramid-RG-Attn Fusion (PRGAF), aims for peak detection accuracy with the highest computational overhead. Extensive evaluations on simulated and real-world datasets show our framework delivers state-of-the-art detection accuracy with high flexibility and efficiency. GitHub Link.

title: A Research on Depthwise Separable Convolution Accelerator Based on Tensor Permutation
authors: Ge An, Zhixiang Jiang, Jinqiao Kou, Min Wu, Yanlong Huang
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: In embedded systems with limited resources, deploying lightweight neural networks has become the key to achieving on-device intelligence. Depthwise Separable Convolution (DSC) reduces computational complexity and parameter count by decomposing standard convolution into depthwise and pointwise convolutions, making it particularly suitable for embedded devices constrained by performance, memory, and energy efficiency. However, despite the structural advantages of DSC, its hardware acceleration design still faces several challenges: depthwise convolution lacks efficient algorithmic optimization; hardware utilization is low when switching between depthwise and pointwise convolutions; and inconsistent data layouts lead to significant memory access overhead. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a high-performance depthwise separable convolution hardware accelerator for embedded scenarios. The core contributions of this work are as follows: First, a dedicated depthwise convolution acceleration unit based on the Winograd algorithm is designed, which significantly reduces the number of multiplications and computational overhead. Second, a highly reusable and scalable hardware architecture is proposed, allowing the pointwise convolution unit to share key components with the depthwise convolution unit, thereby maximizing the utilization of computational resources. Third, to address the different data layout requirements of depthwise and pointwise convolutions, an efficient data rearrangement and address mapping mechanism is introduced to optimize both on-chip and off-chip memory access efficiency and reduce layout conversion latency. Through simulation and evaluation using the MobileNetV2 model, the proposed design achieves a 55.6% reduction in memory access and a 23.6% reduction in clock cycles compared to conventional approaches, demonstrating its efficiency and practicality in resource-constrained environments.

title: Performance Benchmarking of Multiple Ensemble Classifiers for Predictive Modelling of Chronic Kidney Disease
authors: Gagandeep Kaur, Manik Rakhra
publish: 2025 1st IEEE Uttar Pradesh Section Women in Engineering International Conference on Electrical Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPWIECON)
abstract: The rapid evolution of machine learning (ML) and artificial intelligence (AI) has significantly advanced the early detection and diagnostic precision of critical medical conditions, including chronic kidney disease (CKD). CKD, or chronic renal failure, is characterized by a progressive decline in renal filtration efficiency, impairing the body's ability to eliminate metabolic waste, regulate electrolytes, and maintain fluid balance. The gradual nature of CKD progression leads to the accumulation of uremic toxins, posing severe systemic health risks. Without timely medical intervention, individuals with end-stage renal dysfunction typically have a survival window of approximately two to three weeks, underscoring the urgent need for renal replacement therapies such as haemodialysis or kidney transplantation. In this research, a comparative performance analysis of multiple supervised ML algorithms was conducted to classify CKD from clinical datasets. Seven classification models-including widely adopted ensemble methods such as Random Forest and Decision Tree-were systematically evaluated. The experimental results reveal that the Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LGBoost) exhibited the highest predictive capability, achieving a classification accuracy of 98.3 %. Moreover, Stochastic Gradient Boosting and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) models demonstrated competitive performance, each attaining an accuracy of 97.5<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">%</sup>, highlighting their potential utility in automated CKD screening systems.

title: Anomaly Detection for Rare Breast Cancer Subtypes
authors: Modukuri Gowthami, V.H. Prasad Reddy, Dasari Venkata Partheep Raghava Rayulu, Nischitha Bitra
publish: 2025 14th International Conference on System Modeling &amp; Advancement in Research Trends (SMART)
abstract: Metaplastic and others uncommon breast cancers, Medullary, and mucinous carcinomas are accounting for less than 5 % of instances and present significant diagnostic challenges due to their low prevalence and heterogeneous histopathological and genomic pro files. Traditional diagnostic methods often misclassify these subtypes, delaying treatment and worsening patient outcomes. We propose a novel anomaly detection framework that combines unsupervised and semi-supervised machine learning to identify uncommon breast cancer subtypes from multimodal data. Convolutional autoencoders are used to analyze histopathological image classification with isolation forests for genomic data processing supplemented by synthetic data generation with conditional generative adversarial networks (GANs). Our framework is validated on large dataset that integrates public repositories and synthetic samples, achieving 93.2 % sensitivity, 90.1 % specificity, and an AUC-ROC of 0.95. Comparative analysis shows our approach outperforms baseline methods,highlighting the potential of anomaly detection for improving early diagnosis and personalized treatment of orphan breast cancer subtypes.

title: Prediction of Electromagnetic Scattering with a Small Dataset Based on Active Learning
authors: De-Hua Kong, Wen-Chi Huang, Wen-Wei Zhang, Jia-Ning Cao, Chao-Fu Wang, Ming-Yao Xia
publish: 2024 IEEE MTT-S International Conference on Numerical Electromagnetic and Multiphysics Modeling and Optimization (NEMO)
abstract: The high cost of generating training datasets is a thorny problem for researchers exploring the applications of artificial intelligence (AI), especially for intelligent electromagnetic (EM) computations, because EM modelling datasets are expensive to produce. The method of active learning (AL) can reduce the cost by selectively labelling data. In this paper, an AL approach is developed to cut down the size of dataset for AI-based prediction of EM scattering problems. By using the electric field boundary conditions, more pertinent targets are screened to constitute a refined dataset. Numerical experiment results show that compared with full supervised learning method, the proposed AL scheme can achieve similar or higher prediction accuracy using a much smaller dataset.

title: Sizing of Feedforward Compensated Operational Amplifiers with TF
authors: Jingwei Zhou, Qin Duan, Guodong Su, Jun Liu
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: This paper presents a no-capacitor feedforward (NCFF) compensation operational amplifier design methodology guided by a combination of transfer function analysis and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$g_{m}/I_{D}$</tex> lookup tables. By deriving the small-signal equivalent circuit model, a transfer function is obtained, from which an analytical expression for pole estimation is formulated. Leveraging <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$g_{m}/ I_{D}$</tex> lookup tables, initial transistor dimensions that enable pole-zero cancellation are determined, thereby mitigating the design ambiguity inherent in unconstrained sizing. A two-stage NCFF amplifier is implemented in UMC 28 nm CMOS. Simulation results show that this approach allows for accurate estimation of both zero-pole locations and unity-gain frequency, effectively guiding the overall amplifier design. The proposed amplifier architecture is well-suited for high-speed analog-to-digital converter applications.

title: A Wideband Millimeter-Wave Eight-Way Power Divider Using Substrate Integrated Coaxial Line
authors: Weihao Zhang, Changjiang Liu, Hailong Wang, Rongqing Xu, Ming Luo, Junjie Huang, Jianwei Xiong, Hua Gan
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: This article presents a wideband eight-way power divider implemented with substrate integrated coaxial lines (SICLs) for millimeter-wave (mmW) applications. In contrast to conventional dispersive transmission lines typically employed in printed circuit board designs, the SICL exhibits a non-dispersive planar structure that supports transverse electromagnetic wave propagation. This characteristic enables the realization of broadband mmW power dividers with enhanced performance. Then, based on SICL technology, a wideband 1-to-2 power divider is designed, and seven such power dividers are integrated for the 1-to-8 power divider network. In addition, a low-loss broadband planar I/O transition structure is designed to achieve efficient power transfer from ground coplanar waveguide (GCPW) to SICL. The proposed eight-way power divider achieves a simulated insertion loss under 2.36 dB and a return loss below -18 dB over the entire 20–40 GHz frequency range. Moreover, the eight-way power divider achieves amplitude imbalance and phase imbalance within the range of ± 0.12 dB and ± 0.7°, respectively, making it a highly promising candidate for various mmW applications.

title: A Multi-domain Image Translative Diffusion StyleGAN for Iris Presentation Attack Detection
authors: Shivangi Yadav, Arun Ross
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: An iris biometric system can be compromised by presentation attacks (PAs) where artifacts such as artificial eyes, printed eye images, or cosmetic contact lenses are presented to the system. To counteract this, several presentation attack detection (PAD) methods have been developed. However, there is a scarcity of datasets for training and evaluating iris PAD techniques due to the implicit difficulties in constructing and imaging PAs. To address this, we introduce the Multi-domain Image Translative Diffusion StyleGAN (MID-StyleGAN), a new framework for generating synthetic ocular images that captures the PA and bonafide characteristics in multiple domains such as bonafide, printed eyes and cosmetic contact lens. MID-StyleGAN combines the strengths of diffusion models and generative adversarial networks (GANs) to produce realistic and diverse synthetic data. Our approach utilizes a multi-domain architecture that enables the translation between bonafide ocular images and different PA domains. The model employs an adaptive loss function tailored for ocular data to maintain domain consistency. Extensive experiments demonstrate that MID-StyleGAN outperforms existing methods in generating high-quality synthetic ocular images. The generated data was used to significantly enhance the performance of PAD systems, providing a scalable solution to the data scarcity problem in iris and ocular biometrics. For example, on the LivDet2020 dataset, the true detect rate at 1% false detect rate improved from 93.41% to 98.72%, showcasing the impact of the proposed method.

title: An optimal allocation method for reactive power compensation considering both steady-state and transient voltage support
authors: Jing Wang, Chun Zheng, Shaoxuan Zhu, Mingming Bo, Jiakai Shen, Hua Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy Power and Electrical Technology (CEPET)
abstract: Aiming at the practical problem of simultaneous steady-state overvoltage and transient voltage instability in power grids after high-proportion distributed generation integration, this paper proposes an optimal allocation method for reactive power compensation that coordinates both steady-state voltage regulation and transient voltage support. By calculating the steady-state overvoltage risk assessment index and the transient voltage severity index, high-risk overvoltage areas and transient voltage instability areas are identified respectively. Subsequently, a configuration and verification scheme for dynamic reactive power compensation devices is proposed. On this basis, the optimal allocation scheme for shunt capacitors/reactors is determined by calculating the improved inductive compensation degree. Ultimately, the optimized configuration of reactive power compensation devices that balances both steady-state voltage regulation and transient support is achieved, effectively enhancing the steady-state and transient voltage stability levels of the power grid.

title: Next-Generation Digital Twin Analytics in Smart Manufacturing using Cross-Layered Edge Cloud Deep Learning
authors: Saisuman Singamsetty, Sudheer Singamsetty, S Satyanarayana, Puranam Revanth Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: The emergence of digital twins is transforming smart manufacturing by enabling real-time system monitoring, predictive maintenance, and optimization of industrial processes. Despite these advantages, traditional cloud-centric analytics face limitations such as high latency, bandwidth constraints, and reduced responsiveness, which hinder deployment in dynamic factory environments. To address these challenges, a cross-layered edge–cloud framework integrated with deep learning is proposed. The architecture employs long short-term memory (LSTM) networks to model temporal dependencies in sensor data, enabling accurate prediction of equipment degradation. Edge devices are responsible for local data acquisition, preprocessing, and lightweight LSTM-based inference, while the cloud layer is utilized for large-scale model training, digital twin synchronization, and advanced analytics. A bidirectional communication strategy ensures that updated cloud models are periodically transferred back to edge nodes, supporting continuous refinement of predictive intelligence while maintaining low-latency inference. The framework is validated using publicly available datasets, including the AI4I 2020 predictive maintenance dataset. Experiments show that our proposed learning model can improve the performance of the cloud manufacturing platform in real-life applications efficiently. Finally, the integration of deep learning with cross-layered edge–cloud computing establishes a scalable and adaptive foundation for digital twin analytics in next-generation smart manufacturing.

title: Optimizing Power System Operation: A Comparative Study of Integrated Power Flow Controller with PI and Fuzzy Logic-Based Controllers During Fault Scenarios
authors: Shruti Shirbhate, Prashant Jagtap
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Electrical energy as the trigger of the “Industrial Revolution” has been considerably important and a constant need to have a large and steady supply of power has been on both residential and industrial uses even today. Electricity transmission costs are difficult to absorb particularly large amounts of electricity, and it is also challenging because it is not flexible. The use of traditional protection and relay systems could not be implemented any longer as it was not designed to handle large loads and the use would impose a high operation cost. To reduce such issues, it has taken the shape of FACTS or flexible AC power transmission system as an efficient means of improving the performance and control of power systems. The paper shall be a comparative study or research of the “Unified Power Flow Controller (UPFC)” operation in a single line to ground (LG) fault situation. Two configurations of the UPFC were also simulated on MATLAB/SIMULINK to study the behavior of the UPFM with a “conventional Proportional-Integral (PI) controller, and a Fuzzy Logic Controller”. The results show that the controller which has been developed with the fuzzy logic method is more efficient and competent in comparison to the traditional PI method.

title: A Multi-Directional Dynamic 3D Scanning System for Underwater Pipelines
authors: Qinghan Hu, Haijiang Zhu, Quan Qiu, Zhengqiang Fan, Xinle Liu, Zhiqing Li
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Refined underwater 3D scanning in dynamic environments is essential for the long-term inspection and maintenance of long-distance pipelines. Existing methods typically rely on pose estimation methods by sensor fusion to align point clouds obtained by multi-frame scans. However, these methods suffer from poor accuracy and robustness under complex underwater disturbances. Additionally, most studies focus on system performance in single-direction scanning, without a thorough evaluation of multi-directional capabilities. This significantly limits their generalizability in variable scenarios. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a highprecision, multi-directional dynamic 3D scanning system for underwater pipelines. The system combines a refined structured light scanning strategy with an improved pose estimation method, enabling robust performance across varying scenes and motion states. Firstly, the rotational scanning is assessed by measuring the point cloud information of a calibration target obtained through 3D scanning. Then, extensive forward and side scanning experiments are carried out under uniform and variable speed conditions. Both visualization and quantitative evaluations confirm that the proposed system significantly improves 3D scanning accuracy and robustness in dynamic scenarios. This demonstrates its strong potential for deployment in underwater robot pipeline inspection.

title: Machine Learning and Deep Learning for Crop Recommendation and Yield Estimation: A Study on Wardha Agricultural Data
authors: Aishwarya Kadu, K.T.V Reddy
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: This research investigates the application of machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) models for agricultural decision-making, specifically focusing on crop recommendation and yield estimation. Utilizing a dataset comprising 3500 entries with 16 features, including village information, soil nutrient levels (Nitrogen, Phosphorus, Potassium, pH, Zinc, Iron, Copper, Manganese), and environmental factors (Rainfall, Temperature, Humidity, Cloud Cover, Wind Speed), alongside historical crop and yield data, we developed and evaluated various models. For crop recommendation (classification), we applied and contrasted the output of Random Forest, Decision Tree, and Logistic Regression, K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) and multilayer perceptron (MLP) based Artificial Neural Network (ANN). Accuracy, precision, recall and F1-score were used to measure model performance. Random Forest Classifier was the most effective in this task with an accuracy of 0.9071, a precision of 0.9119, recall of 0.9071 and a F1-score of 0.9067. For crop yield estimation (regression), we applied and evaluated Random Forest Regressor, Linear Regression, XGBoost, and a Dense layer-based ANN model, using Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), and R-squared (R2) score as evaluation metrics. In this case, the Linear Regression model demonstrated the best performance with a Mean Squared Error (MSE) of 65670.75, Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 196.38, Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of 256.26, and an R-squared (R2) score of 0.85. These findings highlight the potential of both traditional ML and DL techniques in providing valuable insights for optimizing agricultural practices, with specific model suitability depending on the task. Further research could explore hyperparameter tuning and feature importance analysis to enhance predictive accuracy and practical applicability.

title: Differential Privacy-Based Protection Against Web Traffic Analysis Attacks
authors: Qifan Liu
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: Web traffic analysis attacks pose a significant threat to user privacy, as adversaries can infer sensitive browsing behaviors by exploiting statistical patterns of encrypted traffic. To tackle this challenge, this paper proposes a differential privacy-based protection framework for web traffic data. It first constructs a web traffic dataset, selecting key traffic features and labeling sensitive behaviors. Second, three machine learning models—Naive Bayes, Support Vector Machine, and Gradient Boosting Classifier—are trained as predictors to simulate traffic analysis attacks. Third, a differential privacy protection mechanism based on Gaussian noise injection is designed: it clips extreme traffic values to control data sensitivity, calculates noise variance based on privacy parameters, and injects calibrated noise to preserve privacy. Finally, the framework is evaluated under different privacy budgets to quantify the privacy-utility tradeoff, with model performance and traffic distribution changes analyzed to validate its effectiveness. This confirms the framework effectively resists web traffic analysis attacks while retaining the utility of traffic data for normal analysis tasks.

title: Concentric Circle Modeling and SKC Index for Quantitative Analysis of Water-Induced Ballast Degradation in Heavy-Haul Railways
authors: Feng Liu, Fei Chen, Zhi Xu, Jiawei Lan, Tao Su, Zhao Yu
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: This paper presents an electromagnetic simulation framework for quantitative assessment of water-induced ballast degradation in heavy-haul railways. The proposed concentric circle model employs hierarchical spherical units with large-circle-wrapping-small-circle configurations to simulate moisture propagation in ballast-soil systems. By integrating electromagnetic forward modeling with wavelet time-frequency analysis and statistical metrics (kurtosis, skewness, frequency centroid), the framework effectively extracts degradation signatures. A novel sigmoid-based SKC index is developed to correlate dielectric permittivity (ε=5-81) with moisture severity levels (0.00085-0.939), demonstrating strong sensitivity to critical damage thresholds (SKC>0.5). Experimental results validate the framework's capability to bridge theoretical analysis and practical maintenance decision-making, providing a science-based tool for railway infrastructure assessment.

title: Domain-Aware Enhancements to Vision-Language Models for Urban Traffic Safety Question Answering
authors: Vu Thanh Dat Ha, Tuan Huy Tran, Gang Thep Dong, Ngoc Chien Chu, Huan Vu, Tien Cuong Nguyen
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: In the domain of urban traffic safety, accurate scene understanding is essential for applications such as accident prevention and intelligent traffic control. In response to the AI City Challenge 2025 Track 2, we present a significantly enhanced framework based on CityLLaVA, tailored for complex video-based question answering and captioning tasks in traffic environments. Our approach includes: (1) a bounding-box-guided preprocessing pipeline combined with strategic view selection to emphasize critical visual regions; (2) integration of state-of-the-art vision-language models such as Qwen2.5-VL and InternVL3 for robust multi-modal reasoning; and (3) a novel expert-information fusion approach that encodes pedestrian presence, spatial relations, and attention cues to boost model accuracy. We also employ sentence-level decomposition for fine-grained caption supervision and apply lightweight adaptation techniques for efficient model training. Our system achieves a final score of 57.1133 on the official leaderboard and ranks 6th among competing teams, demonstrating the effectiveness of our methods in addressing the nuanced challenges of urban traffic video understanding.

title: Graphene: Leveraging Transformers with Control Flow Modalities for Malware Detection
authors: Andrew Wheeler, Kshitiz Aryal, Maanak Gupta
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: The proliferation of malicious programs for the Windows operating system has highlighted the need for detection frameworks that protect these vital systems. Traditional malware detection approaches struggle to generalize across the diverse and evolving behavioral patterns that are demonstrated by modern malware. In order to gain an upper hand against malware developers, researchers have experimented with varying representations of programs that can be used in conjunction with machine learning models to automatically detect and mitigate malware threats.This work presents Graphene, a graph-based malware detection framework that leverages a RoBERTa-based classification model for malware detection. Graphene encodes program behavior through graph representations that are subsequently linearized into function-call sequences, enabling the use of advanced language-model architectures for malware detection. We evaluate Graphene on a dataset of approximately 150,000 Windows Portable Execubable (PE) files and benchmark its performance against two conventional machine learning models: a deep neural network (DNN) and a recurrent neural network (RNN). Experimental results demonstrate that Graphene significantly outperforms these baselines, achieving 94% accuracy with a false positive rate below 4%. These findings show the effectiveness of transformer-based architectures, when informed by graph representations, in advancing the state of the art automated malware detection.

title: Nonlinear Admittance Control Enhanced by Sliding Mode for Collaborative Manipulators
authors: Mengdan Wang, Zeping Zhan, Fupeng Li, Yisheng Guan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Achieving both compliance and robustness in collaborative robot interaction remains a significant challenge. This paper proposes a dual-channel compliant control framework that integrates sliding mode control with a shearthickening fluid admittance model. An event-triggered gain state machine dynamically enhances damping and compensation capabilities under strong disturbances. The upper control layer employs a saturation-based sliding mode controller to suppress uncertainties and external disturbances, while the lower layer adaptively modulates damping via a power-law model. A three-mode state machine governs control switching based on acceleration and force thresholds. Simulation results demonstrate that, compared with conventional approaches, the proposed method significantly improves disturbance velocity suppression while maintaining high compliance. The method effectively enhances safety and adaptability in physical human-robot interaction and provides a viable strategy for collaborative control in dynamic and uncertain environments.

title: A BioCM-Based Multi-Exposure Multi-Focused Target Recognition Approach for LDI Machines
authors: Ruizhou Wang, Peihan Liu, Qifeng Zhong
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: The printed-circuit-board (PCB) warpage, out-of-focused-plane (OFP) blur, target anomalies, or illumination abnormalities impact the accuracy and robustness of the target recognition system for laser direct-imaging (LDI) machines. A multi-exposure (ME) multi-focused (MF) target recognition method is proposed in this paper. Drawing inspiration from pigeons, a bionic compliant mechanism (BioCM) was designed for a new target recognition system. ME and MF image fusion algorithms were developed using the wavelet decomposition. A revised randomized Hough transform (RRHT) algorithm was employed to suppress the OFP blur, repair defect targets, and compensate for illumination abnormalities. A dedicated target dataset of target defects for LDI machines was constructed. The quantitative evaluation indicators were derived. The prototype of the BioCM was fabricated and tested. The proposed BioCM, ME, MF, and RRHT algorithms were examined inside an LDI machine. Test results under disturbances demonstrate that the recognition accuracy of the BioCM-based ME method was improved by 29.7%, and the accuracy of the BioCM-based MF approach was improved by 19.7%. The BioCM-based MEMF approach contributes to developing a robust target recognition system for LDI machines.

title: Design of Wideband High-Gain Millimeter-Wave Antenna-in-Package Based on LTCC Technology
authors: Yulin Peng, Pin Wen, Xiaosheng Yu, Jinyu Liu
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: This paper presents a millimeter-wave antenna-in-package (AiP) based on low-temperature co-fired ceramic (LTCC) technology. The design employs a composite feeding structure integrating grounded coplanar waveguide (GCPW) and substrate integrated waveguide (SIW). Efficient electromagnetic coupling between the radiating structure and feeding network is achieved by an H-shaped slot. To enhance the antenna's bandwidth and gain, a square radiating patch is adopted in the radiating structure, with a 2×2 array of parasitic patches superimposed above its four corners. Simulation results demonstrate that within the 31.72-34.98 GHz millimeter-wave band, the antenna exhibits a return loss below −10 dB, achieving a relative bandwidth of 9.8%. Furthermore, the gain exceeds 7 dBi across most of the impedance bandwidth, with a peak gain reaching 8 dBi. Compared to the millimeter-wave AiP without parasitic patches, the proposed design achieves a bandwidth expansion of 2.96 GHz and a peak gain improvement of 3.02 dBi.

title: A Robotic Arm Control Method Integrating Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning and Fuzzy Control
authors: Yuchen Feng, Fengtao Wang, Shitong Peng, Jianan Gu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: With the advancement of industrial automation, intelligent robotic arm control has become a prominent research direction, though it still faces numerous challenges. This paper proposes a hierarchical reinforcement learning algorithm for robotic arm control that incorporates fuzzy control. We first review existing robotic arm control methods and their associated challenges. To address issues such as complex reward design, long training time, and low success rates in reinforcement learning, we propose a control strategy that integrates fuzzy control with a proximal policy optimization framework. This method employs a hierarchical reinforcement learning structure based on a “pre-training + integration” paradigm. In the training of low-level tasks, fuzzy control is embedded to guide learning. Fuzzy control is combined in the training of the underlying task. Experimental results demonstrate that incorporating fuzzy control helps quickly correct errors, reducing unnecessary exploration and thereby accelerating training and improving task success rates. Specifically, compared with traditional PPO, Fuzzy-PPO converges approximately 20% faster, with an average success rate for underlying tasks increasing by 10%, while also being more stable than traditional PPO. Overall, the proposed method effectively addresses key limitations of reinforcement learning and offers a more precise control strategy for robotic arms.

title: Leveraging T5 and Recurrent Gans for Improved Sarcasm Generation in Natural Language Processing
authors: Ashish Kumar Verma, Shruti, Manik Rakhra, Girish Kumar, Mandeep Singh, Kapil Jairath
publish: 2025 1st IEEE Uttar Pradesh Section Women in Engineering International Conference on Electrical Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPWIECON)
abstract: Sarcasm, a type of metaphorical language, is an important part of interpersonal interactions that frequently conveys meanings that are contradictory to the literal reading. Sarcasm, while common in ordinary interactions, poses particular challenges for natural language processing (NLP) systems since it is subtle and context dependent. This paper presents a novel method for generating sarcasm that employs advanced techniques such as text enhancement, transfer learning, along with assessment strategies. Our technique begins with pretraining a Transformer-based T5 model on a large dataset of sardonic and non-sarcastic texts. The model is then fine-tuned with an updated dataset produced by Recurrent Generational Adversarial Networks (RGAN). We improve the generation process by using sampling approaches to encourage a variety of outputs, and we evaluate the model's effectiveness with sarcasm detection systems. When compared to typical baseline models, experimental results show significant gains in creating varied and context-appropriate sarcastic responses.

title: Design Phase-Locked Loop with ADC and DAC for Gyro Sensing Applications
authors: Wen-Cheng Lai, Chun-Yu Chiu, Bo-Han Peng
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: The proposed charge pump phase-locked loop (CPPLL) with successive approximation register analog -to- digital converter (SAR-ADC) and multi-bit digital-to-analog converter (DAC) of current-steering are applied and implemented. Designed CPPLL contains Colpittis voltage-controlled oscillator (VCO), injection locked frequency divider (ILFD) with re-timer and MASH 1-1-1 Σ-Δ Modulator, time-amplifying phase-frequency detector (TAPFD), respectively. The injection locked frequency divider (ILFD) is utilized in first divider stage to handle the sensing frequency signal for Gyro sensing applications.

title: Application of a ResNet50 Model Incorporating Attention Mechanism for Dangerous Driving Behavior Recognition
authors: Hengshu Liu, Quan Zhang, Jundi Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Communication (ICAIRC)
abstract: This paper proposes an improved ResNet50 dangerous driving behavior recognition algorithm that incorporates multi-scale spatial-channel attention (MSCA) to improve recognition accuracy and robustness in complex driving scenarios. The algorithm embeds an MSCA module within the residual blocks of the third and fourth stages of the ResNet50 model. It extracts fine-grained to coarse-grained channel features through multi-dilation rate convolutions and fuses multi-resolution spatial features to generate joint attention weights for optimized feature representation. Experiments were conducted on a public Kaggle dataset and a self-built complex scene dataset (27,424 images totaling 10 categories of behavior). Using the same training configuration, the original ResNet50, ResNet50+SE, and ResNet50+CBAM were compared. The results show that MSCA-ResNet50 achieves precision, recall, and F1 scores of 97.8%, 96.9%, and 97.3%, respectively. These improvements represent 5.5,6.8, and 6.1 percentage points over ResNet50, and 2.7, 3.4, and 3.0 percentage points over CBAM. The inference speed is 70 FPS, meeting real-time requirements. Ablation experiments demonstrate that multi-scale attention significantly outperforms single-scale approaches, with the fourth embedding stage achieving the best performance. Robustness tests show that low-light accuracy (100–300 lux) remains at 94.1%, and accuracy remains at 93.5% with hand occlusion of 40%-50%, a 3.9 percentage point improvement over CBAM. The results demonstrate the robustness of the proposed method under varying lighting conditions and occlusion, providing a highly accurate and deployable solution for identifying dangerous driving behaviors in the intelligent transportation sector.

title: An Efficient Transients Simulation Model of Grid-Connected Battery Energy Storage System
authors: Jiale Zhang, Peng Zhao, Yue Xia
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: This paper formulates a multi-scale grid-connected model customized for simulating various transients in battery energy storage systems (BESSs). The proposed model is composed of three fundamental components: a primary system, a control system, and an interface model. Specifically, the electrical performance of pivotal components in the primary system, including the multi-scale voltage source converter (VSC) and filter, is characterized via analytic signals, whose Fourier spectra are capable of being shifted. Conversely, the battery model and control system are established on real signals. To achieve effective integration between the analytic signals of the primary system and the real signals of the control system, a high-fidelity interface is constructed. The model incorporates shift frequency as a newly defined parameter to enlarge the simulation time-step. Setting the shift frequency to zero allows the model to directly process instantaneous signals that represent natural waveforms; when assigned a value matching the primary system's characteristic frequency, the analytic signals are transformed into envelope form, enabling accurate envelope tracking. The shift frequency is adaptively determined according to the transient characteristics detected during simulation. Test cases verify the proposed multi-scale grid-connected model, showing its capacity to enable accurate and efficient simulation of both electromagnetic and electromechanical transients in a single simulation iteration.

title: Generalizable Sign Language Recognition via Local Temporal Convolutions and Region-Aware Pose Encoding
authors: Sieu Tran, Duc Nguyen Minh, Truong Nguyen Thanh, Hao Vo, Thanh Duc Ngo, Tien Do, Khiem Le, Duy-Dinh Le
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Continuous Sign Language Recognition (CSLR) aims to translate sign language videos into gloss-level annotations. While RGB-based models leveraging global sequence models have shown strong performance, they often face challenges in generalizing to unseen sentence compositions due to their reliance on visual appearance and long-range context modeling. In this work, we present a lightweight poseonly CSLR framework that focuses on improving robustness in the unseen sentence setting, where sentence compositions at test time differ from those observed during training. Our approach uses pose keypoints as the sole input modality to reduce visual redundancy and background noise. We explore a locally conditioned decoding strategy based on temporal convolution to mitigate potential overfitting associated with global sequence models. Through empirical analysis on a large-scale signer-independent benchmark, we find that our method achieves competitive performance compared to published RGB-based baselines, despite relying solely on 2D pose input. These results suggest that pose-driven representations, combined with localized temporal modeling and multilevel supervision, offer a promising and efficient direction for building robust CSLR systems.

title: Cybersecurity Architecture for Cloud Database Infrastructure- A Case Study of Oracle Autonomous Database on Microsoft Azure
authors: Chaitanya Kulkarni, Chandrashekhar Medicherla, Bharadwaj Vulugundam, Rajesh Purushothaman, Isan Sahoo, Arun Kumar Elengovan
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: Enterprise cloud adoption continues to accelerate, yet securing database infrastructure across public cloud platforms remains a persistent challenge for organizations handling sensitive data. This paper presents a comprehensive security framework for Oracle Autonomous Database deployed on Microsoft Azure, addressing the complete lifecycle of cloud database security from network isolation through compliance automation. Our five-layer architecture integrates Oracle's database security capabilities with Azure's native security services, creating a defense-in-depth approach validated through production deployments. The framework encompasses network security using private endpoints and Virtual Network isolation, federated identity management via Azure Entra ID, customer-managed encryption with Azure Key Vault integration, database-level controls through Oracle Database Vault and Data Safe, and unified compliance monitoring across 25+ regulatory frameworks. Production implementations demonstrate 99.99% uptime while maintaining automated security patching, sub-5-minute threat detection response, and zero-downtime key rotation. We provide detailed implementation patterns for Azure Key Vault transparent data encryption, OAuth2 authentication flows, and DevSecOps automation templates. While focused on Azure deployment, the architectural principles establish foundational patterns applicable to broader multicloud database strategies. This work contributes practical security guidance for enterprise architects deploying mission-critical database workloads in public cloud environments.

title: A Fast Simulation Method for Large-Scale Phased Array Antennas
authors: Yixian Mei, Zhangyou Chen, Wei Cheng
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel fast simulation method for large-scale phased array antenna that effectively addresses the prohibitive computational costs associated with conventional full-wave techniques. The methodology combines element grouping with full-information stitching, where elements exhibiting similar electromagnetic characteristics are identified through rigorous symmetry and mutual coupling analysis. By simulating only representative elements using full-wave techniques and storing their coupling/radiation data, the approach enables arbitrary beam steering through phase adjustment and linear superposition without requiring repeated simulations. Validation studies conducted on both 5×5 and 100×80 back-fed microstrip patch arrays demonstrate excellent agreement with CST simulations, achieving accurate beam pointing while input impedance deviations below 5.4%. Notably, the method reduces the simulation time for a 100×80 array from several weeks to mere minutes, facilitating real-time analysis and design optimization. Compared to existing mutual coupling and array synthesis techniques, this approach uniquely maintains full-wave-level accuracy while dramatically reducing computational overhead, making it particularly valuable for applications in 5G/6G systems, radar technology, and satellite communications, especially for dynamic beamforming, fault diagnosis, and rapid prototyping scenarios.

title: A Method for Detecting the Outer Diameter of High-Voltage Cable Joints Based on 3D Point Clouds
authors: Pengcheng Xu, Yunchu Mei, Yikai Wu, Jianjun Wang, Tianjiao Li, Yuchuan Zheng, Hongyan Wu
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: The quality inspection of high-voltage cable laying still relies on manual work, and issues such as irregular shapes after manual grinding result in low measurement accuracy. Additionally, since two-dimensional images only contain color and grayscale information, existing machine vision measurement technologies are limited in measuring the outer diameter parameters. This paper proposes a non-contact method for measuring the outer diameter parameters of high-voltage cable joints based on three-dimensional point clouds. First, the threedimensional point cloud data is preprocessed; then, a bidirectional point cloud slicing method is proposed to extract effective cable information for accurate measurement. Next, the normalized mean curvature is used as a weight for averaging point cloud coordinates, constructing key points of the surface slices to detect the outer diameter parameters of the cable joints. Finally, a userfriendly cable joint processing and quality inspection device will be developed to provide a convenient and reliable detection method for practical applications.

title: Research on the Division Method and Optimization Control Strategy of Large Scale Distributed Photovoltaic Clusters
authors: Xinwei Li, Qiang Zhang, Chao Wang, Yao Fu, Xiaowei Gong
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: The power output of distributed photovoltaic clusters heavily relies on meteorological conditions, which leads to significant intermittency in their power output. This characteristic has had a certain impact on the stable operation of the power grid.
