This paper presents a novel fractional-order hybrid control strategy for AVR systems using a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$P I^{\lambda}-\left(1+P D^{\mu}\right)-F$</tex> controller, where the fuzzy logic controller (F) acts as a dynamic gain modulator for the fractional <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$P D^{\mu}$</tex> path. The proposed control scheme synergistically combines the memory and flexibility of fractional calculus with the adaptive decision-making capability of fuzzy inference, resulting in superior control performance under varying system conditions. The controller parameters, including the fractional orders (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\lambda$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu$</tex>) and scaling factors of the fuzzy interface, are optimally tuned using a metaheuristic algorithm called Tree-seed algorithm (TSA) to achieve an optimal trade-off among settling time, peak overshoot, and steady-state accuracy. Simulation studies on the AVR model validate that the proposed controller superior to traditional PI, FOPI, and fuzzy-PID variants, particularly in scenarios involving load perturbations and parameter uncertainties, thereby offering an efficient and intelligent control framework for voltage regulation in modern power systems.

title: CDMIRRT*: Multi-Strategy Motion Planning with Conditional Diffusion Models
authors: Xinzhi Zhou, Zhouzhou Huang, Fan Wu, Xuanhao Zhao, Yiqi Ma, Wenyu Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Sampling-based motion planning (SBMP) is widely favored for its ability to navigate complex environments by implicitly representing the state space via probe samples. While traditional approaches often rely on uniform sampling to ensure coverage, robotic systems typically operate within constrained sub-manifolds due to differential limitations or obstacle avoidance. To improve planning efficiency, it is es-sential to develop non-uniform sampling strategies that focus on high-probability regions. In this work, we introduce a hybrid framework that leverages a learned sampling distri-bution derived from demonstrations, integrated with Informed RRT*. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a non-uniform sampling method that learns a sampling distribution from demonstrations and combines it with Informed RRT* for biased sampling. This sampling distribution is learned using a conditional diffusion model (CDM), which generates samples from pure Gaussian noise conditioned on the planning problem's specific requirements. This methodology is general-izable, effectively exploits the underlying structure of planning problems, and importantly, preserves the theoretical guarantees of sampling-based methods. Experimental results demonstrate that the method can effectively learn representations of relevant regions of the state space, thereby converging to lower-cost paths in fewer iterations.

title: Gas Sensor Drift Compensation and Concentration Quantification
authors: Saurabh Suman, Amit Gudadhe, Naresh Naathi, Pooja G. Dhawane, Abhishek Kumar, Rahul Raushan
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Gas sensors are important for environmental monitoring, industrial safety, and even healthcare diagnostics. Sensor drift, the systematic deviation of sensor response over time, plays a critical role in affecting long-term reliability. This study attempts to tackle the issue by proposing a machine learning framework based on the Random Forest Regressor so that gas concentrations could be predicted while compensating for the effect of sensor drift. The authors demonstrate the capability of the model to generalize across time drift conditions with a large dataset of 13,910 measurements from 16 different chemical sensors exposed to six gases (Ammonia, Acetaldehyde, Acetone, Ethylene, Ethanol, and Toluene) over 36 months. Important innovations include exhaustive preprocessing, dynamic feature selection focusing on transient sensor responses, and hyperparameter optimization using grid search. The model achieved a validation MSE of 78.12 and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex> of 0.997 with strong performance on unseen test data (MSE: 901.07, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}: 0.969$</tex>), outperforming Gradient Boosting (MSE: 1245.14, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex>: 0.958). Analysis of feature importance highlighted Sensor 106 (32.4%) and Sensor <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$98(21.5 \%)$</tex> as actively resistant to drift, thus stressing the importance of transient response dynamics. The gradual performance drop in later batches (e.g. Batch <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10 \mathrm{R}^{2}: 0.92$</tex>) showed that the model could still outperform previous works. With this, the practical usefulness of Random Forests has been duly agreed on for drift compensation, which can result in a 40 % less frequent need for calibration. Further directions will include hybrid architectures that integrate LSTM with edge deployment for real-time monitoring and incorporate environmental variables to enhance robustness. This work unites the two worlds of sensor reliability and machine learning in an affirmative step on the path to scalable solutions for industrial safety and environmental preservation.

title: AI-Driven Digital Twins and Data-Driven Smart Manufacturing in the Textile Industry: Integrating Domain Expertise with Intelligent Systems
authors: Sehajpreet Kaur, Pushpinder Singh, Navjot Kaur Cheema, Nemai Chandra Ray, Sarwan Singh
publish: 2025 1st IEEE Uttar Pradesh Section Women in Engineering International Conference on Electrical Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPWIECON)
abstract: The research explores the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine Learning (ML), and Digital Twin technologies within the textile industry to facilitate the transition from traditional manufacturing practices to smart, data-driven systems under the Industry 4.0 paradigm. Leveraging over three decades of industrial expertise in textile engineering combined with advanced knowledge of AI and ML, we present a comprehensive framework for modernizing textile operations. Applications include yarn quality parameters prediction, real-time Knitting machine/Loom efficiency monitoring, Sentiment analysis through customer review/ feedback data, prediction of mixing category required for specific yarn quality parameters, predictive maintenance and computer-vision based defect detection. This study highlights the role of AI-driven digital twins in simulating, analyzing, and optimizing textile processes, offering a roadmap for achieving higher efficiency, flexibility, and sustainability in textile production.

title: An Experimental Evaluation of Prompt Injection Attacks on LLM-Based Content Moderation Systems
authors: Fabio Tortora, Vincenzo De Angelis
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Social Networks Analysis, Management and Security (SNAMS)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed to automate content moderation on social platforms, offering efficiency and scalability far beyond human capabilities. However, they are highly vulnerable to prompt injection—maliciously crafted inputs designed to manipulate model behavior and bypass intended safeguards. In this paper, we experimentally evaluate the robustness of three widely used LLMs—OpenAI, Gemini, and Mistral—against direct prompt injection attacks in a controlled moderation setting. Under identical system prompts and attack conditions, the three models displayed different levels of robustness. Initial results showed varying performance impacts, with some models experiencing more noticeable degradation than others. Moreover, a refined injection using a differently concatenated user prompt was able to further reduce the effectiveness of the moderator when considering the model that initially achieved the best performance, suggesting that subtle variations in attack design can significantly influence outcomes. These findings highlight significant differences in model robustness and underscore the importance of rigorous evaluation and informed model selection for highstakes content moderation applications, where failures can have real-world consequences.

title: Neuralink: Transforming Brain–Computer Interfaces for Medical Applications and Human—AI Collaboration
authors: Prajwal Bandre, Subodh Daronde, Palash Gourshettiwar, Diksha D. Gabhane, Trishul Dhale, Sanchita Barade
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Neuralink was founded by Elon Musk with the aim of creating devices for accurately detecting and changing brain signals using tiny implants, a way of connecting the brain to computers. The ultra-thin, flexible wires are attached to a small chip called the Link, which enables the mind to talk directly to machines outside the body and wirelessly send data. The setup consists of wireless data transmission, signal processing elements, hair-like wires in the brain, and their safe positioning with the use of a robot-assisted technique. Besides discussing structure, practical health applications include enabling the movement of paralyzed individuals, treating nerve-related sickness, and making good control of prosthetic limbs possible. The study of upgraded humans also examines body compatibility, personal data, and issues of safety and ethics. Generally speaking, Neuralink symbolizes a big move towards blending brain science with intelligent devices, and this might be the beginning of new development either in health technology or human-AI collaboration.

title: Soft Helical Structure Design and Experimental Validation for Capsule Robots
authors: Jiakai Cheng, Xinkai Yu, Jiaole Wang, Shuang Song
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: At present, most commercial capsule robots are passive, which is inefficient and uncontrollable. To improve the diagnosis and treatment effect, it is necessary to incorporate active control and reduce tissue damage. This paper studies the soft helical structure of the capsule with the spiral locomotion. Multiple sets of silicone rubber soft thread prototypes are designed and fabricated. The mechanical model of the interaction between the intestine and the helical structure is established. Through motion experiments in porcine intestines and simulated liquid environments, the influence of various factors on the motion performance of the soft helical structure is studied. The optimal parameters were selected by comprehensively considering the driving effect and safety. In the simulated liquid environment, the average speed could reach <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.62 \text{mm} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> in horizontal line and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.21 \text{mm} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> in the 20° inclined linear climb. In isolated porcine intestines, the average speed could reach <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$21.48 \text{mm} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> in horizontal line, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$13.01 \text{mm} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> in the curved path and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$19.08 \text{mm} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> in the 20° inclined linear climb.

title: Adaptive Reconstruction for Collaborative Prediction of Multi-Channel EEGs
authors: Yunbing Wei
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Communication (ICAIRC)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel Adaptive Phase Space Reconstruction-Diffusion Maps-Gaussian Process Regression (APSR-DM-GPR) framework for multi-channel EEG signal prediction. Traditional EEG prediction methods often struggle with the nonlinear, non-stationary characteristics of neural signals and fail to effectively utilize spatiotemporal correlations across multiple channels. To address these limitations, our framework integrates nonlinear dynamics theory with modern machine learning techniques through three core components: adaptive parameter estimation for phase space reconstruction, diffusion maps for nonlinear dimensionality reduction, and Gaussian process regression for probabilistic prediction. Experimental results on a 64-channel EEG dataset demonstrate the framework's exceptional performance, achieving an average coefficient of determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex>) of 0.9909, significantly outperforming LSTM (0.9396) and ARIMA (0.8896) models. The proposed method exhibits strong robustness, multi-channel synergy, and interpretability, making it suitable for clinical applications including epileptic seizure prediction, sleep stage analysis, brain-computer interfaces, and neurodegenerative disease monitoring. The standardized design ensures easy extension to other spatiotemporal sequence prediction tasks, offering a new technical pathway for neural signal processing research.

title: Agricultural Productivity Revolution: The Combined Synergies of Remote Sensing and Artificial Intelligence
authors: Indresh Bahadur Rajwade, Alok Misra, Binnu Paul
publish: 2025 14th International Conference on System Modeling &amp; Advancement in Research Trends (SMART)
abstract: This paper analyzes the potential change capability of remote sensing technologies and Artificial Intelligence (AI) on sustainable agricultural development. In the context of increasing food demand in the world market and the growing threat of climate change, the development of precision agriculture with the help of these high-tech achievements becomes a very important direction in efficient production of crops, optimization of resources, and predictability of crops. The paper has examined existing and new remote sensing methods that offer crucial information on crop health, soil characteristics/environment. In addition, it also brings to the fore the use of machine learning and deep learning algorithms to analyze massive data to come up with actionable intelligence that can be used to manage crops better, diagnose pests and diseases earlier, optimize inputs, and optimally predict yields. Based on the field studies, the paper highlights the practicality of the integration and synergistic advantages to remote sensing and AI towards advancing precision, sustainability and economic gains in the Indian agricultural sector. Moreover, the paper critically assesses the technical, infrastructural, policy and skill factors hindering a broad-based adoption with its recommendations provided in areas like digital infrastructure, open access to data, capacity development, and collaboration between the government and the private sector. Placing remote sensing and AI as an enabler of resilience and inclusivity as source of data will reinforce this fact: the two are capable of doing more than enhancing productivity and sustainability; they can shape the future of agriculture in India amid the global challenge.

title: Design and Analysis of High Torque Density Joint for Humanoid Biped Robot
authors: Jianyin Tang, Mingguang Dai, Qiaoman Zhu, Chao Fang, Bin Lan, Kun Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Legged robots require rapid motion response capabilities to adapt to complex environments. The joints of the legged robots need to have the ability to provide instantaneous high torque. This paper develops high torque density modular joints and applies them to a humanoid biped robot. We optimize the design of the outer rotor frameless torque motor based on the magnetic circuit method and multi-objective optimization approach. Subsequently, we develop two highly integrated types of modular joints. Compared with some current modular joints, these two types can deliver greater torque under the same volume. All output ends of the joints are equipped with crossed roller bearings to prevent axial forces from affecting the internal structure of the modular joints. Finally, we obtain the current-torque curves of the joints through a dynamic loading system. The peak torque densities of these two types of modular joints are 200 Nm/kg and 90 Nm/kg respectively. Additionally, we construct a prototype of a humanoid biped robot and conduct in-place stepping and disturbance rejection experiments using reinforcement learning algorithms. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed joints meets the motion requirements of humanoid robots.

title: Using Multi-Layer Attention Mechanism Graph Neural Network to Achieve Accurate Fault Location in Distribution Network
authors: Tao Ji, Yuhong Wu, Cheng Wu, Jinwei Lin, Mingjie Xu
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: Accurate fault identification and precise localization remain critical challenges in modern distribution network operations. To overcome the limitations of conventional methods in handling complex grid topologies, we propose a Hierarchical Meta-path-guided Attentive Fault Detection framework for Distribution Networks (HMA-FDDN). Our approach transforms the grid topology into a heterogeneous graph structure, where nodes represent electrical components and edges model connectivity branches. The framework employs graph embedding techniques to extract latent topological features and introduces a novel meta-path-guided hierarchical attention mechanism that prioritizes learning of critical fault-related nodes through multi-hop connectivity patterns. Comprehensive experimental evaluations demonstrate significant improvements in both fault classification accuracy and localization precision, with cross-validation results confirming enhanced robustness against measurement noise and topology variations. This advancement provides a data-driven solution for intelligent fault diagnosis in evolving smart grid infrastructures.

title: Smart Operation and Maintenance Technology for Integrated Energy Systems Based on Digital Twins
authors: Zhenlan Dou, Chunyan Zhang, Hongkun Lyu, Jiaying Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy Power and Electrical Technology (CEPET)
abstract: Due to fragmented and delayed operational information in integrated energy systems, maintenance efficiency remains low. This paper proposes a digital twin-based intelligent operation and maintenance technology for integrated energy systems. Sensors are scientifically selected and adapted for different energy subsystems and critical equipment. High-speed, stable data acquisition devices convert and transmit data. Multisource data fusion technology integrates and analyzes heterogeneous data from multiple sources to build a comprehensive and efficient state-sensing network. Based on system data from the perception network, a comprehensive digital twin model is constructed. This facilitates information exchange and closed-loop control across layers, achieving intelligent lifecycle maintenance. The experiment shows that the proposed method keeps the deviation rate between the plan and the actual below $5 \%$, significantly improving the operational efficiency of the integrated energy system.

title: Analyzing the Influence of Propagation Models on Effectiveness of Ad hoc Routing Protocols
authors: K. C. Kullayappa Naik, P. Ramana Reddy, Ch. Balaswamy
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: Mobile Ad hoc Network (MANET) is a kind of wireless ad hoc network consists of set of low power wireless nodes that can move arbitrarily in any direction and forming a temporary network without any pre-existing infrastructure. Due to its mobility nature of network, distance between the nodes in the network increases and received signal strength becomes decreases gradually which in turn reduces the performance of MANET. Therefore, propagation models are incorporated in the network and plays a significant role for exchanging data between the nodes. The performance of the network depends on several factors including node density, mobility model, transmission range, and node movement. In this paper, various propagation models have been taken into consideration for enhancing the connectivity between the nodes, which ultimately enhances the performance of protocol in ad hoc network. The simulations have been performed in NS-3 by taking various performance evaluation metrics. Finally, it has been concluded that Optimized Link State Routing (OLSR) protocol with Nakagami model provides best performance over other propagation models because received signal strength varies due to multipath fading in Nakagami model. The simulation results have revealed that the propagation model has a direct impact on the performance of protocols in ad hoc network.

title: Robotic USB-A Insertion Assembly Strategy for Pose Uncertainty
authors: Xianda Wang, Qinghua Lu, Weilin Chen, Shijie Tang, Qinghua Zhang, Lufeng Luo, Mingyou Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: The USB-A interface is widely used in electronic devices, however, the current USB cable quality inspection process heavily relies on manual labor, in order to achieve automated testing of USB cables, the first step is to achieve USBA insertion assembly. This paper proposes a USB-A insertion assembly strategy that combines visual, force feedback, and pose information to address the uncertainty of initial contact pose. The entire assembly process uses a hybrid force/position control scheme based on admittance control to realize the interaction between the robot and the environment. First, The method for adjusting the position between the peg and hole was determined by analyzing the assembly clearance, and the search operation was completed by combining the search trajectory generated by vision. Then, by attempting to insert action perception orientation and using a “lift-rotate” action for orientation adjustment. Finally, an assembly experimental platform is built to verify the effectiveness of the proposed assembly strategy. The experimental results show that the success rate of the USB-A assembly strategy proposed in this paper is 100 %.

title: An Analytical Current Model for Subwavelength Gaps Loaded Metasurfaces
authors: Shaohua Zhang, Di Zhang, Yikai Chen, Shiwen Yang
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: Due to the introduction of strong capacitive coupling effect in subwavelength gaps loaded metasurfaces, there is a clear discrepancy between the actual current distribution on the metasurface and traditional analytical current model. This paper presents an accurate analytical current model derived from spectral Green's function, which incorporates the frequency-dependent and angle-dependent information. Furthermore, the scattering parameters are computed from the equivalent circuit model (ECM) through Floquet modal expansion, which could offer an efficient approach in the design process.

title: A Multimode Resonator Bandpass Filter for X-Band Applications
authors: Junshi Zhao, Guodong Su, Xiwang Dai, Jun Liu
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: This paper proposes a multimode resonator bandpass filter for X-band applications, which features broad bandwidth, high rejection capability, and ease of fabrication. By employing a multimode resonator structure, multiple transmission zeros are excited and coupled with each other. Additionally, the integration of parallel microstrip lines and open stubs between the coupled lines and port microstrip lines, along with the addition of open stubs between the coupled lines, combined with the multimode resonance strategy, significantly expands the filter's bandwidth while enhancing its out-of-band suppression level and roll-off coefficient. Simulation results show that the filter achieves a center frequency of 10.25 GHz, an insertion loss of 0.83 dB, and a relative bandwidth of 80.74%.

title: Legal Documentation Using AI & NLP Techniques
authors: Aryan Zingade, Sakshi Jadhav, Archana Lakhe, Shuvra Das
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: The analysis of legal documentation and summarizing, is a very time-consuming task and requires great expertise. Although there are many tools to ease the life of legal professionals, they are in some way incapable of offering full-fledged efficient solutions that meet multifaceted challenges related to legal practice. This paper proposes the conceptual design of an advanced legal analysis and research tool that aims to bridge those gaps currently existing through state-of-the-art natural language processing models and techniques in machine learning. In addition, efficiency and accuracy in the process of legal documents are the main objectives of the proposed tool. Of course, much has been done in this space already, wherein Named Entity Recognition models for detecting critical entities in clauses. The solutions above are more or less fragmented and not well-integrated, hence less efficient. This project develops further from the existing models and integrates them into a coherent system for improving at all stages of legal document processing. We train and fine-tune our models using a set of legal document templates and case law databases. That means by developing a model like Legal Pegasus for paraphrasing and summarization, it will be able to classify the documents correctly, and legal workflow will further become streamlined and be able to categorize documents effectively, further streamlining the legal workflow. Unlike prior systems that focus on isolated tasks such as NER or summarization, our framework integrates multiple NLP modules, namely summarization, question answering, and case law recommendation, into a unified legal automation pipeline. This holistic design improves accuracy, efficiency, and usability compared to earlier standalone tools.

title: From Threads to Tiles: T2T, a Compiler for CUDA-to-NPU Translation via 2D Vectorization
authors: Shuaijiang Li, Jiacheng Zhao, Ying Liu, Shuoming Zhang, Lei Chen, Yijin Li, Yangyu Zhang, Zhicheng Li, Runyu Zhou, Xiyu Shi, Chunwei Xia, Yuan Wen, Xiaobing Feng, Huimin Cui
publish: 2026 IEEE/ACM International Symposium on Code Generation and Optimization (CGO)
abstract: CUDA’s programming model, exposing massive parallelism via fine-grained scalar threads, has become the de facto standard for GPU computing. Concurrently, NPUs are emerging as highly efficient accelerators, but their architecture is fundamentally different, relying on coarse-grained, explicit 2-D tile-based instructions. This creates a critical challenge: bridging the semantic gap "From Threads to Tiles". A direct translation is infeasible, as it requires lifting the implicit parallelism of CUDA’s scalar model into the explicit, multi-dimensional vector space of NPUs, a problem we formalize as a lifting challenge.This paper introduces T2T, a compiler framework that automates this "Threads to Tiles" translation via the 2-D Vectorization technique. T2T first transforms a CUDA kernel’s implicit SIMT parallelism into a structured, explicit loop nest via our Unified Parallelism Abstraction (UPA), making the parallelism analyzable. From this representation, T2T’s core vectorization engine systematically selects optimal pairs of loops and maps them onto the NPU’s 2-D tile instructions to maximize hardware utilization. To ensure correctness and handle performance-critical CUDA features, a final set of semantics-preserving optimizations is applied, including efficient control-flow management and vectorization of warp-level intrinsics.We implement T2T based on Polygeist and evaluate representative NPU architectures. On a diverse set of benchmarks, kernels translated by T2T achieve up to 73% of native CUDA performance on an A100 GPU and outperform baseline translation approaches by up to 6.9×. Our work demonstrates that a systematic, compiler-driven approach to 2-D vectorization is a principled and high-performance path for porting the rich CUDA ecosystem to the evolving landscape of NPU accelerators.

title: Design and Analysis of a Self-Propelled and Steerable Planetary Subsurface Drilling Robot
authors: Haoyang Liu, Jiabin Liu, Ran Xu, Guangfei Zhang, Hang Xiao, Kun Xu, Xilun Ding, Tao Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Regolith sampling and analysis are essential for investigating planetary geology and searching for traces of life and water. However, extreme surface conditions and long-term weathering hinder the preservation of early biosignatures. To address these challenges and access deeper subsurface layers, this paper proposes a novel self-propelled, steerable regolith drilling robot with an innovative mechanical design. A modular approach is adopted, comprising four functional units, including excavation, borehole stabilization, propulsion and steering, and chip removal. The mechanical performance of the robot is systematically analyzed, and its motion process is verified through discrete element simulations. This study provides technical support for future in-situ resource utilization on planetary bodies.

title: Performance Comparison of ANN and CNN for Intrusion Detection System in IoMT Networks
authors: Pragya Bajpayi, Sandeep Mathur, Madhu Sharma Gaur
publish: 2025 1st IEEE Uttar Pradesh Section Women in Engineering International Conference on Electrical Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPWIECON)
abstract: Sensitive patient data and mission-critical monitoring systems are now vulnerable to increasingly complex cyber threats due to the Internet of Medical Things' (IoMT) explosive growth. The implementation of highly effective Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) that can identify multiclass threats in real time is required to guarantee the resilience and integrity of IoMT networks. The usefulness of convolutional neural networks (CNN) for multiclass intrusion detection in IoMT environments is demonstrated in this paper. Our studies show that CNN performs better than ANN on all metrics, with an accuracy of roughly 0.97 and an F1-score of 0.99, using the common classification metrics of Accuracy, Precision, Recall, and FI-Score. This outstanding performance demonstrates CNN's powerful feature extraction capabilities and effectiveness in detecting complex, real-time threats in IoMT systems. These results highlight the applicability of CNNs for actual medical networks that require extreme precision and efficiency for multiclass attack detection.

title: Myocardial Infarction Detection and Classification from ECG Signals Using CNN
authors: S. Rajesh, Meeniga Nandini, Medagam Rahithya, P. Naga Lokesh Chowdari
publish: 2025 14th International Conference on System Modeling &amp; Advancement in Research Trends (SMART)
abstract: This study presents a deep learning framework for detecting myocardial infarction (MI) using ECG image analysis, focusing on Anterior MI, Inferior MI, and Posterior MI. Anterior MI affects the front wall of the left ventricle due to left anterior descending (LAD) artery occlusion. Inferior MI involves the lower heart, caused by right coronary artery (RCA) blockage, while Posterior MI impacts the back wall, often linked to RCA or circumflex artery obstruction. The model uses a sequential architecture combined with Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to get accurate MI patterns. This is trained on a diverse ECG dataset which achives highest diagnostic accuaracy. TThis facilitates early diagnosis and enhances decision making.

title: Advances and Challenges in AI-Powered Medical Chatbots: Leveraging LLMs, CNNs, and Multimodal Systems for Clinical Applications
authors: Nikhil Pardhi, Shital Hajare, Tanvi Chute, Adithyan S, Nishant Agade, Devanshi Timande
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI)-powered medical chatbots are emerging as transformative tools in healthcare, offering capabilities that range from patient triage and diagnostic assistance to chronic disease management and medical education. Recent advancements in large language models (LLMs), convolutional neural networks (CNNs), and multimodal architectures have significantly enhanced their ability to process complex medical data and deliver contextually accurate, patient-specific responses. This paper critically examines 26 recent works, with a focused analysis of ten primary studies, to evaluate the methodologies, performance metrics, and clinical applicability of state-of-the-art medical chatbot systems. The review highlights models such as MiniGPT-Med, ECG-Chat, MediGPT, and CNN-LSTM hybrids, which achieved accuracies exceeding 90% in specific diagnostic tasks, while also identifying limitations related to dataset diversity, interpretability, and real-world validation. The discussion underscores key challenges, including regulatory compliance, privacy preservation, and integration into existing clinical workflows. By synthesizing cross-disciplinary insights, this paper provides a consolidated view of current progress, critical gaps, and strategic directions necessary to enable safe, ethical, and scalable deployment of AI-based medical chatbots in global healthcare systems.

title: Safety Monitoring System for PPE Detection, Personnel Counting, and Facial Recognition using Computer Vision
authors: Wilfred Yong Kang Loy, Andrew Keong Ng, Sean Yu Haw Goh, Thiam Siew Lim
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Service Operations and Logistics, and Informatics (SOLI)
abstract: This paper presents the development and validation of an automated safety monitoring system leveraging computer vision techniques. The system is designed to enhance operational safety in dynamic environments, such as train maintenance areas, by providing real-time detection of personal protective equipment (PPE), identifying maintenance personnel through facial recognition, and accurately counting personnel for track access control. The core of the system utilises a YOLOv11m model for object detection tasks, including PPE and facial feature identification, complemented by the BoT-SORT algorithm for robust multi-object tracking. System architecture incorporates a Google Firestore backend for data persistence and Google Cloud Functions for event-driven alerting, with user interaction facilitated through a web interface and a bot on Telegram. The system demonstrated high accuracy in detecting protective helmets, safety vests, and safety boots, as well as reliable personnel identification and counting, achieving inference times suitable for real-time applications. This paper successfully establishes a proof of concept to significantly improve safety compliance monitoring and operational efficiency through automated detection and tracking.

title: Fine-Grained Multi-modal Fusion for Malware Detection via Cross-Modal Self-Attention
authors: JinXuan Zhang, Wenjie Guo, Jingfeng Xue
publish: 2025 IEEE 13th International Conference on Smart City and Informatization (iSCI)
abstract: Malicious software, as a major security threat in cyberspace, has seen its quantity grow exponentially and the iteration of its variants accelerate. Techniques such as shelling and obfuscation make it highly concealed. Traditional single-modal detection and simple multimodal fusion methods are no longer sufficient to cope with it. To this end, we propose a multimodal fine-grained fusion method for malware detection based on a self-attention mechanism. Binary executables are transformed into grayscale images, from which global and local visual features are extracted using a dual-branch network consisting of ViT and ResNet. In parallel, assembly-level semantic features are obtained by disassembling binaries and encoding function representations with CodeBERT model. Finally, a multi-head self-attention mechanism is employed to achieve fine-grained cross-modal fusion, enabling more precise correlation modeling between binary visual textures and assembly semantics. Experiments on the Microsoft BIG 2015 and VirusShare datasets demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms mainstream models in accuracy and recall, validating its effectiveness.

title: Forecasting and Overcommit Pipelines for Cloud Storage: A Model for Persistent Disk Capacity Management
authors: Hareendra Sura
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: One of the major challenges for the administration of persistent disks, particularly in terms of disk size allocation to workloads is inefficiency. Thus, this paper reviews an implemented production-grade pipeline in forecasting storage demand and safe overcommitting of capacity. It replaces static, cluster-level hard-coded overcommit percentage with a dynamic, data-driven framework based on historical usage patterns and storage consumption to achieve a 35% improvement in correct resource allocation. This paper describes how to develop an operational model that takes historical usage data and current operational data and processes it through a statistical forecasting model to automate the decision-making process around ‘overcommitting’ resources. The model has been evaluated over multiple years on various regulated workloads at a large private cloud provider and has produced consistent improvements in allocation accuracy and operational safety. Results found that predictive resource forecasting and automated infrastructure control improved not only resilience in operation but also the use of resources within mission-critical cloud environments.

title: Internet of Things Security System Based on Dynamic Context Negotiation
authors: Xinrong Li
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Information Science, Parallel and Distributed Systems (ISPDS)
abstract: With the rapid expansion of the Internet of Things (IoT) ecosystem, heterogeneous IoT networking faces critical challenges in protecting message content. Existing application layer security schemes rely on fixed encryption policies and lack application/context awareness, leading to resource waste in lowrisk scenarios or insufficient protection in high-risk contexts. This paper proposes an application-layer adaptive secure communication method for IoT devices, which builds a protocol-agnostic security sublayer atop application protocols such as MQTT to provide message content protection with dynamic algorithm adaptation. The method integrates context-aware security negotiation, lightweight policy dictionary mapping, scenario-based key derivation, real-time algorithm switching, and an asynchronous processing architecture. Experiments on an OpenHarmony-based testbed demonstrate that the proposed method improves security efficacy by 12% in high-security scenarios, reduces CPU consumption by 28% in low-security scenarios, achieves a crossdevice message parsing success rate of 98.4%, and incurs no connection interruptions during algorithm switches.

title: A Comparative Study of Machine Learning Methods for Fetal Health Prediction Using CTG Data
authors: Pradnyawant M. Gote, Chetan Puri, Praveen Kumar, Prajyot Yesankar, Adesh Pawar, Prajwal Bandre
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: The health of the fetus is constantly monitored through fetal monitoring which is an essential part of pre-natal care that aims to secure the health and growth of the fetus through the monitoring of physiological and anatomical parameters. Conventional methods like cardiotocography (CTG) and ultrasound, although beneficial, suffer from the subjectivity of interpretation and variability of clinical competence. The present research investigates fetal health assessment through machine learning by analyzing a mixture of clinical data that incorporates CTG traces, maternal vital signs, and electronic health records. Fetal health status is classified into normal, suspicious, or abnormal stages with the help of machine learning algorithms, starting from traditional methods to deep learning, which in turn leads to quick and accurate diagnosis and in-terventions. Moreover, it permits customized prenatal care and provides the possibility of distant monitoring, thus increasing the supply of quality healthcare. Despite facing challenges like data imbalance, model interpretability, and privacy concerns. This research aims to discover the most favorable machine learning models for fetal health prediction, hence reducing diagnostic bias and empowering practitioners to make timely, well-informed decisions that will ultimately improve maternal and neonatal outcomes.

title: Combining Transformers and CNNs for Efficient Object Detection in High-Resolution Satellite Imagery
authors: Nicolas Drapier, Aladine Chetouani, Aurélien Chateigner
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: We present GLOD, a transformer-first architecture for object detection in high-resolution satellite imagery. GLOD replaces CNN backbones with a Swin Transformer for end-to-end feature extraction, combined with novel UpConvMixer blocks for robust upsampling and Fusion Blocks for multi-scale feature integration. Our approach achieves 32.95% on xView, outperforming SOTA methods by 11.46%. Key innovations include asymmetric fusion with CBAM attention and a multi-path head design capturing objects across scales. The architecture is optimized for satellite imagery challenges, leveraging spatial priors while maintaining computational efficiency.

title: FUSELOC: Fusing Global and Local Descriptors for Fast and Robust 2D-3D Matching in Visual Localization
authors: Son Tung Nguyen, Alejandro Fontan, Michael Milford, Tobias Fischer
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Hierarchical visual localization methods achieve state-of-the-art accuracy but require substantial memory as they need to store all database images. Direct 2D-3D matching requires significantly less memory but suffers from lower accuracy due to the larger and more ambiguous search space. We address this ambiguity by fusing local and global descriptors using a weighted average operator. This operator rearranges the local descriptor space so that geographically nearby local descriptors are closer in the feature space according to the global descriptors. This decreases the number of irrelevant competing descriptors, especially if they are geographically distant, thus increasing correct matching likelihood. We consistently improve the accuracy over local-only systems and we achieve performance close to hierarchical methods while using 43% less memory and running 1.6 times faster. Extensive experiments on four challenging datasets - Cambridge Landmarks, Aachen Day/Night, RobotCar Seasons, and Extended CMU Seasons - demonstrate that, for the first time, direct matching algorithms can benefit from global descriptors without compromising computational efficiency. Our code is available at https://github.com/sontung/descriptor-disambiguation.

title: Bridge Surface Defect Detection Based on Machine Learning
authors: Huicheng Zhang, Heping Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Bridge defect detection is vital for infrastructure safety and durability. Traditional inspection methods are laborintensive, time-consuming, and prone to errors. This study introduces an automated, efficient, and objective deep learning approach using an enhanced YOLOv8 object detection model with CBAM attention mechanism and staged training strategy to identify bridge defects. We developed a comprehensive dataset of real-world bridge images featuring various defects, including corrosion, delamination, and fractures. This dataset was used to train and evaluate our enhanced YOLOv8 model, comparing its performance against standard YOLOv8 and other state-of-theart models. Experimental results show that our enhanced YOLOv8 achieves a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$98.7 \%$</tex> F1-score (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0. 3 \%$</tex>, 5-fold crossvalidation, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n=500$</tex>) on a combined test set of self-collected and public Bridge-DB images, significantly outperforming the logistic regression baseline (65.7 % F1-score). The model maintains real-time inference speed (37 FPS on RTX 3060). This research advances automated bridge inspection by demonstrating the effectiveness of CBAM-attention and staged layer unfreezing for challenging defect detection scenarios, maintaining fast inference speed suitable for real-time applications.

title: Simulating Refractive Distortions and Weather-Induced Artifacts for Resource-Constrained Autonomous Perception
authors: Moseli Mots'oehli, Feimei Chen, Hok Wai Chan, Itumeleng Tlali, Thulani Babeli, Kyungim Baek, Huaijin Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: The scarcity of autonomous vehicle datasets from developing regions, particularly across Africa's diverse urban, rural, and unpaved roads, remains a key obstacle to robust perception in low-resource settings. We present a procedural augmentation pipeline that enhances low-cost monocular dashcam footage with realistic refractive distortions and weather-induced artifacts tailored to challenging African driving scenarios. Our refractive module simulates optical effects from low-quality lenses and air turbulence, including lens distortion, Perlin noise, Thin-Plate Spline (TPS), and divergence-free (incompressible) warps. The weather module adds homogeneous fog, heterogeneous fog, and lens flare. To establish a benchmark, we provide baseline performance using three image restoration models. To support perception research in underrepresented African contexts, without costly data collection, labeling, or simulation, we release our distortion toolkit, augmented dataset splits, and benchmark results.

title: Guardians of Generation: Dynamic Inference-Time Copyright Shielding with Adaptive Guidance for AI Image Generation
authors: Soham Roy, Abhishek Mishra, Aakash Sen Sharma, Shirish Karande, Murari Mandal
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Modern text-to-image generative models can inadvertently reproduce copyrighted content memorized in their training data, raising serious concerns about potential copy-right infringement. We introduce Guardians of Generation, a model-agnostic inference-time framework for dy-namic copyright shielding in AI image generation. Our approach requires no retraining or modification of the gener-ative model's weights, instead integrating seamlessly with existing diffusion pipelines. It augments the generation process with an adaptive guidance mechanism comprising three components: a detection module, a prompt rewriting module, and a guidance adjustment module. The detection module monitors user prompts and intermediate generation steps to identify features indicative of copyrighted content before they manifest in the final output. If such content is detected, the prompt rewriting mechanism dynamically transforms the user's prompt, sanitizing or replacing references that could trigger copyrighted material while preserving the prompt's intended semantics. The adaptive guidance module adaptively steers the diffusion process away from flagged content by modulating the model's sampling trajectory. Together, these components form a robust shield that enables a tunable balance between preserving creative fidelity and ensuring copyright compliance. We validate our method on a variety of generative models (Stable Diffusion, SDXL, Flux), demonstrating substantial reductions in copyrighted content generation with negligible impact on output fidelity or alignment with user intent. This work provides a practical, plug-and-play safeguard for generative image models, enabling more responsible deployment under real-world copyright constraints. Source code is available at this link.

title: T3D: Advancing 3D Medical Vision-Language Pre-Training by Learning Multi-View Visual Consistency
authors: Che Liu, Cheng Ouyang, Yinda Chen, Cesar Quilodran-Casas, Lei Ma, Jie Fu, Yike Guo, Anand Shah, Wenjia Bai, Rossella Arcucci
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: While 3D visual self-supervised learning (vSSL) shows promising results in capturing visual representations, it over-looks the clinical knowledge from radiology reports. Mean-while, 3D medical vision-language pre-training (MedVLP) remains underexplored due to the lack of a large-scale, pub-licly available 3D medical image-report dataset. To bridge this gap, we introduce CT-3DVLP, the first and largest pub-lic 3D volume-report dataset, establishing a comprehensive benchmarkfor 3D MedVLP research. Meanwhile, we pro-pose the T3D framework, which enhances 3D MedVLP be-yond naive CLIP-style alignment that directly pairs volumes with reports but neglects local visual representations. In-stead, we introduce Text-informed Multi-view Alignment (TMA), a novel approach that clusters volumetric data while enforcing consistency across different views of the same volume-report pair. TMA integrates textual features into fine-grained visual representations, ensuring contextual coher-ence across views. We evaluate T3D across multiple down-stream tasks in both unimodal and cross-modal settings, in-cluding zero-shot and fine-tuned classification, cross-modal retrieval, report generation, and semantic segmentation. Our results show that T3D consistently outperforms existing vSSL and multimodal methods, demonstrating superior zero-shot and fine-tuning capabilities and setting a new benchmark for 3D medical image understanding.

title: Toward Socially Aware Vision-Language Models: Evaluating Cultural Competence Through Multimodal Story Generation
authors: Arka Mukherjee, Shreya Ghosh
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: As Vision-Language Models (VLMs) achieve widespread deployment across diverse cultural contexts, ensuring their cultural competence becomes critical for responsible AI systems. While prior work has evaluated cultural awareness in text-only models and VLM object recognition tasks, no research has systematically assessed how VLMs adapt outputs when cultural identity cues are embedded in both textual prompts and visual inputs during generative tasks. We present the first comprehensive evaluation of VLM cultural competence through multimodal story generation, developing a novel multimodal framework that perturbs cultural identity and evaluates 5 contemporary VLMs on a downstream task: story generation. Our analysis reveals significant cultural adaptation capabilities, with rich culturally-specific vocabulary spanning names, familial terms, and geographic markers. However, we uncover concerning limitations: cultural competence varies dramatically across architectures, some models exhibit inverse cultural alignment, and automated metrics show architectural bias contradicting human assessments. Cross-modal evaluation shows that culturally distinct outputs are indeed detectable through visual-semantic similarity (28.7% within-nationality vs. 0.2% cross-nationality recall), yet visualcultural understanding remains limited. In essence, we establish the promise and challenges of cultural competence in multimodal AI. We publicly release our codebase and data: https://github.com/ArkaMukherjeeO/mmCultural

title: Adapting Stereo Vision from Objects to 3D Lunar Surface Reconstruction with the StereoLunar Dataset
authors: Clémentine Grethen, Simone Gasparini, Géraldine Morin, Jérémy Lebreton, Lucas Marti, Manuel Sanchez Gestido
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Accurate 3D reconstruction of lunar surfaces is essential for space exploration. However, existing stereo vision re-construction methods struggle in this context due to the Moon's lack of texture, difficult lighting variations, and atypical orbital trajectories. State-of-the-art deep learning models, trained on human-scale datasets, have rarely been tested on planetary imagery and cannot be transferred directly to lunar conditions. To address this issue, we introduce LunarStereo, the first open dataset of photorealistic stereo image pairs of the Moon, simulated using ray tracing based on high-resolution topography and reflectance models. It covers diverse altitudes, lighting conditions, and viewing angles around the lunar South Pole, offering physically grounded supervision for 3D reconstruction tasks. Based on this dataset, we adapt the MASt3R model to the lunar domain through fine-tuning on LunarStereo. We validate our approach through extensive qualitative and quantitative experiments on both synthetic and real lunar data, evaluating 3D surface reconstruction and relative pose estimation. Extensive experiments on synthetic and real lunar data validate the approach, demonstrating significant improvements over zero-shot baselines and paving the way for robust cross-scale generalization in extraterrestrial environments.

title: Design and Implement Sentiment Analysis Approach: Using Ensemble Deep Learning
authors: Anoopa Arya, Alankrita Aggarwal, Navjot Singh
publish: 2025 14th International Conference on System Modeling &amp; Advancement in Research Trends (SMART)
abstract: Sentiment Analysis (SA), commonly known as opinion mining, constitutes a segment of machine learning tasked with discerning the overall sentiment conveyed in a word, text, phrase, paragraph, or even an entire document. Leveraging machine learning algorithms and natural language processing methods, SA extracts subjectivityrelated information from documents and categorizes them based on polarity, such as positive, negative, or neutral sentiments. SA proves invaluable for businesses seeking to analyze various texts or reviews to glean opinions about their brands, as well as for stock markets aiming to forecast stock trends. More recently, Deep Learning Techniques have gained traction in this field due to their promising ability to accurately decipher emotions from videos, audios, and textual data found on the internet. This paper presents experiments conducted using various deep learning techniques to enhance the extraction of opinions and emotions from audio databases like UrbanSound8k, RAVDESS, and others alike.

title: Self-Supervised Denoising Based on TriDec-UNet Architecture
authors: Ye Fan, Xinlong Liu, Xinhou Wang, Shuhui Liu
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: Self-supervised image denoising has mitigated information loss in blind-spot frameworks but remains limited by weak feature representation, inadequate signal-noise separation, overfitting risk, and insufficient utilization of spatial-channel dependencies. In this work, a TriDec-UNet architecture is proposed integrating Triplet Attention (TA) and Depthwise Separable Convolution (DSC). Cross-dimensional attention is embedded in the encoder to enhance adaptive feature calibration, effectively distinguishing noise from image structures. Dual TA modules combined with DSC are applied in the decoder to improve feature aggregation and blind-spot information integration while maintaining low computational cost. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior denoising accuracy and generalization with minimal parameter overhead.

title: Temporal Knowledge Graph Reasoning with Dynamic Frequency-Aware and Historical Attention Mechanisms
authors: Mengqi Zhong, Kai Chen, Shasha Li, Ting Wang, JinTao Tang, Shijie Li
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing (MLNLP)
abstract: Temporal knowledge graph extrapolation reasoning aims to infer future events using historical information, representing a significant research area. Among existing methods, logical rule-based methods have obvious advantage in term of interpretability. However, current logical rule-based methods primarily focus on rule induction, often overlooking the problem of temporal variability and temporal sparsity. To address these issues, we propose a novel approach, DFHA, which introduces an entity frequency-aware scoring mechanism and a historical relationship attention reward layer for rule-based temporal knowledge graph reasoning. Specifically, DFHA incorporates frequency information by prioritizing entities that have appeared frequently in the past, assigning them higher scores during reasoning, information that is often overlooked in rule-based methods. Furthermore, a historical relationship attention mechanism captures the relative importance of past events to the current query, thereby differentiating the contributions of distinct historical events to the inference. Finally, the model is optimized using the semantic similarity between the query quadruple and the extracted path, combined with the computed reward score. Extensive experiments demonstrate the superiority and effectiveness of our DFHA approach.

title: Dual-Stream Deep Learning Architecture for Deepfake Detection Using Spatial and Frequency Domain Analysis
authors: D. Rajeswara Rao, Munnangi Jahnavi, Thottempudi Sri Sai Yatheesh, Kocherla Vinutna
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: The proliferation of deepfake technology has emerged as a significant threat to digital media authenticity, with potential implications for security, privacy, and social trust. This paper presents a novel dual-stream deep learning architecture that combines spatial and frequency domain analysis for robust deepfake detection. Unlike traditional approaches that rely solely on spatial features, our method leverages both conventional CNN-based image analysis and frequency domain characteristics to capture subtle manipulation artifacts. The proposed architecture utilizes a pre-trained Xception network for spatial analysis, coupled with a custom CNN designed specifically for processing frequency domain features obtained through Fast Fourier Transform (FFT). These complementary streams are integrated through an ensemble classifier that combines high-level features from both domains. Additionally, we implement a Grad-CAM based localization mechanism that provides interpretable visualizations of detected manipulations. Extensive experiments on the Hemg deepfake dataset demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach, achieving 97.68% accuracy and 0.9978 AUC-ROC score. Our localization capability achieves 89% correspondence with expert-annotated manipulation regions, providing trans-parent and explainable detection results. The proposed system advances the state-of-the-art in deepfake detection by offering both high accuracy and interpretability, crucial features for real-world applications in digital media forensics.

title: Transient stability analysis of power system based on low latency data acquisition
authors: Junyi Chen, Jingxin Xia, Yurui Li, Xiaoruo Chen, Jiade Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy Power and Electrical Technology (CEPET)
abstract: Transient stability analysis of power systems is crucial for rapid fault response and grid safety. This study proposes a transient stability analysis method based on low latency data acquisition to address the shortcomings of traditional methods in terms of data latency and accuracy. Dynamic load balancing of cluster nodes is achieved through targeted task allocation algorithms, combined with a hierarchical caching lifecycle mechanism to optimize data storage efficiency. At the same time, the convolutional pooling structure of CNN is utilized to extract deep features of power grid time-series data, and an end-to-end training method is adopted to establish a transient stability assessment model. This fusion scheme achieves closed-loop optimization from data collection to stability analysis through multi-stage collaborative processing such as weight calculation, task allocation, and feature extraction, significantly improving the real-time monitoring and decision-making capabilities of the power system. The test results show that the data acquisition delay of this method is stable at $\mathbf{1 0. 2}-\mathbf{1 3. 6 m s}$, and the accuracy of system state instability discrimination remains above 90%.

title: AI-Enhanced Wearable Sensors Connected to Cloud Platforms for Continuous Health Monitoring in Aging Populations
authors: Keshav Kaushik, Ojasvi Razdan, Karthick Cherladine, Ronish Balvantbhai Patel, Renu Kumawat, Mukesh Soni
publish: 2025 1st IEEE Uttar Pradesh Section Women in Engineering International Conference on Electrical Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPWIECON)
abstract: As the world's population ages, there is an increasing demand for new ways for maintaining an eye on wellness all the time. This paper introduces a comprehensive framework that integrates AI-enhanced mobile sensors and cloud-connected services for real-time and non-invasive health monitoring of elderly people. The system uses the latest machine learning technologies in wearables to smartly decode cardio-respiratory data like heart rate, blood pressure, blood oxygen levels, and mobility features. With cloud infrastructure, you can store data when you need it, process it from a distance, and get feedback that is specific to you. This lets doctors act quickly and makes things easier for healthcare systems. The developed architecture ensures data privacy, interoperability, and energy efficiency, providing a solid basis for advanced geriatric healthcare. Prototyping and experimental outcomes illustrate the efficacy of this methodology in enabling early anomaly detection and predicting future health trends, signalling a new epoch of elderly care underpinned by continuous monitoring driven by artificial intelligence.

title: Image Enhancement and Feature Matching Optimization for Visual SLAM in Weak Texture Scenes
authors: Changjiang Jiang, Yu Huang
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: Aiming at the problem that traditional ORB- SLAM3 has insufficient robustness in feature matching in weak texture scenes, an improved visual SLAM method EF -vSLAM (Enhancement & Feature-based visual SLAM) is proposed which combines image enhancement with deep learning feature matching. Firstly, a multi-stage image preprocessing process is designed to improve the visibility of weak texture regions through adaptive contrast enhancement and noise reduction. The image detail features are strengthened by combining sharpening filtering and high-frequency component superposition, which effectively improved the feature expression under low-quality input conditions. Secondly, the original ORB feature extraction methods are replaced with the SuperPoint feature extractor based on deep learning, and the lightweight matching network LightGlue is introduced to achieve high-precision feature matching, which significantly improving the robustness to repeated textures and dynamic interference. Experiments show that on the TUM public dataset, the detection accuracy of the proposed method is significantly im-proved compared with the mainstream visual SLAM algorithm in a weak texture environment, which fully verifies the effectiveness of the method.

title: Cardiac Disease Prediction
authors: Venkata SP Dendukuri, Sambangi Shivani, Singamreddy Siri, Sahitya Allu
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: According to the World Health Organization, cardiovascular diseases are the foremost cause of global mortality, causing approximately 17.9 million deaths each year. Timely identification and adjustments to lifestyle can markedly reduce this toll. This paper proposes a machine learning-based heart disease prediction system that unifies clinical variables with lifestyle-related inputs. Two separate predictive pipelines are developed: a Naïve Bayes model that evaluates clinical indicators such as blood pressure and cholesterol, and a lifestyle-focused model that analyzes habits including exercise, diet, and tobacco use. Combined, the ensemble produced an overall accuracy of 99.31% on the test data. Outputs from both models are fused, with greater emphasis given to clinical features, to compute a consolidated risk score. The solution is deployed as a web application enabling users to enter personal health data and receive tailored risk predictions accompanied by graphical visuals, recommended healthy parameter ranges, and alert flags. Additionally, an interactive dashboard assists patients and clinicians in interpreting results, which is especially valuable for settings with restricted medical resources.

title: A Multi-Path Routing Algorithm for Offshore Heterogeneous Communication Networks Based on TGNN-DRL Algorithm
authors: Ning Zhang, Yibo Liu, Wenyu Cai, Yong Yin, Congwen Jiang
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Information Science, Parallel and Distributed Systems (ISPDS)
abstract: In maritime environments, the mobility of nodes leads to dynamic changes in network topology, making it essential to design routing algorithms that effectively integrate heterogeneous communication modes, link evaluation, and throughput optimization. This paper proposes a multi-path routing algorithm, termed TGNN-DRL, which combines a Temporal Graph Neural Network (TGNN) with Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) to optimize Quality of Service (QoS). Built upon a Software-Defined Networking (SDN) architecture, the algorithm enables centralized routing control and adaptive policy optimization through a reinforcement learning framework. The main innovation lies in employing a Relational Graph Attention Network (RGAT) to address heterogeneous multi-link communication between nodes and incorporating a Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) structure to extract temporal features for dynamic adaptation. Furthermore, the algorithm utilizes the Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3) method to learn and generalize across different network topologies. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed TGNN-DRL algorithm achieves superior packet delivery ratio and bandwidth utilization under high-load conditions while maintaining strong generalization capability.

title: Velocity-Guided BEV Cross-Attention for Real-Time 3D Object Detection with 4D Imaging Radar and Monocular Camera
authors: Zenglong Yue, Lin Cheng, Zeting An
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: This paper presents Vel-IPM-CA, a LiDAR-less calibration fusion pipeline that effectively integrates 4D imaging radar and monocular camera for robust 3D object detection. Addressing the challenge of missing SNR information in public datasets like TJ4DRadSet, our approach introduces three key innovations: velocity magnitude as pseudo-intensity for 5D radar pillar formation, inverse perspective mapping with assumed geometric parameters for BEV semantic mask generation, and a lightweight cross-attention module for adaptive feature fusion. Extensive evaluations on the TJ4DRadSet benchmark demon-strate that our method achieves a 2.0% improvement in Car 3D AP@0.7 on the full validation set and a 2.5% improvement on challenging rain/night subsets, while maintaining real-time performance at 27 FPS. Crucially, our approach eliminates the need for LiDAR-based extrinsic calibration while utilizing practical assumptions about camera geometry, representing a significant step toward deployable all-weather perception systems.

title: Two-stage Text-Guided Diffusion Models for Disentangled Industrial Defect Generation
authors: Jing Wei, Qingfeng Shi, Fei Shen, Zhengtao Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Defect image generation is crucial to address the data scarcity problem in industrial inspection. However, current generation methods struggle to produce high-quality defect images with limited training samples. The reason lies in that they focus solely on imaging characteristics rather than industrial manufacturing. To tackle this issue, this paper proposes a two-stage method to simulate the actual manufacturing process of defective products by disentangling normal products and defect operations. Firstly, we design the normal adapter to fine-tune pre-trained diffusion models and generate normal images under the guidance of product-related text prompts and global uniform masks. Secondly, the defect adapter is proposed to learn the product-agnostic damage operations applied to the normal features from the first stage with the guidance of defect name prompts and binary masks, combined with the proposed local structural consistency loss and contrastive loss to improve the quality of the generated defects. Subsequently, the Segment Anything Model is employed to refine the mask annotations, further enhancing the quality of the generated samples. Experimental results demonstrate that our method can generate high-quality, diverse, and annotated defect samples with limited training samples, leading to an improvement of up to 7% in average precision of segmentation performance, outperforming existing state-of-the-art methods. Moreover, our defect adapter enables defects to transfer across different products, achieving high-quality zero-shot defect generation.

title: Development of a Machine Learning-Derived Model to Predict Unplanned ICU Admissions After Major Non-Cardiac Surgery
authors: Devanshi Gandhi, Ranjana Lanjewar, Imran Ali Khan, Anup Zade
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Unplanned intensive care unit (ICU) admissions following major non-cardiac surgery are linked to increased postoperative complications, prolonged hospital stays, and greater healthcare costs. Early identification of patients at risk can help optimize perioperative management and resource allocation. This study aimed to develop and validate a machine learning—based model to predict the risk of unplanned ICU admission after major non-cardiac surgery. A retrospective cohort of [4658] adult patients who underwent major noncardiac procedures between [2011–2016] was analyzed. Preoperative, intraoperative, and early postoperative data were extracted from institutional electronic health records. Several machine learning algorithms, including logistic regression, random forest, and gradient boosting, were trained and evaluated using cross-validation. Model performance was assessed by accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and the area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC). The gradient boosting model achieved the highest predictive performance, with an AUC of [0.80]. The most important predictors of unplanned ICU admission included advanced age, higher ASA physical status, lower preoperative hemoglobin, longer surgical duration, and greater intraoperative blood loss. The developed model demonstrated strong predictive capability and has the potential to serve as a clinical decision-support tool to assist in perioperative risk stratification and ICU resource planning. Prospective multi-center validation is warranted to confirm its generalizability and support clinical implementation

title: A Preliminary Study of Dual-Band Metamaterial Applied to Magnetic Resonance Human Body Communication
authors: Sumei Hong, Jinghao Huang, Yueming Gao
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: Dual-band operation in magnetic resonance human body communication (MR-HBC) enables concurrent device communication and enhanced adaptability across diverse scenarios. This paper presents a dual-band metamaterial composed of symmetrically arranged spiral resonators, which achieves simultaneous negative permeability at the 6.78 MHz and 13.56 MHz ISM bands. Simulation results in a human muscle model demonstrate significant path loss reduction of approximately 5.29 dB and 4.55 dB at the two target frequencies, respectively. The proposed metamaterial provides an effective approach to realizing robust dual-band MR-HBC systems with potential for frequency-division multiplexing applications.

title: Mode Conversion and Radiation Structure of Broadband Tunable Magnetrons
authors: Jianlong Zhang, Bo Zhao, Yiang Chen, Zhipeng Yan, Biao Hu, Haiyang Wang, Tianming Li, Hao Li
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: To meet the compact radiation requirements of a broadband tunable magnetron, this paper proposes a compact broadband radiation system. The structure consists of a TEM-TE<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf> broadband mode converter loaded with a multi-stage step structure combined with a small-aperture conical horn. It achieves broadband radiation while ensuring the compactness of the system. Meanwhile, a hemispherical shell-shaped radome is innovatively adopted to adapt to the divergent beam of the small-aperture horn. Simulation and test results show that the radiation system has a return loss of less than −20dB, a conversion efficiency of more than 99%, and a relative bandwidth of 19% in the frequency range of 1.9 – 2.3GHz. The gain at the center frequency is 10.7 dB, the power capacity exceeds 100MW, and the antenna aperture is 120mm, which meets the compact radiation requirements of the broadband tunable magnetron.

title: Proactive Discrepancy Detection at Scale: A Continuous Validation Framework for Distributed Data Systems
authors: Hareendra Sura
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: In environments that rely on eventual consistency, when the system is not functioning, it is difficult to detect a high-risk silent data corruption during such transitions. Large-scale distributed systems inevitably undergo data migrations and phased rollouts as architectures evolve. This paper presents how to safeguard the data migration with a framework designed to support rollout with the live traffic. When we do pre or post launch migration audits then the framework defines temporary validation, rather than relying on long-lived data pipelines or post migration audits, performing massively parallel validation that compares the data states within bounded time windows. Deployed in a production inventory platform, the framework processes over 100 million records per day and reduces post-launch data discrepancies by 80%, providing operators with quantitative confidence for rollout, rollback, and long-term system correctness.

title: Swing Leg Motion Strategy for Heavy-Load Legged Robot Based on Force Sensing
authors: Pengxiang Zang, Ze Fu, Yinghui Li, Weizhong Guo
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: The heavy-load legged robot has strong load carrying capacity and can adapt to various unstructured terrains. However, substantial weight imposes more stringent requirements on motion stability and environmental perception. In order to utilize force sensing information to improve its motion performance, in this paper, we propose a finite state machine model for the swing leg in the static gait by imitating the movement of the elephant. Based on the presence or absence of additional terrain information, different trajectory planning strategies are provided for the swing leg to enhance the success rate of stepping and save energy. The experimental results on a novel quadruped robot show that our method has strong robustness and can enable heavy-load legged robots to pass through various complex terrains autonomously and smoothly.

title: Improving Temporal Ordering with External Data
authors: Rahul Cherekar
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: This paper explores the efficacy of leveraging external data sources to improve the temporal ordering of historical events. Using machine reading techniques and supervised classification models, we investigate the extraction of contextual information from related articles to enhance pairwise event ordering accuracy. Experimental results show significant improvements, with up to a 30% increase in accuracy compared to approaches relying on event titles alone. By leveraging Wikipedia as a primary external source of contextual data, this work demonstrates the value of external information for temporal reasoning and highlights promising directions for further research.

title: Writer Identification in Khmer Handwriting Using Recurrent Neural Networks
authors: Kimlong Ngin, Dona Valy, Sokkhey Phauk, Sokroeurn Ang, Seangleng Ny
publish: 2025 International Conference on Robotics and Artificial Intelligence (ICRAI)
abstract: Writer identification is a challenge task in pattern recognition that plays a crucial role in handwriting analysis. Biometric handwriting recognition has attracted a lot of attention in the domains of biometric verification and authentication systems. This paper presents a novel approach that uses Recurrent neural networks (RNNs) within a Siamese neural network (SNN) architecture for writer identification in Khmer Script. The SNN network was applied during both the training and testing phases using a specially curate dataset consisting of (x, y) coordinate sequences, designed to ensure accuracy, consistency, and relevance for Khmer writer identification.
