The curation process involved several key steps: cleaning, to remove incomplete or corrupted handwriting samples; validation, to ensure that all coordinate pairs were accurately labeled as either positive (same writer) or negative (different writers); and enhancement, which included coordinate normalization and integration of pen-state information to standardize data collected from various devices and individuals. This experimentation demonstrated that the proposed model achieves high accuracy on both the training and testing sets. The SNN method, effectively addresses the complexities of Khmer handwriting, accurately identifying authorship. This research contributes to the advancement of writer identification techniques for Khmer script, with potential applications in forensic analysis, document verification, and linguistic studies.

title: Model Ensemble to Fuse Geometric and Learning Solutions for Camera Rotation Estimation
authors: Bhuvan Aggarwal, Amit More, Mudit Soni, S Divakar Bhat
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Camera rotation estimation is an important task in many computer vision applications, such as Bird's Eye View (BEV) generation for autonomous driving to better understand the surroundings. In this work we focus on single-image camera rotation estimation, particularly roll and pitch. Recently, many deep learning based approaches are proposed to solve this task with complex pipelines to fuse geometric information into the learning process. Contrary to this common approach, we present a simple model ensemble approach to merge learning based and geometric solutions. This approach allows us to take advantage of the best of both worlds, i.e. stability of learning-based solutions and high accuracy of geometric solutions. In particular, the proposed geometric solution has very high accuracy when parallel lines are present and detected, whereas a learning-based solution still provides stable estimates when geometric approach fails in the absence of such conditions. Such a simple approach is highly robust and accurate at the same time and outperforms all the complex pipelines in the literature. Our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on the Google Street View, TartanAir and MegaDepth datasets giving a median error of 0.71°, 0.89° and 0.94° for pitch angle and 0.27°, 0.40° and 0.32° for roll angle estimation.

title: TRICKY 2025 HouseCat6D Object Pose Estimation Challenge with Specular and Transparent Surfaces
authors: Weihang Li, Junwen Huang, Hyunjun Jung, Guangyao Zhai, Pierluigi Zama Ramirez, Alex Costanzino, Fabio Tosi, Matteo Poggi, Luigi Di Stefano, Jean-Baptiste Weibel, Doris Antensteiner, Markus Vincze, Jing He, Yiqing Wang, Kexin Zhang, Licheng Jiao, Lingling Li, Fang Liu, Wenping Ma, Benjamin Busam
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: This paper presents the TRICKY 2025 HouseCat6D Category-Level Object Pose Estimation Challenge, held in conjunction with the ICCV 2025 workshop on Transpar-ent and Reflective Objects in the Wild. The challenge ad-dresses the critical limitations of existing pose estimation systems when applied to non-Lambertian surfaces, such as glass and metal. Leveraging the HouseCat6D dataset com-prising realistic home environments with a diverse range of transparent and specular objects, the challenge pushes state-of-the-art algorithms to estimate object pose, scale, and shape in photometrically complex scenes. Unlike traditional benchmarks focused on texture-rich, opaque objects, this challenge emphasizes robustness under reflective high-lights, refractions, and partial transparency. By promoting research in these underexplored conditions, the challenge contributes toward generalizable and category-level object understanding in unconstrained real-world settings.

title: GRANII: Selection and Ordering of Primitives in GRAph Neural Networks using Input Inspection
authors: Damitha Lenadora, Vimarsh Sathia, Gerasimos Gerogiannis, Serif Yesil, Josep Torrellas, Charith Mendis
publish: 2026 IEEE/ACM International Symposium on Code Generation and Optimization (CGO)
abstract: Over the years, many frameworks and optimization techniques have been proposed to accelerate graph neural networks (GNNs). In contrast to the optimizations explored in these systems, we observe that different matrix re-associations of GNN computations lead to novel input-sensitive performance behavior. We leverage this observation to propose GRANII, a system that exposes different compositions of sparse and dense matrix primitives based on different matrix re-associations of GNN computations and selects the best among them based on input attributes. GRANII executes in two stages: (1) an offline compilation stage that enumerates all valid re-associations leading to different sparse-dense matrix compositions and uses input-oblivious pruning techniques to prune away clearly unprofitable candidates, and (2) an online runtime system that explores the remaining candidates and uses lightweight cost models to select the best re-association based on the input graph and the embedding sizes. On a wide range of configurations, GRANII achieves a geo-mean speedup of 1.56× for inference and 1.4× for training across multiple GNN models and systems. We also show GRANII’s technique functions on diverse implementations and with techniques such as sampling.

title: LLM-VeriOpt: Verification-Guided Reinforcement Learning for LLM-Based Compiler Optimization
authors: Xiangxin Fang, Jiaqin Kang, Rodrigo Rocha, Sam Ainsworth, Lev Mukhanov
publish: 2026 IEEE/ACM International Symposium on Code Generation and Optimization (CGO)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) for compiler optimization have recently emerged as a frontier research direction, with many studies demonstrating their potential to automate and improve low-level code transformations. While various techniques have been proposed to enhance LLMs’ ability to optimize LLVM IR or assembly code, ensuring the semantic equivalence of transformed instructions remains a fundamental prerequisite for safe and effective performance improvement. At the same time, code generated by LLMs is often so far away from being correct that it is very difficult to work out how to improve them to proceed in generating optimizations using the output.In this work, we present LLM-VeriOpt, a novel reinforcement-learning methodology that incorporates feedback from a formal verifier, Alive2, to guide the training of a small-scale model, Qwen-3B. This facilitates the use of Guided Reinforcement via Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), using semantic-equivalence signals from the Alive2 formal verification tool as part of the reward function. This allows the model to self-correct based on observing and subsequently learning to give correctness feedback during training, giving high code coverage by successfully transforming large amounts of code, while also optimizing it significantly.We demonstrate our technique by designing an LLM-based peephole optimizer over LLVM-IR. Our method significantly improves the correctness of IR optimizations versus the base LLM Qwen-3B applied with just a prompt and no fine-tuning — achieving a 5.4× improvement in code successfully modified. The resulting model produces verifiably correct output 90% of the time, comfortably outperforming larger state-of-the-art LLMs, including Meta’s LLM Compiler. This yields speedups of 2.3× over O0-optimized code, comparable to the handwritten LLVM-instcombine pass, and producing emergent optimizations that outperform it in 20% of cases.

title: Relation-Anchored Local Cross-Spectral Alignment for Multispectral NTRM Detection
authors: Tengfei Chen, Taiyuan Cao, Zeen Yin, Diwei Yang, Zhenjun Huang, Kui Yang, Hua Li, Qingpeng Li
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: In industrial vision tasks such as non-tobacco related material (NTRM) sorting and agricultural product quality inspection, multispectral imagery provides rich cues for object recognition by capturing surface texture and material variations across spectral bands. However, due to the uneven surfaces of tobacco leaves, NTRM stacking, and multi-view acquisition, local spatial misalignment between RGB and multispectral images persists even after reflectance calibration and coarse alignment. Conventional convolutional networks struggle to extract consistent features under such spatial offsets, degrading detection performance. To address this issue, we propose the Relation-Anchored Local Cross-Spectral Alignment Network (RALCA-Net), an enhanced multispectral object detection framework. We introduce a query-centered, relation-anchored local cross-spectral alignment module into the low- and mid-level stages of the YOLO11 backbone. The RALCA module uses RGB features as spatial anchors and performs cross-spectral attention within local windows to adaptively align multispectral features, enabling fine-grained correspondence across spectral bands. Without modifying the neck or detection head of YOLO11, the proposed architecture effectively mitigates cross-spectral spatial misalignment and improves feature fusion. Experimental results on a multispectral NTRM detection dataset show absolute gains of +5.0% mAP@0.5 and +4.8% mAP@0.5:0.95 over the YOLO11 baseline. These results confirm that the proposed local cross-spectral alignment strategy significantly enhances detection accuracy and robustness in challenging multispectral industrial scenarios.

title: Advanced Hydroponic Crop Management: A Smart IoT Architecture Leveraging Hybrid and Transformer-Based Deep Learning Models
authors: Neha Tyagi, Tushar Kumar, D Omkar Murty, Sayantani Banerjee, Pritam Das, Sabyasachi Bhattacharyya
publish: 2025 1st IEEE Uttar Pradesh Section Women in Engineering International Conference on Electrical Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPWIECON)
abstract: The integration of Internet of Things (IoT) technology with deep learning has revolutionized precision agriculture, particularly within hydroponic systems. This study presents an advanced IoT-enabled crop management framework that utilizes a hybrid deep learning approach, incorporating Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), and Transformer models. By employing sensors to track critical environmental parameters and processing both image data and time-series information, the system facilitates predictive analytics and automated adjustments to optimize growth conditions. Comparative analysis and ablation studies validate the individual contributions of these components. The paper concludes by out- lining potential advancements, such as edge computing, federated learning, and environmentally sustainable AI-driven agricultural practices.

title: Towards Path-Aware Coverage-Guided Fuzzing
authors: Giacomo Priamo, Daniele Cono D’Elia, Mathias Payer, Leonardo Querzoni
publish: 2026 IEEE/ACM International Symposium on Code Generation and Optimization (CGO)
abstract: Automated fuzz testing is now standard practice, yet key blind spots persist. Coverage-guided fuzzers typically rely on edge coverage as a lightweight proxy for program behavior. However, this metric captures path variations only weakly: it cannot differentiate executions that follow distinct control-flow paths but traverse the same edges—causing many path-dependent bugs to go undetected. Path awareness would offer a richer coverage view but has been considered too costly for fuzzing.We introduce a lightweight method for tracking intra-procedural execution paths, enabling efficient path-aware feedback. This enhances the fuzzer’s ability to detect subtle bugs, even in well-tested software. To counter the resulting seed explosion, we evaluate two strategies—culling and opportunistic path-aware fuzzing—that balance precision and throughput. Our findings show that path-aware fuzzing, when properly guided, uncovers more bugs and reveals untapped potential in fuzzing research.

title: Context-Aware Image Caption Editing via Hallucination-Resistant Visual Instruction Tuning
authors: Yoonhyung Kim, Byung Ok Kang, Hwa Jeon Song
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: The goal of image caption editing is to revise a given caption so that it is semantically aligned with its corresponding image. Since it involves not only identifying inconsistencies in the caption but also correcting them based on the image, image caption editing remains a challenging problem. Previous approaches establish multimodal frameworks specifically designed for image caption editing. However, these methods do not leverage vision-language models (VLMs) built upon large language models (LLMs), which are expected to provide enhanced contextual understanding capabilities. In this paper, we propose leveraging recent VLMs for image caption editing. We observe that applying existing instruction-tuned VLMs often results in hallucinations for image caption editing. To address this issue, we introduce a new instruction tuning dataset which consists of two tasks for hallucination-resistant image caption editing. The first one is the multi-object selection task where the VLM is required to select the objects present in the image from a set of given objects, some of which include hallucination-inducing ones. The second task is the context-aware image caption editing task, where the model is required to modify a given image caption to align it with its corresponding image, while preserving the context of the original caption. Comparative results on two baseline VLMs demon-strate that our proposed instruction dataset leads to state-of-the-art performance in image caption editing. In addition, we empirically demonstrate that instruction tuning for context-aware image caption editing enhances robustness of VLMs against hallucinations.

title: SmartHeart+: An Advanced Deep Learning Approach Combining CNN-GRU and Feature Selection for Cardiovascular Risk Forecasting
authors: Febeena Ezhil Jothi S, E Anbalagan, M Babu, Mahmoud Odeh, Bolagani Balaji
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases (CVDs) remain the leading cause of mortality globally, necessitating the development of robust and intelligent predictive systems to facilitate early diagnosis and intervention. This paper introduces SmartHeart+, an advanced deep learning framework that synergistically integrates Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) with Gated Recurrent Units (GRU), enhanced by optimized feature selection techniques, for accurate cardiovascular risk forecasting. The proposed model leverages the CNN’s powerful spatial feature extraction capability to analyze complex patterns in clinical and physiological data, while the GRU component captures temporal dependencies and sequential trends inherent in medical time-series records.

title: Perceptual Classifiers: Detecting Generative Images Using Perceptual Features
authors: Krishna Srikar Durbha, Asvin Kumar Venkataramanan, Rajesh Sureddi, Alan C. Bovik
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Image Quality Assessment (IQA) models are employed in many practical image and video processing pipelines to reduce storage, minimize transmission costs, and improve the Quality of Experience (QoE) of millions of viewers. These models are sensitive to a diverse range of image distortions and can accurately predict image quality as judged by hu-man viewers. Recent advancements in generative models have resulted in a significant influx of “Gen/AI” content on the internet. Existing methods for detecting GenAI content have progressed significantly with improved generalization performance on images from unseen generative models. Here, we leverage the capabilities of existing IQA models, which effectively capture the manifold of real images within a bandpass statistical space, to distinguish between real and AI-generated images. We investigate the generalization ability of these perceptual classifiers to the task of GenAI image detection and evaluate their robustness against vari-ous image degradations. Our results show that a two-layer network trained on the feature space of IQA models demonstrates state-of-the-art performance in detecting fake images across generative models, while maintaining significant robustness against image degradations.

title: Visual Language Model-Based Food Safety Support for Persons with Blindness and Low Vision
authors: Ryan Hansen, Hardik Setia, Giles Hamilton-Fletcher, Aryan Jain, Zirui Liu, Mariam Zoair, Reem Aboutaleb, Qing Wen, Yu Li, John-Ross Rizzo
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Persons with blindness and low vision (pBLV) often lack access to visual indicators of food spoilage, which increases their risk of foodborne illnesses. To assess whether Visual Language Models (VLMs) could assist pBLV on food safety tasks we assessed four advanced VLMs — ChatGPT-4 Vision, LLaVA-OneVision, Gemini 1.5 flash, and VisPercep. VLMs were tasked with answering ‘Is this safe to eat?’ for each image in a 1) fresh vs rotten food task (120 images, 6 food types) and 2) a time-lapse sequence task of food spoiling (352 images, 16 food types). Here we report ‘true positives’ (“safe”+fresh), ‘true negatives’ (“unsafe” + rotten), ‘false negatives’ (“unsafe”+fresh) and crucially, ‘false positives’ (FP, “safe”+rotten) which are dangerous for the user. For task 1, VisPercep had the highest overall accuracy at 87.50% and always provided a definitive yes/no answer, but also had 13 false positives (21.67%); By contrast, ChatGPT-4 Vision was the safest, with an accuracy of 84.17%, only 1 false positive (1.67%), but gave no choice on 17/120 trials. Gemini had 84.17% accuracy, 12 FP, and 4 no choices, while LLaVA had the lowest performance with 46.67% accuracy, 16 FP, and 44 no choices. For the timelapse data, we compared VLMs against the cutoff point for being ‘safe to eat’ according to majority rules from 5 human participants. Here we found that Gemini had the highest accuracy at 80.97% and 20 FP, while ChatGPT-4 Vision was safer with a 71.02% accuracy and 14 FP. Vis-Percep had a 70.74% accuracy, with 96 FP, and LLaVA had 60.80% accuracy with 127 FP. Overall, we show large variations in the performance profile of these VLMs and highlight key issues that will need to be addressed to improve their food safety judgments for pBLV in the future.

title: A Safe Interaction Method for Tooth Preparation Robot Based on Hybrid Virtual Constraints
authors: Runqiu Peng, Dianhao Wu, Jingang Jiang, Xinying Zhang, Kun Qian
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Communication (ICAIRC)
abstract: Tooth preparation is a core step in fixed dental restoration. While robot-assisted tooth preparation can enhance precision, existing systems lack force constraints and guidance when operating in confined oral spaces. This makes it difficult for clinicians to stably control the robot for safe interaction and precise positioning. This paper proposes a safety interaction strategy for dental preparation robots based on hybrid feedback force. 3D reconstruction foraffected tooth was performed using CBCT images to establish virtual constraint boundaries. Based on caries lesion boundaries, interactive and non-interactive zones are delineated to prevent over-preparation of non-lesional tooth structure. A hybrid hierarchical bounding box tree structure was introduced to achieve fast collision detection. A zone-specific force feedback mechanism was constructed based on a spring-damper model, which virtual prohibitive forces protect non-interaction zones, while virtual progressive repulsive forces stabilize handpiece motion in interaction zones. The optical navigation system was used to provide real-time intraoperative pose tracking and calibration. A master-slave tooth preparation robot system was constructed, and in vitro experiments were conducted using bionic teeth. Experiments demonstrate that the master-slave tracking errors in the x, y, and z directions are within ±0.4mm, ±0.3mm, and ±0.8mm, respectively. The average error between the robot end effector and the target point at the tooth center is 0.60±0.44mm. The average tooth preparation accuracy is approximately 0.30mm, meeting clinical precision requirements. By employing zone-specific force feedback combined with realtime pose calibration, positioning errors are minimized, thereby enhancing surgical safety and precision. This strategy demonstrates significant potential in enhancing the safety, precision, and operability of tooth preparation procedures, making it clinically viable.

title: Weakly Supervised MaxN Estimation in Baited Remote Underwater Videos
authors: Amelia Sorrenti, Leonardo Giuseppe Russo, Sarinda Samarasinghe, Simone Palazzo, Concetto Spampinato
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Monitoring marine biodiversity is crucial for ecological research and conservation efforts, particularly in regions where traditional survey methods are invasive or laborintensive. Knowing the peak abundance of each species aids marine biologists in assessing ecosystem health. In this paper, we present a framework for estimating the Maximum Number (MaxN) of individuals of each fish species from Baited Remote Underwater Video (BRUV) footage from the Tyrrhenian Sea. Our data for this task is weakly labeled, providing only the MaxN count for each species and a single corresponding timestamp per video. Our proposed solution consists of two stages. First, we employ a state-of-the-art object detector to perform negative frame mining, efficiently identifying frames with no fish to supplement our sparse positive labels. Second, we leverage the DINOv2 vision transformer for feature extraction, feeding these features into a lightweight convolutional model to estimate the MaxN count. This approach circumvents the need for expensive bounding-box annotations, offering a scalable and cost-effective solution for large-scale marine monitoring. Our method achieves a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 4.23 on fish counts ranging from 1 to about 300, corresponding to an average relative error of 1%.

title: Focused Metasurface Based on Time Reversal in Enclosed Space
authors: Tongtong Li, Jun-Hui Ou, Jing-Tao Zeng, Yutao Zhang, Xiu Yin Zhang
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: In this paper, efficient electromagnetic focusing in enclosed spaces is achieved through metasurface. The phase distributioon of the proposed metasurface is obtained based on time-reversal algorithm. By this study realizes electro-magnetic focusing at 10 GHz at single-point and double-point in enclosed environment, the position of the focal point could be adjusted by the position of the source antenna. Simulational results indicate that the system achieved an improvement of at least 17 dB in the transmission efficiency when compared with the systems without introducing metasurface, This method is easy to sample the time-reversal field, which can be used for efficient and low-cost wireless energy transfer in enclosed spaces.

title: Automated Mathematical Expression Retrieval from Live Websites and PDF Documents
authors: Jagdish Yadav, Rehan Khan, Hitanshu Dadhich, Vasudevan Gounder, Vanshika Chawla
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: This research-backed project automates the extraction and retrieval of mathematical expressions from live websites as well as from static documents. Initially designed for web pages, the system now extends its functionality to deal with document formats such as PDFs and Word documents, pushing the accessibility of mathematical data further. Users can enter URLs or upload files, upon which content is dynamically retrieved and processed through automated tools. Mathematical expressions extracted are recognized and processed through LaTeX and MathJax to maintain semantic correctness and platform independence. These expressions are stored as safe, user-specific data and can be reused for the generation of personalized documents. The system successfully solves problems involving dynamic loading of content, nested parsing of math expressions, and handling of multi-source data. By far decreasing manual labor to extract and recycle academic mathematics content, this solution empowers teachers, researchers, and content producers.

title: FLD+: Data-Efficient Evaluation Metric for Generative Models
authors: Pranav Jeevan, Neeraj Nixon, Amit Sethi
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: We introduce a new metric to assess the quality of generated images that is more reliable, data-efficient, compute-efficient, and adaptable to new domains than the previous metrics, such as Fréchet Inception Distance (FID). The proposed metric is based on normalizing flows, which allows for the computation of density (exact log-likelihood) of images from any domain. Thus, unlike FID, the proposed Flow-based Likelihood Distance Plus (FLD+) metric exhibits strongly monotonic behavior with respect to different types of image degradations, including noise, occlusion, diffusion steps, and generative model size. Additionally, because normalizing flow can be trained stably and efficiently, FLD+ achieves stable results with two orders of magnitude fewer images than FID (which requires more images to reliably compute Fréchet distance between features of large samples of real and generated images). We made FLD+ computationally even more efficient by applying normalizing flows to features extracted in a lower-dimensional latent space instead of using a pre-trained network. We also show that FLD+ can easily be retrained on new domains, such as medical images, unlike the networks behind previous metrics - such as InceptionNetV3 pre-trained on ImageNet.

title: AI-Enabled Academic Research and Achievement Management Platform
authors: Vidya S. Kubde, Divyansh Mishra, Krish Patil, Aniket Golhait, Omkar Pardeshi
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Managing academic research outputs, faculty achievements, and student accomplishments is a significant challenge for higher education institutions. Current tools often operate in isolation, focusing solely on publications, credential verification, or reporting, which limits their overall effectiveness. This paper introduces an AI-enabled Academic Research and Achievement Management Platform that brings these areas together into one system. Key features include automated certificate verification using Optical Character Recognition (OCR), AI-assisted publication tracking through ORCID and Google Scholar integration, and auto-generated CVs for faculty and students. The system also supports accreditation reporting (NBA, NAAC, NIRF) with customizable exports and visual dashboards. By using artificial intelligence, the platform reduces redundant data entry, ensures accuracy, and simplifies academic administration. A comparison with traditional methods shows better efficiency, transparency, and scalability, making it a robust solution for digital transformation in higher education.

title: TriPlanNet: Triangle Path Planning Network for A Variable Truss Robot with Deep Learning
authors: Choonghan Lee, Leah Harris, Sehyun Oh, JooHyoung Cha, Jemin Lee, Yongin Kwon, Andrew Jang-ho Bae
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: We propose TriPlanNet, a learning-based locomotion planning algorithm for Variable Topology Truss (VTT) robots. Unlike traditional sampling-based planners that rely on repeated random sampling, TriPlanNet predicts the next support polygon using a deep neural network conditioned on the environment and goal. Trained on trajectories generated by the Polygon-Based Random Tree (PRT) algorithm, TriPlanNet achieves comparable or higher success rates while significantly reducing planning time and the number of required samples. Experimental results on simulated environments confirm that TriPlanNet enables faster and more efficient locomotion planning for reconfigurable truss robots.

title: Investigating the Synergistic Effects of Learning Rate, Optimizer, and Activation Function on Convolutional Neural Network Training
authors: Abhishek Kumar, Manimaran A
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) is changing the various fields, particularly computer vision, demonstrating unparalleled performance in work like image detection and computer vision. However, achieving optimal CNN performance on selection of hyperparameters, which are critical yet not learned during training. Among these, the learning rate, optimizer, and activation function significantly influence a network's convergence speed, training stability, and ultimate generalization capability. While individual studies often focus on optimizing a single hyperparameter, the interplay and synergistic effects between these three crucial components are less comprehensively explored. This research empirically investigates how different combinations of learning rates, optimizers and activation functions collectively impact CNN training dynamics and performance. Through systematic experimentation on benchmark image datasets, we aim to identify optimal configurations and uncover the complex relationships that emerge when these hyperparameters are considered in concert. Our findings reveal that how a parameter change effect the performance of the model and also the dynamic learning rate affect the performance of model.

title: Attention-Driven Hybrid Architecture for Automated Classification of Multiple Sclerosis
authors: Jampana Harshith Kumar, K Venkata Ratna Prabha, Gunaparthi Karthikeya, Seelanki Manoj Kumar, Chinni Hima Bindu, M Bindu Priya
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: Multiple sclerosis (MS) is a chronic autoimmune disease that mainly attacks the central nervous system (CNS) and results in neurological impairments. If not identified early, it can be life-threatening. This study introduces a deep learning-based hybrid model for automated detection of MS using brain magnetic resonance imaging (MRI). The model combines two pre-trained convolutional neural networks, InceptionV3 and ResNet50. The outputs of both networks are fused to obtain features, which are then reshaped and processed using a bidirectional long short-term memory (BiLSTM) network for feature learning. Evaluation on test images demonstrated strong performance, achieving 99.28% accuracy, 98.92% precision, 99.64% sensitiviy, and an F1-score of 99.28%. Future work will focus on lesion segmentation and expanding datasets to further improve generalizability.

title: A Glass-Based Wideband Patch Antenna with Reconfigurable Polarization and Beam-Pointing
authors: Yuan Ji, Linhui Pu, Jiwen Sun, Qingbo Li
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: This paper presents a miniaturized wideband microstrip patch antenna with a hybrid glass-air substrate, designed for agile polarization and beam-pointing control. The circular patch antenna achieves wideband performance and miniaturization primarily by concentrically placing a hollow cylindrical high-permittivity dielectric beneath it. The centrally positioned glass, with its relatively high permittivity, contributes to patch size reduction. Meanwhile, the glass significantly lowers the resonant frequencies of higher-order modes, while the surrounding air dielectric helps maintain the fundamental mode resonance, thereby enabling broadband characteristics across different radiation modes. Furthermore, directional and omnidirectional radiation patterns can be switched by selectively exciting four symmetrically placed disk-loaded probes. In addition, three orthogonal linear polarizations and two circular polarizations can be dynamically switched by controlling the phases and amplitudes of the four feeding probes. A 2×2 antenna array incorporating a reconfigurable microstrip feeding network is further designed to demonstrate the feasibility of electronic polarization and radiation pattern control.

title: A Bio-inspired Method and System for Accurate Detection of Spatial Objects via Visual-Tactile Fusion
authors: Yuheng Wang, Meng Chen, Tao Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: In the task of precise recognition and localization of objects in complex environments, tactile perception, or visual-tactile fusion perception, plays a significant role. Compared with the perception process using only visual sensors, the integration of tactile sensors enables the actuator to make contact with the object to be detected, perceive the physical properties of the environment, and improve the accuracy and stability of detection. However, in current research on tactile perception, there remains much room for exploration in aspects such as ensuring the safety of the target object during contact, collecting appropriate tactile data, and enabling the contact process itself to become a form of perception. By referencing the human proprioceptive mechanism, this paper designs relevant methods to transfer it to the visual-tactile fusion perception process of intelligent systems, thereby proposing solutions to the above three objectives. In addition, we have also designed and deployed other algorithmic modules, constructing a complete system capable of accomplishing the task of precise recognition and localization of objects. The results show that the method proposed in this paper has improved the accuracy of object detection, ensured the reliability of tactile data, and guaranteed the safety of the collection process.

title: Research on Human-Machine Cooperative Control Strategy for the Dual-Person Lower Limb Exoskeleton-Stretcher System
authors: Wang Sheng, Chen Chun-Jie, Zhang Jia-Le, Wang Zhuo, Cao Wu-Jing
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: As wearable robots capable of enhancing human movement abilities and assisting in rehabilitation, lower-limb exoskeletons have attracted widespread attention. Achieving efficient cooperative control between humans and lower-limb exoskeletons is crucial to optimizing their performance. However, research on cooperative control in multi-machine scenarios remains relatively limited. This study focuses on a dual-person lower-limb exoskeleton-stretcher system and proposes two cooperative control strategies: terrain information sharing and stretcher stability control. Terrain information sharing utilizes multi-source signal fusion using the depth camera and inertial measurement units of the lower-limb exoskeleton on the front side of the stretcher, providing terrain information and control predictions for the rear stretcher bearer and their lower-limb exoskeleton. This effectively compensates for the rear bearer's restricted field of vision during stretcher transportation. The dual-person lower-limb exoskeleton-stretcher system exhibits significant vertical stretcher vibrations during operation. To solve this problem, the natural frequency of the system is analyzed, and a method for stability control is proposed. Experiments show that this method can reduce the vibration amplitude of the dual-person lower-limb exoskeleton-stretcher system.

title: Dynamic Control of Radar High-Resolution Range Profiles for Non-Contact Targets Based on Time-Coded Metasurfaces
authors: Jin Feng Kang, Shao Nan Chen, Rui Bi, Miao Li, Si Ran Wang, Hui Dong Li, Jun Yan Dai, Qiang Cheng
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: Material stealth and metasurface stealth represent the mainstream approaches currently employed to achieve multispectral stealth for equipment. However, these techniques generally rely on functional layers that are permanently attached to the target surface, which restricts the flexibility of stealth control. To overcome this limitation, this paper introduces a non-contact dynamic stealth control method utilizing time-coded metasurfaces (TCM). The core of this method involves using high-degree-of-freedom harmonic modulation to generate high-resolution range profiles (HRRP) dummy targets with controllable range and equivalent scattering phase. These dummy targets interact with the scattering characteristics of the actual target in the same range unit, leading to superimposed feature vectors. The superposition ultimately results in either enhancement or weakening of the overall scattering signal. Crucially, simply switching the TCM coding enables seamless adjustment between stealth and enhancement. The effectiveness of the proposed method is demonstrated through theoretical simulations and experimental measurements, confirming its capability to modulate the echo characteristics of non-contact targets for either scattering enhancement or stealth. Consequently, this approach provides a solution for radar target signature control under non-contact conditions and contributes fresh perspectives to the development of dynamic stealth technology for stationary targets.

title: QGraphLLM: Graph Generation with Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Li Kang, Yihua Guo, Lei Zhao, Haoyu Lu, Sheng Xiang
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: In recent years, multimodal large language models (LLMs) have made significant advancements, particularly in text generation and understanding tasks. However, existing language models face limitations in processing and generating graph-structured data, particularly in understanding and generating graph structures. This study proposes QGraphLLM, a graph generation method based on multimodal large models, designed to address this gap. QGraphLLM integrates graph structure information into the input space of language models through a cross-modal projector, enabling the language model to understand and generate a wide range of graph structures, including molecular graphs, social network graphs, and knowledge graphs. Specifically, QGraphLLM employs a Q-Former architecture as the cross-modal projector, connecting graph encoders with language models. Through effective graph representation mapping, it supports text-conditioned graph generation tasks. Additionally, QGraphLLM introduces a uni-modal adapter (such as LoRA) to enhance the model's adaptability and training efficiency for downstream tasks. We conduct extensive experiments on multiple graph generation tasks, and the results show that QGraphLLM significantly outperforms traditional graph generation methods in terms of graph fidelity, flexibility, and task performance, providing a novel direction for the generation and application of graph-structured data.

title: Robotic Yarn Splicing in Warping: Uncertainty-Aware Perception and Manipulation
authors: Wei Shuai, Yang Gao, Peichen Wu, Guowei Cui
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Full-process automation of yarn splicing in the warping process faces significant challenges due to the highly uncertain state of yarn, which is thin, lacks texture, and exhibits complex behavior. Traditional methods relying on high-precision perception and complex modeling encounter substantial difficulties in this task. This paper proposes a novel approach: instead of pursuing high-precision perception and complex modeling of yarn state, we explicitly incorporate uncertainty into the motion planning framework. By leveraging physical constraints and coordinated robotic actions, we actively reduce uncertainty and simplify operational complexity. Specifically, our solution utilizes multi-view observations from a 2D industrial camera and YOLO-based segmentation for 3D yarn localization. For the multiple uncertainties in gripping, transferring, and splicing processes, we design a motion strategy combining physical constraints and domain randomizationbased soft motion planning. This approach achieves automatic splicing without requiring high-precision perception or complex closed-loop control. In the lab scenario, the robot autonomously completed 4 groups of 8 complete yarn splicing operations without any operator intervention. Results demonstrate that our method significantly improves operational stability and success rate, providing new insights for the automation of yarn manipulation.

title: The Architect of Advantage: How Robust Data Curation and Edge-Case Analysis Provide a Disproportionate Market Edge
authors: Rupam Priya
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: This is a technologically advanced era and most organizations have access to a lot of raw data, and therefore, there is a great deal of parity among them with regard to their ability to compete. This paper illustrates one key strategic flaw in losing valuable data signals due to the use of BI statistical filtering of complexity based on abnormal behavior (edge cases). Data curation represents a process that validates, adds value to, and provides strategic interpretation to complex proprietary data anomalies-which is a true source of sustainable competitive advantage. Therefore, this paper introduces a two-stage ML framework with adjustment to transform edge cases into high fidelity signals from simple statistical noise. In terms of application and impact, within a high velocity pervasive gaming environment, data curation produced an increase in top-line operating revenue of 14.5% during a time of year when tourist activity was at its lowest. From a technical standpoint, the proposed framework increases predictive model accuracy by a factor of 5. The findings of this research provide a direct quantitative measure of the relationship between superior data quality capability and market outperformance, and thus prove that data curation methodology is an essential component of the architectural foundation of developing a reliable interactive and mobile computing system.

title: Globally Optimal Registration of Dense Terrestrial Laser Scans from Coarse Sampling
authors: Dorian Kempf, Guillaume Caron, El Mustapha Mouaddib, Fumio Kanehiro
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: 3D point cloud registration is paramount for mapping complex environments. However, aligning overlapping clouds remains computationally demanding due to the increasing size and density of data from modern Terrestrial Laser Scanners. In this paper, we propose a lightweight, memory-efficient registration approach that operates directly on a coarse level of detail extracted from octree structures. Rather than relying on keypoints, we construct full coarse representations and associate each point with a descriptor to form global feature maps. We evaluate several point distribution strategies for building these representations and show that our approach significantly reduces processing time and memory usage while maintaining or improving registration accuracy.

title: Research on the Construction of Pragmatic Identity of College English Teachers Under AI-Enabled Teaching
authors: Ting Zhao
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing (MLNLP)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of artificial intelligence, College English teaching is increasingly enriched by AI-enabled tools. Grounded in Pragmatic Identity Theory and Rapport Management Theory, this paper investigates how College English teachers construct pragmatic identities in AI-enabled classrooms and what discourse strategies they use to realize these identities for effective communication. The paper observed the teachers' classes and transcribed the recordings to form a small corpus. And NVivo software was used to conduct qualitative analysis and to derive quantitative statistics, supplemented by the analysis of paralinguistic features, to explore the pragmatic identities and discourse strategies constructed by teachers in the classroom. The findings indicate teachers deploy discourse strategies–address form, phrases and words selection, speech acts, and paralinguistic feature–to construct pragmatic identities such as knowledge transmitter, technology facilitator, class organizer, teaching evaluator, and co-learner. AI-enabled College English teaching not only improves instructional effectiveness but also fosters a harmonious classroom climate.

title: Fixing Intersect Regions in Racetrack Generation
authors: Chatrtime Chatramornrat, Vishnu Kotrajaras
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Data Science and Information Technology (DSIT)
abstract: Procedural content generation (PCG) enables racing games to offer fresh tracks on demand, but naive generators often yield repetitive layouts or self-intersections that break playability. This paper presented an algorithm for automatic racetrack generation that combined chain-code-based generation with a robust intersection-resolution stage. We first synthesized diverse candidate tracks from various image-derived chain codes and enforced circuit validity. We then detected self-intersections and removed them by re-routing local connections and applying constrained smoothing while preserving global track shape. Finally, we used curvature, straight segment length, directional complexity, and feasible average speed to select or label tracks matching target challenge levels. The method produced varied closed circuits that were usable in the game. We described the full pipeline, provided qualitative examples, and discussed the effect of the proposed intersection-fixing methodology.

title: Comparison of STW and SWG for Low-Loss High-Power Terahertz TWT
authors: Yinyu Zhang, Yuan Zheng, Yuxin Wang, Yubin Gong
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: Traveling wave tubes (TWTs) operating over the terahertz (THz) frequency band face challenges in reducing losses and improving the output power of the slow wave structure (SWS). In this work, the adaptability of the sawtooth waveguide (STW) and serpentine waveguide (SWG) for THz TWTs is quantitatively compared. The algorithm optimizes the STW and SWG to achieve their respective maximum saturated output powers under identical operating conditions. The optimized STW reveals smoother dispersion and a balanced high-frequency characteristic, featuring adequate impedance and reduced loss, compared to the optimized SWG. Furthermore, the output performance is evaluated using the particle-in-cell (PIC) method. Comparative results show that the optimized STW delivers 12.7% higher output power and a 50% wider 3-dB bandwidth, while its saturated-length circuit exhibits 48.9% lower insertion loss relative to the optimized SWG. The STW demonstrates outstanding adaptability for low-loss, high-power THz TWT.

title: A Rain Streak-Aware Diffusion Model for Image Deraining with Stochastic Rain Synthesis and Multiscale Feature Fusion
authors: Yuxin Su
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Communication (ICAIRC)
abstract: Single-image deraining aims at obtaining clear images from rain-streak-polluted inputs and plays an essential role in alleviating visual degradation and promoting high-level vision tasks. In this paper, we propose a Rain-Streak-Aware Diffusion Model (RSADM) that integrates physically guided degradation with a novel denoising process to efficiently eliminate rain streaks of varying intensities and orientations. Specifically, the forward diffusion phase progressively adds realistic rain streaks with controllable physical parameters such as streak length, density, blur factor, and angle, and the reverse denoising phase employs a U-Net with a Local feature-Embedded Global feature extraction Module (LEGM) for improved contextual feature learning. The model also employs Multi-Scale Feature Fusion (MFF) to achieve balanced structure preservation and rain removal. Extensive experiments on a range of benchmarking datasets confirm that RSADM achieves superior deraining performance.

title: Adaptive Oscillators for Three-Plane Rhythmic Tracking: A Novel Solution for Complex Motion Patterns
authors: Haoran Zhang, Yue Ma, Yichen Lin, Jianquan Sun, Xiangyang Wang, Yu Zhu, Xiuyuan Wu, Xinyang Du, Zengle Ren, Bo Yuan, Xinyu Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Reliable rhythm tracking of lower-limb movements is essential for rehabilitation assessment and the control of wearable exoskeletons, as it supports both movement-mode interpretation and continuous phase estimation. Current approaches are often limited by two factors: (i) the interaction between motion modes and phase evolution is usually weakly modeled, and (ii) single-plane kinematic signals may not capture the rhythmic structure of complex activities. To overcome these limitations, we develop a three-plane rhythm tracking framework built on adaptive oscillators. The method integrates multiplane feature construction with plane-guided signal selection, enabling robust phase tracking under diverse motion patterns. Experimental results show that the proposed framework reduces phase-estimation RMSE by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 9. 7 \%} \boldsymbol{\sim} \mathbf{9 1. 9 \%}$</tex> compared with a uniaxial signal extraction baseline, demonstrating improved accuracy and robustness.

title: Operational and Economic Assessment of 750 kVA Three-Phase and Three 250 kVA Single-Phase Dry-Type Transformers
authors: Kamran Dawood, Muhammed Alperen Çakir, Güven Kömürgöz, Semih Tursun
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: In power distribution systems, engineers and transformer designers often face a critical design decision when selecting transformer configurations: whether to use a three-phase transformer or a bank of three single-phase transformers. This paper presents a comparative analysis of a 750 kVA three-phase dry-type transformer versus a bank of three 250 kVA single-phase dry-type transformers. A three-phase transformer is connected in a Dd configuration to deliver the same total capacity as a bank of three single-phase transformers. The aim is to evaluate the operational implications of each approach under equivalent conditions. The experimental study covers electrical performance parameters such as efficiency, cost, load losses, and no-load losses. This study aims to assist transformer designers, researchers, and facility managers in making optimized decisions when selecting transformer configurations. It concludes that the optimal choice depends on the specific priorities of the application, whether favoring compact efficiency and cost or modular redundancy and logistical flexibility.

title: Empirical Study of Microwave Oven Electromagnetic Leakage and its Impact on WLAN Signal Quality
authors: L. Zhang, J. Li
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: Microwave ovens operate in the 2.4 GHz frequency band, overlapping with the most widely used wireless local area network (WLAN) standard. While they are designed with shielding to minimise leakage, deterioration over time can cause electromagnetic interference (EMI). This paper investigates the effect of microwave oven operation on WLAN signal strength and transmission rate through controlled measurements in real environments. The experimental results show that WLAN performance degrades significantly when in proximity to an ageing oven (used for 17 years), particularly when approaching from specific angles (e.g., 45 degrees incidence) where leakage is stronger. Analysis indicates that interference is caused by wideband noise from magnetron radiation leakage, with directionality effects, polarisation matching, and multipath propagation contributing to the observed results. Possible mitigation measures, including shielding enhancement and frequency planning, are discussed.

title: Retinex-Based Dual-Branch Semantic Segmentation Network for Nighttime Driving Scene
authors: Shilin Zhang, Ying Zhang, Shuang Liu, Cui-Hua Zhang, Changchun Hua
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: In nighttime autonomous driving scene, accurate pixel-level semantic segmentation is crucial. Most of the existing methods are designed for daytime scenes, and directly applying them to nighttime data leads to a decline in accuracy. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a Retinex-based dual-branch semantic segmentation model specifically designed for nighttime scenes, i.e., RDN, which consists of three core components: Adaptive Decomposition based on Retinex (ADR), Illumination-assisted Segmentation Module (ISM), and Learnable Guided Filter (LGF). 1) Based on the Retinex theory, ADR is proposed to decompose low-light nighttime images into high-quality light-independent reflection and illumination. 2) We proposes ISM based on the attention mechanism to extract the semantic information hidden in important regions of the illumination. 3) Building upon the classical guided filter and adding dynamically learnable parameters, the LGF is developed to adaptively refine the blurred segmentation boundaries. Extensive experimental results on NightCity-fine and BDD100k-night demonstrate that the proposed RDN achieves superior performance, exceeding that of mainstream segmentation methods. The method enhances the adaptability and robustness of the segmentation network in nighttime scenes.

title: Research on a Vision-Inertial-Laser Tightly Coupled SLAM System for Indoor Low-Visibility Scenarios
authors: Jin Tian, Ning Xu, Junli Wang, Yingda Zhang, Liwei Sun
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: Despite demonstrating significant potential in robotic localization tasks, visual inertial odometry (VIO) performs poorly in indoor low-visibility scenarios, frequently leading to tracking loss and increased estimation errors. Existing solutions either rely on expensive sensor hardware or lack a systematic framework to address such complex environments. To address this, this paper proposes a tightly coupled visual-inertial-laser SLAM system tailored for indoor low-visibility scenarios. This study constructs a vision-driven tightly coupled framework incorporating image enhancement, dual dynamic feature rejection, and ground plane constraints to comprehensively enhance positioning accuracy and robustness in challenging environments. Experiments conducted on a self-built indoor low-visibility dataset and selected public datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach in improving positioning accuracy and strengthening system robustness, with performance surpassing a range of mainstream solutions.

title: Artificial Potential Field with Fuzzy Models for Gait Planning of Lower Limb Exoskeleton Robots on Different Stairs
authors: Chaobin Zou, Guangkui Song, Chen Yang, Ziyi Jiang, Xinhao Zhang, Yilin Wang, Jing Qiu, Hong Cheng
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Robotic Lower Limb Exoskeletons (LLEs) hold great potential for assisting human walking and facilitating gait training in daily life, thus attracting significant attention in the research community. However, terrain varies in daily scenarios, and gait patterns adapt accordingly; generating adaptive gait patterns for stairs of different sizes therefore remains a critical challenge. While several studies have explored the generation of adaptive gait trajectories for robots, most of these methods demand substantial computational resources and are unsuitable for real-time applications. To address this issue, this paper proposes a novel adaptive gait planning approach based on Type-2 fuzzy models and artificial potential field. This approach employs type-2 fuzzy model-based movement primitives to construct gait primitives and with the artificial potential field to form a universal gait planning framework applicable to diverse terrains. Compared with state-of-the-art methods, the proposed approach can generate more effective gait primitives for different terrains with lower computational demands, making it crucial for real-time robotic applications. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach is capable of generating human-like gait patterns for stairs with step heights ranging from 0.05 m to 0.20 m, and its computational efficiency is improved by over 90 % compared with existing methods.

title: IoT-Powered Waste Segregation System for Environmental Sustainability
authors: Madhuri Sahu, K.T.V Reddy, Sachine Harne, Pranjali Deshmukh, Abhishek Kumar, Hansaraj Wankhede
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Waste segregation in India is essential to reduce landfill waste, prevent pollution, and enable efficient recycling of materials like plastic, paper, metal, glass, and cardboard. It supports environmental sustainability, boosts the economy, and protects public health. A solution using CNN and IoT devices involves deploying smart waste bins equipped with cameras and sensors that classify waste (plastic, paper, metal, glass, cardboard) using CNN algorithms. The system employs a CNN-based object detection model to accurately identify and categorize waste types in real time. Trained on a comprehensive dataset, it efficiently classifies waste materials enabling precise sorting. The IoT devices then send real-time data to waste management systems for efficient collection and recycling. The system achieved 95% accuracy in classifying cardboard, glass, metal, paper, and plastic using a CNN trained on 5587 samples, with a validation accuracy of 95%. Integrating IoT and computer vision enabled real-time waste classification and segregation effectively. Finally, the incorporation of CNN and IoT in municipal trash segregation is a possible answer to the challenges of modern garbage management.

title: High-Fidelity 4x Neural Reconstruction of Real-Time Path Traced Videos
authors: Zhiqiang Lao, Zekai Chen, Jiali Cui, Marcos Conde Osorio, Heather Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: The demand for high-fidelity, high-resolution video rendering in real-time applications presents significant challenges, particularly under low-sample path tracing where inputs are both noisy and low-resolution (LR). Conventional video super-resolution methods excel at spatial upsampling but assume clean inputs, while image-based denoising and super-resolution pipelines ignore temporal dependencies, often resulting in frame-level artifacts. We propose a unified deep learning framework for video reconstruction that integrates denoising and super-resolution into a temporally consistent pipeline. Specifically, our method first applies low-resolution denoising using paired noisy/clean data to ensure stable inputs, followed by a patch-level video upsampling stage for temporal super-resolution. To further enhance scene fidelity, we introduce a novel scene-level refinement stage that facilitates training with overlooked regions, enforcing consistency beyond patch-based sampling. This design effectively recovers high-frequency details and preserves temporal coherence. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method achieves high-quality video reconstructions with substantial fidelity improvements.

title: Adaptive Quality Management in Industry 4.0 Using a Federated Reinforcement Learning Framework
authors: Lakshmaiya N
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Industry 4.0 integrates cyber-physical systems, IoT, and AI to enhance industrial efficiency, but interconnected processes demand adaptive quality control to prevent system-wide disruptions. Traditional federated learning (FL) enables decentralized training without sharing sensitive data but lacks adaptability in real-time fault management. This work introduces a Federated Reinforcement Learning (FRL) framework that combines policy-based reinforcement learning with FL to enable adaptive quality control and management in Industry 4.0 environments. The framework dynamically updates policies based on rewards from the environment, allowing timely actions such as fault detection and process resumption. Experimental validation using a gear fault diagnosis dataset from Kaggle achieved 95.28 % accuracy, 95.25 % F1-score, and a cumulative reward of 89 %, outperforming conventional FL approaches. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of the FRL framework in enabling decentralized, adaptive, and intelligent quality management for smart manufacturing systems.

title: Mode-Diverse Wave-in-Substrate Antenna for Coexisting Travelling and Standing Waves
authors: Wen-Jian Sun, Hang Wong
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: Future wireless systems aiming at digital-twin and integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) scenarios require antennas capable of supporting multiple functions within compact platforms. This work presents a mode-diverse wave-in-substrate antenna that exploits the natural dielectric image line (DIL) geometry to host coexisting transverse-magnetic (TM) and transverse-electric (TE) modes for the first time. An improved perturbation structure enhances their separation, allowing the TM mode to form a controllable travelling-wave radiation channel while the TE mode establishes a stable standing-wave resonance. Based on the modal analysis, a prototype incorporating 16 standing-wave elements and a continuous TM branch is developed, achieving approximately 55° forward scanning range with two complementary beam-forming regimes. The results verify that the substrate itself can act as a multifunctional electromagnetic region once its internal mode diversity is properly harnessed. The proposed approach provides a compact and versatile solution toward multimode, multifunctional antennas for next-generation intelligent wireless front-ends.

title: B-Vote: A Novel Stratification Framework for Fake Text Analysis
authors: J. Sahaya Sheeba Mangalam, R. Radha, R. Srividhya
publish: 2025 14th International Conference on System Modeling &amp; Advancement in Research Trends (SMART)
abstract: Misinformation which has also been referred to as fabricated news, consists of information that is either erroneous or misleading but propagated and presented as genuine journalistic reporting. Psychological factors affect the propensity of people to spread false news: empirical studies show that people have an increased interest in new and unusual events, which, consequently, increases neural activity. Given significant technological advancements, the challenge of rapidly disseminating misinformation has become increasingly salient. This paper addresses these challenges by detailing a machine learning process designed to identify and categorize fake news effectively. To tackle misinformation effectively, this study proposes a novel methodology grounded in Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing, aimed at identifying misinformation, with particular emphasis on news articles originating from sources considered unreliable. This study uses a dataset with equal parts authentic and fabricated news to explore how advanced machine learning algorithms can detect fake news. The methodology for identifying fake news follows a structured process: 1) Data acquisition, 2) Data preprocessing, which includes text cleansing and TF-IDF vectorization, and 3) Application of various classification algorithms. This research advocates for the use of ensemble machine learning methods, explaining how combining multiple algorithms enhances the accuracy of automated news article classification. The study examines a variety of textual features to distinguish fabricated content from genuine information. Using these capabilities, it is possible to train a synthesis of various machine learning algorithms using a variety of ensemble methods and assess its performance. The suggested study will ensure that the adopted methodology defines a sound preprocessing tool to increase precision of classification during the later phases of investigation. Analysis performed is simulated and the results are achieved successfully.

title: Neural Network-Based Approach for Breast Cancer Prediction
authors: Rushikesh Thakare, Rajendra Rewatkar, Rasika Ghodkhande, Sushil Kale, Pradnyawant Gote, Chandrayani Rokde
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Breast cancer is still one of the world's top causes of death for women, therefore increasing survival rates requires early and precise identification. Using image processing and artificial neural network (ANN) approaches, a breast cancer detection prototype was created for this study. Users can upload mammography pictures to the system, and adaptive median filtering is used to preprocess the images and remove noise. Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) segmentation is used to separate suspicious regions, and an ANN-based predictor is then used to classify the regions. To verify practical applicability, the model was further tested using mammography images. As measured by evaluation measures such as accuracy, precision, recall, and F1score, the system performed well, attaining up to 90.5% accuracy for malignant cases and 80.9% accuracy for benign instances. The potential of neural network-based methods as clinical decisionsupport aids is demonstrated by these findings. Although the prototype cannot replace expert diagnosis, it can help radiologists by lowering diagnostic errors, cutting down on unneeded biopsies, and speeding up breast cancer screening.

title: Suburban community characteristics and pandemic spread: Australian retrospectives and machine learning-based insights
authors: Fangyu Zhou, Tasadduq Imam, Shahadat Uddin, Shakir Karim
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 18th Health Informatics Knowledge Management Conference
abstract: None

title:
The Effect of Magnetization Direction and Stacking of Layers on Charge to Spin Conversion in Graphene on 1T-TaS
                    <sub>2</sub>
authors: Juraj Mnich, Marko Milivojević, Martin Gmitra
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: Spintronic devices require effective control of the electrons’ spins. Using the Rashba-Edelstein effect, one converts charge current to non-equilibrium spin accumulation. The efficiency of the process is determined by the intrinsic properties of the material’s band structure. Graphene-based van der Waals heterostructures prove to be a class of materials with highly tunable properties controlled by their composition and relative twist between the layers. Proximity-induced spin-orbit coupling in graphene due to the charge density wave phase in nearby transition-metal dichalcogenides significantly affects its spin physics and related charge to spin conversion efficiencies. Here we present a comprehensive study of the charge to spin conversion coefficients in the graphene on 1T-TaS<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> monolayer for different magnetization directions and charge density wave phase in 1T-TaS<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>.

title: Phase Current Measurement Based on Magnetic Field Sensor Array for SLG Fault in Three-Core Cable
authors: Qirong Wu, Xueyang Zhang, Guogang Zhang, Jinghan Xu, Zhaoyan Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Phase current is a significant parameter for condition monitoring of three-core cable, especially in detection of single line-to-ground (SLG) fault. Conventional current transducer is unable to measure phase current of three-core cable directly and need to rely on optimization algorithms for inversion. However, factors such as rotation, eccentricity, and tilt during measurements have not been comprehensively considered, as well as the attenuation of high-frequency transient magnetic fields through the magnetic steel tape of the three-core cable during faults. Thus, this article presents a phase current transducer based on magnetic field sensor array for phase current measurement of SLG fault in three-core cable. First, the magnetic field distribution of three-core cable is considered to improve the magnetic sensor array, and the phase current calculation method is proposed accounting for rotation, eccentricity, and tilt of phase current transducer. Meanwhile, the attenuation of high-frequency transient magnetic flux density B through the magnetic steel tape of the three-core cable is analyzed. Then, compensate for the attenuation current and implement the three-core cable phase current inversion by differential evolution (DE) computation method. The feasibility and effectiveness of the designed transducer are validated through phase current measurement experiments under rated current, continuous SLG fault, intermittent SLG fault, and full-scale prototype SLG fault. The experimental results show that the designed transducer satisfies the requirements for engineering applicability of phase current measurement in SLG fault detection.
