title: Data-Driven Control of T-Product-Based Dynamical Systems
authors: Ziqin He, Yidan Mei, Shenghan Mei, Xin Mao, Anqi Dong, Ren Wang, Can Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: Data-driven control is a powerful tool that enables the design and implementation of control strategies directly from data without explicitly identifying the underlying system dynamics. While various data-driven control techniques, such as stabilization, linear quadratic regulation, and model predictive control, have been extensively developed, these methods are not inherently suited for multilinear dynamical systems, where the states are represented as higher-order tensors. In this article, we propose a novel framework for data-driven control of T-product-based dynamical systems (TPDSs), where the system evolution is governed by the T-product or its variants between a third-order dynamic tensor and a third-order state tensor. In particular, we offer necessary and sufficient conditions to determine the data informativity for system identification, stabilization by state feedback, and T-product quadratic regulation of TPDSs with detailed complexity analyses. Finally, we validate our framework through both synthetic and real-world examples.

title: TRO-Based Dual-Domain Voltage Co-Regulation of Digital Logic and SRAM in SoCs
authors: Zhaoxu Wang, Xi Deng, Mingyang Gong, Zhenhao Li, Runze Yu, Zhenglin Liu, Xuecheng Zou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: Conventional low-power system-on-chips (SoCs) commonly regulate digital logic using adaptive voltage and frequency scaling (AVFS), while SRAM voltage is managed by an independent scaling policy. This separation leaves cross-domain SRAM-related critical paths over-margined and prevents system-level energy optimization. This brief presents a unified voltage co-regulation framework that jointly tunes the digital and SRAM supply rails to minimize total SoC energy. A tunable replica oscillator (TRO) is repurposed from a digital timing monitor into a dual-mode delay allocator: its programmable level sets the digital timing slack, and an all-digital AVFS loop adjusts the digital supply to lock the target frequency. The released cycle budget is then converted into SRAM voltage reduction, determined by a cache-based canary test. Domain-level power is profiled on-chip to enable a measurement-driven search for the dual-domain minimum-energy point (DD-MEP) without relying on PVT-dependent model parameters. Silicon results from a taped-out 110-nm Cortex-M3 SoC demonstrate up to 11.4% total energy reduction compared with digital-only AVFS, with only 0.05% area overhead.

title: Bio-Channel Aware Adaptive Signal Processing: Enabling Efficient Wireless Optogenetic Neural Control
authors: Yitong Zhang, Chengyu Guo, Xiao Liu, Wenrui Li, Xiqing Liu, Tianyi Yan
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications
abstract: Wireless optogenetics represents a transformative approach to neural control, enabling precise, non-invasive modulation of brain circuits through light-sensitive proteins. However, integrating wireless communication with optogenetic stimulation faces significant challenges, including signal attenuation in biological tissues, high energy consumption, latency issues, and limited spatiotemporal precision. This article introduces the Bio-Channel Aware Adaptive Signal Processing (Bio-CAASP) framework, a novel solution that dynamically estimates bio-tissue channel characteristics, including multi-path scattering and attenuation, and adapts signal processing techniques, such as compressive sensing and beamforming, for optimized wireless transmission. By incorporating real-time artificial intelligence (AI)-driven adjustments, Bio-CAASP can potentially reduce energy consumption and improve targeting precision through tissue-aware adaptation. This article also outlines its design choices and reports representative simulation case studies. Drawing on recent advances like AI-assisted wireless implants and closed-loop optrodes, this framework paves the way for applications in brain-machine interfaces, epilepsy suppression, and behavioral studies in freely moving subjects. Ultimately, Bio-CAASP bridges wireless communications and neuroscience, fostering energy-efficient, reliable neuromodulation technologies.

title: High-Selectivity Sensing of 2-Butanone by Ni-Doped ZnO Nanoparticles Derived from ZIF-8
authors: Zhuangzhuang Mu, Fangling Zhou, Zhenyu Yuan, Fanli Meng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Nanotechnology
abstract: 2-Butanone is a common colorless, flammable volatile organic compound (VOC) in chemical, electronics, new energy, and petroleum fields. However, its hazards to human health and its danger as a raw material for crystal methamphetamine production underscore the high necessity for developing detection technologies targeting it. In this study, Ni-doped ZnO was synthesized from a ZIF-8 derivative precursor for gas sensing applications. The phase composition, microstructure, and elemental chemical states of the samples were characterized by XRD, SEM, TEM, and XPS. Gas sensing tests showed that Ni doping reduced the optimal operating temperature from 320°C for pure ZnO to 300°C. The Ni-doped ZnO exhibited a significantly enhanced response (R<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">a</sub>/R<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">g</sub> = 287.5) to 100 ppm 2-butanone at 300°C, along with excellent selectivity and repeatability. Furthermore, the sensor achieved effective detection of 2-butanone at concentrations as low as 0.1 ppm (R<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">a</sub>/R<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">g</sub> = 2.5). This study confirms that utilizing ZIF-8 as a precursor combined with Ni doping is an effective strategy for enhancing the detection performance of ZnO-based sensors for 2-butanone, providing a viable direction for developing high-sensitivity metal oxide (MOX) gas sensors.

title: FedHRA: A Joint Optimization Framework for Fast Convergent Decentralized Federated Learning in LEO Satellite Networks_supp1-3664674.pdf
authors: Zejun Li
publish: N/A
abstract: Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellites are playing an important role in earth observation, providing valuable images for training machine learning (ML) models used in tasks such as environmental monitoring and pattern recognition. However, due to unstable communication links and limited downlink bandwidth, it is economically impractical to transmit all raw images to ground stations (GSs) for model training. Federated learning (FL), a privacy-preserving distributed machine learning method, can reduce the communication overhead by exchanging model parameters. Generally, FL needs a fixed central server to aggregate the global model, which is challenging in LEO satellite networks, given the dynamic nature of satellite orbits. To overcome this, we propose a decentralized federated learning (DFL) framework that enables efficient model aggregation through satellite collaboration. Specifically, the proposed framework, named FedHRA, is based on model-agnostic meta-learning (MAML), which jointly optimizes hyperparameters and resource allocation to mitigate straggler effect and address statistical heterogeneity. Extensive numerical results on MNIST and CIFAR-10 datasets demonstrate that FedHRA achieves shorter learning time and higher model accuracy compared to the benchmark frameworks.

title: Diffusion Stochastic Learning Over Adaptive Competing Networks_supp1-3666428.pdf
authors: Yike Zhao
publish: N/A
abstract: This paper studies a stochastic dynamic game between two competing teams, each consisting of a network of collaborating agents. Unlike fully cooperative settings, where all agents share a common objective, each team in this game aims to minimize its own distinct objective. In the adversarial setting, their objectives could be conflicting as in zero-sum games. Throughout the competition, agents share strategic information within their own team while simultaneously inferring and adapting to the strategies of the opposing team. We propose diffusion learning algorithms to address two important classes of this network game: i) a zero-sum game characterized by weak cross-team subgraph interactions, and ii) a general non-zero-sum game exhibiting strong cross-team subgraph interactions. We analyze the stability performance of the proposed algorithms under reasonable assumptions and illustrate the theoretical results through experiments on Cournot team competition and decentralized GAN training.

title: Classical and Sliding Mode Controllers Applied to the Reaction Wheel Inverted Pendulum
authors: Rafael Máximo Da Silva, Jean Marcos De Souza Ribeiro, Rodrigo Cardim, Marcelo Carvalho Minhoto Teixeira
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In this work, the practical implementation of the control system for the reaction wheel inverted pendulum (RWIP) was carried out using low-cost hardware components. A switch control strategy was adopted to operate the swing up and stabilization controllers. This switch control method integrates two distinct controllers: one designed to raise the pendulum to the inverted region, and another to stabilize it at the unstable equilibrium point in the inverted position. A nonlinear swing up controller was designed to address the problem of moving the pendulum from the stable equilibrium position to a favorable position for the application of a stabilization controller. For the stabilization region, linear controllers, proportional– integral–derivative (PID) and eigenvalue-placement—were designed, as well as a nonlinear controller based on the conventional sliding mode control (SMC) technique. The control laws were programmed using scripts developed on the Arduino IDE with the ESP32 prototyping board, and data collection was performed through serial communication between MATLAB® and the ESP32. Performance indices were calculated to evaluate the response of the controlled variable in the stabilization region.

title: Decoding Neuroal Activity ’streams’ in Cultured Networks: A Transfer Learning Approach Using VGG16 and Signal-to-Image Transformation
authors: Hiroki Asada, Suguru N. Kudoh
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Decoding stimulus-evoked electrical activity in cultured neuronal networks is essential for elucidating the fundamental mechanisms of brain function. Using a microelectrode array, we captured the spatiotemporal dynamics of neuronal population activity and developed an analytical framework based on stream representation of neuronal activity propagation. From the reconstructed instantaneous spatial patterns (ISPs), we generated two types of images: time-information-prioritized neuronal activity pattern (TIP-NAP) images and space-information-prioritized neuronal activity pattern (SIP-NAP) images. Each image type was trained and classified using transfer learning with the VGG-16 convolutional neural network. The results showed that the classification accuracy of TIP-NAP images (99.9 ± 0.5 %) significantly outperformed that of SIP-NAP images (96.3 ± 9.1%) and the conventional SVM-based method (97.3–97.7%). The primary contributions of this study are threefold: First, we established that our proposed Signal-to-Image Transformation technique, based on ISPs, effectively represents the stream of neuronal activity. Second, we demonstrated that converting TIP-NAP images enables highly accurate decoding via VGG-16 transfer learning. Third, our comparative analysis provides compelling evidence that temporal continuity—the temporal structure of neuronal activity—plays a more critical role in information encoding than instantaneous spatial synchronization within biological networks.

title: IoT-Based Loneliness Monitoring for Older Adults: Lessons from Japanese and US Communities
authors: Teruhiro Mizumoto, Viktor Erdélyi, Xiayan Ji, Ahhyun Yuh, Takeshi Nakagawa, Takashi Suehiro, Hajime Nagahara, Yasuyuki Gondo, George Demiris, Oleg Sokolsky, Insup Lee, Teruo Higashino
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Social isolation and loneliness pose critical health risks to elderly populations, with mortality impacts that rival smoking and obesity. The COVID-19 pandemic has intensified these risks and exposed the limitations of current monitoring approaches. Most sensor-based monitoring systems remain impractical for wide deployment due to their complex maintenance needs and heavy technical overhead. They often demand dense sensor networks, frequent interventions, and substantial infrastructure - barriers that prevent adoption beyond controlled research settings. To address these challenges, we present a simplified sensing system that enables practical monitoring of elderly loneliness across different cultural contexts. Our approach uses a small set of carefully chosen sensors to capture key behavioral indicators while minimizing maintenance needs. Tests with 33 participants across Japan and US senior communities demonstrate practical accuracy (MAE = 3.98 for Japan and MAE = 6.35 for the US) with significantly reduced complexity. Post-deployment feedback revealed high system acceptability and low privacy concerns among participants, supporting the feasibility of long-term monitoring. The deployments revealed several differences in how social isolation manifests across cultures - from sleep patterns to activity rhythms. Through systematic feature selection based on these behavioral differences, we achieved improved predictive performance.We detail the technical architecture, deployment methods, and maintenance protocols needed for community-scale implementation. Our results show that effective elderly monitoring is possible without a large number of sensors, opening the path for sustainable, community-managed deployments.

title: ADF-SN: An Adaptive Dual-Domain Fusion and Self-Supervised Network for Wafer Image Denoising
authors: Zhongxu Li, Qian Zhan, Qihan He, Tundong Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Wafer image denoising is essential in semiconductor manufacturing, impacting yield and defect detection accuracy. Unlike general images, wafer images exhibit unique micron-scale structures, low-contrast defects, and fine edges, making denoising particularly challenging. However, traditional methods perform poorly with complex noise, and supervised learning methods rely on clean labeled data that is difficult to acquire, limiting robustness in practical applications. In this study, we propose an Adaptive Dual-domain Fusion and Self-supervised Network (ADF-SN). First, noise suppression and edge enhancement are performed in transform and spatial domains to obtain complementary feature representations. Then, an adaptive dual-domain fusion mechanism achieves dynamic balance between denoising and structure preservation. Finally, we present a Dual Attention Self-Supervised Network, which exploits redundant information from multiple noisy observations for high-quality restoration without requiring clean reference images. Experimental results on our Wafer4 dataset show that ADF-SN achieves average PSNR of 31.2722 dB and SSIM of 0.8461. This method has been deployed in wafer inspection equipment, completing the closed-loop from algorithm to application and demonstrating effectiveness, robustness, and practicality in semiconductor manufacturing.

title: Dual-mode magnetoelectric sensor based on multi-frequency magnetic field modulation principle for broadband measurements of weak magnetic fields
authors: Yulong Liu, Kang Wu, Hongcheng Zhou, Youqiang Wu, Tianhao Han, Biao Dong, Zhongming Yan, Yu Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Magnetoelectric (ME) composites demonstrate exceptional sensitivity to magnetic fields at specific resonant frequencies, whereas broadband detection remains challenging attributed to frequency-dependent ME conversion efficiency. In this study, a longitudinal series-connected ME sensor comprising three Mn-doped Pb(Mg1/3Nb2/3)O3-PbZrO3-PbTiO3 (Mn-PMN-PZT) single crystals bonded to a Metglas magnetostrictive substrate was designed, featuring dual-mode operation for extended frequency coverage while maintaining a low LOD (limit of detection). In the passive mode, the sensor achieved a remarkable ME voltage coefficient αV of 306.1 V/Oe at the resonant frequency of 12.34 kHz, with a LOD as low as 18.83 fT. In the active mode, a generalized formula for multi-frequency magnetic field modulation was derived and experimentally validated, with LODs of 92.9 pT, 86.9 pT, and 76.4 pT at 1 Hz, 10 Hz, and 100 Hz, respectively. Additionally, a critical frequency fc was identified for guiding the operational strategy as follows: the active mode and passive mode were employed for signals below fc and signals above fc, respectively. Furthermore, the sensor revealed a LOD of 114 pT in dual-mode operation across a broad frequency range of 1 Hz to 100 kHz. The temperature and vibration stability experiments indicate that the active mode is more effective in suppressing thermal drift and mechanical disturbances, thereby exhibiting superior temperature and vibration stability. The proposed dual-mode ME sensor enables wideband detection of weak magnetic fields, rendering it highly promising for applications in magnetic positioning, navigation, and other fields requiring precise magnetic field measurements.

title: Evolving Markov Chains: Online Mode Discovery and Recognition from Data Streams
authors: Kutalmlş Coşkun, Borahan Tümer, Bjarne C. Hiller, Martin Becker
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Markov chains are simple yet powerful mathematical structures to model temporally dependent processes. They generally assume stationary data, i.e., fixed transition probabilities between observations/states. However, live, real-world processes, like in the context of activity tracking, biological time series, or industrial monitoring, often switch behavior over time. Such behavior switches can be modeled as transitions between higher-level modes (e.g., running, walking, etc.). Yet all modes are usually not previously known, often exhibit vastly differing transition probabilities, and can switch unpredictably. Thus, to track behavior changes of live, real-world processes, this study proposes an online and efficient method to construct Evolving Markov chains (EMCs). EMCs adaptively track transition probabilities, automatically discover modes, and detect mode switches in an online manner. In contrast to previous work, EMCs are of arbitrary order, the proposed update scheme does not rely on tracking windows, only updates the relevant region of the probability tensor, and enjoys geometric convergence of the expected estimates. Our evaluation of synthetic data and real-world applications on human activity recognition, electric motor condition monitoring, and eye-state recognition from electroencephalography (EEG) measurements illustrates the versatility of the approach and points to the potential of EMCs to efficiently track, model, and understand live, real-world processes.

title: OmniSketch: Sketch-Guided Text-to-3D Generation with High-Fidelity Geometry and Texture
authors: Xinbin Sun, Zhentong Xu, Guodong Wang, Fuqing Duan, Junli Zhao, Zhenkuan Pan, Mingquan Zhou
publish: IEEE Computer Graphics and Applications
abstract: Sketch-based or text-based 3D content generation, which rapidly converts abstract concepts into 3D models, plays a vital role in accelerating digitalization. However, the sparse sketch, absent texture and unaligned dataset pose considerable challenges for cross-modal synthesis. To address these issues, we introduce OmniSketch, a sketch-guided text-to-3D generation framework that decouples the complex sketch-to-3D task into a three-stage pipeline. Firstly, we employ a conditional diffusion model to fuse structural constraints from the sketch with semantic descriptions, generating a high-quality intermediate reference view. Subsequently, a Rectified Flow model is utilized to produce a 3D model with high-fidelity geometry. Finally, we apply a geometry-guided multi-view image synthesis method to furnish the model with view-consistent, high-quality textural details. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method surpasses state-of-the-art approaches in geometric fidelity and texture consistency. Leveraging our framework, we also construct a multi-modal sketch-to-3D dataset and an interactive platform to promote the application of digital content generation.

title: 100 Gbit/s Secure and Real-Time Vacuum Fluctuation Based Quantum Random Number Generator
authors: Axl Bomhals, Cédric Bruynsteen, Caro Meysmans, Achim Vandierendonck, Xin Yin
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: True randomness is an essential requirement in numerous classical and quantum cryptographic systems and applications to guarantee secure communication, necessitating the research and design of promising quantum random number generators. As communication systems become more randomness consuming, current generators fail to deliver the required throughput mainly due to slow and often delayed post-processing. To address these performance issues, this work presents an FPGA-based quantum random number generator based on our previous work that uses a low optical power, custom-designed integrated vacuum fluctuation source. An adaptive, efficient and fast Toeplitz hashing scheme is developed with a configurable security factor, making it suitable for a wide range of applications. The high clearance and large bandwidth of the low-noise photonic-electronic detector in combination with hardware accelerated processing makes it possible for the presented dual channel generator to deliver a record generation rate of 100.3 Gbit/s for secure usage in real-time. Due to the small form factor, the design shows promise to be fully integrated into a custom-packaged device.

title: Semi-Supervised Contrastive Learning for Label-Efficient Electrocardiogram Noise Detection
authors: Saifur Rahman, Chandan Karmakar
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Electrocardiogram (ECG) signals are highly sensitive to various noise artifacts, which can degrade the reliability of automated diagnostic systems. Therefore, accurate noise detection is a critical pre-processing step to improve downstream classification performance and ensure clinical reliability. However, manual annotation of noisy and clean ECG segments is labour-intensive and not scalable. This paper presents a novel semi-supervised contrastive learning framework for robust and scalable detection of noisy ECG signals across multiple benchmark datasets. Extensive evaluations were conducted to assess the semi-supervised learning framework’s performance in mixed-database, individual database and cross-dataset transferability. The proposed approach achieved classification performance even with minimal labelled data, attaining an average F1-score of 96.87% with only 5% labelled samples in mixed-database scenarios. The model demonstrated consistent high accuracy and F1-scores exceeding 99% on prominent datasets such as PTB-XL and CINC 2014 with 5% labelled data. Cross-dataset transfer experiments further validated the generalizability of the learned representations, with most source-target pairs achieving above 99% accuracy and F1 scores with only 5% labelled data. An ablation study confirmed that employing a single projection head yields optimal performance, balancing accuracy and model complexity. These results highlight the effectiveness and practical applicability of the proposed framework for ECG quality assessment in real-world clinical settings.

title: RARTC: Resource Allocation in RIS-UAV and THz Enabled Cloud-Edge-End CPN for IoT Based on Multi-Agent Soft Actor-Critic Approach
authors: Kaiwen Pan, Meng Li, Suyu Lv, Pengbo Si, Haijun Zhang, F. Richard Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: The large-scale expansion of the Internet of Things (IoT), coupled with its integration into application scenarios like smart transportation, remote healthcare, and intelligent agriculture, has led to surging demands for enhanced computational capabilities to process massive real-time data. These demands pose significant challenges to meet the quality of service (QoS) requirements. In order to address these issues, this paper proposes a cloud-edge-end collaborative computing power network (CPN) architecture based on Terahertz (THz) communication and assisted by reconfigurable intelligent surface unmanned aerial vehicle (RIS-UAV). Here, CPN enables flexible resource collaboration for task offloading; THz communication supports large-volume data transmission, while RIS-UAV mitigates THz's limitations of small coverage and blockage sensitivity. To unify control of the computation resource and transmission resource allocation, we introduce the Resource-Allocation Multi-Agent Soft Actor-Critic (RA-MASAC) approach, which is a multi-agent reinforcement learning-based algorithm for joint optimization of resource allocation. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms the existing baselines significantly.

title: Residual Structural Traces in Logic Locking: Insights from Redundancy Analysis and Beyond
authors: Leon Li, Alex Orailoglu
publish: IEEE Design & Test
abstract: The integrated circuit (IC) supply chain has undergone a profound transformation over the past two decades, propelled by escalating manufacturing complexity, rising fabrication facility costs, and the imperative for rapid time-to-market. To address these pressures, the semiconductor industry has adopted a horizontally distributed business model in which companies strategically outsource design, fabrication, and testing stages to specialized entities worldwide. This globalized approach delivers significant gains in production efficiency and cost-effectiveness, yet simultaneously exposes the supply chain to serious security vulnerabilities. Foremost among these are intellectual property piracy, overproduction, and reverse engineering, each posing substantial threats to the proprietary designs and technological advancements in which companies invest heavily. Figure 1 illustrates the principal stages and stakeholders in the IC design and manufacturing pipeline, together with the corresponding security threats at each stage. These realities make it imperative to establish robust trust mechanisms across the global supply chain to safeguard innovation and ensure the continued viability of this distributed production model.

title: Enhanced Empirical Green’s Function Reconstruction Using Adaptive Stacking Strategy for Seismic Interferometry
authors: Zhiwen Li, Qinxu Zhang, Renjie He, Xiaohua Zhou, Zubin Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Seismic interferometry reconstructs empirical Green’s functions (EGFs) by cross-correlating and stacking ambient noise recordings, and has demonstrated significant potential in crustal imaging and seismic monitoring. Conventional approaches typically require stacking data over several months or even years to achieve a high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). To address the limitations posed by uneven noise source distribution and insufficient data observation periods, this study proposes an adaptive stacking strategy. First, a root–mean–square ratio selection criterion (RMSR) is employed to identify a subset of cross–correlation functions (CCFs) that positively contribute to EGF reconstruction. Then, an adaptive covariance filter (ACF) is applied to enhance coherent signals. Finally, a time–frequency phase–weighted stacking (tf–PWS) technique is introduced to improve instantaneous phase coherence and suppress random–phase noise. To evaluate the proposed method, we systematically compare multiple stacking strategies under both time–domain and frequency–domain cross–correlation frameworks, including: traditional linear stacking, Selective stacking, RMSR, the combination of ACF with conventional PWS, ACF with tf–PWS, and integrated schemes incorporating Selective and RMSR stacking. Synthetic experiments show that the proposed approach effectively suppresses phase–incoherent CCFs and significantly enhances the SNR of the reconstructed EGFs. Furthermore, the method is applied to continuous seismic data from the China National Seismic Network and dense array field data recorded in southwestern China under complex noise conditions. Results demonstrate that high–SNR, stable CCFs can be obtained using only approximately ten days of ambient noise. The proposed approach efficiently attenuates incoherent energy in dispersion images, yielding more continuous and concentrated energy distributions and significantly improving surface–wave dispersion curve extraction, while also enhancing computational efficiency.
