title: Multi-Scale Tensorial Summation and Dimensional Reduction Guided Neural Network for Edge Detection
authors: Lei Xu, Mehmet Yamac, Mete Ahishali, Moncef Gabbouj
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Edge detection has garnered considerable attention because of its exceptional ability to enhance performance in downstream computer vision tasks. In recent years, various deep learning methods have been explored for edge detection tasks, yielding significant performance improvements compared to conventional computer vision algorithms. In neural networks, edge detection tasks require considerably large receptive fields to provide satisfactory performance. In a typical convolutional operation, such a large receptive field can be achieved by utilizing a significant number of consecutive layers, which yields deep network structures. Recently, a Multi-scale Tensorial Summation (MTS) factorization operator was presented, which can achieve very large receptive fields even from the initial layers, In this paper, we propose a novel MTS Dimensional Reduction (MTS-DR) module guided neural network, MTS-DR-Net, for the edge detection task The MTS-DR-Net utilizes the MTS layers and the corresponding MTS-DR blocks as a new backbone to remove redundant information initially. Such a dimensional reduction module enables the neural network to focus specifically on relevant information (i.e., necessary subspaces). Finally, a weight U-shaped refinement module follows MTS-DR blocks in MTS-DR-Net We conducted extensive experiments on two benchmark edge detection datasets: BSDS500 and BIPEDv2, to verify the effectiveness of our model. The implementation of the proposed MTS-DR-Net can be found in https://github.com/LeiXuAI/MTS-DR-Net.git.

title: PerFairX: Is There a Balance Between Fairness and Personality in Large Language Model Recommendations?
authors: Chandan Kumar Sah
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: The integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) into recommender systems has enabled zero-shot, personality-based personalization through prompt-based interactions, offering a new paradigm for user-centric recommendations. However, incorporating user personality traits via the OCEAN model highlights a critical tension between achieving psychological alignment and ensuring demo-graphic fairness. To address this, we propose PerFairX, a unified evaluation framework designed to quantify the trade-offs between personalization and demographic equity in LLM-generated recommendations. Using neutral and personality-sensitive prompts across diverse user profiles, we benchmark two state-of-the-art LLMs, ChatGPT and DeepSeek, on movie (MovieLens 10M) and music (Last.fm 360K) datasets. Our results reveal that personality-aware prompting significantly improves alignment with individual traits but can exacerbate fairness disparities across demo-graphic groups. Specifically, DeepSeek achieves stronger psychological fit but exhibits higher sensitivity to prompt variations, while ChatGPT delivers stable yet less person-alized outputs. PerFairX provides a principled benchmark to guide the development of LLM-based recommender sys-tems that are both equitable and psychologically informed, contributing to the creation of inclusive, user-centric AI applications in continual learning contexts.

title: Introduction to the First Workshop on Vision Foundation Models and Generative AI for Accessibility
authors: Yapeng Tian, Yuhang Zhao, Jon E. Froehlich, Chu Li, Yuheng Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Vision foundation models and generative AI have recently made remarkable advances, enabling groundbreaking applications across computer vision, multimodal reasoning, and interactive AI systems. These models hold immense potential to enhance accessibility by powering assistive technologies that improve the daily lives of individuals with disabilities. At the same time, applying foundation models and generative AI in accessibility contexts presents key challenges, including fairness and bias, data scarcity, interpretability, and real-world deployment. This CV4A11y workshop brings together researchers from computer vision, human-computer interaction, and accessibility communities to identify challenges, explore emerging opportunities, and foster collaboration toward building inclusive, efficient, and responsible AI-powered accessibility systems.

title: Autoencoder-Based Dual Noise Suppression for Brain CT Imaging: SNR Optimization Toward Accurate Clinical Diagnostics
authors: Shubham Vishwakarma, Mayank Kathane, Jeet Nakrani, Dhiraj Nair, Archana Lakhe
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: In the realm of healthcare, systems including brain CT scans are affected by inevitable degradation that occurs in two forms, Poisson noise and periodic noise, which could interfere with diagnostic interpretation. In this work, we propose a deep learning denoising architecture that suppresses both types of noise through utilizing a convolutional autoencoder model while retaining important diagnostic information. The method we propose is completely compliant with DICOM files, so it can easily fit into the clinical workflow. We assessed the model performance on image quality by using the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) as our primary measure of importance and are able to show a major improvement after denoising. To add clinical relevancy, we also assessed whether our denoised images improved accuracy in downstream disease classification tasks to show improvement in accuracies. This work provides a scalable, efficient, and near real-time solution for interpreting CT scans and represents a major step forward toward artificial intelligence-enhanced diagnostic systems.

title: Enabling Practical Dexterous Grasping: A Low-Cost VR Teleoperation System with Diffusion Policy and Factor Analysis
authors: Lingzheng Dong, Zhihao Zhang, Yaxun Yang, Sören Schwertfeger
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Achieving human-level dexterity in robotic manipulation remains a central challenge in robotics, particularly in unstructured real-world environments. In this work, we present a low-cost, end-to-end imitation learning system for dexterous grasping that emphasizes data efficiency and practical accessibility. Our system combines a modular robotic platform—composed of a Kinova Gen2 arm and the open-source LEAP Hand—with an immersive VR-based teleoperation interface for intuitive and efficient demonstration collection. Using an existing diffusion policy model, we show that highquality grasping behaviors can be learned from a limited number of demonstrations. We further conduct systematic real-world experiments to analyze the influence of key factors on policy performance, including the amount of demonstrations, action sequence length, demonstration generalization level and action space representation. Our findings provide actionable insights for developing reliable and reproducible learning-based robotic systems. This work is open-sourced at https://github.com/donglzh789/EPDG.

title: BAdd: Bias Mitigation Through Bias Addition
authors: Ioannis Sarridis, Christos Koutlis, Symeon Papadopoulos, Christos Diou
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Computer vision datasets often exhibit biases in the form of spurious correlations between certain attributes and target variables. While recent efforts aim to mitigate such biases and foster bias-neutral representations, they fail in complex real-world scenarios. In particular, existing methods excel in controlled experiments on benchmarks with single-attribute injected biases, but struggle with complex multi-attribute biases that naturally occur in established CV datasets. In this paper, we introduce BAdd, a simple yet effective method that allows for learning bias-neutral representations invariant to bias-inducing attributes. This is achieved by injecting features encoding these attributes into the training process. BAdd is evaluated on seven benchmarks and exhibits competitive performance, surpassing state-of-the-art methods on both single- and multi-attribute bias settings. Notably, it achieves +27.5% and +5.5% absolute accuracy improvements on the challenging multi-attribute benchmarks, FB-Biased-MNIST and CelebA, respectively.

title: WaveDamp: Enhancing Natural Robustness in Endoscopy Through Wavelet-Based Frequency Damping
authors: Haiko Middeljans, Carolus H.J. Kusters, Tim J.M. Jaspers, Martijn R. Jong, Rixta A.H. van Eijck van Heslinga, Florance C. Slooter, Albert J. de Groof, Jacques J. Bergman, Peter H.N. de With, Fons van der Sommen
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: In recent years, research has shown that convolutional neural networks tend to rely heavily on high-frequency (HF) information in images. However, this HF content is highly susceptible to corruptions such as blur and noise, which can significantly degrade model performance. In practical applications such as endoscopy, distortions like blur and overexposure similarly impair the performance of deep learning-based computer-aided detection (CADe) systems. A widely adopted strategy to increase the generalization and robustness of neural networks is the use of data augmentation. However, these methods are primarily evaluated on artificially corrupted images covering specific perturbation types, rather than on naturally corrupted images encountered in practice. To improve the robustness of CADe systems on naturally distorted images, we propose WaveDamp, a novel augmentation pipeline that suppresses HF content in images through a wavelet-based frequency damping architecture. WaveDamp forces the model to focus on robust features, thereby enhancing its reliability under distortions. To quantify the robustness improvements, we benchmark models trained using WaveDamp and various state-of-the-art (SOTA) augmentation methods on endoscopic images spanning a broad quality range. Moreover, the frequency and shape biases of models are analyzed to explore their contribution to the robustness increase. Our experimental results demonstrate that WaveDamp improves the area under the receiver operating characteristic curve by up to 0.084 over the baseline and 0.050 compared to other SOTA methods. Code is available at https://github.com/HaikoMidlWaveDamp.

title: GLAD: Generalizable Tuning for Vision-Language Models
authors: Yuqi Peng, Pengfei Wang, Jianzhuang Liu, Shifeng Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Pre-trained vision-language models, such as CLIP, show impressive zero-shot recognition ability and can be easily transferred to specific downstream tasks via prompt tuning, even with limited training data. However, existing prompt tuning methods face two main challenges: (1) In few-shot scenarios, data scarcity often leads to overfitting, making the model sensitive to changes in the input domain. (2) To mitigate overfitting, these methods typically rely on complex task-specific model architectures and sensitive hyperparam-eter tuning, severely restricting their general applicability. To address these issues, we propose a simpler and more general framework called GLAD (Generalizable LoRA tuning with RegulArized GraDient). We show that merely applying LoRA achieves performance in downstream tasks comparable to current state-of-the-art prompt-based meth-ods. While LoRA is effective and easy to use, it remains susceptible to overfitting in few-shot learning scenarios. To mitigate this risk, we introduce a gradient-based regular-ization technique. This technique effectively steers the op-timization trajectory, encouraging the model to find a more stable parameter region that is robust to variations in data distribution. Through extensive experiments conducted on 15 benchmark datasets, we demonstrate that GLAD out-performs previous tuning approaches in terms of base-to-novel class generalization, image domain generalization, and cross-dataset generalization. The code is available at https://github.com/YuqiPeng77/GLAD.

title: Learning Robust Aligned Representations Across Multiple Visual Modalities in Human Action Recognition
authors: David J. Lerch, Bastian Rothenburger, Zeyun Zhong, Manuel Martin, Frederik Diederichs, Rainer Stiefelhagen
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: We propose Cross-Modal Video Representation Alignment (CMVRA), a novel framework for human action recognition that leverages multiple visual modalities—RGB, infrared (IR), depth, and skeleton data—to learn robust, generalizable representations with reduced reliance on annotated data. By employing contrastive learning, CMVRA effectively aligns these modalities, enhancing the model's ability to integrate complementary information and capture richer representations across domains. This multi-modal alignment is crucial for improving recognition performance in diverse and challenging contexts. We propose a unified multi-modal embedding framework that aligns RGB, depth, infrared, and skeleton data, enhancing robustness and feature diversity, while also advancing alignment techniques by demonstrating that fully integrated multi-modal alignment outperforms traditional pairwise strategies. Extensive experiments conducted on the NTU and Drive&Act datasets confirm the effectiveness of our approach. CMVRA achieves a 3.01% improvement in 3D skeleton-based activity recognition on Drive&Act, outperforming state-of-the-art methods. Experiments on NTU show that CMVRA closes the gap between self-supervised and supervised learning methods. These results highlight the potential of self-supervised multi-modal activity recognition and emphasize the benefits of leveraging contrastive learning across diverse modalities. Our findings suggest promising directions for future research, particularly in multi-modal contrastive learning and the integration of vision-language models for action recognition. Our code and the generated video captions will be made available on GitHub.

title: Calibrated, Physics-Informed Conformal Fusion for Smart-City RF-EMF Compliance Mapping, Monitoring, and Decision Support
authors: Jie Tian, Zhaojie Liang, Yan Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Communication (ICAIRC)
abstract: As 5G/NR networks densify, municipalities need exposure maps with calibrated uncertainty to assess compliance. This paper introduces PhysConf-Fusion, a modular fusion-and-calibration approach for tabular RF-EMF mapping. The method augments standard covariates with physics-aware morphology proxies (e.g., sky-view factor, occlusion, LoS/NLoS, 2D/3D distance), applies lightweight post hoc probabilistic calibration, and uses split/Mondrian conformal prediction to deliver blockwise valid prediction intervals or label sets. Tree ensembles, tabular transformers, and geostatistical learners can be combined via uncertainty-weighted fusion or a small gating network; base learners are calibrated post hoc, without retraining. The approach is evaluated on 45,865 geo-referenced measurements from two base-station datasets and a dense mobile campaign. Under a leave-one-district-out protocol with a 300 m buffer, PhysConf-Fusion attains target coverages 0.90 and 0.95 while producing narrower intervals than competitive baselines (18-30% reduction), improved calibration (expected calibration error, ECE≈0.028), and higher decision utility for Top-K re-measurement. Gains are most pronounced in high-exposure locations and NLoS street canyons. Overall, the results point to a practical path toward calibrated, auditable exposure analytics for city-scale compliance assessments. In addition, outputs are provided as block-level, conformal indicators suitable for long-term environmental monitoring (e.g., ITU-T K.83) and weekly re-measurement scheduling.

title: Generalizable Visual Localization for Gaussian Splatting Scene Representations
authors: Fadi Khatib, Dror Moran, Guy Trostianetsky, Yoni Kasten, Meirav Galun, Ronen Basri
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: We introduce GSVisLoc, a visual localization method designed for 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) scene representations. Given a 3DGS model of a scene and a query image, our goal is to estimate the camera's position and orientation. We accomplish this by robustly matching scene features to image features. Scene features are produced by downsampling and encoding the 3D Gaussians while image features are obtained by encoding image patches. Our algorithm proceeds in three steps, starting with coarse matching, then fine matching, and finally by applying pose refinement for an accurate final estimate. Importantly, our method leverages the explicit 3DGS scene representation for visual localization without requiring modifications, retraining, or additional reference images. We evaluate GSVisLoc on both indoor and outdoor scenes, demonstrating competitive localization performance on standard benchmarks while outperforming existing 3DGS-based baselines. Moreover, our approach gen-eralizes effectively to novel scenes without additional training.

title: 3DGS-to-PC: 3D Gaussian Splatting to Dense Point Clouds
authors: Lewis A G Stuart, Andrew Morton, Ian Stavness, Michael P Pound
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) excels at producing highly detailed 3D reconstructions, but these scenes often require specialised renderers for effective visualisation. In contrast, point clouds are a widely used 3D representation and are compatible with most popular 3D processing soft-ware packages. In this work, we introduce 3DGS-to-PC, a highly customisable framework for transforming 3DGS scenes into dense, accurate point clouds. We first render new Gaussian colours by calculating the visual contributions that each Gaussian made to each pixel, and then as-signing these Gaussians with the colour of the pixel they contributed most to. We then remove Gaussians that contribute little to the scene, reducing noise. Finally, we gen-erate new points by sampling from each Gaussian as a mul-tivariate normal distribution, while removing erroneously generated points. The number of points per Gaussian is determined based on its relative scale and pixel contributions. The result is a dense point cloud that accurately represents each scene. At time of writing, there is no ded-icated method for converting a 3DGS scene into a point cloud. We evaluate our method against 3DGS meshing techniques - from which points can be sampled - and show that our method is competitive with state-of-the-art approaches that require training of the scene, while performing more efficiently than standard meshing methods, such as Pois-son Surface Reconstruction. 3DGS-to-PC is efficient, typically taking under a minute to process complex scenes, thus providing an effective tool for converting 3DGS data into robust point clouds. Our codebase can be accessed via https://github.com/Lewis-Stuart-11/3DGS-to-PC.

title: Ensemble learning model using Inception-v4 and Dense Net for early detection of rice diseases
authors: Srihari.ch, Sumesh E P
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: More than half of the world’s population depends on rice as their main food supply in many nations and is extremely vulnerable to a wide range of pests and diseases. Disease control is a crucial part of sustainable rice production, which affects the accuracy and reliability of categorization systems—a challenging and intricate task in the similarities between visual symptoms across many diseases. The heterogeneity in disease symptoms throughout various growth stages might show up in a variety of ways depending on factors like temperature, humidity, soil quality and plant development. Without early detection, these diseases might result in significant yield loss. Existing models like VGG16 remains performing baseline for rice disease classification, but is often outperformed by lighter due to computational constrains. Inception-v4 and Dense Net, two deep learning models, are popular automated tools for accurately classifying and identifying rice leaf diseases. Inception v4 can detect complex disease patterns, such Bacterial Blight or Rice Blast, by recording multi-scale features that are indicative of rice disease symptoms, including uneven patterns, large lesions, and microscopic spots. Dense Net is Ideal for retaining high accuracy with few samples to practice and learning fine-grained leaf characteristics. High classification accuracy is enabled by the suggested combination of Inception V3 and Dense Net, which may greatly enhance early identification, tracking, reduce crop loss. The claimed results are as high as 91.63% accuracy, 94.55% sensitivity a custom dataset. These results show a significant improvement in rice disease prediction and detection when compare with existing models

title: The Social Life of Industrial Arms: How Arousal and Attention Shape Human-Robot Interaction
authors: Roy EI-Helou, Matthew K.X.J. Pan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: This study explores how human perceptions of a non-anthropomorphic robotic manipulator can be shaped by two key dimensions of behaviour: arousal, defined as the robot's movement energy and expressiveness, and attention, defined as the robot's capacity to selectively orient toward and engage with a user. We present an integrated behaviour system that applies and extends existing movement-centric design principles to non-anthropomorphic robots. Our system combines a gaze-like attention engine with an arousal-modulated motion layer to explore how expressive and interactive behaviours influence social perception in robotic manipulators. In a user study, we find that robots exhibiting high attention-actively directing their focus toward users-are perceived as warmer and more competent, intentional, and lifelike. In contrast, high arousal-characterized by fast, expansive, and energetic motions-increases perceptions of discomfort and disturbance. Importantly, a combination of focused attention and moderate arousal yields the highest ratings of trust and sociability, while excessive arousal diminishes social engagement. These findings offer design insights for endowing non-humanoid robots with expressive, intuitive behaviours that support more natural human-robot interaction.

title: Multi-Disease Risk Prediction: Leveraging Machine Learning for Heart Disease and Diabetes Prediction
authors: Rohit Kanauzia, Mridula, Kamal Kumar Gola, Renu Bahuguna, Mansi, Sidak Singh
publish: 2025 1st IEEE Uttar Pradesh Section Women in Engineering International Conference on Electrical Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPWIECON)
abstract: Heart disease is one of the leading causes of death worldwide, affecting primarily middle aged and older adults, and men are more likely to be affected than women. The International Diabetes Federation also states that there are 382 million people with diabetes worldwide. That number is also expected to rise to 592 million by the year of 2035. In this research study we will present a machine learning model that predicts heart disease and diabetes using data from Kaggle, Data World and the UCI repository. We were operating under the empirical goal of developing a model that could help us when attempting to predict heart disease and diabetes, so that fewer human beings die from each respectively. Our aim contrived the development of a model that is expected to gain a higher percentage of accuracy by combining the results of several machine learning techniques. The machine learning techniques used were KNN, logistic regression, random forest, SVM and finally gradient- boosting. The ultimate conception of our research study was to determine the best model to use when predicting heart disease and diabetes.

title: Optically Transparent Intelligent Reflecting Surface Assisted 5G Network Coverage Enhancement
authors: Zhuo Huang, Mustafa K. Taher Al-Nuaimi, Xin-Yu Liu, Guan-Long Huang
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: The deployment of intelligent reflecting surfaces (IRS) has emerged as a promising solution for enhancing wireless communication performance, particularly in dense urban environments. However, conventional IRS implementations are physically opaque, limiting their application in infrastructure such as building windows or vehicle windshields. In this paper, an optically transparent IRS based on indium tin oxide (ITO) is proposed to improve 5G network coverage. To address the high surface impedance and reflection losses associated with ITO films, a hybrid dielectric substrate and air gap design are introduced. The proposed IRS employs two distinct unit cell structures to achieve a full 360° phase shift range while maintaining reflection losses below −4 dB across all states. A transparent IRS panel comprising 40 × 40 unit cells (196 mm × 196 mm) was designed. Simulation results in the millimeter-wave band (24–30 GHz) demonstrate the system's ability to reflect signals from a hypothetical 5G base station located 12 meters away, redirecting them from an incident angle of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\theta_{i} =-15^{\circ}$</tex> to a reflection angle of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\theta_{r}=37^{\circ}$</tex>.

title: Artificial Intelligence-Driven Weather Forecasting: A Deep Learning Foundation Model with Physics-Informed Attention for High-Resolution Meteorological Prediction
authors: Jiedi Chen
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: Short-term weather forecasting faces fundamental challenges in capturing complex atmospheric dynamics from high-frequency observations while maintaining thermodynamic consistency. We present a comprehensive deep learning framework that integrates hierarchical temporal embedding, transformer-based foundation model adaptation, and probabilistic ensemble prediction for meteorological time series forecasting. Our methodology introduces physics-aware attention mechanisms that encode domain knowledge including temperature-humidity coupling and pressure-wind geostrophic balance, combined with Low-Rank Adaptation for parameter-efficient fine-tuning. Evaluated on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}$</tex>-minute resolution measurements spanning 20 atmospheric variables, our approach achieves superior performance in synoptic-scale circulation prediction and extreme precipitation forecasting at 5-day lead times. The foundation model demonstrates interpretable attention patterns aligned with established physical relationships, while the dynamic ensemble framework maintains scale-appropriate uncertainty quantification across forecast horizons through conformal prediction intervals.

title: Visual Design from Cultural Heritage through Inpainting-Based Restoration
authors: Jungmin Lee, Guillaume Mougeot
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: We explore an inpainting-based restoration framework for generating commercial designs from cultural heritage paintings. Cultural heritage paintings often exhibit accumulated damage, including surface contamination, color alteration, and physical deterioration, which compromises the quality of generative design outputs when processed directly. Our approach addresses this challenge by preprocessing damaged paintings through inpainting techniques before applying generative design algorithms. We employ damage-specific restoration strategies that include cleaning to remove surface contamination, coloring to restore the paint layer, and pattern extraction to isolate decorative elements. Through comparative analysis of designs generated from damaged originals and restored paintings, we demonstrate that inpainting preprocessing significantly enhances visual quality while preserving cultural authenticity. The restored designs show improved commercial viability and meet contemporary design market standards. This approach bridges historical preservation with modern design innovation, offering market-ready designs that maintain rich cultural resonance in diverse applications.

title: Characteristics of Low-Level Winds in Zhejiang Province in Spring and Application of artificial intelligence methods in Correction of Low-Level Wind Speed Forecasts
authors: Xiaoni Liang, Jun Lei, Fengxue Qiao, Nan Fang, Yang Ding, Wencan Zhao, Jiaxing Liu
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: This study utilizes data from 17 wind profiler radars across Zhejiang Province to analyze the characteristics of low-level wind fields under different topographical and geomorphological conditions during spring. By calculating the springtime average wind speed distribution at various altitudes from 2022 to 2024, as well as examining the temporal variation in vertical wind profiles, wind shear coefficients, and statistical characteristics of low-level jets, a comprehensive analysis of the low-level wind field in Zhejiang Province under different terrain conditions is conducted. Analysis shows that in mountainous regions, represented by the Kaihua station, the low-level wind speeds at all heights are generally lower due to surface friction and the blocking effect of hills and mountains; however, the wind shear coefficient is higher and low-level jets are less frequent. Using a random forest algorithm and incorporating wind pro filer radar data along with topographic factors, a correction model for wind speed forecasts was developed for the Zhejiang Province short-term numerical weather prediction system. The explained variance of the corrected wind speed can reach up to 0.75.

title: Multi-Layered Defense Proxy for Home Assistant utilizing Large Language Models
authors: Faran Yazdani, Stephen Antezana, Mohamed Gebril, Sherif Abdelhamid
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on AI in Cybersecurity (ICAIC)
abstract: The ever-increasing integration of large language models, LLMs, in IoT environments necessitates the implementation of robust security measures. Because such systems have access to physical devices, attacks against them can have real-world consequences. Therefore, to address the growing need for security in this realm, we propose a proxy that leverages LLMs to detect prompt injection attempts in a home IoT setting. In this paper, various methods of performing prompt injections on LLMs that have been given access to control physical IoT devices in a home are investigated. We will discuss the relationship between our work and related efforts aimed at securing LLMs, and provide an overview of the contributions this work makes to the field. We will discuss the benefits of our approach and briefly outline potential extensions to our work, both in the realm of IoT devices and in other contexts where LLMs are used.

title: A Hybrid PKPD-Machine Learning Framework for Precision Dosing and Drug Repurposing: Advancing AI Applications in Personalized Pharmacotherapy
authors: Baraniraj Selvarasu, Nishikant A Ingole
publish: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)
abstract: Developing new drugs takes a long time, costs a lot, and often fails. Because of this, drug repurposing and personalised dosing are becoming more popular. Artificial intelligence (AI) helps by utilising large datasets, such as medical records, genetics, and scientific articles, to identify new applications for existing drugs and optimise dosing. This review examines various AI tools, including neural networks, reinforcement learning, language processing, and graph models. One model by Hughes et al. (2023) is especially useful. It combines drug behaviour models with machine learning to help treat cancer. Using data from over 9,000 patients, it predicts serious side effects from chemo more accurately than older models. It's also easier for doctors to understand and use. Still, challenges remain. These include messy data, complex rules, difficult-to-understand AI models, and high computational requirements. However, the scope of AI in this field remains vast, with emerging hybrid models bridging the gap between mechanistic understanding and data-driven prediction. This study highlights the importance of integrating physiological insights with AI methodologies to drive clinically actionable, patient-specific dosing and drug repurposing strategies. As regulatory frameworks and validation practices evolve, AIenabled pharmacotherapy holds the potential to revolutionise individualised care in precision medicine.

title: CAB-DRL: A Context-Aware Budget-Constrained Approach for Real-Time Bidding
authors: Anurag Pathak, Dilip Kumar Sharma, Anant Kumar
publish: 2025 14th International Conference on System Modeling &amp; Advancement in Research Trends (SMART)
abstract: Real-Time Bidding (RTB) is the core of programmatic advertising in the present-day market, allowing advertisers to vie for impressions in auctions which are highly dynamic and change very rapidly on a minute-by-minute basis. While conventional methods use heuristic bidding strategies or tabular reinforcement learning (RL) that have limitations in terms of generalization, temporal awareness, and budget constraints in largescale, volatile environments, this paper goes beyond those and puts forward Context-Aware Budget-Constrained Deep Reinforcement Learning (CAB-DRL). It is a multi-agent RL framework for RTB that utilizes the iPinYou dataset. CAB-DRL not only captures temporal context and budget pacing but also accommodates continuous action spaces and stability measures in order to resolve eight issues posed by RTB: agent warmstart, effective training and generalization, safety in decision-making, batching to reduce noise, handling continuous bid values, temporal auction dynamics, advanced reward shaping, and large state spaces management. The method introduced uses actorcritic methods with recurrent layers for temporal state encoding, context embeddings for user and auction features, and a budget-constrained controller to ensure fiscal discipline while at the same time optimizing business-oriented metrics such as Click-Through Rate (CTR) and Return on Investment(ROI).

title: Research on Cypher Expansion Order Optimization Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Siqi Liu, Chuan Hu, Zhihong Shen
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: With the widespread adoption of graph databases, Cypher has emerged as a predominant query language. Query execution time is highly sensitive to the path expansion order, where traditional rule-based and cost-based optimizers often fail to find optimal plans for complex patterns. Although machine learning has shown promise for SQL join order optimization, these methods cannot be directly applicable to Cypher. To address this gap, we introduce a novel approach that leverages Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) to optimize the Cypher expansion order. We formulate this task as a sequential decision problem and train a Deep Q-Network (DQN) agent to select an efficient expansion sequence. A key aspect of our work is the modeling of Cypher query features into vector representations, which are then used to train the model. We evaluated our method on a real-world graph dataset, and the experimental results demonstrate that our approach significantly reduces query execution times, validating the effectiveness and efficiency of the proposed solution.

title: Weighed Quantum Wolf Optimization with ET Analysis for Accurate Sugarcane Crop Disease Identification
authors: V. Sharmila, P. Ezhumalai, D.M.Kalai Selvi, Ch.V.Rakesh, Vamsi Kiran. K, Srinadh Babu. T
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: Sugarcane is a vital cash crop but is highly vulnerable to various diseases that significantly affect productivity and economic returns. Existing disease detection methods rely on manual inspection, which is tedious, error-prone, and inefficient, particularly for early-stage diagnosis. To overcome these challenges, this study proposes a Weighed Quantum Wolf Optimization with Evapotranspiration Assessment (WQWO-EA) framework for precise detection of sugarcane crop viruses. The framework integrates Evapotranspiration (ET) data to assess plant water stress, a key factor influencing disease progression, with DL-based feature extraction. WQWO is employed to optimally tune hyper parameters and select the most relevant features, thereby improving diagnostic performance. The main objectives are to: (i) develop an automated system capable of reliably identifying multiple sugarcane diseases, (ii) enhance model accuracy through WQWO optimization, and (iii) incorporate environmental factors such as ET to refine predictive capability. High-resolution leaf images and ET data are pre-processed and fed into an enhanced DL architecture. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework outperforms conventional approaches, offering more accurate, consistent, and robust disease detection. This study highlights the significance of integrating DL techniques, environmental indicators, and bio-inspired optimization algorithms to enable early intervention, improve disease management, and promote the sustainability of sugarcane production.

title: A Study of an IPCVS-Based Feeding Method for Assistive Feeding Robots
authors: Yitao Chen, Haoqi Huang, Weiming Wu, Binshan Hu
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: In current assistive feeding systems, once a food item is picked up, the robot must align with the user's mouth quickly and precisely to enable feeding. Closed-loop control with visual feedback can perform this alignment reliably and efficiently, substantially improving practical utility. To fully exploit visual servoing, we introduce Image-Pose Coupled Visual Servoing (IPCVS), which combines IBVS's close-range accuracy with PBVS's faster far-range convergence and is designed for small, low-cost platforms. Compared with standalone PBVS or IBVS under identical experimental conditions, our method increases the feeding success rate by 10 %, improves overall motion speed by over 9 %, and reduces the final alignment error by about 59.4 %, thereby markedly improving mouth localization accuracy. These results indicate that IPCVS enhances the accuracy, speed, and robustness of visual servoing in assistive feeding, offering a practical pathway for human-robot interaction in complex mealtime environments.

title: Identification and Prediction of Material Flow Chain in Manufacturing System Based on Deep Learning
authors: Yuanhao Li, Qiwei Xu, Hao Bai, Yifan Pang
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Robotics, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (RICAI)
abstract: In the context of Industry 4.0 and intelligent manufacturing, workshop level manufacturing systems face challenges such as equipment information isolation and data flow barriers, resulting in insufficient global observability of the system and difficulties in optimizing production efficiency. This paper proposes a matrix reconstruction method for workshop manufacturing systems based on deep learning and complex network theory to address the identification and prediction of material flow links within a single workshop. By constructing a workshop level multi-layer perception architecture for industrial Internet of Things, based on observable material flow data between devices, deep learning models are used to accurately infer the material workpiece conversion relationship within the workshop and reconstruct key manufacturing matrices. This method includes four core modules: matrix learning, dynamic feature fusion, device network modeling, and production optimization. Through system level modeling and multi-layer coupling, it achieves a complete link reconstruction from the overall production network of the workshop to the manufacturing parameters at the unit device level. The experimental results show that this method can effectively identify and predict the material flow path in the workshop manufacturing system, providing reliable technical support for building a transparent, collaborative, and overall optimized intelligent workshop.

title: Anatomy-Informed Facial Region Segmentation and SE-Enhanced VGG-19 for Expression Recognition
authors: Jiarui Yu, Ningwei Xu, Ki-Young Song
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: This study presents a region-based facial expression recognition (FER) pipeline that augmented the classical VGG-19 backbone with lightweight channel-wise feature recalibration. The proposed approach integrated two key components. First, a landmark-guided segmentation strategy decomposes each face into 24 anatomically defined regions of interest, allowing the model to learn localized, expression-relevant cues and providing interpretable links between predictions and facial parts. Second, we refine the VGG-19 architecture by Batch Normalization, Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) blocks, and a streamlined GlobalAveragePooling-based classifier, reducing the parameter counts by 89.7% while improving feature discriminability. Comprehensive experiments on two datasets demonstrated that region-based input significantly improves accuracy and consistency over full-face input, boosting mean accuracy from 64.9% to 82.9% and yielding the lowest coefficient of variation (CV) and interquartile range (IQR) among compared strong CNN baseline models. Ablation studies confirm the complementary impact of both contributions, with fine facial segmentation capturing subtle muscular variations, and the SE-based structure yielding the largest single performance gain (F <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+8.6$</tex> percentage points). These findings suggest that combining anatomically informed input with efficient attention mechanisms enables interpretable, high-performance FER suitable for real-time human-computer interaction under practical constraints.

title: Multiphysics Design & Analysis of Silver-Based Low-Emissivity Coating Technology for Energy Saving Sustainable Windows Applications
authors: L. Salman, D. Mateychuk, K. Ghaffari, A. Leger, A. Pati
publish: 2024 IEEE MTT-S International Conference on Numerical Electromagnetic and Multiphysics Modeling and Optimization (NEMO)
abstract: Modern energy saving windows, using low-emissivity (Low-E) technology, are unique and cost-effective solution. This manufacturing technology has been utilized in buildings and has increasingly been recommended in the transportation sector with mobile communication enablement. The presented work will leverage the utilization of physics-based simulation to characterize both the high frequency electromagnetic and optical performance for silver-based low-E Coating designs. Validation with published measurements for double and triple silver coatings are presented. Wave optics and ray tracing analysis are combined to account for sub-micron features while modeling the larger window models. In addition, high frequency analysis is performed to characterize the signal attenuation through the windows in realistic scenarios.

title: Asymmetric Convolutional Depth Network for Sparse LiDAR Completion in Mobile Robotics
authors: Zhiyuan Zhang, Jun Liang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Accurate dense depth estimation from sparse LiDAR measurements is a fundamental challenge in mobile robotics, particularly for service robots, delivery robots, and autonomous mobile platforms operating in complex indoor and outdoor environments. The economic constraints of deploying high-resolution LiDAR sensors in cost-sensitive robotic applications necessitate efficient depth completion methods that can work with affordable, low-resolution sensors. In this work, we present ACDNet (Asymmetric Convolutional Depth Network), a novel architecture designed to address the sparse-to-dense depth completion problem for robotic perception through principled multi-modal fusion and architectural innovation. Specifically, ACDNet introduces three core contributions: (1) an asymmetric convolutional decomposition framework that reduces parameter complexity while effectively capturing the structural regularities prevalent in robotic operating environments, including both indoor scenes with dominant horizontal/vertical structures and outdoor terrains with varying geometric patterns; (2) a frequency-domain adaptive attention mechanism that leverages spectral analysis to selectively enhance depth-relevant features critical for robotic navigation and manipulation tasks; and (3) a hierarchical spatial propagation network with learnable affinity matrices, enabling efficient aggregation of sparse depth cues across multiple scales essential for obstacle detection and path planning. The effectiveness of ACDNet is validated through comprehensive experiments on standard depth completion benchmarks and robotic-specific scenarios, with theoretical analysis elucidating the advantages of each architectural component. These results demonstrate that ACDNet provides a robust and computationally efficient solution for dense depth estimation in resource-constrained mobile robotic systems.

title: SAM- and μSAM- Based Inference of Nuclear Materials Processing History from SEM Imagery
authors: Mayolo Valencia Mendoza, Alexei N. Skurikhin, Judith D. Cohn, Luther McDonald, Kari Sentz
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Particle and microstructure morphology of nuclear materials, as observed in scanning electron microscopy (SEM), is an emerging signature with the potential to assist with the identification of key factors of processing history. We introduce a method for inferring the calcination temperature of nuclear materials without dependence on incomplete or subjective image annotations. Leveraging foundation segmentation models, Segment Anything Model (SAM) [23] and its microscopy-tuned variant, μSAM [7], we aggregate shape features from all segmented particles in each image. In contrast to previous supervised approaches that trained models to detect only a small set of particles similar to those in the labeled training set, our method exploits the statistical benefits of large sample sizes derived from all segmented particles in the image and enables the extraction of statistical summaries of particle shape characteristics that reflect underlying processing conditions. These statistical summaries are sufficiently distinctive that logistic regression can successfully categorize calcination temperature classes with high accuracy. In evaluations on a dataset of nuclear materials imagery, our approach achieved 92.1% ± 4.3% accuracy in predicting calcination temperatures categories, outperforming a previous alternative method by 8.8%.

title: Gait Planning and Stability Analysis for Multi-Legged Robots with Varying Leg Configurations
authors: Manjia Su, Sihang Zheng, Ruiwei Liu, Haoyu Yang, Xinming Li, Chaoda Chen, Jian Teng, Nvjie Ma, Shichao Gu, Yisheng Guan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Multi-legged robots exhibit exceptional adaptability and stability in complex environments. However, maintaining locomotion capability when legs are damaged or missing poses significant challenges. This study focuses on a hexapod biomimetic spider robot and proposes a gait planning and stability analysis method applicable to varying numbers of legs. For hexapod robots, three gaits are considered; for pentapod robots (one leg missing), three gaits are analyzed; and for quadruped robots (two legs missing), two gaits are examined. The Center of Gravity (CoG) projection range for stable postures is determined by integrating the CoG projection method with the SP method. Corresponding gait optimization and adjustment strategies are proposed. To validate the proposed method, both simulation platforms and physical robots were developed and tested. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach enables the hexapod robot to maintain good locomotion stability even when legs are missing.

title: Study on Structural Stability of Photovoltaic Supports
authors: Long Li, Gang Li, Sicong Xu, Guobin Dai
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy Power and Electrical Technology (CEPET)
abstract: With the transformation of the global energy structure, photovoltaic power generation, as an important form of clean energy, has achieved rapid development. As a key supporting structure of the photovoltaic system, the structural stability of the photovoltaic bracket is crucial to the safety and reliability of the entire system.Photovoltaic brackets are usually made of high-strength aluminum alloy or steel, and possess properties such as corrosion resistance, wind and snow resistance, so as to maintain stability under various harsh weather conditions.Starting from the structural characteristics of photovoltaic brackets, this paper conducts an in-depth analysis of the factors affecting their stability, including load factors, material properties, geometric parameters and structural details. Combined with finite element simulation and engineering case analysis, the paper proposes optimized design methods and stability improvement strategies, such as strengthening the structural construction of the support frame, appropriately increasing the value of the main control load that affects the stability of the support, and enhancing the conceptual design of the structure provide a theoretical basis and technical support for the design and construction of photovoltaic supports.

title: A Transmissive Metasurface for Generating Broad-Band High-Order Bessel Beams with Stable Non-Diffractive Distance
authors: Dianshan Tang, Shuai Huang, Ran Zhang, Tongxing Huang, Xiaofan Xu, Zewei Wu
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: This paper proposes a phase optimization method for generating broadband high-order Bessel beams with a metasurface. The array is divided into two sub-regions and each designed with a distinct operating frequency to achieve broad-band performance. The designed metasurface unit achieves full 360° phase shift while maintaining a transmittance above −3 dB in the operating bandwidth. Full-wave simulation shows that the metasurface can generate a first-order Bessel beam with stable non-diffractive distance across 18–28 GHz (43.5% fractional bandwidth), while maintaining a mode purity above 85%.

title: Irregular Subarray Design for Large-Scale Phased Array Antennas Based on Hierarchical Topology Optimization
authors: Qianlei Zhuang, Pengfei Gu, Siyu Wu, Yu Qiao, Dazhi Ding
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: Based on the irregular subarray design technique for phased array antennas, this paper innovatively proposes a hierarchical topology optimization model. This model equivalently transforms the subarray design problem for large-scale phased arrays into two progressive subproblems: a module-level topology optimization based on a low-predictability model and a subarray-level topology optimization based on a tri-directional maximum entropy model. This approach effectively addresses the difficulty of designing subarray partitions for large-scale arrays. While ensuring array gain and overall performance, it reduces the number of required T/R components to 50% of that in an equally large conventional array and lowers the design complexity to 1/24 of the original level, thereby significantly cutting the cost of phased arrays. Numerical validation on a phased array with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N=576$</tex> elements shows that after applying the proposed subarray partitioning method, the sidelobe level increases by only 0.15 dB, and the directivity decreases by merely 0.88 dBi, meeting the design requirements.

title: Research on Sensitive HHT Feature Extraction of Transient Operation Degradation in Ship Power Systems
authors: Zhen Zhang, Weifeng Shi
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: Transient degradation faults exist in the power system of ships. This paper focuses on extracting the degradation features of the operating state based on the Hilbert-Huang Transform (HHT) method. A sensitive HHT feature extraction technology for degradation in the operating state is proposed. By selecting the intrinsic mode function (IMF) characteristics that are sensitive to the degradation state as the analysis basis, and by evaluating the similarity between the vibration signal and its corresponding intrinsic mode function, as well as the similarity between the intrinsic mode function of the vibration signal during degradation and the normal signal, effective processing of transient signals is achieved. The entire research process is completed using the MATLAB-Simulink platform for signal simulation and feature extraction experiments. Through comparative studies of the original signal containing fault information and its processed version, the superior performance of the proposed method in terms of accuracy and stability is verified.

title: Robust Experts: the Effect of Adversarial Training on CNNs with Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Layers
authors: Svetlana Pavlitska, Haixi Fan, Konstantin Ditschuneit, J. Marius Zöllner
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Robustifying convolutional neural networks (CNNs) against adversarial attacks remains challenging and often requires resource-intensive countermeasures. We explore the use of sparse mixture-of-experts (MoE) layers to improve robustness by replacing selected residual blocks or convolutional layers, thereby increasing model capacity without additional inference cost. On ResNet architectures trained on CIFAR-100, we find that inserting a single MoE layer in the deeper stages leads to consistent improvements in robustness under PGD and AutoPGD attacks when combined with adversarial training. Furthermore, we discover that when switch loss is used for balancing, it causes routing to collapse onto a small set of overused experts, thereby concentrating adversarial training on these paths and inadvertently making them more robust. As a result, some individual experts outperform the gated MoE model in robustness, suggesting that robust subpaths emerge through specialization. Our code is available at https://github.com/KASTEL-MobilityLab/robust-sparse-moes.

title: Knowledge-Driven Query Network with Adaptive Cross-View Attention for Structured Radiology Report Generation
authors: Xuege Hou, Yali Li, Shengjin Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: Automatically generating chest radiology reports can significantly enhance radiologists' efficiency and assist less experienced practitioners in avoiding misdiagnoses. However, current methods, which are typically trained from scratch on limited datasets, often struggle with aligning image-text information and producing comprehensive reports. Large language models, with extensive pre-training on diverse datasets, have the potential to improve the quality and coherence of text generation tasks. Although recent LLM-based approaches have successfully incorporated domain-specific knowledge to describe medical findings, they still face challenges in integrating image features with structured clinical information to produce coherent and clinically relevant reports. To address these issues, we propose a Structured Radiology Report Generation (SRRG) method, which incorporates a Knowledge-Driven Query Network (KDQN) and Adaptive Cross-View Attention (ACVA). KDQN directs the model's attention using specific medical semantics, enabling a deeper understanding of intricate structures and abnormalities within medical images. Additionally, the ACVA mechanism dynamically adjusts the weighting of information from frontal and lateral views, allowing the model to capture more spatial and structural details, thus generating more comprehensive image descriptions. Extensive experiments on MIMIC-CXR and IU X-RAY demonstrate that SRRG achieves state-of-the-art performance, outperforming prior methods by 3.2% BLEU-4 and 1.72% Precision. Notably, SRRG generates reports with improved clinical interpretability, as supported by feedback from board-certified radiologists, and accurately localizes lesions without pixel-level supervision.

title: A Near-Field-to-Far-Field Transform (NFFFT) for RCS Determination Using Monostatic Data
authors: Wen-Chi Huang, Wen-Wei Zhang, Jia-Ning Cao, Jia-Qi Kang, Ming-Yao Xia
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: A near-field-to-far-field transformation (NFFFT) algorithm for RCS determination by monostatic measurements is proposed. Based on prior knowledge of the target, an equivalent surface enveloping the target is constructed, and the scattered field is expressed by an induced effective current on the surface. The effective current is formally obtainable from the tangential components of incident electric and magnetic fields through a pair of kernel functions that are inherent for the target. The discretized kernel function, termed inherent feature parameters (IFPs), are then solved using monostatic data within a scanning airspace. Last, the RCS can be determined using the IFPs by setting the incident field to be a plane wave. Simulation results show that the proposed approach is feasible for complex targets.

title: Multimodal Decision Making System for LBW Outs in Cricket
authors: Naman Bhalotia, Hitesh Mohapatra, Asish Kumar Dalai, Udit Narayana Kar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Signal Processing (AISP)
abstract: Leg Before Wicket (LBW) decisions in cricket remain one of the most challenging and contentious aspects of the game, often influencing match outcomes. Human umpires, despite their expertise, are prone to perceptual and situational errors due to the high speed of play and complex ball bat pad interactions. Modern Decision Review Systems (DRS) integrate multiple sensing technologies such as Hawk Eye ball tracking, Snickometer audio analysis, and Hot Spot infrared imaging to aid in objective decision making. However, reliance on a single modality can lead to inconsistent results in marginal calls. This paper presents a multimodal decision making framework that fuses visual tracking, acoustic profiling, and thermal imaging to enhance LBW adjudication accuracy. The proposed approach leverages computer vision techniques for ball trajectory prediction, audio signal processing for bat-ball contact detection, and thermal pattern recognition for impact localization. Experimental results on match footage demonstrate improved consistency and reduced false positives compared to single technology systems. Such a unified system has the potential to significantly improve fairness and transparency in cricket officiating.

title: Mask-Guided Multi-Task nnU-Net for Automated Segmentation and Classification of Blunt Traumatic Aortic Injury
authors: Yue Cao, Yi Sun, Xiao-Hu Zhou, Mei-Jiang Gui, Tian-Yu Xiang, De-Xing Huang, Bo-Xian Yao, Xiao-Liang Xie, Shi-Qi Liu, Jiang Xiong, Zeng-Guang Hou
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Blunt thoracic aortic injury (BTAI) is a lifethreatening vascular emergency with high mortality rates, where rapid and accurate diagnosis from computed tomography angiography (CTA) is critical for patient survival. However, the inherent complexity of radiological interpretation and its dependence on clinician expertise often lead to diagnostic delays in acute trauma settings. To address these clinical challenges, we propose a mask-guided multi-task nnU-Net framework for automated BTAI diagnosis and assessment. The framework jointly performs lesion segmentation and subtype classification through a shared encoder architecture, while a novel segmentationguided classification branch leverages spatial lesion information to enhance subtype prediction accuracy. Our method, evaluated on a dataset of 59 cases spanning three BTAI subtypes (intimal tear/dissection, pseudoaneurysm, and intramural hematoma), achieves a classification accuracy (ACC) of 91.7%, a segmentation Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC) of 88.5%, and an average inference time of just 15 seconds per case. This automated framework demonstrates substantial potential for reducing diagnostic delays in emergency settings while maintaining clinical-grade accuracy, ultimately supporting timely treatment decisions that could improve patient outcomes and reduce BTAI-associated mortality.

title: From Neural Activity to Computation: Biological Reservoirs for Pattern Recognition in Digit Classification
authors: Ludovico Iannello, Luca Ciampi, Fabrizio Tonelli, Gabriele Lagani, Lucio Maria Calcagnile, Federico Cremisi, Angelo Di Garbo, Giuseppe Amato
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: In this paper, we present a biologically grounded approach to reservoir computing (RC), in which a network of cultured biological neurons serves as the reservoir substrate. This system, referred to as biological reservoir computing (BRC), replaces artificial recurrent units with the spontaneous and evoked activity of living neurons. A multi-electrode array (MEA) enables simultaneous stimulation and readout across multiple sites: inputs are delivered through a subset of electrodes, while the remaining ones capture the resulting neural responses, mapping input patterns into a high-dimensional biological feature space. We evaluate the system through a case study on digit classification using a custom dataset. Input images are encoded and delivered to the biological reservoir via electrical stimulation, and the corresponding neural activity is used to train a simple linear classifier. To contextualize the performance of the biological system, we also include a comparison with a standard artificial reservoir trained on the same task. The results indicate that the biological reservoir can effectively support classification, highlighting its potential as a viable and interpretable computational substrate. We believe this work contributes to the broader effort of integrating biological principles into machine learning and aligns with the goals of human-inspired vision by exploring how living neural systems can inform the design of efficient and biologically plausible models.

title: HIVE: A Hyperbolic Interactive Visualization Explorer for Representation Learning
authors: Thijmen Nijdam, Derck Prinzhorn, Jurgen de Heus, Thomas Brouwer
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: We present HIVE, an interactive dashboard that supports exploration and interpretation of hyperbolic embeddings in deep learning. Hyperbolic spaces naturally capture hierarchical structure, yet existing visualization tools are either designed for Euclidean geometry or remain static when curvature is taken into account. HIVE closes this gap by offering 2D projections in the Poincaré disk and integrating configurable dimensionality-reduction algorithms for hyperbolic space, including CO-SNE and HoroPCA. From expert interviews, we distilled key analytic needs and realized them in four interaction modes: compare, traverse, tree, and neighbors. These modes enable real-time, multimodal analysis through semantic hierarchy tracing, geodesic interpolation, and projection comparison. A small but targeted user study demonstrates that HIVE supports practical analysis and uncovers meaningful hyperbolic structure. While currently limited to image and text embeddings, the dashboard shows promise for broader applications, such as reinforcement learning and graph discovery, highlighting HIVE's potential as a useful tool for future hyperbolic learning scenarios. Source code and a demo are available at https://github.com/thijmennijdam/HIVE.

title: Active Tension-Disturbance Rejection Hybrid Control for Cable Driven Manipulator Based on Learning-Optimized Dynamic Model
authors: Haobin Wang, Shuting Wang, Hongyang Zhang, Yuanlong Xie, Youmin Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Cable-driven manipulators (CDM) offer distinct advantages in confined spaces where access by driving mechanisms is restricted. However, by virtue of the flexibility of cables in the CDM structure and the joint coupling arising from the series connection of cables, dynamic modeling of cable distribution constitutes a significant challenge, while enhancing positional accuracy represents another critical issue. To address these issues, this paper proposes an active tension-disturbance rejection hybrid control for CDM based on learning-optimized dynamic model. Within this framework, an online learning method is employed to approximate dynamic compensation terms and optimize the desired cable tension in the tension control loop. Furthermore, an extended state observer is utilized to estimate the disturbances originating from the tension loop or the external environment, with active compensation employed to enhance both the control accuracy of CDM and the robustness of the position control loop. Experimental results validate that the proposed strategy effectively reduces the dynamical modeling error while simultaneously enhancing the positioning accuracy of the position control loop.

title: Dynamic Trajectory Planning for Multi-UAV Target Localization Based on CNN-LSTM Framework
authors: Haifeng Lin, Cheng Xu
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing (MLNLP)
abstract: To address the challenge of insufficient positioning accuracy in trajectory planning for target localization based on bearing-only measurements using multiple unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), this paper proposes a dynamic trajectory planning method based on the CNN-LSTM model. By establishing a linear relationship between the target and observation positions through geometric derivation, the proposed method constructs a deep learning framework that integrates spatial feature extraction and time series modeling to achieve autonomous trajectory planning. The experimental results demonstrate that compared with fixed formation configurations, the circular error probable (CEP) performance is improved by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$63.6 \%, 63.3 \%$</tex> for stationary and linear moving targets, respectively. In addition, the simulation results indicate that the UAVs can autonomously adjust the separation angle to optimize the observation geometry, thus enhancing target localization accuracy.

title: A Novel Underactuated Robot Gripper with Scott-Russell Linkages for Human-Inspired Adaptive Grasp in Environmental Constraints
authors: Shijie Qu, Che Zhang, Wenzeng Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: This paper introduces Gamma-E gripper., a novel underactuated robotic gripper designed for adaptive desktop object manipulation., including thin-object scooping. The collision-resistant design enables automatic height adaptation through table-surface interaction., triggering passive finger flexion that transitions from parallel gripping to scooping mode upon reaching critical bending angles. The mechanism integrates Scott-Russell linkages with spring constraints., driven by a single linear actuator to achieve multi-modal operation: pinching., symmetric/asymmetric scooping., and adaptive enveloping. Its compact configuration allows integration with commercial robotic arms. Leveraging linear motion characteristics of the Scott-Russell linkage and geometric constraints., the gripper adaptively handles diverse objects while accommodating environmental constraints through inclined trajectories. This enables anthropomorphic adaptation to height variations., steps., and gradients. Symmetric scooping configuration excels at large thin objects., while asymmetric tilting enhances sheet-object stability. The design provides new perspectives for precision environment-adaptive grippers., expanding potential of underactuated systems in complex manipulation scenarios.

title: Wideband Filtering Power Divider Based on Stepped-Impedance Transmission Lines and Three-Line Coupled Structures
authors: Chuan-Yun Wang, Qian Cao, Pin Wen
publish: 2025 Cross Strait Radio Science and Wireless Technology Conference (CSRSWTC)
abstract: The filtering power divider (FPD) proposed in this paper has the dual advantages of wideband and high frequency selectivity. A pair of stepped impedance transmission lines and three-line coupled structures (TLCSs) make up the proposed wideband FPD. A wideband can be achieved through the introduction of a pair of stepped impedance transmission lines. The pair of TLCS-based bandpass filters (BPFs) are respectively loaded at the port 2 and port 3, which achieves excellent filtering performance by generating transmission zeros on both sides of the passband, resulting in high frequency selectivity. A wideband FPD prototype operating at 2.86 GHz is designed. Simulated results exhibit −10 dB fractional bandwidths of 62.6%.

title: Phase Error Monitoring for CVT Based on Mode Decomposition and Local Outlier Detection
authors: Xiaodong Han, Peng Lan, Qiang Zhao, Guobin Li, Shouguo Zhao, Fenggang Sun
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy Engineering (SGEE)
abstract: The measurement accuracy of Capacitor Voltage Transformers (CVTs) is critical for power system safety and fair energy trading. However, conventional methods are often compromised by electromagnetic interference and environmental noise, degrading their long-term reliability. To address this, we propose a novel online detection method for CVT measurement errors that integrates mode decomposition with Local Outlier Factor (LOF) analysis. The methodology first employs Ensemble Empirical Mode Decomposition (EEMD) and sample entropy for multi-scale signal analysis. Bayesian wavelet denoising is then used to extract high-fidelity operational features. Finally, the LOF algorithm identifies anomalies within a feature set constructed from the CVT's ratio deviation. Experimental results demonstrate high efficacy, with a detection accuracy of 94.55% for ratio errors and an anomaly contribution rate of 95.36 %. This significant performance enhancement validates the method's effectiveness and suitability for practical application in power systems.

title: Comparative Analysis of Image-Based Deep Learning and Genomic Models for Yield and Protein Content Prediction in Winter Wheat
authors: Xiaoran Chen, Paraskevi Nousi, Mike Boss, Michele Volpi, Lukas Roth
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: We perform a comparative analysis of various image-based and genomic selection deep learning models as a benchmark on the FIP 1.0 dataset, a multimodal crop phenotyping dataset offering data collected in multiple years, including time-series imagery, agronomic traits such as yield, protein content, and heading dates, as well as genetic markers and environmental variables including temperature and precipitation. We trained widely used models, including ResNet-50, ConvNeXt, and DINOv2, and evaluated their performance on predictive tasks focused on trait estimation using image sequences and multimodal inputs that integrate imagery with plant height data. Our analysis includes vi-sualizations of temporal feature importance across growth stages and an exploration of learned embeddings to char-acterize variation across years and genotypes. This benchmark underscores both the challenges and the potential of leveraging heterogeneous data sources for crop modeling, with the goal of advancing genotype-by-environment interaction prediction and interpretation.

title: Interpretable Open-Vocabulary Referring Object Detection with Reverse Contrast Attention
authors: Drandreb Earl O. Juanico, Rowel O. Atienza, Jeffrey Kenneth Go
publish: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW)
abstract: We propose Reverse Contrast Attention (RCA), a plug-in method that enhances object localization in vision-language transformers without retraining. RCA reweights final-layer attention by suppressing extremes and amplifying mid-level activations to let semantically relevant but subdued tokens guide predictions. We evaluate it on Open Vocabulary Referring Object Detection (OV-RefOD), introducing FitAP, confidence-free average precision metric based on IoU and box area. RCA improves FitAP in 11 out of 15 open-source VLMs, with gains up to +26.6%. Effectiveness aligns with attention sharpness and fusion timing; while late-fusion models benefit consistently, models like DeepSeek-VL2 also improve, pointing to capacity and disentanglement as key factors. RCA offers both interpretability and performance gains for multimodal transformers. Codes and data set in https://github.com/earl-juanico/rca

title: Development of a Multifunctional Modular Flexible Rehabilitation Exoskeleton Based on a Retractor Module
authors: Jiadong Zhang, Haotian Ju, Weimao Wangl, Yao Huang, Tianjiao Zheng, Jie Zhao, Yanhe Zhu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Biomimetics (ROBIO)
abstract: Upper and lower extremity exoskeletons for rehabilitation of stroke patients have been extensively studied, but fewer exoskeletons are currently available for both upper and lower extremity rehabilitation. To expand the rehabilitation scope of the exoskeleton and improve its adaptability to the patient's joint movements during rehabilitation training, while avoiding the risk of rigid collision, a rope-driven flexible rehabilitation exoskeleton is designed in this paper. The main structure of the exoskeleton is composed of high-strength nylon material, which is both lightweight and durable. The core structure and load-bearing structure are made of aluminum alloy. The total weight is less than 30 kg. The drive unit is not mounted on the patient's body, but is placed on an external support frame, which allows the exoskeleton to have a larger workspace as well as be applicable to the rehabilitation training of different limbs. At the same time, the exoskeleton has a modular design, which allows the exoskeleton to be self-assembled according to different rehabilitation modes.

title: A Novel Multi-Layer Framework for Human and AI-Voice Detection Using Machine Learning and Network Architecture
authors: Simar Singh Rayat, Sujal Thapa, Raj Basnet, Vikrant Sharma, Susheela Dahiya, Arun Pratap Singh Rathod
publish: 2025 14th International Conference on System Modeling &amp; Advancement in Research Trends (SMART)
abstract: The rapid advancement in the field of Artificial Intelligence has led to the development of AI-voice systems capable of delivering informative conversations, including factual, general, and even emotionally adaptive responses. However, some individuals have exploited these systems for self-serving purposes. Traditional methods lack the capability to distinguish between human and AI voices, causing a potential leading to an increase in fraudulent and unethical activities, thereby creating an urgent need for a more advanced solution. To address this limitation, we propose our framework that integrates Machine Learning techniques with an adversarial reverse engineering framework equipped with a multi-layered decision architecture incorporating feature extraction and source utility handling. The proposed framework aims to provide real-time edge processing, equipped with adversarial robustness and supporting multilingual capabilities. It handles multiple edge cases such as data cleansing and content moderation by leveraging architectures including convolutional neural networks (abbreviated as CNN), recurrent neural networks (abbreviated as RNN), Transformers, and other specific techniques. The model was evaluated under multiple edge cases, such as content and linguistic variations, adversarial and deceptive inputs, synthetic and contextual channel effects, using publicly accessible datasets such as Standard Speech & TTS, Fake-or-Real, ASVspoof 2019, and MLAAD. The proposed framework achieved an accuracy of 92.18% for classifying human and AI voices and report generation and distinctly classified other factors such as an equal error rate (abbreviated as EER) of about 6.8%, recall of around 93.16%, and F1-score around 92.84%, confirming the effectiveness of the proposed innovative solution in both detection accuracy and robustness.
